Genesis39:11 And it fortuned about the same season, that Joseph entered into the house, to do his business: and there was none of the household by, in the house." 
Genesis39:12 And she caught him by the garment saying: come sleep with me. And he left his garment in her hand and fled and got him out. 
Genesis39:13 When she saw that he had left his garment in her hand, and was fled out," 
Genesis39:14 she called unto the men of the house, and told them saying: See, he hath brought in an Hebrew unto us to do us shame: for he came in to me, for to have slept with me. But I cried with a loud voice." 
Genesis39:15 And when he heard, that I lift up my voice and cried, he left his garment with me and fled away and got him out." 
Genesis39:16 And she laid up his garment by her, until her lord came home." 
Genesis39:17 And she told him according to these words saying: This Hebrews' servant which thou hast brought unto us came in to me to do me shame. 
Genesis39:18 But as soon as I lift up my voice and cried, he left his garment with me and fled out." 
Genesis39:19 When his master heard the words of his wife which she told him saying: after this manner did thy servant to me, he waxed wroth." 
Genesis39:20 And he took Joseph and put him in prison: even in the place where the king's prisoners lay bound. And there continued he in prison," 
Genesis39:21 but the LORD was with Joseph and shewed him mercy, and got him favour in the sight of the keeper of the prison" 
Genesis39:22 which committed to Joseph's hand all the prisoners that were in the prison house. And whatsoever was done there, that did he." 
Genesis39:23 And the keeper of the prison looked unto nothing that was under his hand, because the LORD was with him, and because that whatsoever he did, the LORD made it come luckily to pass." 
Genesis40:1 And it chanced after this, that the chief butler of the king of Egypt and his chief baker had offended their lord the king of Egypt." 
Genesis40:2 And Pharao was angry with them and put them in ward in his chief marshal's house: 
Genesis40:3 even in the prison where Joseph was bound. 
Genesis40:4 And the chief marshal gave Joseph a charge with them, and he served them. And they continued a season in ward." 
Genesis40:5 And they dreamed either of them in one night: both the butler and the baker of the king of Egypt which were bound in the prison house, either of them his dream, and each man's dream of a sundry interpretation." 
Genesis40:6 When Joseph came in unto them in the morning, and looked upon them: behold, they were sad." 
Genesis40:7 And he asked them saying, wherefore look ye so sadly today?" 
Genesis40:8 They answered him, we have dreamed a dream, and have no man to declare it. And Joseph said unto them. Interpreting belongeth to God, but tell me yet." 
Genesis40:9 And the chief butler told his dream to Joseph and said unto him: In my dream me thought there stood a vine before me," 
Genesis40:10 and in the vine were three branches, and it was as though it budded, and her blossoms shot forth: and the grapes thereof waxed ripe." 
Genesis40:11 And I had Pharao's cup in my hand, and took of the grapes and wrung them into Pharao's cup, and delivered Pharao's cup into his hand." 
Genesis40:12 And Joseph said unto him, this is the interpretation of it." 
Genesis40:13 The three branches are three days: for within three days shall Pharao lift up thine head, and restore thee unto thine office again, and thou shalt deliver Pharao's cup into his hand, after the old manner, even as thou didst when thou wast his butler." 
Genesis40:14 But think on me with thee, when thou art in good case, and shew mercy unto me. And make mention of me to Pharao, and help to bring me out of this house:" 
Genesis40:15 for I was stolen out of the land of the Hebrews, and here also have I done nothing at all wherefore they should have put me into this dungeon." 
Genesis40:16 When the chief baker saw that he had well interpreted it, he said unto Joseph, me thought also in my dream, that I had three wicker baskets on my head?" 
Genesis40:17 And in the uppermost basket, of all manner bakemeats for Pharao. And the birds ate them out of the basket upon my head." 
Genesis40:18 Joseph answered and said: this is the interpretation thereof. The three baskets are three days," 
Genesis40:19 for this day three days shall Pharao take thy head from thee, and shall hang thee on a tree, and the birds shall eat thy flesh from off thee." 
Genesis40:20 And it came to pass the third day which was Pharao's birthday, that he made a feast unto all his servants. And he lifted up the head of the chief butler and of the chief baker among his servants." 
Genesis40:21 And restored the chief butler unto his butlership again, and he reached the cup into Pharao's hand," 
Genesis40:22 and hanged the chief baker: even as Joseph had interpreted unto them. 
Genesis40:23 Notwithstanding the chief butler remembered not Joseph, but forgot him." 
Genesis41:1 And it fortuned at two years' end, that Pharao dreamed, and thought that he stood by a river's side," 
Genesis41:2 and that there came out of the river seven goodly kine and fat fleshed, and fed in a meadow." 
Genesis41:3 And him thought that seven other kine came up after them out of the river evil favored and lean fleshed and stood by the other upon the brink of the river. 
Genesis41:4 And the evil favored and lean fleshed kine: ate up the seven well favored and fat kine: and he awoke therewith. 
Genesis41:5 And he slept again and dreamed the second time, that seven ears of corn grew upon one stalk rank and goodly." 
Genesis41:6 And that seven thin ears blasted with the wind, sprang up after them:" 
Genesis41:7 and that the seven thin ears devoured the seven rank and full ears. And then Pharao awaked: and see, here is his dream." 
Genesis41:8 When the morning came, his spirit was troubled; And he sent and called for all the soothsayers of Egypt and all the wise men thereof, and told them his dream: but there was none of them that could interpret it unto Pharao." 
Genesis41:9 Then spake the chief butler unto Pharao saying: I do remember my fault this day. 
Genesis41:10 Pharao was angry with his servants, and put in ward in the chief marshal's house both me and the chief baker." 
Genesis41:11 And we dreamed both of us in one night and each man's dream of a sundry interpretation. 
Genesis41:12 And there was with us a young man, an Hebrew born, servant unto the chief marshal. And we told him, and he declared our dreams to us according to either of our dreams." 
Genesis41:13 And as he declared them unto us, even so it came to pass. I was restored to mine office again, and he was hanged." 
Genesis41:14 Then Pharao sent and called Joseph. And they made him haste out of prison. And he shaved himself and changed his raiment, and went in to Pharao." 
Genesis41:15 And Pharao said unto Joseph: I have dreamed a dream and no man can interpretate it, but I have heard say of thee that as soon as thou hearest a dream, thou dost interpretate it." 
Genesis41:16 And Joseph answered Pharao saying: God shall give Pharao an answer of peace without me. 
Genesis41:17 Pharao said unto Joseph: in my dream me thought I stood by a river's side," 
Genesis41:18 and there came out of the river seven fat fleshed and well favored kine, and fed in the meadow." 
Genesis41:19 And then seven other kine came up after them, poor and very evil favored and lean fleshed: so that I never saw their like in all the land of Egypt in evil favoredness." 
Genesis41:20 And the seven lean and evil favored kine ate up the first seven fat kine. 
Genesis41:21 And when they had eaten them up, a man could not perceive that they had eaten them: for they were still as evil favored as they were at the beginning. And I awoke." 
Genesis41:22 And I saw again in my dream seven ears spring out of one stalk full and good," 
Genesis41:23 and seven other ears withered, thin and blasted with wind, spring up after them." 
Genesis41:24 And the thin ears devoured the seven good ears. And I have told it unto the soothsayers, but no man can tell me what it meaneth." 
Genesis41:25 Then Joseph said unto Pharao: both Pharao's dreams are one. And God doth shew Pharao what he is about to do. 
Genesis41:26 The seven good kine are seven year: and the seven good ears are seven year also, and is but one dream." 
Genesis41:27 Likewise, the seven thin and evil favored kine that came out after them, are seven years: and the seven empty and blasted ears shall be seven years of hunger." 
Genesis41:28 This is that which I said unto Pharao, that God doth shew Pharao what he is about to do." 
Genesis41:29 Behold there shall come seven year of great plenteousness throughout all the land of Egypt. 
Genesis41:30 And there shall arise after them seven years of hunger. So that all the plenteousness shall be forgotten in the land of Egypt. And the hunger shall consume the land: 
Genesis41:31 so that the plenteousness shall not be once a seen in the land by reason of that hunger that shall come after, for it shall be exceeding great." 
Genesis41:32 And as concerning that the dream was doubled unto Pharao the second time, it betokeneth that the thing is certainly prepared of God, and that God will shortly bring it to pass." 
Genesis41:33 Now therefore let Pharao provide for a man of understanding and wisdom, and set him over the land of Egypt." 
Genesis41:34 And let Pharao make officers over the land, and take up the fifth part of the land of Egypt in the seven plenteous years" 
Genesis41:35 and let them gather all the food of these good years that come, and lay up corn under the power of Pharao: that there may be food in the cities," 
Genesis41:36 and there let them keep it: that there may be food in store in the land, against the seven years of hunger which shall come in the land of Egypt, and that the land perish not thorow hunger." 
Genesis41:37 And the saying pleased Pharao and all his servants. 
Genesis41:38 Then said Pharao unto his servants: where shall we find such a man as this is, that hath the spirit of God in him?" 
Genesis41:39 wherefore Pharao said unto Joseph: forasmuch as God hath shewed thee all this, there is no man of understanding nor of wisdom like unto thee." 
Genesis41:40 Thou therefore shalt be over my house, and according to thy word shall all my people obey: only in the king's seat will I be above thee." 
Genesis41:41 And he said unto Joseph: behold, I have set thee over all the land of Egypt." 
Genesis41:42 And he took off his ring from his finger, and put it upon Joseph's finger, and arrayed him in raiment of byss, and put a golden chain about his neck" 
Genesis41:43 and set him upon the best chariot that he had save one. And they cried before him Abrech, and that Pharao had made him ruler over all the land of Egypt." 
Genesis41:44 And Pharao said unto Joseph: I am Pharao, without thy will, shall no man lift up either his hand or foot in all the land of Egypt." 
Genesis41:45 And he called Joseph's name Zaphnath Paenea. And he gave him to wife Asnath the daughter of Potiphar priest of On. Then went Joseph abroad in the land of Egypt. 
Genesis41:46 And he was thirty year old when he stood before Pharao king of Egypt. And then Joseph departed from Pharao, and went thorow out all the land of Egypt." 
Genesis41:47 And in the seven plenteous years they made sheaves 
Genesis41:48 and gathered up all the food of the seven plenteous years which were in the land of Egypt and put it into the cities. And he put the food of the fields that grew round about every city: even in the same. 
Genesis41:49 And Joseph laid up corn in store, like unto the sand of the sea in multitude out of measure, until he left numbering: For it was without number." 
Genesis41:50 And unto Joseph were born two sons before the years of hunger came, which Asnath the daughter of Potiphar priest of On, bare unto him." 
Genesis41:51 And he called the name of the first son Manasse, for God (said he) hath made me forget all my labour and all my father's household." 
Genesis41:52 The second called he Ephraim, for God (said he) hath caused me to grow in the land of my trouble." 
Genesis41:53 And when the seven years of plenteousness that was in the land of Egypt were ended," 
Genesis41:54 then came the seven years of dearth, according as Joseph had said. And the dearth was in all lands: but in the land of Egypt was there yet food." 
Genesis41:55 When now all the land of Egypt began to hunger, then cried the people to Pharao for bread. And Pharao said unto all Egypt: go unto Joseph, and what he saith to you that do." 
Genesis41:56 And when the dearth was thorow out all the land, Joseph opened all that was in the cities, and sold unto the Egyptians. And hunger waxed sore in the land of Egypt." 
Genesis41:57 And all countries came to Egypt to Joseph for to buy corn: because that the hunger was so sore in all lands. 
Genesis42:1 When Jacob saw that there was corn to be sold in Egypt, he said unto his sons: why are ye negligent?" 
Genesis42:2 behold, I have heard that there is corn to be sold in Egypt. Get you thither and buy us corn from thence, that we may live and not die." 
Genesis42:3 So went Joseph's ten brethren down to buy corn in Egypt," 
Genesis42:4 for Ben Jamin Joseph's brother would not Jacob send with his other brethren: for he said: some misfortune might happen him. 
Genesis42:5 And the sons of Israel came to buy corn among other that came, for there was dearth also in the land of Canaan." 
Genesis42:6 And Joseph was governor in the land, and sold corn to all the people of the land. And his brethren came, and fell flat on the ground before him." 
Genesis42:7 When Joseph saw his brethren, he knew them: But made strange unto them, and spake roughly unto them saying: Whence come ye? and they said: out of the land of Canaan, to buy vitaille." 
Genesis42:8 Joseph knew his brethren, but they knew not him." 
Genesis42:9 And Joseph remembered his dreams which he dreamed of them, and said unto them: ye are spies, and to see where the land is weak is your coming." 
Genesis42:10 And they said unto him: nay, my lord: but to buy vitaille thy servants are come." 
Genesis42:11 We are all one man's sons, and mean truly, and thy servants are no spies." 
Genesis42:12 And he said unto them: nay verily, but even to see where the land is weak is your coming." 
Genesis42:13 And they said: we thy servants are twelve brethren, the sons of one man in the land of Canaan. The youngest is yet with our father, and one no man woteth where he is." 
Genesis42:14 Joseph said unto them, that is it that I said unto you, that ye are surely spies." 
Genesis42:15 Here by ye shall be proved. For by the life of Pharao, ye shall not go hence, until your youngest brother be come hither." 
Genesis42:16 Send therefore one of you and let him fetch your brother, and ye shall be in prison in the mean season. And thereby shall your words be proved, whether there be any truth in you: or else by the life of Pharao, ye are but spies." 
Genesis42:17 And he put them in ward three days. 
Genesis42:18 And Joseph said unto the third day: This do and live, for I fear God.(Gode)" 
Genesis42:19 If ye mean no hurt, let one of your brethren be bound in the prison, and go ye and bring the necessary food unto your households," 
Genesis42:20 and bring your youngest brother unto me: that your words may be believed, and that ye die not; And they did so." 
Genesis42:21 Then they said one to another: we have verily sinned against our brother, in that we saw the anguish of his soul when he besought us, and would not hear him: therefore is this trouble come upon us." 
Genesis42:22 Ruben answered them saying: said I not unto you that ye should not sin against the lad? but ye would not hear; And now verily see, his blood is required." 
Genesis42:23 They were not aware that Joseph understood them, for he spake unto them by an interpreter." 
Genesis42:24 And he turned from them and wept, and then turned to them again and communed with them, and took out Simeon from among them and bound him before their eyes," 
Genesis42:25 and commanded to fill their sacks with corn, and to put every man's money in his sack, and to give them vitaille to spend by the way. And so it was done to them." 
Genesis42:26 And they laded their asses with the corn and departed thence. 
Genesis42:27 And as one of them opened his sack, for to give his ass provender in the Inn, he spied his money in his sack's mouth." 
Genesis42:28 And he said unto his brethren: my money is restored me again, and is even in my sack's mouth. Then their hearts failed them, and were astonied and said one to another: how cometh it that God dealeth thus with us?" 
Genesis42:29 And they came unto Jacob their father unto the land of Canaan, and told him all that had happened them saying:" 
Genesis42:30 The lord of the land spake roughly to us, and took us for spies to search the country." 
Genesis42:31 And we said unto him: we mean truly and are no spies. 
Genesis42:32 We be twelve brethren sons of our father, one is away, and the youngest is now with our father in the land of Canaan." 
Genesis42:33 And the lord of the country said unto us: hereby shall I know if ye mean truly: leave one of your brethren here with me, and take food necessary for your households and get you away," 
Genesis42:34 and bring your youngest brother unto me; And thereby shall I know that ye are no spies, but mean truly: So will I deliver you your brother again, and ye shall occupy in the land." 
Genesis42:35 And as they emptied their sacks, behold: every man's bundle of money was in his sack. And when both they and their father saw the bundles of money, they were afraid." 
Genesis42:36 And Jacob their father said unto them: Me have ye robbed of my children: Joseph is away, and Simeon is away, and ye will take Ben Jamin away. All these things fall upon me." 
Genesis42:37 Ruben answered his father saying: Slay my two sons, if I bring him not to thee again. Deliver him therefore to my hand, and I will bring him to thee again:" 
Genesis42:38 And he said: my son shall not go down with you. For his brother is dead, and he is left alone. Moreover some misfortune might happen upon him by the way which ye go. And so should ye bring my gray head with sorrow unto the grave." 
Genesis43:1 And the dearth waxed sore in the land. 
Genesis43:2 And when they had eaten up that corn which they brought out of the land of Egypt, their father said unto them: go again and buy us a little food." 
Genesis43:3 Then said Juda unto him: the man did testify unto us saying: look that ye see not my face except your brother be with you. 
Genesis43:4 Therefore if thou wilt send our brother with us, we will go and buy the food." 
Genesis43:5 But if thou wilt not send him, we will not go: for the man said unto us: look that ye see not my face, except your brother be with you." 
Genesis43:6 And Israel said: wherefore dealt ye so cruelly with me, as to tell the man that ye had yet another brother?" 
Genesis43:7 And they said: The man asked us of our kindred saying: is your father yet alive? have ye not another brother? And we told him according to these words. How could we know that he would bid us bring our brother down with us? 
Genesis43:8 Then said Juda unto Israel his father: Send the lad with me, and we will rise and go, that we may live and not die: both we, thou and also our children." 
Genesis43:9 I will be surety for him, and of my hands require him. If I bring him not to thee and set him before thine eyes, then let me bear the blame for ever." 
Genesis43:10 For except we had made this tarrying: by this we had been there twice and come again. 
Genesis43:11 Then their father Israel said unto them: if it must needs be so now: then do thus, take of the best fruits of the land in your vessels, and bring the man a present, a courtesy balm, and a courtesy of honey, spices and myrrh, dates and almonds." 
Genesis43:12 And take as much money more with you. And the money that was brought again in your sacks, take it again with you in your hands, peradventure it was some oversight." 
Genesis43:13 Take also your brother with you, and arise and go again to the man." 
Genesis43:14 And God almighty give you mercy in the sight of the man and send you your other brother and also Ben Jamin, and I will be as a man robbed of his children." 
Genesis43:15 Thus took they the present and twice so much more money with them, and Ben Jamin. And rose up, went down to Egypt, and presented themself to Joseph." 
Genesis43:16 When Joseph saw Ben Jamin with them, he said to the ruler of his house: bring these men home, and slay and make ready: for they shall dine with me at noon." 
Genesis43:17 And the man did as Joseph bade, and brought them in to Joseph's house." 
Genesis43:18 When they were brought to Joseph's house, they were afraid, and said: because of the money that came in our sacks' mouths at the first time, are we brought, to pick a quarrel with us and to lay some thing to our charge: to bring us in bondage and our asses also." 
Genesis43:19 Therefore came they to the man that was the ruler over Joseph's house, and communed with him at the door" 
Genesis43:20 and said: Sir, we came hither at the first time to buy food," 
Genesis43:21 and as we came to an inn and opened our sacks: behold, every man's money was in his sack with full weight: But we have brought it again with us," 
Genesis43:22 and other money have we brought also in our hands, to buy food, but we can not tell who put our money in our sacks." 
Genesis43:23 And he said: be of good cheer, fear not: Your God and the God of your fathers hath put you that treasure in your sacks, for I had your money. And he brought Simeon out to them" 
Genesis43:24 and led them into Joseph's house, and gave them water to wash their feet, and gave their asses provender:" 
Genesis43:25 And they made ready their present against Joseph came at noon, for they heard say that they should dine there." 
Genesis43:26 When Joseph came home, they brought the present into the house to him, which they had in their hands, and fell flat on the ground before him." 
Genesis43:27 And he welcomed them courteously saying: is your father that old man which ye told me of, in good health? and is he yet alive?" 
Genesis43:28 they answered: thy servant our father is in good health, and is yet alive. And they bowed them selves and fell to the ground." 
Genesis43:29 And he lift up his eyes and beheld his brother Ben Jamin his mother's son, and said: is this your youngest brother of whom ye said unto me? And said: God be merciful unto thee my son." 
Genesis43:30 And Joseph made haste (for his heart did melt upon his brother) and sought for to weep, and entered into his chamber, for to weep there." 
Genesis43:31 And he washed his face and came out and refrained himself, and bade set bread on the table." 
Genesis43:32 And they prepared for him by himself, and for them by them selves, and for the Egyptians which ate with him by them selves, because the Egyptians may not eat bread with the Hebrews, for that is an abomination unto the Egyptians." 
Genesis43:33 And they sat before him: the eldest according unto his age, and the youngest according unto his youth. And the men marvelled among them selves." 
Genesis43:34 And they brought rewards unto them from before him: but Ben Jamin's part was five times so much as any of theirs. And they ate and they drank, and were drunk with him." 
Genesis44:1 And he commanded the ruler of his house saying: fill the men's sacks with food, as much as they can carry," 
Genesis44:2 and put every man's money in his bag mouth, and put my silver cup in the sack's mouth of the youngest and his corn money also. And he did as Joseph had said." 
Genesis44:3 And in the morning as soon as it was light, the men were let go with their asses." 
Genesis44:4 And when they were out of the city and not yet far away, Joseph said unto the ruler of his house: up and follow after the men and overtake them, and say unto them: wherefore have ye rewarded evil for good?" 
Genesis44:5 is that not the cup of which my lord drinketh, and doth he not prophesy therein? ye have evil done that ye have done." 
Genesis44:6 And he overtook them and said the same words unto them. 
Genesis44:7 And they answered him: wherefore saith my lord such words? God forbid that thy servants should do so. 
Genesis44:8 Behold, the money which we found in our sack's mouths, we brought again unto thee, out of the land of Canaa: how then should we steal out of my lord's house, either silver or gold?" 
Genesis44:9 with whosoever of thy servants it be found let him die, and let us also be my lord's bondmen." 
Genesis44:10 And he said: Now therefore according unto your words, he with whom it is found, shall be my servant: but ye, shall be harmless." 
Genesis44:11 And at once every man took down his sack to the ground, and every man opened his sack." 
Genesis44:12 And he searched, and began at the eldest and left at the youngest. And the cup was found in Ben Jamin's sack." 
Genesis44:13 Then they rent their clothes, and laded every man his ass and went again unto the city." 
Genesis44:14 And Juda and his brethren came to Joseph's house, for he was yet there, and they fell before him on the ground." 
Genesis44:15 And Joseph said unto them: what deed is this which ye have done? wist ye not that such a man as I can prophesy? 
Genesis44:16 Then said Juda: what shall we say unto my lord, what shall we speak or what excuse can we make? God hath found out the wickedness of thy servants. Behold, both we and he with whom the cup is found, are thy servants." 
Genesis44:17 And he answered: God forbid that I should do so, the man with whom the cup is found, he shall be my servant: but go ye in peace unto your father." 
Genesis44:18 Then Juda went unto him and said: oh my lord, let thy servant speak a word in my lord's audience,(ear) and be not wroth with thy servant: for thou art even as Pharao." 
Genesis44:19 My lord asked his servant saying: have ye a father or a brother? 
Genesis44:20 And we answered my lord, we have a father that is old, and a young lad which he begat in his age: and the brother of the said lad is dead, and he is all that is left of that mother. And his father loveth him." 
Genesis44:21 Then said my lord unto his servants bring him unto me, that I may set mine eyes upon him." 
Genesis44:22 And we answered my lord, that the lad could not go from his father, for if he should leave his father, he were but a dead man." 
Genesis44:23 Then saidest thou unto thy servants: except your youngest brother come with you, look that ye see my face no more." 
Genesis44:24 And when we came unto thy servant our father, we shewed him what my lord had said." 
Genesis44:25 And when our father said unto us, go again and buy us a little food:" 
Genesis44:26 we said, that we could not go. Nevertheless if our youngest brother go with us then will we go, for we may not see the man's face, except our youngest brother be with us." 
Genesis44:27 Then said thy servant our father unto us. Ye know that my wife bare me two sons. 
Genesis44:28 And the one went out from me and it is said of a surety that he is torn in pieces of wild beasts, and I saw him not since." 
Genesis44:29 If ye shall take this also away from me and some misfortune happen upon him, then shall ye bring my gray head with sorrow unto the grave." 
Genesis44:30 Now therefore when I come to thy servant my father, if the lad be not with me: seeing that his life hangeth by the lad's life," 
Genesis44:31 then as soon as he seeth that the lad is not come, he will die. So shall we thy servants bring the gray head of thy servant our father with sorrow unto the grave." 
Genesis44:32 For I thy servant became surety for the lad unto my father and said: if I bring him not unto thee again. I will bear the blame all my life long. 
Genesis44:33 Now therefore let me thy servant bide here for the lad, and be my lord's bondman: and let the lad go home with his brethren." 
Genesis44:34 For how can I go unto my father, and the lad not with me: lest I should see the wretchedness that shall come on my father." 
Genesis45:1 And Joseph could no longer refrain before all them that stood about him, but commanded that they should go all out from him, and that there should be no man with him, while he uttered himself unto his brethren." 
Genesis45:2 And he wept aloud, so that the Egyptians and the house of Pharao heard it." 
Genesis45:3 And he said unto his brethren: I am Joseph: doth my father yet live? But his brethren could not answer him, for they were abashed at his presence." 
Genesis45:4 And Joseph said unto his brethren: come near to me, and they came near. And he said: I am Joseph your brother whom ye sold into Egypt." 
Genesis45:5 And now be not grieved therewith, neither let it seem a cruel thing in your eyes, that ye sold me hither. For God did send me before you to save life." 
Genesis45:6 For this is the second year of dearth in the land, and five more are behind in which there shall neither be earing nor harvest." 
Genesis45:7 Wherefore God sent me before you to make provision, that ye might continue in the earth and to save your lives by a great deliverance." 
Genesis45:8 So now it was not ye that sent me hither, but God: and he hath made me father unto Pharao and lord over all his house, and ruler in all the land of Egypt." 
Genesis45:9 Haste you and go to my father and tell him, this sayeth thy son Joseph: God hath made me lord over all Egypt. Come down unto me and tarry not." 
Genesis45:10 And thou shalt dwell in the land of Gosan and be by me: both thou and thy children, and thy children's children: and thy sheep, and beasts and all that thou hast." 
Genesis45:11 There will I make provision for thee: for there remain yet five years of dearth, lest thou and thy household and all that thou hast perish." 
Genesis45:12 Behold, your eyes do see, and the eyes also of my brother Ben Jamin, that I speak to you by mouth." 
Genesis45:13 Therefore tell my father of all my honour which I have in Egypt and of all that ye have seen, and make haste and bring in father hither." 
Genesis45:14 And he fell on his brother Ben Jamin's neck and wept, and Ben Jamin wept on his neck." 
Genesis45:15 Moreover he kissed all his brethren and wept upon them. And after that, his brethren talked with him." 
Genesis45:16 And when the tidings was come unto Pharao's house that Joseph's brethren were come, it pleased Pharao well and all his servants." 
Genesis45:17 And Pharao spake unto Joseph: say unto thy brethren, this do ye: lade your beasts and get you hence. And when ye be come unto the land of Canaan," 
Genesis45:18 take your father and your households and come unto me, and I will give you the best of the land of Egypt, and ye shall eat the fat of the land." 
Genesis45:19 And commanded also. This do ye: take chariots with you out of the land of Egypt, for your children and for your wives: and bring your father and come." 
Genesis45:20 Also, regard not your stuff, for the goods of all the land of Egypt shall be yours." 
Genesis45:21 And the children of Israel did even so. And Joseph gave them chariots at the commandment of Pharao, and gave them vitaille also to spend by the way." 
Genesis45:22 And he gave unto each of them change of raiment: but unto Ben Jamin he gave three hundred pieces of silver and five changes of raiment. 
Genesis45:23 And unto his father he sent after the same manner: ten he asses laden with goods out of Egypt, and ten she asses laden with corn, bread and meat: to serve his father by the way." 
Genesis45:24 So sent he his brethren away, and they departed. And he said unto them: see that ye fall not out by the way." 
Genesis45:25 And they departed from Egypt and came into the land of Canaan unto Jacob their father, and told him saying:" 
Genesis45:26 Joseph is yet alive and is governor over all the land of Egypt. And Jacob's heart wavered, for he believed them not." 
Genesis45:27 And they told him all the words of Joseph which he had said unto them. But when he saw the chariots which Joseph had sent to carry him, then his spirits revived." 
Genesis45:28 And Israel said: I have enough, if Joseph my son be yet alive: I will go and see him, yer that I die." 
Genesis46:1 Israel took his journey with all that he had, and came unto Berseba and offered offerings unto the God of his father Isaac." 
Genesis46:2 And God said unto Israel in a vision by night, and called unto him: Jacob, Jacob. And he answered: here am I." 
Genesis46:3 And he said: I am that mighty God of thy father, fear not to go down into Egypt. For I will make of thee there a great people." 
Genesis46:4 I will go down with thee into Egypt, and I will also bring thee up again, and Joseph shall put his hand upon thine eyes." 
Genesis46:5 And Jacob rose up from Berseba. And the sons of Israel carried Jacob their father, and their children and their wives in the chariots which Pharao had sent to carry him." 
Genesis46:6 And they took their cattle and the goods which they had gotten in the land of Canaan, and came into Egypt: both Jacob and all his seed with him," 
Genesis46:7 his sons and his sons' sons with him: his daughters and his sons daughters and all his seed brought he with him into Egypt. 
Genesis46:8 These are the names of the children of Israel which came into Egypt, both Jacob and his sons: Ruben Jacob's first son." 
Genesis46:9 The children of Ruben: Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron and Charmi." 
Genesis46:10 The children of Simeon: Jemuel, Jami, Ohad, Jachin, Zohar and Saul the son of a Cananitish woman." 
Genesis46:11 The children of Levi: Gerson, Kahath and Merari." 
Genesis46:12 The children of Juda: Er, Onan, Sela, Pharez and Zerah, but Er and Onan died in the land of Canaan. The children of Pharez, Hezron, and Hamul." 
Genesis46:13 The children of Isachar: Tola, Phua, Job and Semnon." 
Genesis46:13 The children of Isachar: Tola, Phua, Job and Semnon." 
Genesis46:14 The children of Zabulon: Sered, Elon and Jaheleel." 
Genesis46:15 These be the children of Lea which she bare unto Jacob in Mesopotamia with his daughter Dina. All these souls of his sons and daughters make thirty and six.(.xxx. and .iij.) 
Genesis46:16 The children of Gad: Ziphion, Haggi, Suni, Ezbon, Eri Arodi and Areli." 
Genesis46:17 The children of Asser: Jemna, Jesua, Jesui, Bria and Serah their sister. And the children of Bria were Heber and Malchiel." 
Genesis46:18 These are the children of Silpha whom Laban gave to Lea his daughter. And these she bare unto Jacob in number sixteen souls. 
Genesis46:19 The children of Rahel Jacob's wife: Joseph and Ben Jamin. 
Genesis46:20 And unto Joseph in the land of Egypt were borne: Manasses and Ephraim which Asnath the daughter of Putiphar priest of On bare unto him. 
Genesis46:21 The children of Ben Jamin: Bela, Becher, Asbel, Gera, Naeman, Ehi, Ros, Mupim, Hupim and Ard." 
Genesis46:22 These are the children of Rahel which were born unto Jacob: fourteen souls altogether. 
Genesis46:23 The children of Dan: Husim. 
Genesis46:24 The children Nepthali: Jahezeel, Guni, Jezer and Sillem." 
Genesis46:25 These are the sons of Bilha which Laban gave unto Rahel his daughter, and she bare these unto Jacob, altogether seven souls." 
Genesis46:26 All the souls that came with Jacob into Egypt which came out of his loins (beside his son's wives) were all together sixty and six souls. 
Genesis46:27 And the sons of Joseph, which were born him in Egypt were: two souls. So that all the souls of the house of Jacob which came into Egypt are seventy." 
Genesis46:28 And he sent Juda before him unto Joseph that the way might be shewed him unto Gosan, and they came into the land of Gosan." 
Genesis46:29 And Joseph made ready his chariot and went against(to meet) Israel his father unto Gosan, and presented himself unto him, and fell on his neck and wept upon his neck a good while." 
Genesis46:30 And Israel said unto Joseph: Now I am content to die, insomuch I have seen thee, that thou art yet alive." 
Genesis46:31 And Joseph said unto his brethren and unto his father's house: I will go and shew Pharao and tell him: that my brethren and my father's house which were in the land of Canaan are come unto me," 
Genesis46:32 and how they are shepherds (for they were men of cattle) and they have brought their sheep and their oxen and all that they have with them. 
Genesis46:33 If Pharao call you and ask you what your occupation is," 
Genesis46:34 say: thy servants have been occupied about cattle, from our childhood unto this time: both we and our fathers, that ye may dwell in the land of Gosan. For an abomination unto the Egyptians are all that feed sheep.(For the Egyptians abhor all shepherds.)" 
Genesis47:1 And Joseph went and told Pharao and said: my father and my brethren their sheep and their beasts and all that they have, are come out of the land of Canaan and are in the land of Gosan." 
Genesis47:2 And Joseph took a part of his brethren: even five of them, and presented them unto Pharao." 
Genesis47:3 And Pharao said unto his brethren: what is your occupation? And they said unto Pharao: feeders of sheep(shepherds) are thy servants, both we and also our fathers." 
Genesis47:4 They said moreover unto Pharao: for to sojourn in the land are we come, for thy servants have no pasture for their sheep so sore is the famishment in the land of Canaan. Now therefore let thy servants dwell in the land of Gosan." 
Genesis47:5 And Pharao said unto Joseph: thy father and thy brethren are come unto thee. 
Genesis47:6 The land of Egypt is open before thee: In the best place of the land make both thy father and thy brethren dwell: And even in the land of Gosan let them dwell. Moreover if thou know any men of activity among them, make them rulers over my cattle." 
Genesis47:7 And Joseph brought in Jacob his father and set him before Pharao. And Jacob blessed Pharao. 
Genesis47:8 And Pharao asked Jacob, how old art thou?" 
Genesis47:9 And Jacob said unto Pharao: the days of my pilgrimage are an hundred and thirty years. Few and evil have the days of my life been, and have not attained unto the years of the life of my fathers in the days of their pilgrimages." 
Genesis47:10 And Jacob blessed Pharao and went out from him. 
Genesis47:11 And Joseph prepared dwellings for his father and his brethren, and gave them possessions in the land of Egypt, in the best of the land: even in the land of Rameses, as Pharao commanded." 
Genesis47:12 And Joseph made provision for his father, his brethren and all his father's household, as young children are fed with bread." 
Genesis47:13 There was no bread in all the land, for the dearth was exceeding sore: so that the land of Egypt and the land of Canaan, were famished by the reason of the dearth." 
Genesis47:14 And Joseph brought together all the money that was found in the land of Egypt and of Canaan, for the corn which they bought: and he laid up the money in Pharao's house." 
Genesis47:15 When money failed in the land of Egypt and of Canaan, all the Egyptians came unto Joseph and said: give us sustenance: wherefore sufferest thou us to die before thee: for our money is spent." 
Genesis47:16 Then said Joseph: bring your cattle, and I will give yow for your cattle, if ye be without money." 
Genesis47:17 And they brought their cattle unto Joseph. And he gave them bread for horses and sheep, and oxen and asses: so he fed them with bread for all their cattle that year." 
Genesis47:18 When that year was ended, they came unto him the next year and said unto him: we will not hide it from my lord, how that we have neither money nor cattle for my lord: there is no more left for my lord, but even our bodies and our lands." 
Genesis47:19 Wherefore lettest thou us die before thine eyes, and the land to go to nought? buy us and our lands for bread: and let both us and our lands be bond to Pharao. Give us seed, that we may live and not die, and that the land go not to waste." 
Genesis47:20 And Joseph bought all the land of Egypt for Pharao. For the Egyptians sold every man his land because the dearth was sore upon them: and so the land became Pharao's. 
Genesis47:21 And he appointed the people unto the cities, from one side of Egypt unto the other:" 
Genesis47:22 only the land of the Priests bought he not. For there was an ordinance made by Pharao for the priests, that they should eat that which was appointed unto them: which Pharao had given them wherefore they sold not their lands." 
Genesis47:23 Then Joseph said unto the folk: behold I have bought you this day and your lands for Pharao. Take there seed and go sow the land. 
Genesis47:24 And of the increase, ye shall give the fifth part unto Pharao, and four parts shall be your own, for seed to sow the field: and for you, and them of your households, and for your children, to eat." 
Genesis47:25 And they answered: Thou hast saved our lives. Let us find grace in the sight of my lord, and let us be Pharao's servants." 
Genesis47:26 And Joseph made it a law over the land of Egypt unto this day: that men must give Pharao the fifth part, except the land of the priests only, which was not bond unto Pharao." 
Genesis47:27 And Israel dwelt in Egypt: even in the country of Gosan. And they had their possessions therein, and they grew and multiplied exceedingly." 
Genesis47:28 Moreover Jacob lived in the land of Egypt seventeen years, so that the hole age of Jacob was an hundred and forty seven year." 
Genesis47:29 When the time drew nigh, that Israel must die: he sent for his son Joseph and said unto him: If I have found grace in thy sight, put thy hand under my thigh and deal mercifully and truly with me, that thou bury me not in Egypt:" 
Genesis47:30 but let me lie by my fathers, and carry me out of Egypt, and bury me in their burial. And he answered: I will do as thou hast said." 
Genesis47:31 And he said: swear unto me: and he sware unto him. And then Israel bowed him unto the bed's head. 
Genesis48:1 After these deeds, tidings were brought unto Joseph, that his father was sick. And he took with him his two sons, Manasse and Ephraim." 
Genesis48:2 Then was it said unto Jacob: behold, thy son Joseph cometh unto thee. And Israel took his strength unto him, and sat up on the bed," 
Genesis48:3 and said unto Joseph: God all mighty appeared unto me at Lus in the land of Canaan, and blessed me," 
Genesis48:4 and said unto me: behold, I will make thee grow and will multiply thee, and will make a great number of people of thee, and will give this land unto thee and unto thy seed after thee unto an everlasting possession." 
Genesis48:5 Now therefore thy two sons Manasse and Ephraim which were born unto thee before I came to thee, into Egypt, shall be mine: even as Ruben and Simeon shall they be unto me." 
Genesis48:6 And the children which thou gettest after them, shall be thine own: but shall be called with the names of their brethren in their inheritances." 
Genesis48:7 And after I came from Mesopotamia, Rahel died upon my hand in the land of Canaan, by the way: when I had but a field's brede to go unto Ephrat. And I buried her there in the way to Ephrat which is now called Bethlehem." 
Genesis48:8 And Israel beheld Joseph's sons and said: what are these? 
Genesis48:9 And Joseph said unto his father: they are my sons, which God hath given me here. And he said: bring them to me, and let me bless them." 
Genesis48:10 And the eyes of Israel were dim for age, so that he could not see. And he brought them to him, and he kissed them and embraced them." 
Genesis48:11 And Israel said unto Joseph: I had not thought to have seen thy face, and yet lo, God hath shewed it me and also thy seed." 
Genesis48:12 And Joseph took them away from his lap, and they fell on the ground before him." 
Genesis48:13 Then took Joseph them both: Ephraim in his right hand toward Israel's left hand and Manasse in his left hand, toward Israel's right hand, and brought them unto him." 
Genesis48:14 And Israel stretched out his right hand and laid it upon Ephraim's head which was the younger, and his left hand upon Manasse's head, crossing his hands, for Manasse was the elder." 
Genesis48:15 And he blessed Joseph saying: God before whom my fathers Abraham and Isaac did walk, and the God which hath fed me all my life long unto this day;" 
Genesis48:16 And the angel which hath delivered me from all evil, bless these lads: that they may be called after my name, and after my father Abraham and Isaac, and that they may grow and multiply upon the earth." 
Genesis48:17 When Joseph saw that his father laid his right hand upon the head of Ephraim, it displeased him. And he lift up his father's hand, to have removed it from Ephraim's head unto Manasse's head," 
Genesis48:18 and said unto his father: Not so my father, for this is the eldest. Put thy right hand upon his head." 
Genesis48:19 And his father would not, but said: I know it well my son, I know it well. He shall be also a people and shall be great. But of a troth his younger brother shall be greater than he, and his seed shall be full of people." 
Genesis48:20 And he blessed them saying: At the example of these, the Israelites shall bless and say: God make thee as Ephraim and as Manasse. Thus set he Ephraim before Manasse." 
Genesis48:21 And Israel said unto Joseph: behold, I die. And God shall be with you and bring you again unto the land of your fathers." 
Genesis48:22 Moreover I give unto thee, a portion of land above thy brethren which I gat out of the hands of the Amorites with my sword and with my bow." 
Genesis49:1 And Jacob called for his sons and said: come together, that I may tell you what shall happen you in the last days." 
Genesis49:2 Gather you together and hear ye sons of Jacob, and hearken unto Israel your father." 
Genesis49:3 Ruben, thou art mine eldest son, my might and the beginning of my strength, chief in receiving and chief in power." 
Genesis49:4 As unstable as water wast thou: thou shalt therefore not be the chiefest, for thou wentest up upon thy father's bed, and then defiledest thou my couch with going up." 
Genesis49:5 The brethren Simeon and Levi, wicked instruments are their weapons." 
Genesis49:6 Into their secrets come not my soul, and unto their congregation be my honour not coupled: for in their wrath they slew a man, and in their self will they houghed an ox." 
Genesis49:7 Cursed be their wrath for it was strong, and their fierceness for it was cruel. I will therefore divide them in Jacob, and scatter them in Israel." 
Genesis49:8 Juda, thy brethren shall praise thee, and thine hand shall be in the neck of thine enemies, and thy father's children shall stoop unto thee." 
Genesis49:9 Juda is a lion's whelp. From spoil my son thou art come on high:(an hye) he laid him down and couched himself as a lion, and as a lioness. Who dare stir him up?" 
Genesis49:10 The sceptre shall not depart from Juda, nor a ruler from between his legs, until Silo come, unto whom the people shall hearken." 
Genesis49:11 He shall bind his foal unto the vine, and his ass's colt unto the vine branch, and shall wash his garment in wine and his mantle in the blood of grapes:" 
Genesis49:12 his eyes are roudier than wine, and his teeth whiter then milk." 
Genesis49:13 Zabulon shall dwell in the haven of the sea and in the port of ships, and shall reach unto Sidon." 
Genesis49:14 Isachar is a strong ass, he couched him down between two borders," 
Genesis49:15 and saw that rest was good and the land that it was pleasant, and bowed his shoulder to bear, and became a servant unto tribute." 
Genesis49:16 Dan shall judge his people, as one of the tribes of Israel." 
Genesis49:17 Dan shall be a serpent in the way, and an adder in the path, and bite the horse heels, so that his rider shall fall backward." 
Genesis49:18 After thy saving look I, LORD." 
Genesis49:19 Gad, men of war shall invade him. And he shall turn them to flight." 
Genesis49:20 Of Asser cometh fat bread, and he shall give pleasures for a king." 
Genesis49:21 Nephtali is a swift hind, and giveth goodly words." 
Genesis49:22 That flourishing child Joseph, that flourishing child and goodly unto the eye: the daughters come forth to bear rule.(ran upon the wall.)" 
Genesis49:23 The shooters have envied him and chide with him and hated him," 
Genesis49:24 and yet his bow bode fast, and his arms and his hands were strong, by the hands of the mighty God of Jacob: out of him shall come an herdman, a stone in Israel." 
Genesis49:25 Thy father's God shall help thee, and the almighty shall bless thee with blessings from heaven above, and with blessings of the water that lieth under, and with blessings of the breasts and of the womb." 
Genesis49:26 The blessings of thy father were strong: even as the blessings of my elders, after the desire of the highest(hiest) in the world, and these blessings shall fall on the head of Joseph, and on the top of the head of him that was separated from his brethren." 
Genesis49:27 Ben Jamin is a ravishing wolf. In the morning he shall devour his prey, and at night he shall divide his spoil." 
Genesis49:28 All these are the twelve tribes of Israel, and this is that which their father spake unto them when he blessed them, every man with a several blessing." 
Genesis49:29 And he charged them and said unto them. I shall be put unto my people: see that ye bury me with my fathers, in the cave that is in the field of Ephron the Hethite," 
Genesis49:30 in the double cave that is in the field before Mamre in the land of Canaan. Which field Abraham bought of Ephron the Hethite for a possession to bury in. 
Genesis49:31 There they buried Abraham and Sara his wife, there they buried Isaac and Rebecca his wife. And there I buried Lea:" 
Genesis49:32 which field and the cave that is therein, was bought of the children of Heth." 
Genesis49:33 When Jacob had commanded all that he would unto his sons, he plucked up his feet upon the bed and died, and was put unto his people;" 
Genesis50:1 And Joseph fell upon his father's face, and wept upon him, and kissed him." 
Genesis50:2 And Joseph commanded his servants that were Physicians, to embalm his father, and the Physicians embalmed Israel forty days long," 
Genesis50:3 for so long doth the embalming last, and the Egyptians bewept him seventy days." 
Genesis50:4 And when the days of weeping were ended, Joseph spake unto the house of Pharao saying: If I have found favour in your eyes, speak unto Pharao and tell him, how that" 
Genesis50:5 my father made me swear and said: lo, I die, see that thou bury me in my grave which I have made me in the land of Canaan. Now therefore let me go and bury my father, and then will I come again." 
Genesis50:6 And Pharao said, go and bury thy father, according as he made thee swear." 
Genesis50:7 And Joseph went up to bury his father, and with him went all the servants of Pharao that were the elders of his house, and all the elders of Egypt," 
Genesis50:8 and all the house of Joseph and his brethren and his father's house: only their children and their sheep and their cattle left they behind them in the land of Gosan. 
Genesis50:9 And there went with him also Chariots and horsemen: so that they were an exceeding great company. 
Genesis50:10 And when they came to the field of Atad beyond Jordan, there they made great and exceeding sore lamentation. And he mourned for his father seven days." 
Genesis50:11 When the inhabiters of the land the Cananites saw the mourning in the field of Atad, they said: this is a great mourning which the Egyptians make. Wherefore the name of the place is called Abel mizraim, which place lieth beyond Jordan." 
Genesis50:12 And his sons did unto him according as he had commanded them. 
Genesis50:13 And his sons carried him into the land of Canaan and buried him in the double cave which Abraham had bought with the field to be a place to bury in, of Ephron the Hethite before Mamre." 
Genesis50:14 And Joseph returned to Egypt again and his brethren, and all that went up with him to bury his father, as soon as he had buried him." 
Genesis50:15 When Joseph's brethren saw that their father was dead, they said: Joseph might fortune to hate us and reward us again all the evil which we did unto him." 
Genesis50:16 They did therefore a commandment unto Joseph saying: thy father charged before his death saying: 
Genesis50:17 This wise say unto Joseph, forgive I pray thee the trespass of thy brethren and their sin, for they rewarded thee evil. Now therefore we pray thee, forgive the trespass of the servants of thy father's God. And Joseph wept when they spake unto him." 
Genesis50:18 And his brethren came and fell before him and said: behold we be thy servants. 
Genesis50:19 And Joseph said unto them: fear not, for am not I under God?" 
Genesis50:20 Ye thought evil unto me: but God turned it unto good to bring to pass, as it is this day, even to save much people alive." 
Genesis50:21 Fear not therefore, for I will care for you and for your children, and he spake kindly unto them." 
Genesis50:22 Joseph dwelt in Egypt and his father's house also, and lived an hundred and ten year." 
Genesis50:23 And Joseph saw Ephraim's children, even unto the third generation. And unto Machir the son of Manasse were children born, and sat on Joseph's knees." 
Genesis50:24 And Joseph said unto his brethren: I die; And God will surely visit you and bring you out of this land, unto the land which he sware unto Abraham, Isaac and Jacob." 
Genesis50:25 And Joseph took an oath of the children of Israel and said: God will not fail but visit you: see therefore that ye carry my bones hence. 
Genesis50:26 And so Joseph died, when he was an hundred and ten year old. And they embalmed him and put him in a chest in Egypt. [The end of the first book of Moses.]" 
Exodus1:1 These are the names of the children of Israel, which came to Egypt with Jacob, every man with his household:" 
Exodus1:2 Ruben, Simeon, Levi, Juda," 
Exodus1:3 Isachar, Zabulon, Ben Jamin," 
Exodus1:4 Dan, Nephtali, Gad and Aser." 
Exodus1:5 All the souls that came out of the loins of Jacob, were seventy, and Joseph was in Egypt already." 
Exodus1:6 When Joseph was dead and all his brethren and all that generation: 
Exodus1:7 the children of Israel grew, increased, multiplied and waxed exceeding mighty: so that the land was full of them." 
Exodus1:8 Then there rose up a new king in Egypt which knew not Joseph. 
Exodus1:9 And he said unto his folk: behold the people of the children of Israel are more and mightier than we. 
Exodus1:10 Come on, let us play wisely with them: lest they multiply, and then (if there chance any war) they join them selves unto our enemies and fight against us, and so get them out of the land." 
Exodus1:11 And he set taskmasters over them, to keep them under with burthens. And they built unto Pharao treasure cities: Phiton and Raamses." 
Exodus1:12 But the more they vexed them, the more they multiplied and grew: so that they abhorred the children of Israel." 
Exodus1:13 And the Egyptians held the children of Israel in bondage without mercy," 
Exodus1:14 and made their lives bitter unto them with cruel labour in clay and brick, and all manner work in the fields, and in all manner of service, which they caused them to work cruelly." 
Exodus1:15 And the king of Egypt said unto the midwives of the Hebrews' women, of which the one's name was Sephora and the other Phua:" 
Exodus1:16 when ye midwife the women of the Hebrews and see in the birth time that it is a boy, kill it. But if it be a maid, let it live." 
Exodus1:17 Notwithstanding the midwives feared God, and did not as the king of Egypt commanded them: but saved the men children." 
Exodus1:18 Then the king of Egypt called for the midwives and said unto them: why have ye dealt on this manner and have saved the men children? 
Exodus1:19 And the midwives answered Pharao, that the Hebrews' women were not as the women of Egypt: but were sturdy women, and were delivered yer the midwives came at them." 
Exodus1:20 And God therefore dealt well with the midwives. And the people multiplied and waxed very mighty. 
Exodus1:21 And because the midwives feared God, he made them houses." 
Exodus1:22 Then Pharao charged all his people saying: All the men children that are born, cast into the river and save the maid children alive." 
Exodus2:1 And there went a man of the house of Levi and took a daughter of Levi. 
Exodus2:2 And the wife conceived and bare a son. And when she saw that it was a proper child, she hid him three months long." 
Exodus2:3 And when she could no longer hide him, she took a basket of bulrushes and daubed it with slime and pitch, and laid the child therein, and put it in the flags by the river's brink." 
Exodus2:4 And his sister stood afar off, to wete what would come of it." 
Exodus2:5 And the daughter of Pharao came down to the river to wash herself, and her maidens walked along by the river's side. And when she saw the basket among the flags, she sent one of her maids and caused it to be fetched." 
Exodus2:6 And when she had opened it she saw the child, and behold, the babe wept. And she had compassion on it and said: it is one of the Hebrew's children." 
Exodus2:7 Then said his sister unto Pharao's daughter: shall I go and call unto thee a nurse of the Hebrew's women, to nurse the child?" 
Exodus2:8 And the maid ran and called the child's mother. 
Exodus2:9 Then Pharao's daughter said unto her. Take this child away and nurse it for me, and I will reward thee for thy labour. And the woman took the child and nursed it up." 
Exodus2:10 And when the child was grown, she brought it unto Pharao's daughter, and it was made her son, and she called it Moses, because (said she) I took him out of the water." 
Exodus2:11 And it happened in these days when Moses was waxed great, that he went out unto his brethren and looked on their burthens, and spied an Egyptian smiting one of his brethren an Hebrew." 
Exodus2:12 And he looked round about: and when he saw that there was no man by, he slew the Egyptian and hid him in the sand." 
Exodus2:13 And he went out another day: and behold, two Hebrews strove together. And he said unto him that did the wrong: wherefore smitest thou thine neighbour?" 
Exodus2:14 And he answered: who hath made thee a ruler or a judge over us? Intendest thou to kill me, as thou killedst the Egyptian? Then Moses feared and said: of a surety the thing is known." 
Exodus2:15 And Pharao heard of it and went about to slay Moses: but he fled from Pharao and dwelt in the land of Madian, and he sat down by a well's side." 
Exodus2:16 The priest of Madian had seven daughters which came and drew water and filled the troughs, for to water their father's sheep." 
Exodus2:17 And the shepherds came and drove them away: But Moses stood up and helped them and watered their sheep. 
Exodus2:18 And when they came to Raguel their father, he said: how happeneth it that ye are come so soon today?" 
Exodus2:19 And they answered: there was an Egyptian that delivered us from the shepherds, and so drew us water and watered the sheep." 
Exodus2:20 And he said unto his daughters: where is he? why have ye left the man? Go call him that he may eat bread. 
Exodus2:21 And Moses was content to dwell with the man. And he gave Moses Zephora his daughter which bare a son," 
Exodus2:22 and he called him Gerson: for he said: I have been a stranger in a strange land. 
Exodus2:23 And she bare yet another son, whom he called Elieser saying: the God of my father is mine helper, and hath rid me out of the hands of Pharao. And it chanced in process of time, that the king of Egypt died, and the children of Israel sighed by the reason of labour, and cried." 
Exodus2:24 And their complaint came up unto God from the labour. And God remembered his promise with Abraham, Isaac and Jacob." 
Exodus2:25 And God looked upon the children of Israel and knew them. 
Exodus3:1 Moses kept the sheep of Jethro his father-in-law priest of Madian, and he drove the flock to the backside of the desert, and came to the mountain of God, Horeb." 
Exodus3:2 And the angel of the LORD appeared unto him in a flame of fire out of a bush. And he perceived that the bush burned with fire, and consumed not." 
Exodus3:3 Then Moses said: I will go hence and see this great sight, how it cometh that the bush burneth not." 
Exodus3:4 And when the LORD saw that he came for to see, he called unto him out of the bush and said: Moses Moses; And he answered: here am I." 
Exodus3:5 And he said: come not hither, but put thy shoes off thy feet: for the place whereon thou standest is holy ground." 
Exodus3:6 And he said: I am the God of thy father, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac and the God of Jacob. And Moses hid his face, for he was afraid to look upon God." 
Exodus3:7 Then the LORD said: I have surely seen the trouble of my people which are in Egypt, and have heard their cry, which they have of their taskmasters. For I know their sorrow," 
Exodus3:8 and am come down to deliver them out of the hands of the Egyptians, and to bring them out of that land unto a good land and a large, and unto a land that floweth with milk and honey: even unto the place of the Cananites, Hethites, Amorites, Pherezites, Hevites, and of the Jebusites." 
Exodus3:9 Now therefore behold, the complaint of the children of Israel is come unto me and I have also seen the oppression, wherewith the Egyptians oppress them." 
Exodus3:10 But come, I will send thee unto Pharao, that thou mayst bring my people the children of Israel out of Egypt." 
Exodus3:11 And Moses said unto God: what, am I to go to Pharao and to bring the children of Israel out of Egypt?" 
Exodus3:12 And he said: I will be with thee. And this shall be a token unto thee that I have sent thee: after that thou hast brought the people out of Egypt, ye shall serve God upon this mountain." 
Exodus3:13 Then said Moses unto God: when I come unto the children of Israel and say unto them, the God of your fathers hath sent me unto you, and they say unto me, what is his name, what answer shall I give them?" 
Exodus3:14 Then said God unto Moses: I will be what I will be: and he said, this shalt thou say unto the children of Israel: I will be did send me to you." 
Exodus3:15 And God spake further unto Moses: thus shalt thou say unto the children of Israel: the LORD God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob hath sent me unto you: this is my name for ever, and this is my memorial thorowout all generations." 
Exodus3:16 Go therefore and gather the elders of Israel together and say unto them: the LORD God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac and the God of Jacob, appeared unto me and said: I have been and seen both you and that which is done to you in Egypt." 
Exodus3:17 And I have said it, that I will bring you out of the tribulation of Egypt unto the land of the Cananites, Hethites, Amorites, Pherezites, Hevites and Jebusites: even a land that floweth with milk and honey." 
Exodus3:18 If it come to pass that they hear thy voice, then go, both thou and the elders of Israel unto the king of Egypt, and say unto him: The LORD God of the Hebrews hath met with us: Let us go therefore three days' journey into the wilderness, that we may sacrifice unto the LORD our God." 
Exodus3:19 Notwithstanding I am sure that the king of Egypt will not let you go, except it be with a mighty hand:" 
Exodus3:20 yea and I will therefore stretch out mine hand, and smite Egypt with all my wonders which I will do therein. And after that he will let you go." 
Exodus3:21 And I will get this people favour in the sight of the Egyptians: so that when ye go, ye shall not go empty:" 
Exodus3:22 but every wife shall borrow of her neighbouress and of her that sojourneth in her house, jewels of silver and of gold and raiment. And ye shall put them on your sons and daughters, and shall rob the Egyptians." 
Exodus4:1 Moses answered and said: See, they will not believe me nor hearken unto my voice: but will say, the LORD hath not appeared unto thee." 
Exodus4:2 Then the LORD said unto him: what is that in thine hand? and he said, a rod." 
Exodus4:3 And he said, cast it on the ground, and it turned unto a serpent. And Moses ran away from it." 
Exodus4:4 And the LORD said unto Moses: put forth thine hand and take it by the tail. And he put forth his hand and caught it, and it became a rod again in his hand," 
Exodus4:5 that they may believe that the LORD God of their fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac and the God of Jacob hath appeared unto thee." 
Exodus4:6 And the LORD said furthermore unto him: thrust thine hand into thy bosom. And he thrust his hand into his bosom and took it out. And behold, his hand was leprous even as snow." 
Exodus4:7 And he said: put thine hand into thy bosom again. And he put his hand into his bosom again, and plucked it out of his bosom, and behold, it was turned again as his other flesh." 
Exodus4:8 If they will not believe thee neither hear the voice of the first token: yet will they believe the voice of the second token. 
Exodus4:9 But and if they will not believe the two signs neither hearken unto thy voice, then take of the water of the river and pour it upon the dry land. And the water which thou takest out of the river shall turn to blood upon the dry land." 
Exodus4:10 And Moses said unto the LORD: Oh my Lord.(the Lorde: oh my Lorde.) I am not eloquent, no not in times past and namely since thou hast spoken unto thy servant: but I am slow mouthed and slow tongued." 
Exodus4:11 And the LORD said unto him: who hath made man's mouth, or who hath made the dumb or the deaf, the seeing or the blind? have not I the LORD?" 
Exodus4:12 Go therefore and I will be with thy mouth and teach thee what thou shalt say. 
Exodus4:13 And he said: Oh my Lord,(Lorde)(LORDE) send I pray thee whom thou wilt." 
Exodus4:14 And the LORD was angry with Moses and said: I know Aaron thy brother the Levite that he can speak. And moreover behold, he cometh out against(to meet) thee, and when he seeth thee, he will be glad in his heart." 
Exodus4:15 And thou shalt speak unto him and put the words in his mouth, and I will be with thy mouth and with his mouth, and will teach you what ye shall do." 
Exodus4:16 And he shall be thy spokesman unto the people: he shall be thy mouth, and thou shalt be his God:" 
Exodus4:17 and take this rod in thy hand, wherewith thou shalt do miracles." 
Exodus4:18 And Moses went and returned to Jethro his father-in-law again and said unto him: let me go (I pray thee) and turn again unto my brethren which are in Egypt, that I may see whether they be yet alive. And Jethro said to Moses: go in peace." 
Exodus4:19 And the LORD said unto Moses in Madian: return again into Egypt for they are dead which went about to kill thee. 
Exodus4:20 And Moses took his wife and his sons and put them on an ass, and went again to Egypt, and took the rod of God in his hand." 
Exodus4:21 And the LORD said unto Moses: when thou art come into Egypt again, see that thou do all the wonders before Pharao which I have put in thy hand: but I will harden his heart, so that he shall not let the people go." 
Exodus4:22 And tell Pharao, thus sayeth the LORD: Israel is mine eldest son," 
Exodus4:23 and therefore sayeth unto thee: let my son go, that he may serve me. If thou wilt not let him go: behold, I will slay thine eldest son." 
Exodus4:24 And it chanced by the way in the inn, that the LORD met him and would have killed him." 
Exodus4:25 Then Zepora took a stone and circumcised her son, and fell at his feet, and said: a bloody husband art thou unto me." 
Exodus4:26 And he let him go. She said a bloody husband, because of the circumcision." 
Exodus4:27 Then said the LORD unto Aaron: go meet Moses in the wilderness. And he went and met him in the mount of God and kissed him. 
Exodus4:28 And Moses told Aaron all the words of the LORD which he had sent by him, and all the tokens which he had charged him withal." 
Exodus4:29 So went Moses and Aaron and gathered all the elders of the children of Israel. 
Exodus4:30 And Aaron told all the words which the LORD had spoken unto Moses, and did the miracles in the sight of the people," 
Exodus4:31 and the people believed. And when they heard that the LORD had visited the children of Israel and had looked upon their tribulation, they bowed them selves, and worshipped." 
Exodus5:1 Then Moses and Aaron went and told Pharao, thus sayeth the LORD God of Israel. Let my people go, that they may keep holy day unto me in the wilderness." 
Exodus5:2 And Pharao answered: what fellow is the LORD, that I should hear his voice for to let Israel go?" 
Exodus5:3 I know not the LORD, neither will let Israel go. And they said: the God of the Hebrews hath met with us: let us go (we pray thee) three days' journey into the desert, that we may sacrifice unto the LORD our God: lest he smite us either with pestilence or with sword." 
Exodus5:4 Then said the king of Egypt unto them: wherefore do ye, Moses and Aaron, let the people from their work, get you unto your labour." 
Exodus5:5 And Pharao said furthermore: behold, there is much people in the land, and ye make them play and let their work stand." 
Exodus5:6 And Pharao commanded the same day unto the taskmasters over the people and unto the officers saying: 
Exodus5:7 see that ye give the people no more straw to make brick withal, as ye did in time past: let them go and gather them straw them selves," 
Exodus5:8 and the number of bricks which they were wont to make in time past, lay unto their charge also, and minish nothing thereof. For they be idle and therefore cry saying: let us go and do sacrifice unto our God." 
Exodus5:9 They must have more work laid upon them, that they may labour therein, and then will they not turn them selves to false words." 
Exodus5:10 Then went the taskmasters of the people and the officers out and told the people saying: Thus sayeth Pharao: I will give you no more straw," 
Exodus5:11 but go your selves and gather you straw where ye can find it, yet shall none of your labour be minished." 
Exodus5:12 Then the people scattered abroad thorowout all the land of Egypt for to gather them stubble to be instead of straw. 
Exodus5:13 And the taskmasters hasted them forward saying: fulfil your work day by day, even as when straw was given you." 
Exodus5:14 And the officers of the children of Israel which Pharao's taskmasters had set over them, were beaten. And it was said unto them: wherefore have ye not fulfilled your task in making brick, both yesterday and today, as well as in times past?" 
Exodus5:15 Then went the officers of the children of Israel and complained unto Pharao saying: wherefore dealest thou thus with thy servants? 
Exodus5:16 there is no straw given unto thy servants, and yet they say unto us: make brick. And lo, thy servants are beaten, and thy people is foul intreated." 
Exodus5:17 And he answered: idle are ye idle, and therefore ye say: let us go and do sacrifice unto the LORD." 
Exodus5:18 Go therefore and work, for there shall no straw be given you, and yet see that ye deliver the hole tale of brick." 
Exodus5:19 When the officers of the children of Israel saw themself in shrewd case (in that he said ye shall minish nothing of your daily making of brick) 
Exodus5:20 then they met Moses and Aaron standing in their way as they came out from Pharao," 
Exodus5:21 and said unto them: The LORD look unto you and judge, for ye have made the savour of us stink in the sight of Pharao and of his servants, and have put a sword into their hands to slay us." 
Exodus5:22 Moses returned unto the LORD(Lorde)(LORDE) and said: Lord(LORDE) wherefore dealest thou cruelly with this people: and wherefore hast thou sent me? 
Exodus5:23 For since I came to Pharao to speak in thy name, he hath fared foul with this folk, and yet thou hast not delivered thy people at all;" 
Exodus6:1 Then the LORD said unto Moses. Now shalt thou see what I will do unto Pharao, for with a mighty hand shall he let them go, and with a mighty hand shall he drive them out of his land." 
Exodus6:2 And God spake unto Moses saying unto him: I am the LORD," 
Exodus6:3 and I appeared unto Abraham, Isaac and Jacob an Almighty God: but in my name Jehovah(Iehouah) was I not known unto them." 
Exodus6:4 Moreover I made an appointment with them to give them the land of Canaa: the land of their pilgrimage wherein they were strangers. 
Exodus6:5 And I have also heard the groaning of the children of Israel, because the Egyptians keep them in bondage, and have remembered my promise." 
Exodus6:6 Wherefore say unto the children of Israel: I am the LORD, and will bring you out from under the burdens of the Egyptians, and will rid you out of their bondage, and will deliver you with a stretched out arm and with great judgements." 
Exodus6:7 And I will take you for my people and will be to you a God. And ye shall know that I am the LORD your God which brings you out from under the burthens of the Egyptians. 
Exodus6:8 And I will bring you unto the land over the which I did lift up my hand to give it unto Abraham, Isaac and Jacob, and will give it unto you for a possession: even I the LORD." 
Exodus6:9 And Moses told the children of Israel even so: But they hearkened not unto Moses for anguish of spirit and for cruel bondage. 
Exodus6:10 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying: 
Exodus6:11 Go and bid Pharao king of Egypt, that he let the children of Israel go out of his land." 
Exodus6:12 And Moses spake before the LORD saying: behold, the children of Israel hearken not unto me, how then shall Pharao hear me: seeing that I have uncircumcised lips." 
Exodus6:13 And the LORD spake unto Moses and Aaron and gave them a charge unto the children of Israel and unto Pharao king of Egypt: to bring the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt. 
Exodus6:14 These be the heads of their father's houses. The children of Ruben the eldest son of Israel are these: Hanoh, Pallu, Hezron, Charmi, these be the householders of Ruben." 
Exodus6:15 The children of Simeon are these: Gemuel, Jamin, Ohad, Jachin, Zohar, and Saul the son of a Cananitish wife: these are the kindreds of Simeon." 
Exodus6:16 These are the names of the children of Levi in their generations: Gerson, Kahath and Merari. And Levi lived an hundred and thirty seven year." 
Exodus6:17 The sons of Gerson: Libni and Semei in their kindreds. 
Exodus6:18 The children of Kahath: Amram, Jesear, Hebron and Usiel. And Kahath lived an hundred and thirty three year." 
Exodus6:19 The children of Merari are these: Maheli and Musi: these are the kindreds of Levi in their generations. 
Exodus6:20 And Amram took Jochebed his niece to wife which bare him Aaron and Moses. And Amram lived an hundred and thirty seven year. 
Exodus6:21 The children of Jezear: Korah, Nepheg and Sichri." 
Exodus6:22 The children of Usiel: Misael, Elzaphan and Sithri." 
Exodus6:23 And Aaron took Elizaba daughter of Aminadab and sister of Nahason, to wife: which bare him Nadab, Abehu, Eleazar and Ithamar." 
Exodus6:24 The children of Korah: Assir, Elkana and Abiassaph: these are the kindreds of the Korahites." 
Exodus6:25 And Eleazar Aaron's son took him one of the daughters of Putuel to wife: which bare him Pinehas: these be the principal fathers of the Levites in their kindreds. 
Exodus6:26 These are that Aaron and Moses to whom the LORD said: carry the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt, with their armies." 
Exodus6:27 These are that Moses and Aaron which spake to Pharao king of Egypt, that they might bring the children of Israel out of Egypt." 
Exodus6:28 And in the day when the LORD spake unto Moses in the land of Egypt," 
Exodus6:29 he spake unto him saying, I am the LORD, see that thou speak unto Pharao the king of Egypt all that I say unto thee." 
Exodus6:30 And Moses answered before the LORD: I am of uncircumcised lips, how shall Pharao then give me audience?" 
Exodus7:1 And the LORD said unto Moses: behold, I have made thee Pharao's God, and Aaron thy brother shall be thy prophet." 
Exodus7:2 Thou shalt speak all that I command thee and Aaron thy brother shall speak unto Pharao: that he send the children of Israel out of his land. 
Exodus7:3 But I will harden Pharao's heart, that I may multiply my miracles and my wonders in the land of Egypt." 
Exodus7:4 And yet Pharao shall not hearken unto you, that I may set mine hand upon Egypt and bring out mine armies, even my people the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt, with great judgements." 
Exodus7:5 And the Egyptians shall know that I am the LORD when I have stretched forth my hand upon Egypt, and have brought out the children of Israel from among them." 
Exodus7:6 Moses and Aaron did as the LORD commanded them. 
Exodus7:7 And Moses was eighty year old and Aaron eighty three when they spake unto Pharao. 
Exodus7:8 And the LORD spake unto Moses and Aaron saying: 
Exodus7:9 when Pharao speaketh unto you and sayeth: shew a wonder, then shalt thou say unto Aaron, take the rod and cast it before Pharao, and it shall turn to a serpent." 
Exodus7:10 Then went Moses and Aaron in unto Pharao, and did even as the LORD had commanded. And Aaron cast forth his rod before Pharao and before his servants, and it turned to a serpent." 
Exodus7:11 Then Pharao called for the wise men, and enchanters of Egypt did in like manner with their sorcery." 
Exodus7:12 And they cast down every man his rod, and they turned to serpents: but Aaron's rod ate up their rods:" 
Exodus7:13 and yet for all that Pharao's heart was hardened, so that he hearkened not unto them, even as the LORD had said." 
Exodus7:14 Then said the LORD unto Moses: Pharao's heart is hardened, and he refuseth to let the people go." 
Exodus7:15 Get thee unto Pharao in the morning, for he will come unto the water, and stand(stode) thou upon the river's brink against he come, and the rod which turned to a serpent take in thine hand." 
Exodus7:16 And say unto him: the LORD God of the Hebrews hath sent me unto thee saying: let my people go, that they may serve me in the wilderness: but hitherto thou wouldest not hear." 
Exodus7:17 Wherefore thus sayeth the LORD: hereby thou shalt know that I am the LORD. Behold, I will smite with the staff that is in mine hand upon the waters that are in the river, and they shall turn to blood." 
Exodus7:18 And the fish that is in the river shall die, and the river shall stink: so that it shall grieve the Egyptians to drink of the water of the river." 
Exodus7:19 And the LORD spake unto Moses, say unto Aaron: take thy staff and stretch out thine hand over the waters of Egypt, over their streams, rivers, ponds and all pools of water, that they may be blood, and that there may be blood in all the land of Egypt: both in vessels of wood and also of stone." 
Exodus7:20 And Moses and Aaron did even as the LORD commanded. And he lift up the staff and smote the waters that were in the river, in the sight of Pharao and in the sight of his servants, and all the water that was in the river, turned into blood." 
Exodus7:21 And the fish that was in the river died, and the river stank: so that the Egyptians could not drink of the water of the river. And there was blood thorowout all the land of Egypt." 
Exodus7:22 And the enchanters of Egypt did likewise with their enchantments, so that Pharao's heart was hardened and did not regard them as the LORD had said." 
Exodus7:23 And Pharao turned himself and went into his house, and set not his heart thereunto." 
Exodus7:24 And the Egyptians digged round about the river for water to drink, for they could not drink of the water of the river." 
Exodus7:25 And it continued a week after that the LORD had smote the river.
