Exodus26:13 a cubit on the one side and a cubit on the other side, of that, that remaineth in the length of the curtains of the tabernacle, which shall remain of either side of the habitation to cover it withal." 
Exodus26:14 And thou shalt make another covering for the tent of rams' skins dyed red: and yet another above all of taxus' skins. 
Exodus26:15 And thou shalt make boards for the habitation of sethim wood to stand upright: 
Exodus26:16 ten cubits long shall every board be, and a cubit and an half broad." 
Exodus26:17 Two feet shall one board have to couple them together withal, and so thou shalt make unto all the boards of the habitation." 
Exodus26:18 And thou shalt make twenty boards for the habitation on the south side," 
Exodus26:19 and thou shalt make forty sockets of silver and put them under the twenty boards: two sockets under every board, for their two feet." 
Exodus26:20 In like manner in the north side of the habitation there shall be twenty boards 
Exodus26:21 and forty sockets of silver: two sockets under every board. 
Exodus26:22 And for the west end of the habitation, shalt thou make six boards," 
Exodus26:23 and two boards more for the two west corners of the habitation: 
Exodus26:24 so that these two boards be coupled together beneath and likewise above with clamps. And so shall it be in both the corners. 
Exodus26:25 And so there shall be eight boards in all, and sixteen sockets of silver: two sockets under every board." 
Exodus26:26 And thou shalt make bars of sethim wood five for the boards of the one side of the tabernacle," 
Exodus26:27 and five for the other side, and five for the boards of the west end." 
Exodus26:28 And the middle bar shall go along thorow the midst(myddes) of the boards and bar them together from the one end unto the other. 
Exodus26:29 And thou shalt cover the boards with gold and make golden rings for them to put the bars thorow, and shalt cover the bars with gold also." 
Exodus26:30 And rear up the habitation according to the fashion thereof that was shewed thee in the mount. 
Exodus26:31 And thou shalt make a vail of Jacinth, of scarlet, purple and twined byss, and shalt make it of broidered work and full of cherubins." 
Exodus26:32 And hang it upon four pillars of sethim wood covered with gold and that their knops be covered with gold also, and stand upon four sockets of silver." 
Exodus26:33 And thou shalt hang up the vail with rings, and shall bring in within the vail, the ark of witness. And the vail shall divide the holy from the most holy." 
Exodus26:34 And thou shalt put the mercy seat upon the ark of witness in the holiest place. 
Exodus26:35 And thou shalt put the table without the vail and candlestick over against the table, upon the south side of the habitation. And put the table on the north side." 
Exodus26:36 And thou shalt make an hanging for the door of the tabernacle: of Jacinth, of scarlet, of purple and of twined byss, wrought with needle work." 
Exodus26:37 And thou shalt make for the hanging, five pillars of sethim wood, and cover both them and their knops with gold, and shalt cast five sockets of brass for them." 
Exodus27:1 And thou shalt make an altar of sethim wood: five cubits long and five cubits broad, that it be foursquare, and three cubits high." 
Exodus27:2 And make it horns proceeding out in the four corners of it, and cover it with brass." 
Exodus27:3 And make his ashpans, shovels, basins, fleshhooks, firepans and all the apparel thereof, of brass:" 
Exodus27:4 after the fashion of a net, and put upon the net four rings: even in the four corners of it," 
Exodus27:5 and put it beneath under the compass of the altar, and let the net reach unto the one half of the altar;" 
Exodus27:6 And make staves for the altar of sethim wood, and cover them with brass," 
Exodus27:7 and let them be put in rings along by the sides of the altar, to bear it withal." 
Exodus27:8 And make the altar hollow with boards: even as it was shewed thee in the mount, so let them make it." 
Exodus27:9 And thou shalt make a court unto the habitation, which shall have in the south side hangings of twined byss, being an hundred cubits long," 
Exodus27:10 and twenty pillars thereof with their twenty sockets of brass: but the knops of the pillars and their hoops shall be silver. 
Exodus27:11 In like wise on the north side there shall be hangings of an hundred cubits long and twenty pillars with their sockets of brass, and the knops and the hoops of silver." 
Exodus27:12 And in the breadth of the court westward, there shall be hangings of fifty cubits long, and ten pillars with their ten sockets." 
Exodus27:13 And in the breadth of the court eastward toward the rising of the son, shall be hangings of fifty cubits." 
Exodus27:14 Hangings of fifteen cubits in the one side of it with three pillars, and three sockets:" 
Exodus27:15 and likewise on the other side shall be hangings of fifteen cubits with three pillars and three sockets. 
Exodus27:16 And in the gate of the court shall be a vail of twenty cubits: of jacinth, scarlet, purple and twined byss wrought with needle work, and four pillars with their four sockets." 
Exodus27:17 All the pillars round about the court shall be hooped with silver, and their knops of silver, and their sockets of brass." 
Exodus27:18 The length of the court, shall be an hundred cubits, and the breadth fifty, and the height five, and the hangings shall be of twined byss and the sockets of brass." 
Exodus27:19 And all the vessels of the habitation to all manner service and the pins thereof: yea and the pins also of the court, shall be brass." 
Exodus27:20 And command the children of Israel that they give the pure oil olive beaten for the lights to pour alway into the lamps. 
Exodus27:21 In the tabernacle of witness without the vail which is before the witness, shall Aaron and his sons dress it both even and morning before the LORD: And it shall be a duty for ever unto your generations after you: to be given of the children of Israel." 
Exodus28:1 And take thou unto thee, Aaron thy brother and his sons with him, from among the children of Israel, that he may minister unto me: both Aaron, Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar, Aaron's sons." 
Exodus28:2 And thou shalt make holy raiment for Aaron thy brother, both honourable and glorious." 
Exodus28:3 Moreover speak unto all that are wise hearted, which I have filled with the spirit of wisdom: that they make Aaron's raiment to consecrate him with, that he may minister unto me." 
Exodus28:4 These are the garments which they shall make: a breastlap, Ephod, a tunicle, a strait coat, a mitre and a girdle. And they shall make holy garments for Aaron thy brother and his sons, that he may minister unto me." 
Exodus28:5 And they shall take thereto, gold, jacinth, scarlet, purple and byss." 
Exodus28:6 And they shall make the Ephod of gold, jacinth, scarlet, purple and white twined byss with broidered work." 
Exodus28:7 The two sides shall come together, closed up in the edges thereof." 
Exodus28:8 And the girdle of the Ephod shall be of the same workmanship and of the same stuff: even of gold, jacinth, scarlet, purple and twined byss." 
Exodus28:9 And thou shalt take two onyx stones and grave in them the names of the children of Israel: 
Exodus28:10 fix in the one stone, and the other fix in the other stone: according to the order of their birth." 
Exodus28:11 After the work of a stone graver, even as signets are graven, shalt thou grave the two stones with the names of the children of Israel, and shalt make them to be set in ouches of gold." 
Exodus28:12 And thou shalt put the two stones upon the two shoulders of the Ephod, and they shall be stones of remembrance unto the children of Israel. And Aaron shall bear their names before the LORD upon his two shoulders for a remembrance." 
Exodus28:13 And thou shalt make hooks of gold 
Exodus28:14 and two chains of fine gold: link work and wreathed, and fasten the wreathed chains to the hooks." 
Exodus28:15 And thou shalt make the breastlap of ensample(judgement) with broidered work: even after the work of the Ephod shalt thou make it: of gold, jacinth, scarlet, purple and twined byss shalt thou make it." 
Exodus28:16 Foursquare it shall be and double, an hand breadth long and an hand breadth broad." 
Exodus28:17 And thou shalt fill it with four rows of stones. In the first row shall be a Sardius, a Topas and Smaragdus." 
Exodus28:18 The second row, a Ruby, Sapphire and Diamond." 
Exodus28:19 The third: Ligurius, an Achat and Amethyst." 
Exodus28:20 The fourth: a Turcois Onyx and Jasper. And they shall be set in gold in their enclosures. 
Exodus28:21 And the stones shall be graven as signets be graven: with the names of the children of Israel, even with twelve names every one with his name according to the twelve tribes." 
Exodus28:22 And thou shalt make upon the breastlap two fastening chains of pure gold and wreathen work. 
Exodus28:23 And thou shalt make likewise upon the breastlap two rings of gold and put them on the edges of the breastlap," 
Exodus28:24 and put the two wreathen chains of gold in the two rings which are in the edges of the breastlap. 
Exodus28:25 And the two ends of the two chains thou shalt fasten in the two rings, and put them upon the shoulders of the Ephod: on the foreside of it." 
Exodus28:26 And thou shalt yet make two rings of gold, and put them in the two edges of the breastlap even in the borders thereof toward the inside of the Ephod that is over against it." 
Exodus28:27 And yet two other rings of gold thou shalt make, and put them on the two sides of the Ephod, beneath over against the breastlap, alow where the sides are joined together upon the broidered girdle of the Ephod." 
Exodus28:28 And they shall bind the breastlap by his rings unto the rings of the Ephod with a lace of jacinth, that it may lie close unto the broidered girdle of the Ephod, that the breastlap be not lowsed from the Ephod." 
Exodus28:29 And Aaron shall bear the names of the children Israel in the breastlap of ensample upon his heart, when he goeth into the holy place, for a remembrance before the LORD alway." 
Exodus28:30 And thou shalt put in the breastlap of ensample light and perfectness: that they be even upon Aaron's heart when he goeth in before the LORD and Aaron shall bear the example of the children of Israel upon his heart before the LORD alway. 
Exodus28:31 And thou shalt make the tunicle unto the Ephod, altogether of Jacinth." 
Exodus28:32 And there shall be an hole for the head in the midst of it, and let there be a bond of woven work round about the collar of it: as it were the collar of a partlet, that it rend not." 
Exodus28:33 And beneath upon the hem, thou shalt make pomegranates of Jacincth, of scarlet, and of purple round about the hem," 
Exodus28:34 and bells of gold between them round about: that there be ever a golden bell and a pomegranate, a golden bell and a pomegranate round about upon the hem of the tunicle." 
Exodus28:35 And Aaron shall have it upon him when he ministereth, that the sound may be heard when he goeth into the holy place before the LORD and when he cometh out, that he die not." 
Exodus28:36 And thou shalt make a plate of pure gold, and grave thereon (as signets are graven) The holiness of the LORD," 
Exodus28:37 and put it on a lace of Jacincth and tie it unto the mitre," 
Exodus28:38 upon the forefront of it, that it be upon Aaron's forehead: that Aaron bear the sin of the holy things which the children of Israel have hallowed in all their holy gifts. And it shall be always upon Aaron's forehead, that they may be accepted before the LORD." 
Exodus28:39 And thou shalt make an alb of byss, and thou shalt make a mitre of byss and a girdle of needle work." 
Exodus28:40 And thou shalt make for Aaron's sons also coats, girdles and bonnets honourable and glorious," 
Exodus28:41 and thou shalt put them upon Aaron thy brother and on his sons with him and shalt anoint them and fill their hands and consecrate them, that they may minister unto me." 
Exodus28:42 And thou shalt make them linen breeches to cover their privates:(privities) from the loins unto the thighs shall they reach. 
Exodus28:43 And they shall be upon Aaron and his sons, when they go into the tabernacle of witness, or when they go unto the altar to minister in holiness, that they bear no sin and so die. And it shall be a law for ever unto Aaron and his seed after him." 
Exodus29:1 This is the thing that thou shalt do unto them when thou hallowest them to be my priests. Take one ox and two rams that are without blemish," 
Exodus29:2 and unleavened bread and cakes of sweet bread tempered with oil and wafers of sweet bread anointed with oil (of wheaten flour shalt thou make them) 
Exodus29:3 and put them in a maund and bring them in the maund with the oil and the two rams. 
Exodus29:4 And bring Aaron and his sons unto the door of the tabernacle of witness, and wash them with water," 
Exodus29:5 and take the garments, and put upon Aaron: the strait coat, and the tunicle of the Ephod, and the Ephod and the breastlap: and girth them to him with the broidered girdle of the Ephod." 
Exodus29:6 And put the mitre upon his head and put the holy crown upon the mitre. 
Exodus29:7 Then take the anointing oil and pour it upon his head and anoint him. 
Exodus29:8 And bring his sons and put albes upon them," 
Exodus29:9 and girth them with girdles: as well Aaron as his sons. And put the bonnets on them that the priest's office may be theirs for a perpetual law. And fill the hands of Aaron and of his sons," 
Exodus29:10 and bring the ox before the tabernacle of witness. And let Aaron and his sons put their hands upon his head 
Exodus29:11 and kill him before the LORD in the door of the tabernacle of witness. 
Exodus29:12 And take of the blood of the ox and put it upon the horns of the altar with thy finger and pour all the blood upon the bottom of the altar," 
Exodus29:13 and take all the fat that covereth the inwards, and the caul that is on the liver, and the two kidneys with the fat that is upon them: and burn them upon the altar." 
Exodus29:14 But the flesh of the ox and his skin and his dung, shalt thou burn with fire, without the host. For it is a sin offering." 
Exodus29:15 Then take one of the rams, and let Aaron and his sons put their hands upon the head of the ram," 
Exodus29:16 and cause him to be slain, and take of his blood, and sprinkle it round about upon the altar," 
Exodus29:17 and cut the ram in pieces and wash the inwards of him and his legs, and put them unto the pieces and unto his head," 
Exodus29:18 and burn the whole ram upon the altar. For it is a burnt offering unto the LORD, and a sweet savour of the LORD's sacrifice." 
Exodus29:19 And take the other ram and let Aaron and his sons, put their hands upon his head and let him then be killed." 
Exodus29:20 And take of his blood and put it upon the tip of the right ear of Aaron and of his sons, and upon the thumb of their right hands, and upon the great toe of their right feet and sprinkle the blood upon the altar round about." 
Exodus29:21 Then take of the blood that is upon the altar and of the anointing oil, and sprinkle it upon Aaron and his vestments, and upon his sons and upon their garments also. Then is he and his clothes holy and his sons and their clothes holy also." 
Exodus29:22 Then take the fat of the ram and his rump and the fat that covereth the inwards and the caul of the liver and, the two kidneys, and the fat that is upon them and the right shoulder (for that ram is a full offering)" 
Exodus29:23 and a simnel of bread and a cake of oiled bread and a wafer out of the basket of sweet bread that is before the LORD," 
Exodus29:24 and put all upon the hands of Aaron and on the hands of his sons: and wave them in and out a wave offering unto the LORD. 
Exodus29:25 Then take it from off their hands and burn it upon the altar: even upon the burnt offering, to be a savour of sweetness before the LORD. For it is a sacrifice unto the LORD." 
Exodus29:26 Then take the breast of the ram that is Aaron's full offering and wave it a wave offering before the LORD, and let that be thy part." 
Exodus29:27 And sanctify the breast of the wave offering and the shoulder of the heave offering which is waved and heaved up of the ram which is the full offering of Aaron and of his sons. 
Exodus29:28 And it shall be Aaron's and his sons' duty for ever, of the children of Israel, for it is an heave offering. And the heave offering shall be the LORD's duty of the children of Israel: even of the sacrifice of their peace offerings which they heave unto the LORD." 
Exodus29:29 And the holy garments of Aaron shall be his sons' after him, to anoint them therein, and to fill their hands therein." 
Exodus29:30 And that son that is priest in his stead after him, shall put them on seven days: that he go into the tabernacle of witness, to minister in the holy place." 
Exodus29:31 Then take the ram that is the full offering and seethe his flesh in an holy place. 
Exodus29:32 And Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of him, and the bread that is in the basket: even in the door of the tabernacle of witness." 
Exodus29:33 And they shall eat them, because the atonement was made therewith to fill their hands and to sanctify them: but a stranger shall not eat thereof, because they are holy." 
Exodus29:34 If ought of the flesh of the full offerings, or of the bread remain unto the morning, thou shalt burn it with fire: for it shall not be eaten, because it is holy." 
Exodus29:35 And see thou do unto Aaron and his sons, even so in all things as I have commanded thee: that thou fill their hands seven days" 
Exodus29:36 and offer every day an ox for a sin offering for to reconcile with all. And thou shalt hallow the altar when thou reconcilest it, and shalt anoint it to sanctify it." 
Exodus29:37 Seven days thou shalt reconcile the altar and sanctify it, that it may be an altar most holy: so that no man may twich it but they that be consecrate." 
Exodus29:38 This is that which thou shalt offer upon the altar: two lambs of one year old day by day for ever," 
Exodus29:39 the one thou shalt offer in the morning and the other at even. 
Exodus29:40 And unto the one lamb take a tenth deal of flour mingled with the fourth part of an hin of beaten oil, and the fourth part of an hin of wine, for a drink offering." 
Exodus29:41 And the other lamb thou shalt offer at even and shalt do thereto according to the meat offering and drink offering in the morning, to be an odour of a sweet savour of the sacrifice of the LORD." 
Exodus29:42 And it shall be a continual burnt offering among your children after you, in the door of the tabernacle of witness before the LORD, where I will meet you to speak unto you there." 
Exodus29:43 There I will meet with the children of Israel, and will be sanctified in mine honour." 
Exodus29:44 And I will sanctify the tabernacle of witness and the altar: and I will sanctify also both Aaron and his sons to be my priests. 
Exodus29:45 And moreover I will dwell among the children of Israel and will be their God. 
Exodus29:46 And they shall know that I am the LORD their God that brought them out of the land of Egypt for to dwell among them: even I the LORD their God. 
Exodus30:1 And thou shalt make an altar to burn cense therein, of sethim wood:" 
Exodus30:2 a cubit long, and a cubit broad, even foursquare shall it be and two cubits, high: with horns proceeding out of it," 
Exodus30:3 and thou shalt overlay it with fine gold both the roof and the walls round about, and his horns also, and shalt make unto it a crown of gold round about," 
Exodus30:4 and two golden rings on either side, even under the crown, to put staves therein for to bear it withal." 
Exodus30:5 And thou shalt make the staves of sethim wood and cover them with gold. 
Exodus30:6 And thou shalt put it before the vail that hangeth before the ark of witness, and before the mercy seat that is before the witness, where I will meet thee." 
Exodus30:7 And Aaron shall burn thereon sweet cense every morning when he dresseth the lamps: 
Exodus30:8 and likewise at even when he setteth up the lamps he shall burn cense perpetually before the LORD thorowout your generations. 
Exodus30:9 Ye shall put no strange cense thereon, neither burnt sacrifice nor meat offering, neither pour any drink offering thereon." 
Exodus30:10 And Aaron shall reconcile his horns once in a year, with the blood of the sin offering of reconciling: even once in the year shall he reconcile it thorow your generations. And so is it most holy unto the LORD." 
Exodus30:11 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying: 
Exodus30:12 when thou takest the sum of the children of Israel and tellest them, they shall give every man a reconciling of his soul unto the LORD, that there be no plague among them when thou tellest them." 
Exodus30:13 And thus much shall every man give that goeth in the number: half a sicle, after the holy sicle: a sicle is twenty geras: and an half sicle shall be the heave offering unto the LORD." 
Exodus30:14 And all that are numbered of them that are twenty year old and above shall give an heave offering unto the LORD. 
Exodus30:15 The rich shall not pass, and the poor shall not go under half a sicle, when they give an heave offering unto the LORD for the atonement of their souls." 
Exodus30:16 And thou shalt take the reconciling money of the children of Israel and shalt put it unto the use of the tabernacle of witness, and it shall be a memorial of the children of Israel before the LORD, to make atonement for their souls." 
Exodus30:17 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying: 
Exodus30:18 thou shalt make a laver of brass and his foot also of brass to wash withal, and shalt put it between the tabernacle of witness and the altar and put water therein:" 
Exodus30:19 that Aaron and his sons may wash both their hands and their feet thereout," 
Exodus30:20 when they go into the tabernacle of witness, or when they go unto the altar to minister and to burn the LORD's offering, lest they die." 
Exodus30:21 And it shall be an ordinance for ever unto him and his seed among your children after you. 
Exodus30:22 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying: 
Exodus30:23 take principal spices: of pure myrrh five hundred sicles, of sweet cinnamon half so much, two hundred and fifty sicles:" 
Exodus30:24 of sweet calamite, two hundred and fifty. Of cassia, two hundred and fifty after the holy sicle, and of oil olive an hin." 
Exodus30:25 And make of them holy anointing oil even an oil compound after the craft of the apothecary. 
Exodus30:26 And anoint the tabernacle of witness therewith, and the ark of witness," 
Exodus30:27 and the table with all his apparel, and the candlestick with all his ordinance, and the altar of incense," 
Exodus30:28 and the altar of burnt sacrifice and all his vessels, and the laver and his foot." 
Exodus30:29 And sacrify them that they may be most holy: so that no man twich them but they that be hallowed. 
Exodus30:30 And anoint Aaron and his sons and consecrate them to minister unto me. 
Exodus30:31 And thou shalt speak unto the children of Israel saying: this shall be an holy anointing oil unto me, thorowout your generations." 
Exodus30:32 No man's flesh shall be anointed therewith: neither shall ye make any other after the making of it for it is holy, see therefore that ye take it for holy:" 
Exodus30:33 Whosoever maketh like that, or whosoever putteth any of it upon a stranger, shall perish from among his people." 
Exodus30:34 And the LORD said unto Moses: take unto thee sweet spices: stacte, onycha, sweet galbanum and pure frankincense, of each like much:" 
Exodus30:35 and make cense of them compounded after the craft of the apothecary, mingled together, that it may be made pure and holy." 
Exodus30:36 And beat it to powder and put it before the witness in the tabernacle of witness, where I will meet thee, but let it be unto you holy." 
Exodus30:37 And see that ye make none after the making of that, but let it be unto you holy for the LORD." 
Exodus30:38 And whosoever shall make like unto that, to smell thereto, shall perish from among his people." 
Exodus31:1 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying: 
Exodus31:2 behold, I have called by name, Bezaleel the son of Uri son to Hur of the tribe of Juda." 
Exodus31:3 And I have filled him with the spirit of God, with wisdom, understanding and knowledge: even in all manner work," 
Exodus31:4 to find out subtle feats, to work in gold, silver and brass" 
Exodus31:5 and with the craft to grave stones, to set and to carve in timber, and to work in all manner workmanship." 
Exodus31:6 And behold, I have given him to be his companion Ahaliab the son of Ahisamach of the tribe of Dan, and in the hearts of all that are wise hearted I have put wisdom to make all that I have commanded thee:" 
Exodus31:7 the tabernacle of witness, and the ark of witness, and the mercyseat that is there upon, all the ornaments of the tabernacle," 
Exodus31:8 and the table with his ordinance, and the pure candlestick with all his apparel, and the altar of incense," 
Exodus31:9 and the altar of burnt offerings with all his vessels, and the laver with his foot," 
Exodus31:10 and the vestments to minister in, and the holy garments for Aaron the priest, and the garments of his sons to minister in," 
Exodus31:11 and the anointing oil and the sweet cense for the sanctuary: according to all as I have commanded thee shall they do. 
Exodus31:12 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying: 
Exodus31:13 speak unto the children of Israel and say: in any wise see that ye keep my Sabbath, for it shall be a sign between me and you in your generations for to know, that I the LORD do sanctify you." 
Exodus31:14 Keep my Sabbath therefore, that it be an holy thing unto you. He that defileth it, shall be slain therefore. For whosoever worketh therein, the same soul shall be rooted out from among his people." 
Exodus31:15 Six days shall men work, but the seventh day is the Sabbath of the holy rest of the LORD: so that whosoever doeth any work in the Sabbath day, shall die for it." 
Exodus31:16 Wherefore let the children of Israel keep the Sabbath, that they observe it thorowout their generations, that it be an appointment for ever." 
Exodus31:17 For it shall be a sign between me, and the children of Israel for ever. For in six days the LORD made heaven and earth, and the seventh day he rested and was refreshed." 
Exodus31:18 And when he(the LORDe) had made an end of communing with Moses upon the mount Sinai, he gave him two tables of witness: which were of stone and written with the finger of God." 
Exodus32:1 And when the people saw that it was long or Moses came down out of the mountain, they gathered them selves together and came unto Aaron and said unto him: Up and make us a god to go before us: for of this Moses the fellow that brought us out of the land of Egypt, we wot not what is become." 
Exodus32:2 And Aaron said unto them: pluck off the golden earings which are in the ears of your wives, your sons and of your daughters: and bring them unto me." 
Exodus32:3 And all the people plucked off the golden earings that were in their ears, and brought them unto Aaron." 
Exodus32:4 And he received them of their hands and fashioned it with a graver and made it a calf of molten metal. And they said: This is thy God, O Israel, which brought thee out of the land of Egypt." 
Exodus32:5 And when Aaron saw that, he made an altar before it, and made a proclamation saying tomorrow shall be holy day unto the LORD." 
Exodus32:6 And they rose up in the morning and offered burnt offerings, and brought offerings of atonement also. And then they sat them down to eat and drink, and rose up again to play." 
Exodus32:7 Then the LORD said unto Moses: go get thee down, for thy people which thou broughtest out of the land of Egypt," 
Exodus32:8 have marred all they are turned at once out of the way which I commanded them, and have made them a calf of molten metal, and have worshipped it and have offered thereto and have said: This is thy God thou Israel, which hath brought thee out of the land of Egypt." 
Exodus32:9 And the LORD said unto Moses: behold, I see this people, that it is a stiffnecked people," 
Exodus32:10 and now therefore suffer me that my wrath may wax hot upon them, and that I may consume them: and then will I make of thee a mighty people." 
Exodus32:11 Then Moses besought the LORD his God and said: O LORD, why should thy wrath wax hot upon thy people which thou hast brought out of the land of Egypt with great power and with a mighty hand?" 
Exodus32:12 wherefore should the Egyptians speak and say: For a mischief did he bring them out: even for to slay them in the mountains, and to consume them from the face of the earth. Turn from thy fierce wrath, and have compassion over the wickedness of thy people." 
Exodus32:13 Remember Abraham, Isaac and Israel thy servants, to whom thou sworest by thine own self and saidest unto them: I will multiply your seed as the stars of heaven, and all this land which I have said, I will give unto your seed: and they shall inherit it for ever." 
Exodus32:14 And the LORD refrained himself from that evil, which he said he would do unto his people." 
Exodus32:15 And Moses turned his back and went down from the hill, and the two tables of witness in his hand: which were written on both the leaves" 
Exodus32:16 and were the work of God, and the writing was the writing of God graven upon the tables." 
Exodus32:17 And when Josua heard the noise of the people as they shouted, he said unto Moses: there is a noise of war in the host." 
Exodus32:18 And he said: it is not the cry of them that have the mastery, nor of them that have the worse: but I do hear the noise of singing." 
Exodus32:19 And as soon as he came nigh unto the host and saw the calf and the dancing, his wrath waxed hot, and he cast the tables out of his hand, and brake them even at the hill foot." 
Exodus32:20 And the took the calf which they had made and burned it with fire, and stamped it unto powder and strowed it in the water, and made the children of Israel drink." 
Exodus32:21 And then Moses said unto Aaron: what did this people unto thee that thou hast brought so great a sin upon them? 
Exodus32:22 And Aaron said: let not the wrath of my Lord(Lorde) wax fierce, thou knowest the people that they are even set on mischief:" 
Exodus32:23 they said unto me: make us a god to go before us, for we wot not what is become of Moses the fellow that brought us out of the land of Egypt." 
Exodus32:24 And I said unto them: let them that have gold, take and bring it me: and I cast it into the fire, and thereof came out this calf." 
Exodus32:25 When Moses saw that the people were naked (for Aaron had made them naked unto their shame when they made insurrection) 
Exodus32:26 he went and stood in the gate of the host and said: If any man pertain unto the LORD, let him come to me. And all the sons of Levi gathered them selves together and came unto him." 
Exodus32:27 And he said unto them, thus sayeth the LORD of Israel: put every man his sword by his side, and go in and out from gate to gate thorowout the host: and slay every man his brother, every man his friend and every man his neighbour." 
Exodus32:28 And the children of Levi did as Moses had said. And there were slain of the people the same day, about three thousand men." 
Exodus32:29 Then Moses said: fill your hands unto the LORD this day, every man upon his son and upon his brother: to bring upon you a blessing this day." 
Exodus32:30 And on the morrow, Moses said unto the people: Ye have sinned a great sin. But now I will go up unto the LORD, to wit whether I can make an atonement for your sin." 
Exodus32:31 And Moses went again unto the LORD and said: Oh, this people have sinned a great sin and have made them a god of gold:" 
Exodus32:32 Yet forgive them their sin I pray thee: If not wipe me out of thy book which thou hast written. 
Exodus32:33 And the LORD said unto Moses: I will put him out of my book that hath sinned against me. 
Exodus32:34 But go and bring the people unto the land which I said unto thee: behold, mine angel shall go before thee. Neverthelater in the day when I visit, I will visit their sin upon them." 
Exodus32:35 And the LORD plagued the people, because they made the calf which Aaron made." 
Exodus33:1 And the LORD said unto Moses: depart and go hence: both thou and the people which thou hast brought out of the land of Egypt, unto the land which I swore unto Abraham, Isaac and Jacob, saying: unto thy seed I will give it." 
Exodus33:2 And I will send an angel before thee, and will cast out the Cananites, the Amorites, the Hethites, the Pherezites, the Hevites, and the Jebusites:" 
Exodus33:3 that thou mayst go in to a land that floweth with milk and honey. But I will not go among you myself, for ye are a stiffnecked people: lest I consume you by the way." 
Exodus33:4 And when the people heard this evil tidings, they sorrowed: and no man did put on his best raiment." 
Exodus33:5 And the LORD spake unto Moses, say unto the children of Israel: ye are a stiffnecked people: I must come once suddenly upon you, and make an end of you. But now put your goodly raiment from you, that I may wete what to do unto you." 
Exodus33:6 And the children of Israel laid their goodly raiment from them even under the mount Horeb. 
Exodus33:7 And Moses took the tabernacle and pitched it without the host, afar off from the host, and called it the tabernacle of witness. And all that would ask any question of the LORD, went out unto the tabernacle of witness which was without the host." 
Exodus33:8 And when Moses went out unto the tabernacle, all the people rose up and stood every man in his tent door and looked after Moses, until he was gone in to the tabernacle." 
Exodus33:9 And as soon as Moses was entered into the tabernacle, the clouden pillar descended and stood in the door of the tabernacle, and he talked with Moses." 
Exodus33:10 And when all the people saw the clouden pillar stand in the tabernacle door, they rose up and worshipped: every man in his tent door." 
Exodus33:11 And the LORD spake unto Moses face to face, as a man speaketh unto his friend. And when Moses turned again in to the host, the lad Josua his servant the son of Nun departed not out of the tabernacle." 
Exodus33:12 And Moses said unto the LORD: see, thou saidest unto me: lead this people forth, but thou shewest me not whom thou wilt send with me. And hast said moreover: I know thee by name and thou hast also found grace in my sight:" 
Exodus33:13 Now therefore, if I have found favour in thy sight, then shew me thy way and let me know thee: that I may find grace in thy sight. And look on this also, how that this nation is thy people." 
Exodus33:14 And he said: my presence shall go with thee, and I will give thee rest." 
Exodus33:15 And he said: If thy presence go not with me, carry us not hence," 
Exodus33:16 for how shall it be known now that both I and thy people have found favour in thy sight, but in that thou goest with us: that both I and thy people have a preeminence before all the people that are upon the face of the earth." 
Exodus33:17 And the LORD said unto Moses: I will do this also that thou hast said, for thou hast found grace in my sight, and I know thee by name." 
Exodus33:18 And he said: I beseech thee, shew me thy glory:" 
Exodus33:19 And he said: I will make all my good go before thee, and I will be called in this name Jehovah(Iehouah) before thee, and will shew mercy to whom I shew mercy, and will have compassion on whom I have compassion." 
Exodus33:20 And he said furthermore: thou mayst not see my face, for there shall no man see me and live." 
Exodus33:21 And the LORD said: behold, there is a place by me, and thou shalt stand upon a rock," 
Exodus33:22 and while my glory goeth forth I will put thee in a clift of the rock, and will put mine hand upon thee while I pass by." 
Exodus33:23 And then I will take away mine hand, and thou shalt see my back parts: but my face shall not be seen." 
Exodus34:1 And the LORD said unto Moses: hew the two tables of stone like unto the first that I may write in them the words which were in the first two tables, which thou brakest." 
Exodus34:2 And be ready against the morning that thou mayst come up early unto the mount of Sinai and stand(stode) me there upon the top of the mount. 
Exodus34:3 But let no man come up with thee, neither let any man be seen thorowout all the mount, neither let sheep nor oxen feed before the hill." 
Exodus34:4 And Moses hewed two tables of stone like unto the first and rose up early in the morning and went up unto the mount of Sinai as the LORD commanded him: and took in his hand the two tables of stone. 
Exodus34:5 And the LORD descended in the cloud, and stood with him there: and he called upon the name of the LORD." 
Exodus34:6 And when the LORD walked before him, he cried: LORD LORD God full of compassion and mercy, which art not lightly angry but abundant in mercy and truth," 
Exodus34:7 and keepest mercy in store for thousands, and forgivest wickedness, trespass and sin (for there is no man innocent before thee) and visitest the wickedness of the fathers upon the children and upon children's children, even unto the third and fourth generation." 
Exodus34:8 And Moses bowed himself to the earth quickly, and worshipped" 
Exodus34:9 and said: If I have found grace in thy sight o Lord,(Lorde) then let my Lord(Lorde) go with us (for it is a stubborn people) and have mercy upon our wickedness and our sin, and let us be thine enheritance." 
Exodus34:10 And he said: behold, I make an appointment before all this people, that I will do marvels: such as have not been done in all the world, neither among any nation. And all the people among which thou art, shall see the work of the LORD: for it is a terrible thing that I will do with thee:" 
Exodus34:11 keep all that I command thee this day, and behold: I will cast out before thee: the Amorites, Cananites, Hethites, Pherezites, Hevites and Jebusites." 
Exodus34:12 Take heed to thyself, that thou make no compact with the inhabiters of the land whither thou goest lest it be cause of ruin among you." 
Exodus34:13 But overthrow their altars and break their pillars, and cut down their groves," 
Exodus34:14 for thou shalt worship no strange god. For the LORD is called jealous, because he is a jealous God:" 
Exodus34:15 lest if thou make any agreement with the inhabiters of the land, when they go a whoring after their gods and do sacrifice unto their gods, they call thee and thou eat of their sacrifice:" 
Exodus34:16 And thou take of their daughters unto thy sons, and when their daughters go a whoring after their gods," 
Exodus34:17 they make thy sons go a whoring after their gods also. Thou shalt make thee no gods of metal. 
Exodus34:18 The feast of sweet bread shalt thou keep, and seven days thou shalt eat unleavened bread (as I commanded thee) in the time appointed in the month of Abib: for in the month of Abib thou camest out of Egypt." 
Exodus34:19 All that breaketh up the matrice shall be mine, and all that breaketh the matrice among thy cattle, if it be male: whether it be ox or sheep." 
Exodus34:20 But the first of the ass thou shalt buy out with a sheep, or if thou redeem him not: see thou break his neck. All the firstborn of thy sons thou must needs redeem. And see that no man appear before me empty." 
Exodus34:21 Six days thou shalt work, and the seventh thou shalt rest: both from earing and reaping." 
Exodus34:22 Thou shalt observe the feast of weeks with the first fruits of wheat harvest, and the feast of ingathering at the years' end." 
Exodus34:23 Thrice in a year shall all your men children appear before the Lord Jehovah(Lorde Iehouah) God of Israel: 
Exodus34:24 for I will cast out the nations before thee and will enlarge thy coasts, so that no man shall desire thy land, while thou goest up to appear before the face of the LORD thy God, thrice in the year." 
Exodus34:25 Thou shalt not offer the blood of my sacrifice with leavened bread: neither shall ought of the sacrifice of the feast of Passover, be left unto the morning." 
Exodus34:26 The first of the first fruits of thy land, thou shalt bring unto the house of the LORD thy God. And see, that thou seethe not a kid in his mother's milk." 
Exodus34:27 And the LORD said unto Moses: write these words, for upon these words I have made a covenant with thee and with the children of Israel." 
Exodus34:28 And he was there with the LORD forty days and forty nights, and neither ate bread nor drank water. And he wrote in the tables the words of the covenant: even ten verses." 
Exodus34:29 And Moses came down from mount Sinai and the two tables of witness in his hand, and yet he wist not that the skin of his face shone with beams of his communing with him." 
Exodus34:30 And when Aaron and all the children of Israel looked upon Moses and saw that the skin of his face shone with beams, they were afraid to come nigh him." 
Exodus34:31 But he called them to him, and then Aaron and all the chief of the company came unto him, and Moses talked with them." 
Exodus34:32 And at the last all the children of Israel came unto him, and he commanded them all that the LORD had said unto him in mount Sinai." 
Exodus34:33 And as soon as he had made an end of communing with them, he put a covering upon his face." 
Exodus34:34 But when he went before the LORD to speak with him, he took the covering off until he came out. And he came out and spake unto the children of Israel that which he was commanded." 
Exodus34:35 And the children of Israel saw the face of Moses, that the skin of his face shone with beams: but Moses put a covering upon his face, until he went in, to commune with him." 
Exodus35:1 And Moses gathered all the company of the children of Israel together, and said unto them: these are the things which the LORD hath commanded to do:" 
Exodus35:2 Six days ye shall work, but the seventh day shall be unto you the holy Sabbath of the LORD's rest: so that whosoever doth any work therein, shall die." 
Exodus35:3 Moreover ye shall kindle no fire thorowout all your habitations upon the Sabbath day. 
Exodus35:4 And Moses spake unto all the multitude of the children of Israel saying: this is the thing which the LORD commanded saying: 
Exodus35:5 Give from among you an heave offering, unto the LORD. All that are willing in their hearts, shall bring heave offerings unto the LORD: gold, silver, brass:" 
Exodus35:6 Jacinth, scarlet, purple, byss and goats' hair:" 
Exodus35:7 rams' skins red and taxus' skins and Sethim wood: 
Exodus35:8 and oil for lights and spices for the anointing oil and for the sweet cense: 
Exodus35:9 and Onyx stones and stones to be set for the Ephod and for the breastlap. 
Exodus35:10 And let all them that are wise hearted among you, come and make all that the LORD hath commanded:" 
Exodus35:11 the habitation and the tent thereof with his covering and his rings, boards, bars, pillars and sockets:" 
Exodus35:12 the ark and the staves thereof with the mercy seat and the vail that covereth it: 
Exodus35:13 the table and his staves with all that pertaineth thereto and the shewbread: 
Exodus35:14 the candlestick of light with his apparel and his lamps and the oil for the lights: 
Exodus35:15 the cense altar and his staves, the anointing oil and the sweet cense and the hanging before the tabernacle door:" 
Exodus35:16 the altar of burnt sacrifices and his brazen gridiron that longeth thereto with his staves and all his ordinance and the laver and his foot: 
Exodus35:17 the hangings of the court with his pillars and their sockets, and the hanging to the door of the court:" 
Exodus35:18 the pins of the habitation and the pins of the court with their boards: 
Exodus35:19 the ministering garments to minister with in holiness, and the holy vestments of Aaron the priest and the vestments of his sons to minister in." 
Exodus35:20 And all the company of the children of Israel departed from the presence of Moses. 
Exodus35:21 And they went (as many as their hearts couraged them and as many as their spirits made them willing) and brought heave offerings unto the LORD, to the making of the tabernacle of witness and for all his uses and for the holy vestments." 
Exodus35:22 And the men came with the women (even as many as were willing hearted) and brought bracelets, earings, rings and girdles and all manner Jewels of gold. And all the men that waved wave offerings of gold unto the LORD" 
Exodus35:23 and every man with whom was found Jacinth, scarlet, purple, byss or goats' hair or red skins of rams' or taxus' skins, brought it." 
Exodus35:24 And all that hove up gold or brass, brought an heave offering unto the LORD. And all men with whom was found sethim wood meet for any manner work or service, brought it." 
Exodus35:25 And all the women that were wise hearted to work with their hands, span, and brought the spun work, both of Jacinth, scarlet, purple and byss." 
Exodus35:26 And all the women that excelled in wisdom of heart, span the goats' hair." 
Exodus35:27 And the lords brought Onyx stones and setstones for the Ephod, and for the breastlap," 
Exodus35:28 and spice and oil: both for the lights and for the anointing oil and for the sweet cense. 
Exodus35:29 And the children of Israel brought willing offerings unto the LORD, both men and women: as many as their hearts made them willing to bring, for all manner works which the LORD had commanded to make by the hand of Moses." 
Exodus35:30 And Moses said unto the children of Israel: behold, the LORD hath called by name Bezaleel the son of Uri the son of Hur of the tribe of Juda," 
Exodus35:31 and hath filled him with the spirit of God, with wisdom, understanding and knowledge, even in all manner work," 
Exodus35:32 and to find out curious works, to work in gold, silver and brass:" 
Exodus35:33 and with graving of stones to set, and with carving in wood, and to work in all manner of subtle works." 
Exodus35:34 And he hath put in his heart the grace to teach: both him and Ahaliab the son of Ahisamach of the tribe of Dan 
Exodus35:35 hath he filled with wisdom of heart, to work all manner of graven work: they are also broiderers and workers with needle, in Jacinth, scarlet, purple and byss, and are weavers that can make all manner work, and can devise subtle works." 
Exodus36:1 And Bezaleel wrought and Ahaliab and all wise hearted men to whom the LORD had given wisdom and understanding, to know how to work all manner work for the holy service, in all that the LORD commanded." 
Exodus36:2 And Moses called for Bezaleel, Ahaliab and all the wise hearted men in whose hearts the LORD had put wisdom, even as many as their hearts couraged to come unto the work to work it." 
Exodus36:3 And they received of Moses all the heave offerings which the children of Israel had brought for the work of the holy service to make it withal. And they brought beside that, willing offerings every morning." 
Exodus36:4 And all the wise men that wrought all the holy work, came every man from his work which they made," 
Exodus36:5 and spake unto Moses saying: the people bring too much and above that is enough to serve for the work which the LORD hath commanded to make. 
Exodus36:6 And then Moses gave a commandment, and they caused it to be proclaimed thorowout the host saying: see that neither man nor woman prepare any more work for the holy heave offering, and so the people were forbidden to bring:" 
Exodus36:7 for the stuff they had, was sufficient for them unto all the work, to make it and too much." 
Exodus36:8 And all the wise hearted men among them that wrought in the work of the habitation made: even ten curtains of twined byss, Jacinth, scarlet and purple, and made them full of Cherubins with broidered work." 
Exodus36:9 The length of one curtain was twenty eight cubits and the breadth four and were all of one size. 
Exodus36:10 And they coupled five curtains by them selves, and other five by them selves." 
Exodus36:11 And they made fifty loops of Jacinth along by the edge of the utmost curtain, even in the selvedge of the coupling curtain: and likewise they made on the side of the utmost coupling curtain on, the other side," 
Exodus36:12 fifty loops they made in the one curtain, and fifty in the edge of the coupling curtain on the other side: so that the loops were one over against another." 
Exodus36:13 And they made fifty rings of gold, and coupled the curtains one to another with the rings: and so was it made a dwelling place." 
Exodus36:14 And they made eleven curtains of goats' hair to be a tent 
Exodus36:15 over the tabernacle thirty cubits long apiece and four cubits broad, and they all eleven of one sise," 
Exodus36:16 and they coupled five by them selves, and six by them selves," 
Exodus36:17 and they made fifty loops along by the border of the utmost coupling courtain on the one side, and fifty in the edge of the coupling curtain on the other side." 
Exodus36:18 And they made fifty rings of brass to couple the tent together that it might be one. 
Exodus36:19 And they made a covering unto the tent of rams' skins red, and yet another of taxus' skins above all." 
Exodus36:20 And they made boards for the dwelling place of sethim wood that stood upright," 
Exodus36:21 every board ten cubits long and a cubit and an half broad. 
Exodus36:22 And they made two feet to every board of the dwelling place joining one to another. 
Exodus36:23 And they made twenty boards for the south side of the habitation," 
Exodus36:24 and forty sockets of silver under the twenty boards, two sockets under every board, even for the two feet of them." 
Exodus36:25 And for the other side of the dwelling toward the north," 
Exodus36:26 they made other twenty boards with forty sockets of silver two sockets under every board. 
Exodus36:27 And behind in the end of the tabernacle toward the west, they made six boards" 
Exodus36:28 and two other boards for the corners of the habitation behind," 
Exodus36:29 and they were joined close both beneath and also above with clamps, and thus they did to both the corners:" 
Exodus36:30 so they were in all eight boards and sixteen sockets, under every board two sockets." 
Exodus36:31 And they made bars of Sethim wood five for the boards of the one side of the habitation 
Exodus36:32 and five for the other, and five for the boards of the west end of the habitation." 
Exodus36:33 And they made the middle bar to shoot thorow the boards: even from the one end to the other," 
Exodus36:34 and overlaid the boards with gold, and made them rings of gold to thrust the bars thorow, and covered the bars with gold." 
Exodus36:35 And they made an hanging of Jacinth, of scarlet, purple and twined byss with Cherubins of broidered work." 
Exodus36:36 And made thereunto four pillars of Sethim wood and overlaid them with gold. Their knops were also of gold, and they cast for them four sockets of silver." 
Exodus36:37 And they made an hanging for the tabernacle door: of Jacinth, scarlet, purple and twined byss of needle work," 
Exodus36:38 and the pillars of it were five with their knops, and overlaid the heads of them and the hoops with gold, with their five sockets of brass." 
Exodus37:1 And Bezaleel made the ark of sethim wood two cubits and an half long and a cubit and a half broad, and a cubit and a half high:" 
Exodus37:2 and overlaid it with fine gold both within and without, and made a crown of gold to it round about," 
Exodus37:3 and cast for it four rings of gold for the four corners of it: two rings for the one side and two for the other," 
Exodus37:4 and made staves of Sethim wood, and covered them with gold," 
Exodus37:5 and put the staves in the rings along by the side of the ark to bear it withal. 
Exodus37:6 And he made the mercyseat of pure gold two cubits and a half long and one cubit and a half broad," 
Exodus37:7 and made two Cherubins of thick gold upon the two ends of the mercy seat: 
Exodus37:8 One Cherub on the one end, and another Cherub on the other end of the mercyseat." 
Exodus37:9 And the Cherubins spread out their wings above on high,(an hye) and covered the mercyseat therewith. And their faces were one to another: even to the mercyseat ward, were the faces of the Cherubins." 
Exodus37:10 And he made the table of sethim wood two cubits long and a cubit broad, and a cubit and an half high," 
Exodus37:11 and overlaid it with fine gold, and made thereto a crown of gold round about," 
Exodus37:12 and made thereto an hoop of an hand breadth round about, and made unto the hoop a crown of gold round about," 
Exodus37:13 and cast for it four rings of gold and put the rings in the four corners by the feet: 
Exodus37:14 even under the hoop to put staves in to bear the table withal. 
Exodus37:15 And he made staves of Sethim wood and covered them with gold to bear the table withal," 
Exodus37:16 and made the vessels that were on the table of pure gold, the dishes, spoons, flat pieces and pots to pour withal." 
Exodus37:17 And he made the candlestick of pure thick gold: both the candlestick and his shaft: with branches, bowls, knops and flowers proceeding out of it." 
Exodus37:18 Six branches proceeding out of the sides thereof, three out of the one side and three out of the other." 
Exodus37:19 And on every branch were three cups like unto almonds, with knops and flowers thorowout the six branches that proceeded out of the candlestick." 
Exodus37:20 And upon the candlestick self, were four cups after the fashion of almonds with knops and flowers:" 
Exodus37:21 under every two branches a knop. 
Exodus37:22 And the knops and the branches proceeded out of it, and were all one piece of pure thick gold." 
Exodus37:23 And he made seven lamps thereto, and the snuffers thereof, and firepans of pure gold." 
Exodus37:24 An hundred weight of pure gold, made both it and all that belonged thereto." 
Exodus37:25 And he made the cense altar of Sethim wood of a cubit long and a cubit broad: even four square, and two cubits high with horns proceeding out of it." 
Exodus37:26 And he covered it with pure gold both the top and the sides round about and the horns of it, and made unto it a crown of gold round about." 
Exodus37:27 And he made two rings of gold unto it, even under the crown upon either side of it, to put staves in for to bear it withal:" 
Exodus37:28 and made staves of Sethim wood, and overlaid them with gold." 
Exodus37:29 And he made the holy anointing oil and the sweet pure incense after the apothecary's craft. 
Exodus38:1 And he made the burnt offering altar of Sethim wood, five cubits long and five cubits broad: even four square, and three cubits high." 
Exodus38:2 And he made horns in the four corners of it proceeding out of it, and overlaid it with brass." 
Exodus38:3 And he made all the vessels of the altar: the cauldrons, shovels, basins, fleshhooks and coalpans all of brass." 
Exodus38:4 And he made a brazen gridiron of network unto the altar round about alow beneath, under the compass of the altar:" 
Exodus38:5 so that it reached unto half the altar, and cast four rings of brass for the four ends of the gridiron to put staves in." 
Exodus38:6 And he made staves of sethim wood and covered them with brass," 
Exodus38:7 and put the staves in the rings along by the altar side to bear it withal, and made the altar hollow with boards." 
Exodus38:8 And he made the laver of brass and the foot of it also of brass, in the sight of them that did watch before the door of the tabernacle of witness." 
Exodus38:9 And he made the court with hangings of twined byss of an hundred cubits long upon the south side," 
Exodus38:10 and twenty pillars with twenty sockets of brass: but the knops of the pillars, and the hoops were silver." 
Exodus38:11 And on the north side the hangings were an hundred cubits long with twenty pillars and twenty sockets of brass, but the knops and the hoops of the pillars were of silver." 
Exodus38:12 And on the west side, were hangings of fifty cubits long, and ten pillars with their ten sockets, and the knops and the hoops of the pillars were silver." 
Exodus38:13 And on the east side toward the son rising, were hangings of fifty cubits:" 
Exodus38:14 the hangings of the one side of the gate were fifteen cubits long, and their pillars three with their three sockets." 
Exodus38:15 And of the other side of the court gate, were hangings also of fifteen cubits long, and their pillars three with three sockets." 
Exodus38:16 Now all the hangings of the court round about, were of twined byss," 
Exodus38:17 and the sockets of the pillars were brass: but the knops and the hoops of the pillars were silver, and the heads were overlaid with silver, and all the pillars of the court were hooped about with silver." 
Exodus38:18 And the hanging of the gate of the court was needlework: of jacinth, scarlet, purple, and twined byss twenty cubits long and five in the breadth, according to the hangings of the court." 
Exodus38:19 And the pillars were four with four sockets of brass, and the knops of silver, and the heads overlaid with silver and hooped about with silver," 
Exodus38:20 and all the pins of the tabernacle and of the court round about were brass. 
Exodus38:21 This is the sum of the habitation of witness, which was counted at the commandment of Moses: and was the office of the Levites by the hand of Ithamar son to Aaron the priest." 
Exodus38:22 And Bezaleel son of Uri son to Hur of the tribe of Juda, made all that the LORD commanded Moses," 
Exodus38:23 and with him Ahaliab son of Ahisamach of the tribe of Dan, a cunning graver and a worker of needle work in jacinth, scarlet, purple and byss." 
Exodus38:24 All the gold that was occupied upon all the work of the holy place (which was the gold of the wave offering) was, twenty nine hundred weight and seven hundred and thirty sicles, according to the holy sicle." 
Exodus38:25 And the sum of silver that came of the multitude, was five score hundred weight and a thousand seven hundred and seventy five sicles of the holy sicle." 
Exodus38:26 Every man offering half a sicle after the weight of the holy sicle among them that went to be numbered from twenty year old and above, among six hundred thousand and three thousand and five hundred and fifty men." 
Exodus38:27 And the five score hundred weight of silver went to the casting of the sockets of the sanctuary and the sockets of the vail: an hundred sockets of the five score hundred weight, an hundred weight to every socket." 
Exodus38:28 And the thousand seven hundred and seventy five sicles, made knops to the pillars and overlaid the heads and hooped them." 
Exodus38:29 And the brass of the wave offering was seventy hundred weight and two thousand, and four hundred sicles." 
Exodus38:30 And therewith he made the sockets to the door of the tabernacle of witness, and the brazen altar, and the brazen gridiron that longeth thereto, and all the vessels of the altar," 
Exodus38:31 and the sockets of the court round about, and the sockets of the court gate, and all the pins of the habitation, and all the pins of the court round about." 
Exodus39:1 And of the jacinth, scarlet, purple and twined byss, they made the vestments of ministration to do service (in) in that holy place, and made the holy garments that pertained to Aaron, as the LORD commanded Moses." 
Exodus39:2 And they made the Ephod of gold, jacinth, scarlet, purple, and twined byss." 
Exodus39:3 And they did beat the gold into thin plates, and cut it into wires: to work it in the jacinth, scarlet, purple, and the byss, with broidered work." 
Exodus39:4 And they made the sides come together, and closed them up by the two edges." 
Exodus39:5 And the broidering of the girdle that was upon it, was of the same stuff and after the same work of gold, jacinth, scarlet, purple and twined byss, as the LORD commanded Moses." 
Exodus39:6 And they wrought Onyx stones closed in ouches of gold and graved as signets are graven with the names of the children of Israel," 
Exodus39:7 and put them on the shoulders of the Ephod that they should be a remembrance of the children of Israel, as the LORD commanded Moses." 
Exodus39:8 And they made the brestlap of cunning work, after the work of the Ephod: even of gold, Jacinct, scarlet, purple and twined byss" 
Exodus39:9 And they made it four square and double, an hand breadth long and an hand breadth broad." 
Exodus39:10 And they filled it with four rows of stones (the first row: Sardios, a Topas and Smaragdus:" 
Exodus39:11 the second row: a Ruby, a Sapphire and a Diamond." 
Exodus39:12 The third row: Ligurius, an Achat and an Amethyst." 
Exodus39:13 The fourth row: a Turquoise, an onyx and a Jasper) closed in ouches of gold in their inclosures." 
Exodus39:14 And the twelve stones were graven as signets with the names of the children of Israel: every stone with his name, according to the twelve tribes." 
Exodus39:15 And they made upon the breastlap, two fastening chains of wreathen work and pure gold." 
Exodus39:16 And they made two hooks of gold and two gold rings, and put the two rings upon the two corners of the breastlap." 
Exodus39:17 And they put the two chains of gold in the two rings, in the corners of the breastlap." 
Exodus39:18 And the two ends of the two chains they fastened in the two hooks, and put them on the shoulders of the Ephod upon the forefront of it." 
Exodus39:19 And they made two other rings of gold and put them on the two other corners of the breastlap along upon the edge of it, toward the inside of the Ephod that is over against it." 
Exodus39:20 And they made yet two other gold rings, and put them on the two sides of the Ephod, beneath on the fore side of it: even where the sides go together, above upon the broidering of the Ephod," 
Exodus39:21 and they strained the breastlap by his rings unto the rings of the Ephod, with laces of jacinth, that it might lie fast upon the broidering of the Ephod, and should not be lowsed from of the Ephod: as the LORD commanded Moses." 
Exodus39:22 And he made the tunicle unto the Ephod of woven work and all together of jacinth," 
Exodus39:23 and the head of the tunicle was in the middest of it as the collar of a partlet, with a band round about the collar, that it should not rent." 
Exodus39:24 And they made beneath upon the hem of the tunicle: pomegranates of jacinth, scarlet, purple, and twined byss." 
Exodus39:25 And they made little bells of pure gold, and put them among the pomegranates round about upon the edge of the tunicle, a bell and a pomegranate," 
Exodus39:26 a bell and a pomegranate round about the hems of the tunicle to minister in, as the LORD commanded Moses." 
Exodus39:27 And they made coats of byss of woven work for Aaron and his sons," 
Exodus39:28 and a mitre of byss, and goodly bonnets of byss, and linen breeches of twined byss," 
Exodus39:29 and a girdle of twined byss, jacinth, scarlet and purple: even of needle work, as the LORD commanded Moses." 
Exodus39:30 And they made the plate of the holy crown of fine gold, and wrote upon it with graven work: the holiness of the LORD," 
Exodus39:31 and tied it to a lace of jacinth to fasten it on high(an hye) upon the mitre, as the LORD commanded Moses." 
Exodus39:32 Thus was all the work of the habitation of the tabernacle of witness, finished. And the children of Israel did, according to all that the LORD had commanded Moses." 
Exodus39:33 And they brought the habitation unto Moses: the tent and all his apparel thereof: the buttons, boards, bars, pillars and sockets:" 
Exodus39:34 and the covering of rams' skins red, and the covering of taxus' skins, and the hanging vail," 
Exodus39:35 and the ark of witness with the staves thereof, and the mercyseat:" 
Exodus39:36 the table and all the ordinance thereof, and the shewbread," 
Exodus39:37 and the pure candlestick, and the lamps prepared thereunto with all the vessels thereof, and the oil for lights," 
Exodus39:38 and the golden altar, and the anointing oil and the sweet cense, and the hanging of the tabernacle door," 
Exodus39:39 and the brasen altar, and the gridiron of brass longing thereunto with his bars and all his vessels, and the laver with his foot," 
Exodus39:40 and the hangings of the court with his pillars and sockets, and the hanging to the court gate, his boards and pins, and all the ordinance that serveth to the habitation of the tabernacle of witness," 
Exodus39:41 and the ministering vestments to serve in the holy place, and the holy vestments of Aaron the priest and his sons' raiments to minister in:" 
Exodus39:42 according to all that the LORD commanded Moses: even so the children of Israel made all the work. 
Exodus39:43 And Moses beheld all the work: and see, they had done it even as the LORD commanded: and then Moses blessed them." 
Exodus40:1 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying: 
Exodus40:2 In the first day of the first month shalt thou set up the habitation of the tabernacle of witness," 
Exodus40:3 and put therein the ark of witness, and cover the ark with the vail," 
Exodus40:4 and bring in the table and apparel it, and bring in the candlestick and put on his lamps," 
Exodus40:5 and set the cense altar of gold before the ark of witness, and put the hanging of the door unto the habitation." 
Exodus40:6 And set the burnt offering altar before the door of the tabernacle of witness," 
Exodus40:7 and set the laver between the tabernacle of witness, and the altar, and put water therein," 
Exodus40:8 and make the court round about, and set up the hanging of the court gate." 
Exodus40:9 And take the anointing oil and anoint the habitation and all that is therein, and hallow it and all that belong thereto: that it may be holy." 
Exodus40:10 And anoint the altar of the burnt offerings and all his vessels, and sanctify the altar that it may be most holy." 
Exodus40:11 And anoint also the laver and his foot, and sanctify it." 
Exodus40:12 Then bring Aaron and his sons unto the door of the tabernacle of witness, and wash them with water." 
Exodus40:13 And put upon Aaron the holy vestments. 
Exodus40:14 And anoint him and sanctify him that he may minister unto me," 
Exodus40:15 that their anointing may be an everlasting priesthood unto them thorowout their generations. 
Exodus40:16 And Moses did according to all that the LORD commanded him. 
Exodus40:17 Thus was the tabernacle reared up the first month in the second year. 
Exodus40:18 And Moses reared up the tabernacle and fastened his sockets, and set up the boards and put in their bars, and reared up the pillars," 
Exodus40:19 and spread abroad the tent over the habitation and put the covering of the tent on high(an hye) above it: as the LORD commanded Moses. 
Exodus40:20 And he took and put the testimony in the ark, and set the staves to the ark and put the mercy seat on high(an hye) upon the ark," 
Exodus40:21 and brought the ark into the habitation and hanged up the vail and covered the ark of witness, as the LORD commanded Moses." 
Exodus40:22 And he put the table in the tabernacle of witness in the north side of the habitation without the vail," 
Exodus40:23 and set the bread in order before the LORD, even as the LORD had commanded Moses." 
Exodus40:24 And he put the candlestick in the tabernacle of witness over against the table in the south side of the habitation," 
Exodus40:25 and set up the lamps before the LORD: as the LORD commanded Moses. 
Exodus40:26 And he put the golden altar in the tabernacle of witness before the vail," 
Exodus40:27 and brent sweet cense thereon as the LORD commanded Moses. 
Exodus40:28 And set up the hanging in the door of the habitation," 
Exodus40:29 and set the burnt offering altar before the door of the tabernacle of witness, and offered burnt offerings and meat offerings thereon as the LORD commanded Moses." 
Exodus40:30 And he set the laver between the tabernacle of witness and the altar, and poured water therein to wash with all." 
Exodus40:31 And both Moses Aaron and his sons washed their hands and their feet thereat: 
Exodus40:32 both when they went into the tabernacle of witness, or when they went to the altar, as the LORD commanded Moses." 
Exodus40:33 And he reared up the court round about the habitation and the altar, and set up the hanging of the court gate: and so Moses finished the work." 
Exodus40:34 And the cloud covered the tabernacle of witness, and the glory of the LORD filled the habitation:" 
Exodus40:35 so that Moses could not enter into the tabernacle of witness, because the cloud abode therein, and the glory of the LORD filled the habitation." 
Exodus40:36 When the cloud was taken up from off the habitation, the children of Israel took their journeys as oft as they journeyed." 
Exodus40:37 And if the cloud departed not, they journeyed not till it departed:" 
Exodus40:38 for the cloud of the LORD was upon the habitation by day, and fire by night: in the sight of all the house of Israel in all their journeys. [The end of the second book of Moses.]" 
Leviticus1:1 And the LORD called Moses, and spake unto him out of the tabernacle of witness saying:" 
Leviticus1:2 Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them: Whosoever of you shall bring a gift unto the LORD, shall bring it of the cattle: even of the oxen and of the sheep." 
Leviticus1:3 If he bring a burntoffering of the oxen he shall offer a male without blemish, and shall bring him to the door of the tabernacle of witness, that he may be accepted before the LORD." 
Leviticus1:4 And let him put his hand upon the head of the burntsacrifice, and favour shall be given him to make an atonement for him," 
Leviticus1:5 and let him kill the ox before the LORD. And let the priests Aaron's sons bring the blood and let them sprinkle it round about upon the altar that is before the door of the tabernacle of witness. 
Leviticus1:6 And let the burntofferings be stripped and hewed in pieces. 
Leviticus1:7 And then let the sons of Aaron the priest put fire upon the altar, and put wood upon the fire," 
Leviticus1:8 and let them lay the pieces with the head and the fat, upon the wood that is on the fire in the altar." 
Leviticus1:9 But the inwards and the legs they shall wash in water, and the priest shall burn altogether upon the altar, that it be a burnt sacrifice, and an offering of a sweet odour unto the LORD." 
Leviticus1:10 If he will offer a burnt sacrifice of the sheep whether it be of the lambs or of the goats: he shall offer a male without blemish. 
Leviticus1:11 And let him kill it on the north side of the altar, before the LORD. And let the priests Aaron's sons sprinkle the blood of it, round about upon the altar." 
Leviticus1:12 And let it be cut in pieces: even with his head and his fat, and let the priest put them upon the wood that lieth upon the fire in the altar." 
Leviticus1:13 But let him wash the inwards and the legs with water, and then bring altogether and burn it upon the altar: that is a burntoffering and a sacrifice of sweet savour unto the LORD." 
Leviticus1:14 If he will offer a burntoffering of the fowls (unto the LORD) he shall offer either of the turtle doves or of the young pigeons. 
Leviticus1:15 And the priest shall bring it unto the altar, and wring the neck asunder of it, and burn it on the altar, and let the blood run out upon the sides of the altar," 
Leviticus1:16 and pluck away his crop and his feathers, and cast them beside the altar on the east part upon the heap of ashes," 
Leviticus1:17 and break his wings, but pluck them not asunder. And then let the priest burn it upon the altar, even upon the wood that lieth upon the fire, a burnt sacrifice and an offering of a sweet savour unto the LORD." 
Leviticus2:1 If any soul will offer a meatoffering unto the LORD, his offering shall be fine flour, and he shall pour thereto oil, and put frankincense thereon," 
Leviticus2:2 and shall bring it unto Aaron's sons the priests. And one of them shall take thereout his handful of the flour, and of the oil with all the frankincense, and burn it for a memorial upon the altar: an offering of a sweet savour unto the LORD." 
Leviticus2:3 And the remnant of the meatoffering shall be Aaron's and his sons, as a thing most holy of the sacrifices of the LORD." 
Leviticus2:4 If any man bring a meatoffering that is baken in the oven, let him bring sweet cakes of fine flour mingled with oil, and unleavened wafers anointed with oil." 
Leviticus2:5 If thy meatoffering be baken in the frying pan, then it shall be of sweet flour mingled with oil." 
Leviticus2:6 And thou shalt mince it small, and pour oil thereon: and so is it a meatoffering." 
Leviticus2:7 If thy meatoffering be a thing broiled upon the gridiron, of flour mingled with oil it shall be." 
Leviticus2:8 And thou shalt bring the meatoffering that is made of these things unto the LORD, and shalt deliver it unto the priest, and he shall bring it unto the altar," 
Leviticus2:9 and shall heave up part of the meatoffering for a memorial, and shall burn it upon the altar: an offering of a sweet savour unto the LORD." 
Leviticus2:10 And that which is left of the meatoffering shall be Aaron's and his sons, as a thing that is most holy of the offerings of the LORD." 
Leviticus2:11 All the meatofferings which ye shall bring unto the LORD, shall be made without leaven. For ye shall neither burn leaven nor honey in any offering of the LORD:" 
Leviticus2:12 Notwithstanding ye shall bring the firstlings of them unto the LORD: But they shall not come upon the altar to make a sweet savour. 
Leviticus2:13 All thy meatofferings thou shalt salt with salt: neither shalt thou suffer the salt of the covenant of thy God to be lacking from thy meatoffering: but upon all thine offerings thou shalt bring salt. 
Leviticus2:14 If thou offer a meatoffering of the first ripe fruits unto the LORD, then take of that which is yet green, and dry it by the fire and beat it small, and so offer the meatoffering of thy first ripe fruits." 
Leviticus2:15 And then pour oil thereto, and put frankincense thereon: and so it is a meatoffering." 
Leviticus2:16 And the priest shall burn part of the beaten corn and part of that oil, with all the frankincense, for a remembrance. That is an offering unto the LORD." 
Leviticus3:1 If any man bring a peaceoffering of the oxen: whether it be male or female, he shall bring such as is without blemish, before the LORD," 
Leviticus3:2 and let him put his hand upon the head of his offering, and kill it before the door of the tabernacle of witness. And Aaron's sons the priests, shall sprinkle the blood upon the altar round about." 
Leviticus3:3 And they shall offer of the peaceoffering to be a sacrifice unto the LORD: the fat that covereth the inwards and all the fat that is upon the inwards: 
Leviticus3:4 and the two kidneys with the fat that lieth upon the loins: and the caul that is on the liver, they shall take away with the kidneys." 
Leviticus3:5 And Aaron's sons shall burn them upon the altar with the burntsacrifice which is upon the wood on the fire. That is a sacrifice of a sweet favour unto the LORD. 
Leviticus3:6 If a man bring a peaceoffering unto the LORD from of the flock: whether it be male, or female, it shall be without blemish." 
Leviticus3:7 If he offer a lamb, he shall bring it before the LORD," 
Leviticus3:8 and put his hand upon his offering's head, and kill it in the door of the tabernacle of witness, and Aaron's sons shall sprinkle the blood thereof round about the altar." 
Leviticus3:9 And of the peaceoffering they shall bring a sacrifice unto the LORD: the fat thereof and the rump altogether, which they shall take off hard by the back bone: and the fat that covereth the inwards and all the fat that is upon the inwards" 
Leviticus3:10 and the two kidneys with the fat that lieth upon them and upon the loins, and the caul that is upon the liver he shall take away with the kidneys." 
Leviticus3:11 And the priest shall burn them upon the altar to feed the LORD's offering withall. 
Leviticus3:12 If the offering be a goat, he shall bring it before the LORD" 
Leviticus3:13 and put his hand upon the head of it and kill it before the tabernacle of witness, and the sons of Aaron shall sprinkle the blood thereof upon the altar round about." 
Leviticus3:14 And he shall bring thereof his offering unto the LORD's sacrifice: the fat that covereth the inwards and all the fat that is upon the inwards 
Leviticus3:15 and the two kidneys and the fat that lieth upon them and upon the loins, and the caul that is upon the liver he shall take away with the kidneys." 
Leviticus3:16 And the priest shall burn them upon the alter to feed the LORD's sacrifice with all and to make a sweet savour. And thus shall all the fat be the LORD's," 
Leviticus3:17 and it shall be a law forever among your generations after you in your dwelling places: that ye eat neither fat nor blood. 
Leviticus4:1 And the LORD talked with Moses saying: 
Leviticus4:2 speak unto the children of Israel and say: when a soul sinneth thorow ignorance, and hath done any of those things which the LORD hath forbidden in his commandments to be done:" 
Leviticus4:3 If the priest that is anointed sin and make the people to do amiss, he shall bring for his sin which he hath done: an ox without blemish unto the LORD for a sinoffering." 
Leviticus4:4 And he shall bring the ox unto the door of the tabernacle of witness before the LORD, and shall put his hand upon the ox's head and kill him before the LORD." 
Leviticus4:5 And the priest that is anointed shall take of the ox's blood, and bring it into the tabernacle of witness," 
Leviticus4:6 and shall dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle thereof seven times before the LORD: even before the hanging of the holy place. 
Leviticus4:7 And he shall put some of the blood upon the horns of the altar of sweet cense before the LORD which is in the tabernacle of witness, and shall pour all the blood of the ox upon the bottom of the altar of burntofferings which is by the door of the tabernacle of witness." 
Leviticus4:8 And he shall take away all the fat of the ox that is the sinoffering: the fat that covereth the inwards and all the fat that is about them," 
Leviticus4:9 and the two kidneys with the fat that lieth upon them and upon the loins, and the caul upon the liver let them take away also with the kidneys:" 
Leviticus4:10 as it was taken from the ox of the peaceoffering, and let the priest burn them upon the altar of burntofferings." 
Leviticus4:11 But the skin of the ox and all his flesh with his head, his legs, his inwards with his dung," 
Leviticus4:12 shall he carry altogether out of the host unto a clean place: even where the ashes are poured out, and burn him on wood with fire: even upon the heap of ashes." 
Leviticus4:13 If the whole commonalty of the children of Israel sin thorow ignorance, and the thing be hid from their eyes: so that they have committed any of these things which the LORD hath forbidden to be done in his commandments and have offended," 
Leviticus4:14 and the sin which they have sinned be afterward known, then shall they offer an ox for a sinoffering and shall bring him before the tabernacle of witness," 
Leviticus4:15 and the elders of the multitude shall put their hands upon his head before the LORD. 
Leviticus4:16 And the priest that is anointed shall bring of his blood into the tabernacle of witness," 
Leviticus4:17 and shall dip his finger in the blood, and sprinkle it seven times before the LORD: even before the vail."
