Numbers22:32 And the angel of the LORD said unto him: Wherefore smitest thou thine ass this three times? behold, I came out to resist thee, for the way is contrary unto me," 
Numbers22:33 and the ass saw me and avoided me three times: or else (had she not turned from me) I had surely slain thee and saved her alive. 
Numbers22:34 And Balam said unto the angel of the LORD: I have sinned: for I wist not that thou stoodest in the way against me. Now therefore if it displease thine eyes, I will turn again." 
Numbers22:35 And the angel said unto Balam, go with the men: but in any wise, what I say unto thee, that say. And Balam went with the lords of Balac." 
Numbers22:36 And when Balac heard that Balam was come he went out against him unto a city of Moab that stood in the border of Arnon, which was the utmost part of his country." 
Numbers22:37 And Balac said unto Balam: did I not send for thee, to call thee? wherefore camest thou not unto me? thinkest thou that I am not able to promote thee unto honour?" 
Numbers22:38 And Balam said unto Balac: Lo I am come unto thee. But I can say nothing at all save what God putteth in my mouth that must I speak. 
Numbers22:39 And Balam went with Balac, and they came unto the city of Huzoth.(large city)" 
Numbers22:40 And Balac offered oxen and sheep and sent for Balam and for the lords that were with him. 
Numbers22:41 And on the morning Balac took Balam and brought him up into the high place of Baal, and thence he saw unto the utmost part of the people." 
Numbers23:1 And Balam said unto Balac: build me here seven altars and provide here seven oxen and seven rams. 
Numbers23:2 And Balac did as Balam said. And Balac and Balam offered on every altar an ox and a ram. 
Numbers23:3 And Balam said unto Balac: stand by the sacrifice, while I go to wete whether the LORD will come and meet me, and whatsoever he sheweth me, I will tell thee, and he went forthwith." 
Numbers23:4 And God came unto Balam, and Balam said unto him: I have prepared seven altars, and have offered upon every altar, an ox and a ram." 
Numbers23:5 And the LORD put a saying in Balam's mouth and said: go again to Balac and say on this wise. 
Numbers23:6 And he went again unto him and lo, he stood by his sacrifice, both he and all the lords of Moab." 
Numbers23:7 And he began his parable and said: Balac the king of Moab hath fetched me from Mesopotamia out of the mountains of the east saying: come and curse me Jacob, come and defy me Israel." 
Numbers23:8 How shall I curse whom God curseth not and how shall I defy whom the LORD defieth not? 
Numbers23:9 from the top of the rocks I see him and from the hills I behold him: Lo, the people shall dwell by himself and shall not be reckoned among other nations." 
Numbers23:10 Who can tell the dust of Jacob and the number of the fourth part of Israel. I pray God that my soul, may die the death of the righteous, and that my last end may be like his." 
Numbers23:11 And Balac said unto Balam, what hast thou done unto me? I fetched thee to curse mine enemies: and behold, thou blessest them." 
Numbers23:12 And he answered and said: must I not keep that and speak it, which the LORD hath put in my mouth?" 
Numbers23:13 And Balac said unto him: Come I pray thee with me unto another place, whence thou shalt see them, and shalt see but the utmost part of them and shalt not see them all and curse me them there." 
Numbers23:14 And he brought him into a plain field where men might see far, even to the top of Pisga, and built seven altars and offered an ox and a ram on every altar." 
Numbers23:15 And he said unto Balac: stand here by thy sacrifice while I go yonder. 
Numbers23:16 And the LORD met Balam and put words in his mouth and said: go again unto Balac and thus say. 
Numbers23:17 And when he came to him: behold, he stood by his sacrifice and the lords of Moab with him; And Balac said unto him: what sayeth the LORD?" 
Numbers23:18 And he took up his parable and said: rise up Balac and hear, and hearken unto me thou son of Ziphor." 
Numbers23:19 The Lord(God) is not a man, that he can lie, neither the son of a man that he can repent: should he say and not do, or should he speak and not make it good?" 
Numbers23:20 behold, I have begun to bless and have blessed, and can not go back therefrom." 
Numbers23:21 He beheld no wickedness in Jacob nor saw Idolatry in Israel: The LORD his God is with him, and the tromp(triumph) of a king among them." 
Numbers23:22 God that brought them out of Egypt, is as the strength of an unicorn unto them," 
Numbers23:23 for there is no sorcerer, in Jacob, nor soothsayer in Israel. When the time cometh, it will be said of Jacob and of Israel, what God hath wrought." 
Numbers23:24 Behold, the people shall rise up as a lioness and heave up himself as a lion, and shall not lie down again, until he have eaten of the prey and drunk of the blood of them that are slain." 
Numbers23:25 And Balac said unto Balam: neither curse them nor bless them. 
Numbers23:26 And Balam answered and said unto Balac: told not I thee saying, all that the LORD biddeth me, that I must do?" 
Numbers23:27 And Balac said unto Balam: come I pray thee, I will bring thee yet unto another place: so peradventure it shall please God, that thou mayst curse them there." 
Numbers23:28 And Balac brought Balam unto the top of Peor, that boweth toward the wilderness." 
Numbers23:29 And Balam said unto Balac: make me here seven altars, and prepare me here seven bullocks and seven rams." 
Numbers23:30 And Balac did as Balam had said, and offered a bullock and a ram on every altar." 
Numbers24:1 When Balam saw that it pleased the LORD that he should bless Israel, he went not as he did twice before to fetch soothsaying, but set his face toward the wilderness," 
Numbers24:2 and lift up his eyes and looked upon Israel as he lay with his tribes, and the spirit of God came upon him." 
Numbers24:3 And he took up his parable and said: Balam the son of Beor hath said," 
Numbers24:4 and the man whose eye is open hath said: he hath said which heareth the words of God and seeth the visions of the almighty, which falleth down and his eyes are opened." 
Numbers24:5 How goodly are the tents of Jacob and thine habitations Israel," 
Numbers24:6 even as the broad valleys and as gardens by the river's side, as the tents which the LORD hath pitched and as cypress trees upon the water." 
Numbers24:7 The water shall flow out of his bucket and his seed shall be many waters, and his king shall be higher than Agag; And his kingdom shall be exalted." 
Numbers24:8 God that brought him out of Egypt is as the strength of an unicorn unto him, and he shall eat the nations that are his enemies and break their bones and pierce them thorow with his arrows." 
Numbers24:9 He couched himself and lay down as a lion and as a lioness, who shall stir him up? blessed is he that blesseth thee, and cursed is he that curseth thee." 
Numbers24:10 And Balac was wroth with Balam and smote his hands together, and said unto him: I sent for thee to curse mine enemies: and behold, thou hast blessed them this three times," 
Numbers24:11 and now get thee quickly unto thy place. I thought that I would promote thee unto honour, but the LORD hath kept thee back from worship." 
Numbers24:12 And Balam said unto Balac: told I not thy messengers which thou sentest unto me saying: 
Numbers24:13 If Balac would give me his house full of silver and gold, I can not pass the mouth of the LORD, to do either good or bad of mine own mind. What the LORD sayeth, that must I speak." 
Numbers24:14 And now behold, I go unto my people: come let me shew thee, what this people shall do to thy folk in the latter days." 
Numbers24:15 And he began his parable and said: Balam the son of Beor hath said, and the man that hath his eye open hath said," 
Numbers24:16 and he hath said that heareth the words of God and hath the knowledge of the most high and beholdeth the vision of the almighty, and when he falleth down hath his eyes opened." 
Numbers24:17 I see him but not now, I behold him but not nigh. There shall come a star of Jacob and rise a scepter of Israel, which shall smite the coasts of Moab and undermine all the children of Seth." 
Numbers24:18 And Edom shall be his possession, and the possession of Seir shall be their enemies, and Israel shall do manfully." 
Numbers24:19 And out of Jacob shall come he that shall destroy the remnant of the cities. 
Numbers24:20 And he looked on Amalek and began his parable and said: Amalek is the first of the nations, but his latter end shall perish utterly." 
Numbers24:21 And he looked on the Kenites, and took his parable and said: strong is thy dwelling place and put thy nest upon a rock." 
Numbers24:22 Neverthelater thou shalt be a burning to Kain, until Assur take thee prisoner." 
Numbers24:23 And he took his parable and said: Alas, who shall live when God doeth this?" 
Numbers24:24 The ships shall come out of the coast of Cittim and subdue Assur and subdue Eber, and he himself shall perish at the last." 
Numbers24:25 And Balam rose up and went and dwelt in his place: and Balac also went his way. 
Numbers25:1 And Israel dwelt in Sittim, and the people began to commit whoredom with the daughters of Moab," 
Numbers25:2 which called the people unto the sacrifice of their gods. And the people ate and worshipped their gods," 
Numbers25:3 and Israel coupled himself unto Baal Peor. Then the LORD was angry with Israel," 
Numbers25:4 and said unto Moses: take all the heads of the people, and hang them up unto the LORD against the son, that the wrath of the LORD may turn away from Israel." 
Numbers25:5 And Moses said unto the judges of Israel: go and slay those men that joined them selves unto Baal Peor. 
Numbers25:6 And behold, one of the children of Israel came and brought unto his brethren, a Madianitish wife even in the sight of Moses and in the sight of all the multitude of the children of Israel, as they were weeping in the door of the tabernacle of witness." 
Numbers25:7 And when Phineas the son of Eleazer the son of Aaron the priest saw it, he rose up out of the company and took a weapon in his hand," 
Numbers25:8 and went after the man of Israel into the whorehouse, and thrust them thorow: both the man of Israel and also the woman even thorow the belly of her. And the plague ceased from the children of Israel." 
Numbers25:9 And there died in the plague twenty four thousand. 
Numbers25:10 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying: 
Numbers25:11 Phineas the son of Eleazer the son of Aaron the priest, hath turned mine anger away from the children of Israel, because he was jealous for my sake among them, that I had not consumed the children of Israel in my jealousy." 
Numbers25:12 Wherefore say: behold, I give unto him my covenant of peace," 
Numbers25:13 and he shall have it and his seed after him, even the covenant of the priest's office for ever, because he was jealous for his God's(goddes) sake and made an atonement for the children of Israel." 
Numbers25:14 The name of the Israelite which was smitten with the Madianitish wife, was Simri the son of Salu, a lord of an ancient house among the Simeonites." 
Numbers25:15 And the name of the Madianitish wife, was Cosbi the daughter of Zur and head over the people of an ancient house in Madian." 
Numbers25:16 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying: 
Numbers25:17 vex the Madianites and smite them," 
Numbers25:18 for they have troubled you with their wiles with thee which they have beguiled you, thorow Peor and thorow their sister Cosbi the daughter of a lord in Madian, which was slain in the day of the plague for Peor's sake." 
Numbers26:1 And after the plague, the LORD spake unto Moses and unto Eleazer saying:" 
Numbers26:2 take the number of all the multitude of the children of Israel from twenty year and above thorowout their fathers' houses, all that are able to go to war in Israel." 
Numbers26:3 And Moses and Eleazer the priest told them in the fields of Moab, by Jordan fast by Jericho," 
Numbers26:4 from twenty year and above, as the LORD commanded Moses. And the children of Israel that came of Egypt were:" 
Numbers26:5 Ruben the eldest son of Israel. The children of Ruben were, Hanoch, of whom cometh the kindred of the Hanochites: and of Palu, cometh the kindred of the Paluites:" 
Numbers26:6 And of Hesron, cometh the kindred of the Hesronites: and of Carmi, cometh the kindred of the Carmites." 
Numbers26:7 These are the kindreds of the Rubenites, which were in number forty three thousand seven hundred and thirty." 
Numbers26:8 And the sons of Palu were Eliab. 
Numbers26:9 And the sons of Eliab were: Nemuel, Dathan and Abiram. This is that Dathan and Abiram councillors in the congregation, which strove against Moses and Aaron in the company of Corah, when they strove against the LORD." 
Numbers26:10 And the earth opened her mouth and swallowed them and Corah also, when the multitude died, what time the fire consumed two hundred and fifty men, and they became a sign:" 
Numbers26:11 Notwithstanding, the children of Corah died not." 
Numbers26:12 And the children of Simeon in their kindreds were: Nemuel, of whom cometh the kindred of the Nemuelites: Jamin, of whom cometh the kindred of the Jaminites: Jachin, of whom cometh the kindred of the Jachinites:" 
Numbers26:13 Serah of whom cometh the kindred of the Serahites: Saul of whom cometh the kindred of the Saulites. 
Numbers26:14 These are the kindreds of the Simeonites: in number twenty two thousand and two hundred. 
Numbers26:15 And the children of Gad in their kindreds were: Zephon, of whom cometh the kindred of the Zephonites: and of Haggi, cometh the kindred of the Haggites: and of Suni, cometh the kindred of the Sunites:" 
Numbers26:16 and of Aseni, cometh the kindred of the Asenites: and of Eri cometh the kindred of the Erites:" 
Numbers26:17 and of Arod cometh the kindred of the Arodites: and of Ariel cometh the kindred of the Arielites. 
Numbers26:18 These are the kindreds of the children of Gad, in number forty thousand and five hundred." 
Numbers26:19 The children of Juda: Er and Onan, which died in the land of Canaan." 
Numbers26:20 But the children of Juda in their kindred were: Sela of whom cometh the kindred of the Selamites: and of Phares cometh the kindred of the Pharesites: and of Serah cometh the kindred of the Serahites. 
Numbers26:21 And the children of Phares were Hesron, of whom cometh the kindred of the Hesronites: and of Hamul cometh the kindred of the Hamulites." 
Numbers26:22 These are the kindreds of Juda, in number seventy six thousand and five hundred." 
Numbers26:23 And the children of Isachar in their kindreds were: Tola, of whom cometh the kindred of the Tolaites: and Phuva, of whom cometh the kindred of the Phuvaites:" 
Numbers26:24 and of Jasub cometh the kindred of the Jasubites: and of Simron cometh the kindred of the Simronites. 
Numbers26:25 These are the kindreds of Isachar in number sixty four thousand and three hundred. 
Numbers26:26 The children of Zabulon in their kindreds were: Sered, of whom cometh the kindred of the Seredites: and Elon, of whom cometh the kindred of the Elonites: and of Jaheliel, cometh the kindred of the Jahelelites." 
Numbers26:27 These are the kindreds of Zabulon: in number sixty thousand and five hundred. 
Numbers26:28 The children of Joseph in their kindreds were: Manasse and Ephraim. 
Numbers26:29 The children of Manasse: Machir, of whom cometh the kindred of the Machirites. And Machir begat Gilead, of whom cometh the kindred of the Gileadites." 
Numbers26:30 And these are the children of Gilead: Hieser, of whom cometh the kindred of the Hieserites: and of Helech cometh the kindred of the Helechites:" 
Numbers26:31 and of Asriel the kindred of the Asrielites: and of Sichem cometh the kindred of the Sichemites: 
Numbers26:32 and of Simida cometh the kindred of the Simidites: and of Hepher cometh the kindred of the Hepherites. 
Numbers26:33 And Zelaphead the son of Hepher had no sons but daughters. And the names of the daughters of Zelaphead were: Mahela, Noa, Hagla, Milcha and Thirza." 
Numbers26:34 These are the kindreds of Manasse, in number fifty two thousand and seven hundred." 
Numbers26:35 These are the children of Ephraim in their kindreds: Suthelah, of whom cometh the kindred of the Suthelahites: and Becher, of whom cometh the kindred of the Becherites: and of Thaha cometh the kindred of the Thahanites." 
Numbers26:36 And these are the children of Suthelah: Eran, of whom cometh the kindred of the Eranites." 
Numbers26:37 These are the kindreds of the children of Ephraim in number thirty two thousand and five hundred. And these are the children of Joseph in their kindreds. 
Numbers26:38 These are the children of BenJamin in their kindreds: Bela, of whom cometh the kindred of the Belaites: and of Asbel cometh the kindred of the Asbelites: and of Ahiram, the kindred of the Ahiramites:" 
Numbers26:39 and of Supham the kindred of the Suphamites: and of Hupham the kindred of the Huphamites. 
Numbers26:40 And the children of Bela were Ard and Naaman from whence come the kindreds of the Ardites and of the Naamites. 
Numbers26:41 These are the children of BenJamin in their kindreds, and in number forty five thousand and six hundred." 
Numbers26:42 These are the children of Dan in their kindreds: Suham, of whom cometh the kindred of the Suhamites. These are the kindreds of Dan in their generations." 
Numbers26:43 And all the kindreds of the Suhamites were in number sixty four thousand and four hundred. 
Numbers26:44 The children of Asser in their kindreds were: Jemna, of whom cometh the kindred of the Jemnites: and Jesui, of whom cometh the kindred of the Jesuites: and of Bria cometh the kindred of Brites." 
Numbers26:45 And the children of Bria were Heber, of whom cometh the kindred of the Heberites: and of Malchiel came the kindred of the Malchielites." 
Numbers26:46 And the daughter of Aser was called Sarah. 
Numbers26:47 These are the kindreds of Aser in number fifty three thousand and four hundred. 
Numbers26:48 The children of Nephthali in their kindreds were: Jaheziel, of whom came the kindred of the Jahezielites: and Guni, of whom came the kindred of the Gunites:" 
Numbers26:49 and of Jezer, came the kindred of the Jezerites: and of Selem the kindred of Selemites." 
Numbers26:50 These are the kindreds of Naphthali in their generations in number forty five thousand and four hundred. 
Numbers26:51 These are the numbers of the children of Israel: six hundred thousand, and a thousand seven hundred and thirty." 
Numbers26:52 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying: 
Numbers26:53 unto these the land shall be divided to inherit, according to the number of names:" 
Numbers26:54 to many thou shalt give the more inheritance and to few the less: to every tribe shall the inheritance be given according to the number thereof. 
Numbers26:55 Notwithstanding, the land shall be divided by lot, and according to the names of the tribes of their fathers, they shall inherit:" 
Numbers26:56 and according to their lot thou shalt divide their land, both to the many and to the few." 
Numbers26:57 These are the sums of the Levites in their kindreds: of Gerson, came the kindred of the Gersonites: and of Cahath came the kindred of the Cahathites: and of Merari came the kindred of the Merarites." 
Numbers26:58 These are the kindreds of Levi: the kindred of the Libnites, the kindred of the Hebronites, the kindred of the Mahelites, the kindred of the Musites, the kindred of the Karahites. Kahath begat Amram," 
Numbers26:59 and Amram's wife was called Jochebed a daughter of Levi, which was born him in Egypt. And she bare unto Amram, Aaron, Moses and Miriam their sister." 
Numbers26:60 And unto Aaron were born, Nadab, Abihu, Eleazer and Ithamar." 
Numbers26:61 But Nadab and Abihu died, as they offered strange fire before the LORD." 
Numbers26:62 And the number of them was twenty three thousand, of all the males from a month old and above. For they were not numbered among the children of Israel, because there was no inheritance given them among the children of Israel." 
Numbers26:63 These are the numbers of the children of Israel which Moses and Eleazer the priest numbered in the fields of Moab, fast by Jordan nigh to Jericho." 
Numbers26:64 And among these there was not a man of the number of the children of Israel which Moses and Aaron told in the wilderness of Sinai. 
Numbers26:65 For the LORD said unto them, that they should die in the wilderness and that there should not be left a man of them: save Caleb the son of Jephune and Josua the son of Nun." 
Numbers27:1 And the daughters of Zelaphead the son of Heber the son of Gilead, the son of Machir the son of Manasse, of the kindreds of Manasse the son of Joseph (whose names were Mahela, Noha, Hagla, Melcha and Thirza)" 
Numbers27:2 came and stood before Moses and Eleazer the priest and before the lords and all the multitude in the door of the tabernacle of witness saying: 
Numbers27:3 our father died in the wilderness, and was not among the company of them that gathered them selves together against the LORD in the congregation of Corah: But died in his own sin, and had no sons." 
Numbers27:4 Wherefore should the name of our father's be taken away from among his kindred, because he had no son? Give unto us a possession among the brethren of our father." 
Numbers27:5 And Moses brought their cause before the LORD. 
Numbers27:6 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying: 
Numbers27:7 The daughters of Zelaphead speak right: thou shalt give them a possession to inherit among their father's brethren, and shalt turn the inheritance of their father unto them." 
Numbers27:8 And speak unto the children of Israel saying: If a man die and have no son, ye shall turn his inheritance unto his daughter." 
Numbers27:9 If he have no daughter, ye shall give his inheritance unto his brethren." 
Numbers27:10 If he have no brethren, ye shall give his inheritance unto his father's brethren." 
Numbers27:11 If he have no father's brethren, ye shall give his inheritance unto him that is next to him of his kindred, and let him possess it. And this shall be unto the children of Israel an ordinance, and a law, as the LORD hath commanded Moses." 
Numbers27:12 And the LORD said unto Moses: get thee up into this mount Abarim, and behold, the land which I have given unto the children of Israel." 
Numbers27:13 And when thou hast seen it, thou shalt be gathered unto thy people also, as Aaron thy brother was gathered unto his people." 
Numbers27:14 For ye were disobedient unto my mouth in the desert of Zin in the strife of the congregation, that ye sanctified me not in the water before their eyes. That is the water of strife in Cades in the wilderness of Zin." 
Numbers27:15 And Moses spake unto the LORD saying: 
Numbers27:16 let the LORD God of the spirits of all flesh, set a man over the congregation," 
Numbers27:17 which may go in and out before them, and to lead them in and out, that the congregation of the LORD be not as a flock of sheep without a shepherd." 
Numbers27:18 And the LORD said unto Moses: take Josua the son of Nun in whom there is spirit, and put thine hands upon him," 
Numbers27:19 and set him before Eleazer the priest and before all the congregation and give him a charge in their sight. 
Numbers27:20 And put of thy praise upon him that all the company of the children of Israel may hear. 
Numbers27:21 And he shall stand before Eleazar the priest which shall ask counsel(councell) for him after the manner of the light(judgement of Urim) before the LORD: And at the mouth of Eleazer shall both he and all the children of Israel with him and all the congregation, go in and out." 
Numbers27:22 And Moses did as the LORD commanded him, and he took Josua and set him before Eleazer the priest and before all the congregation," 
Numbers27:23 and put his hands upon him and give him a charge, as the LORD commanded thorow the hand of Moses." 
Numbers28:1 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying: 
Numbers28:2 give the children of Israel a charge and say unto them, that they take heed to offer unto me the offering of my bread in the sacrifice of sweet savour, in his due season." 
Numbers28:3 And say unto them. This is the offering which ye shall offer unto the LORD: two lambs of a year old without spot day by day to be a burntoffering perpetually. 
Numbers28:4 One lamb thou shalt offer in the morning, and the other at even." 
Numbers28:5 And thereto the tenth part of an Epha of flour for a meatoffering mingled with beaten oil, the fourth part of an hin:" 
Numbers28:6 which is a daily offering ordained in the mount Sinai unto a sweet savour in the sacrifice of the LORD. 
Numbers28:7 And the drinkoffering of the same: the fourth part of an hin unto one lamb, and pour the drinkoffering in the holy place, to be good drink unto the LORD." 
Numbers28:8 And the other lamb thou shalt offer at even, with the meatoffering and the drinkoffering after the manner of the morning: a sacrifice of a sweet savour unto the LORD." 
Numbers28:9 And on the Sabbath day two lambs of a year old apiece and without spot, and two tenth deals of flour for a meatoffering mingled with oil, and the drinkoffering thereto." 
Numbers28:10 This is the burntoffering of every Sabbath, besides the daily burntoffering and his drinkoffering." 
Numbers28:11 And in the first day of your months, ye shall offer a burntoffering unto the LORD: two young bullocks, and a ram, and seven lambs of a year old without spot," 
Numbers28:12 and three tenth deals of flour for a meatoffering mingled with oil unto one bullock, and two tenth deals of flour for a meatoffering mingled with oil unto one ram." 
Numbers28:13 And ever more, a tenth deal of flour mingled with oil, for a meatoffering unto one lamb. That is a burntoffering of a sweet savour in the sacrifice of the LORD." 
Numbers28:14 And their drink offerings shall be half an hin of wine unto one bullock, and the third part of an hin of wine unto a ram and the fourth part of an hin unto a lamb. This is the burntoffering of every month thorowout all the months of the year:" 
Numbers28:15 and one he goat for a sinoffering unto the LORD, which shall be offered with the daily burntoffering and his drinkoffering." 
Numbers28:16 And the fourteenth day of the first month shall be passover unto the LORD. 
Numbers28:17 And the fifteenth day of the same month shall be a feast, in which seven days men must eat unleavened bread." 
Numbers28:18 The first day shall be an holy feast, so that ye shall do no manner of laborious work therein." 
Numbers28:19 And ye shall offer a burntoffering unto the LORD two bullocks, one ram, and seven lambs of a year old without spot," 
Numbers28:20 and their meatoffering of flour mingled with oil three tenth deals unto a bullock, and two tenth deals unto a ram," 
Numbers28:21 and ever more one tenth deal unto a lamb, thorowout the seven lambs:" 
Numbers28:22 and an he goat for a sinoffering to make an atonement for you. 
Numbers28:23 And ye shall offer these, beside the burntoffering in the morning that is always offered." 
Numbers28:24 And after this manner ye shall offer thorowout the seven days, the food of the sacrifice of sweet favour unto the LORD. And it shall be done beside the daily burntoffering and his drinkoffering." 
Numbers28:25 And the seventh day shall be an holy feast unto you, so that ye shall do no laborious work therein." 
Numbers28:26 And the day of your first fruits when ye bring a new meatoffering unto the LORD in your weeks, shall be an holy feast unto you:" 
Numbers28:27 so that ye shall do no labourious work therein. And ye shall offer a burntoffering of a sweet savour unto the LORD: two young bullocks, and a ram, and seven lambs of a year old apiece," 
Numbers28:28 with their meatofferings of flour mingled with oil three tenth deals unto a bullock two tenth deals to a ram," 
Numbers28:29 and ever more one tenth deal unto a lamb thorowout the seven lambs," 
Numbers28:30 and an he goat to make an atonement for you. 
Numbers28:31 And this ye shall do besides the daily burntoffering, and his meatoffering: and they shall be without spot, with their drinkofferings." 
Numbers29:1 Also(and) the first day of the seventh month shall be an holy feast unto you, and ye shall do no labourious work therein. It shall be a day of trumpet blowing unto you." 
Numbers29:2 And ye shall offer a burntoffering of a sweet savour unto the LORD: one young bullock and one ram and seven lambs of a year old apiece that are pure. 
Numbers29:3 And their meatofferings of flour mingled with oil: three tenth deals unto the bullock, and two unto the ram," 
Numbers29:4 and one tenth deal unto one lamb thorow the seven lambs. 
Numbers29:5 And an he goat for a sinoffering to make an atonement for you," 
Numbers29:6 beside the burntoffering of the month and his meatoffering and beside the daily burntoffering and his meatoffering, and the drinkofferings of the same: according unto the manner of them for a savour of sweetness in the sacrifice of the LORD." 
Numbers29:7 And the tenth day of that same seventh month shall be an holy feast unto you, and ye shall humble your souls and shall do no manner work therein." 
Numbers29:8 And ye shall offer a burntoffering unto the LORD of a sweet savour: one bullock, and a ram, and seven lambs of a year old apiece, without fault" 
Numbers29:9 and their meatofferings of flour mingled with oil: three tenth deals to a bullock, and two to a ram" 
Numbers29:10 and always a tenth deal unto a lamb, thorowout the seven lambs." 
Numbers29:11 And one he goat for a sinoffering, beside the sinoffering of atonement and the daily burntoffering, and the meat and drinkofferings that long to the same." 
Numbers29:12 And the fifteenth day of the seventh month shall be holy day and ye shall do no laborious work therein, and ye shall keep a feast unto the LORD of seven days long." 
Numbers29:13 And ye shall offer a burntoffering of a sweet savour unto the LORD: thirteen bullocks two rams and fourteen lambs which are yearlings and pure," 
Numbers29:14 with oil, three tenth deals unto every one of the thirteen bullocks: two tenth deals to either of the rams," 
Numbers29:15 and one tenth deal unto each of the fourteen lambs. 
Numbers29:16 And one he goat unto a sinoffering, beside the daily burntoffering with his meat and drinkofferings." 
Numbers29:17 And the second day twelve young bullocks, two rams, and fourteen yearling lambs without spot:" 
Numbers29:18 and their meatofferings and drinkofferings unto the bullocks, rams and lambs, according to the number of them and after the manner." 
Numbers29:19 And an he goat for a sinoffering, beside the daily burntoffering and his meat and drinkofferings." 
Numbers29:20 And the third day eleven bullocks two rams and fourteen yearling lambs without spot: 
Numbers29:21 and their meat and drinkofferings unto the bullocks, rams and lambs, after the number of them and according to the manner." 
Numbers29:22 And an he goat for a sinoffering, beside the daily burntoffering and his meat and drinkofferings." 
Numbers29:23 And the fourth day ten bullocks two rams and fourteen lambs, yearlings and pure:" 
Numbers29:24 and their meat and drinkofferings unto the bullocks, rams and lambs, according to their number and after the manner." 
Numbers29:25 And an he goat for a sinoffering, beside the daily burntoffering and his meat and drinkofferings." 
Numbers29:26 And the fifth day nine bullocks two rams and fourteen lambs of one year old apiece without spot. 
Numbers29:27 And their meat and drinkofferings unto the bullocks, rams and lambs, according to the number of them and after the manner." 
Numbers29:28 And an he goat for a sinoffering, beside the daily burntoffering and his meat and drinkofferings." 
Numbers29:29 And the sixth day eight bullocks two rams and fourteen yearling lambs without spot. 
Numbers29:30 And their meat and drinkofferings unto the bullocks, rams and lambs, according to the manner." 
Numbers29:31 And an he goat for a sinoffering, beside the daily burntoffering and his meat and drinkofferings." 
Numbers29:32 And the seventh day seven bullocks two rams and fourteen lambs that are yearlings and pure. 
Numbers29:33 And their meat and drinkofferings unto the bullocks, rams and lambs, according to their number and to the manner." 
Numbers29:34 And an hegoat for a sinoffering, beside the daily burntoffering and his meat and drinkofferings." 
Numbers29:35 And the eighth day shall be the conclusion of the feast unto you, and ye shall do no manner laborious work therein." 
Numbers29:36 And ye shall offer a burntoffering of a sweet savour unto the LORD: one bullock, one ram and seven yearling lambs without spot." 
Numbers29:37 And the meat and drinkofferings unto the bullock, ram and lambs, according to their numbers and according to the manner." 
Numbers29:38 And an he goat for a sinoffering beside the daily burntoffering and his meat and drinkofferings. 
Numbers29:39 These things ye shall do unto the LORD in your feasts: beside your vows and freewill offerings, in your burntofferings, meatofferings, drinkofferings and peaceofferings." 
Numbers29:40 And Moses told the children of Israel, according to all that the LORD commanded him." 
Numbers30:1 And Moses spake unto the heads of the tribes of the children of Israel saying: this is the thing which the LORD commandeth. 
Numbers30:2 If a man vow a vow unto the LORD or swear an oath and bind his soul, he shall not go back with his word: but shall fulfil all that proceedeth out of his mouth." 
Numbers30:3 If a damsel vow a vow unto the LORD and bind herself being in her father's house and unmarried: 
Numbers30:4 If her father hear her vow and bond which she hath made upon her soul, and hold his peace thereto: then all her vows and bonds which she hath made upon her soul shall stand in effect." 
Numbers30:5 But and if her father forbid her the same day that he heareth it, none of her vows nor bonds which she hath made upon her soul shall be of value, and the LORD shall forgive her, because her father forbade her." 
Numbers30:6 If she had an husband when she vowed or pronounced ought out of her lips wherewith she bound her soul," 
Numbers30:7 and her husband heard it and held his peace thereat the same day he heard it: Then her vows and her bonds wherewith she bound her soul, shall stand in effect." 
Numbers30:8 But and if her husband forbade her the same day that he heard it, then hath he made her vow which she had upon her of none effect, and that also which she pronounced with her lips wherewith she bound her soul, and the LORD shall forgive her." 
Numbers30:9 The vow of a widow and of her that is divorced, and all that they have bound their souls withal, shall stand in effect with them." 
Numbers30:10 If she vowed in her husband's house or bound her soul with an oath," 
Numbers30:11 and her husband heard it and held his peace and forbade her not: then all her vows and bonds wherewith she bound her soul, shall stand." 
Numbers30:12 But if her husband disannulled them the same day that he heard them, then nothing that proceeded out of her lips in vows and bonds wherewith she bound her soul shall stand in effect: for her husband hath lowsed them, and the LORD shall forgive her." 
Numbers30:13 All vows and oaths that bind to humble the soul, may her husband stablish or break." 
Numbers30:14 But if her husband hold his peace from one day unto another, then he stablisheth all her vows and bonds which she had upon her, because he held his peace the same day that he heard them." 
Numbers30:15 And if he afterward break them, he shall bear her sin himself." 
Numbers30:16 These are the ordinances which the LORD commanded Moses, between a man and his wife, and between the father and his daughter, being a damsel in her father's house." 
Numbers31:1 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying: 
Numbers31:2 avenge the children of Israel of the Madianites, and afterward be gathered unto thy people." 
Numbers31:3 And Moses spake unto the folk saying: Harness some of you unto war, and let them go upon the Madianites and avenge the LORD of the Madianites." 
Numbers31:4 Ye shall send unto the war a thousand of every tribe thorowout all the tribes of Israel. 
Numbers31:5 And there were taken out of the thousands of Israel twelve thousand prepared unto war, of every tribe a thousand." 
Numbers31:6 And Moses sent them a thousand of every tribe, with Phineas the son of Eleazer the priest to war, and the holy vessels and the trumpets to blow with in his hand." 
Numbers31:7 And they warred against the Madianites, as the LORD commanded Moses, and slew all the males." 
Numbers31:8 And they slew the kings of Madian among other that were slain: Eui, Rekem, Zur, Hur and Reba: five kings of Madian. And they slew Balam the son of Beor with the sword." 
Numbers31:9 And the children of Israel took all the women of Madian prisoners and their children, and spoiled all their cattle, their substance and their goods." 
Numbers31:10 And they burnt all their cities wherein they dwelt, and all their castles with fire." 
Numbers31:11 And they took all the spoil and all they could catch, both of men and beasts." 
Numbers31:12 And they brought the captives and that which they had taken and all the spoil unto Moses and Eleazer the priest and unto the company of the children of Israel, even unto the host, in the fields of Moab by Jordan nigh to Jericho." 
Numbers31:13 And Moses and Eleazer the priest and all the lords of the congregation went out of the host against them. 
Numbers31:14 And Moses was angry with the officers of the host, with the captains over thousands and over hundreds, which came from war and battle," 
Numbers31:15 and said unto them: Have ye saved the women alive? 
Numbers31:16 behold, these caused the children of Israel thorow Balam, to commit trespass against the LORD, by the reason of Peor, and there followed a plague among the congregation of the LORD." 
Numbers31:17 Now therefore slay all the men children and the women that have lien with men fleshly: 
Numbers31:18 But all the women children that have not lien with men, keep alive for your selves." 
Numbers31:19 And lodge without the host seven days all that have killed any person and all that have twiched any dead body, and purify both your selves and your prisoners the third day and the seventh." 
Numbers31:20 And sprinkle all your raiments and all that is made of skins, and all work of goats' hair, and all things made of wood." 
Numbers31:21 And Eleazer the priest said unto all the men of war which went out to battle: this is the ordinance of the law which the LORD commanded Moses: 
Numbers31:22 Gold, silver, brass, iron, tin and lead," 
Numbers31:23 and all that may abide the fire, ye shall make it go thorow the fire, and then it is clean. Neverthelater, it shall be sprinkled with sprinkling water. And all that suffereth not the fire, ye shall make go thorow the water." 
Numbers31:24 And wash your clothes the seventh day, and then ye are clean. And afterward come into the host." 
Numbers31:25 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying: 
Numbers31:26 take the sum of the prey that was taken, both of the women and of cattle, thou and Eleazer the priest and the ancient heads of the congregation." 
Numbers31:27 And divide it into two parts, between them that took the war upon them and went out to battle and all the congregation." 
Numbers31:28 And take a portion unto the LORD of the men of war which went out to battle one of five hundred, of the women and of the oxen and of the asses and of the sheep" 
Numbers31:29 and ye shall take it of their half and give it unto Eleazer the priest, an heave offering unto the LORD." 
Numbers31:30 And of the half of the children of Israel, take one of fifty, of the women, of the oxen, of the asses and of the sheep, and of all manner of beasts, and give them unto the Levites which wait upon the habitation of the LORD." 
Numbers31:31 And Moses and Eleazer the priest did as the LORD commanded Moses. 
Numbers31:32 And the booty and the prey which the men of war had caught, was six hundred thousand and seventy five thousand sheep:" 
Numbers31:33 and seventy two thousand oxen: 
Numbers31:34 and sixty one thousand asses: 
Numbers31:35 and thirty two thousand women that had lien by no man. 
Numbers31:36 And the half which was the part of them that went out to war, was three hundred thousand and thirty seven thousand and five hundred sheep:" 
Numbers31:37 And the LORD's part of the sheep was six hundred and seventy five. 
Numbers31:38 And the oxen were thirty six thousand, of which the LORD's part was seventy two." 
Numbers31:39 And the asses were thirty thousand and five hundred, of which the LORD's part was sixty one." 
Numbers31:40 And the women were sixteen thousand, of which the LORD's part was thirty two souls." 
Numbers31:41 And Moses gave that sum which was the LORD's heave offering unto Eleazer the priest: as the LORD commanded Moses. 
Numbers31:42 And the other half of the children of Israel which Moses separated from the men of war 
Numbers31:43 (that is to wete, the half that pertained unto the congregation) was three hundred thousand and thirty seven thousand and five hundred sheep:" 
Numbers31:44 and thirty six thousand oxen: 
Numbers31:45 and thirty thousand asses and five hundred: 
Numbers31:46 and sixteen thousand women. 
Numbers31:47 And Moses took of this half that pertained unto the children of Israel: one of every fifty, both of the women and of the cattle, and gave them unto the Levites which waited upon the habitation of the LORD, as the LORD commanded Moses." 
Numbers31:48 And the officers of thousands of the host, the captains over the thousands and the captains over the hundreds came forth and said unto Moses:" 
Numbers31:49 Thy servants have taken the sum of the men of war, which were under our hand, and there lacked not one man of them." 
Numbers31:50 We have therefore brought a present unto the LORD what every man found of Jewels of gold, chains, bracelets, rings, earrings and spangles, to make an atonement for our souls before the LORD." 
Numbers31:51 And Moses and Eleazer took the gold of them: Jewels of all manner fashions. 
Numbers31:52 And all the gold of the heave offering of the LORD, of the captains over thousands and hundreds was sixteen thousand seven hundred and fifty sicles," 
Numbers31:53 which(for) the men of war had spoiled, every man for himself." 
Numbers31:54 And Moses and Eleazer the priest took the gold of the captains over the thousands and over the hundreds, and brought it into the tabernacle of witness: to be a memorial unto the children of Israel, before the LORD." 
Numbers32:1 The children of Ruben and the children of Gad, had an exceeding great multitude of cattle. And when they saw the land of Jaeser and the land of Gilead that it was an apt place for cattle," 
Numbers32:2 they came and spake unto Moses and Eleazer the priest and unto the lords of the congregation saying: 
Numbers32:3 The land of Ataroth, Dibo and Beon," 
Numbers32:4 which country the LORD smote before the congregation of Israel: is a land for cattle and we thy servants have cattle 
Numbers32:5 wherefore (said they) if we have found grace in thy sight, let this land be given unto thy servants to possess, and bring us not over Jordan." 
Numbers32:6 And Moses said unto the children of Gad and of Ruben: shall your brethren go to war and ye tarry here? 
Numbers32:7 Wherefore discourage ye the hearts of the children of Israel for to go over into the land which the LORD hath given them? 
Numbers32:8 This did your fathers, when I sent them from Cades bernea to see the land." 
Numbers32:9 And they went up even unto the river of Escol and saw the land, and discouraged the hearts of the children of Israel, that they should not go into the land which the LORD had given them." 
Numbers32:10 And the LORD was wroth the same time and sware saying: 
Numbers32:11 None of the men that came out of Egypt from twenty year old and above, shall see the land which I swore unto Abraham, Isaac and Jacob, because they have not continually followed me:" 
Numbers32:12 save Caleb the son of Jephune the Kenesite, and Josua the son of Nun, for they have followed me continually." 
Numbers32:13 And the LORD was angry with Israel, and made them wander in the wilderness forty year, until all the generation that had done evil in the sight of the LORD were consumed." 
Numbers32:14 And behold, ye are risen up in your father's stead, the increase of sinful men, to augment the fierce wrath of the LORD to Israelward." 
Numbers32:15 For if ye turn away from after him, he will yet again leave the people in the wilderness, so shall ye destroy all this folk." 
Numbers32:16 And they went near him and said: we will build sheepfolds here for our sheep and for our cattle, and cities for our children:" 
Numbers32:17 But we our selves will go ready armed before the children of Israel, until we have brought them unto their place. And our children shall dwell in the strong(fenced) cities, because of the inhabiters of the land." 
Numbers32:18 And we will not return unto our houses, until the children of Israel have inherited: every man his inheritance." 
Numbers32:19 For we will not inherit with them on yonder side Jordan forward, because our inheritance is fallen to us on this side Jordan eastward." 
Numbers32:20 And Moses said unto them: If ye will do this thing, that ye will go all harnessed before the LORD to war," 
Numbers32:21 and will go all of you in harness over Jordan before the LORD, until he have cast out his enemies before him," 
Numbers32:22 and until the land be subdued before the LORD, then ye shall return and be without sin against the LORD and against Israel, and this land shall be your possession before the LORD." 
Numbers32:23 But and if ye will not do so, behold, ye sin against the LORD: and be sure your sin will find you out." 
Numbers32:24 Build your cities for your children and folds for your sheep, and see ye do that ye have spoken." 
Numbers32:25 And the Children of Gad and of Ruben spake unto Moses saying: thy servants will do as my lord commandeth. 
Numbers32:26 Our children, our wives, substance and all our cattle shall remain here in the cities of Gilead." 
Numbers32:27 But we thy servants will go all harnessed for the war unto battle before the LORD, as my lord hath said." 
Numbers32:28 And Moses commanded Eleazer the priest: and Josua the son of Nun and the ancient heads of the tribes of the children of Israel," 
Numbers32:29 and said unto them: If the children of Gad and Ruben will go with you over Jordan, all prepared to fight before the LORD: then when the land is subdued unto you, give them the land of Gilead to possess," 
Numbers32:30 but and if they will not go over with you in harness, then they shall have their possessions among you in the land of Canaan." 
Numbers32:31 And the children of Gad and Ruben answered saying: that which the LORD hath said unto thy servants we will do. 
Numbers32:32 We will go harnessed before the LORD into the land of Canaan, and the possession of our inheritance shall be on this side the Jordan." 
Numbers32:33 And Moses gave unto the children of Gad and of Ruben and unto half the tribe of Manasse the son of Joseph, the kingdom of Sehon king of the Amorites, and the kingdom of Og king of Basan, the land that longed unto the cities thereof in the coasts of the country round about." 
Numbers32:34 And the children of Gad built Dibo, ataroth Aroer," 
Numbers32:35 Atroth, Sophan, Jaeser, Jegabea," 
Numbers32:36 Bethnimra and Betharan strong(fenced) cities, and they built folds for their sheep." 
Numbers32:37 And the children of Ruben built Hesebon, Elalea, Kiriathaim," 
Numbers32:38 Nebo, Baal Meon and turned their names, and Sibama also: and gave names unto the cities which they built." 
Numbers32:39 And the children of Machir the son of Manasse went to Gilead and took it, and put out the Amorites that were therein." 
Numbers32:40 And Moses gave Gilead unto Machir the son of Manasse and he dwelt therein. 
Numbers32:41 And Jahir the son of Manasse went and took the small towns thereof, and called them the towns of Jair." 
Numbers32:42 And Nobah went and took kenath with the towns longing thereto, and called it Nobah after his own name." 
Numbers33:1 These are the journeys of the children of Israel which went out of the land of Egypt with their armies under Moses and Aaron. 
Numbers33:2 And Moses wrote their going out by their journeys at the commandment of the LORD: even these are the journeys of their going out. 
Numbers33:3 The children of Israel departed from Rameses the fifteenth day of the first month, on the morrow after Passover and went out with an high hand in the sight of all Egypt," 
Numbers33:4 while the Egyptians buried all their firstborn which the LORD had smoten among them. And upon their gods also the LORD did execution. 
Numbers33:5 And the children of Israel removed from Rameses and pitched in Sucoth. 
Numbers33:6 And they departed from Sucoth and pitched their tents in Ethan, which is in the edge of the wilderness." 
Numbers33:7 And they removed from Ethan and turned unto the entering of Hiroth which is before Baal Zephon, and pitched before Migdol." 
Numbers33:8 And they departed from before Hiroth and went thorow the midst of the sea into the wilderness, and went three days journey in the wilderness of Ethan, and pitched in Marah." 
Numbers33:9 And they removed from Marah and went unto Elim where were twelve fountains and seventy date(palm) trees and they pitched there. 
Numbers33:10 And they removed from Elim and lay fast by the red sea. 
Numbers33:11 And they removed from the red sea and lay in the wilderness of Zin. 
Numbers33:12 And they took their journey out of the wilderness of Zin, and set up their tents in Daphka." 
Numbers33:13 And they departed from Daphka, and lay in Alus." 
Numbers33:14 And they removed from Alus, and lay at Raphedim, where was no water for the people to drink." 
Numbers33:15 And they departed from Raphedim, and pitched in the wilderness of Sinai." 
Numbers33:16 And they removed from the desert of Sinai, and lodged at the graves of lust." 
Numbers33:17 And they departed from the sepulchres of lust, and lay at Hazeroth." 
Numbers33:18 And they departed from Hazeroth, and pitched in Rithma." 
Numbers33:19 And departed from Rithma and pitched at Rimon Parez. 
Numbers33:20 And they departed from Rimon Parez, and pitched in Libna." 
Numbers33:21 And they removed from Libna, and pitched at Rissa." 
Numbers33:22 And they journeyed from Rissa and pitched at Kehelatha. 
Numbers33:23 And they went from Kehelatha, and pitched in mount Sapher." 
Numbers33:24 And they removed from mount Sapher, and lay in Harada." 
Numbers33:25 And they removed from Harada, and pitched in Makeheloth." 
Numbers33:26 And they removed from Makeheloth, and lay at Tahath," 
Numbers33:27 and they departed from Tahath and pitched at Tharath. 
Numbers33:28 And they removed from Tharath, and pitched in Mithca." 
Numbers33:29 And they went from Mithca, and lodged in Hasmona." 
Numbers33:30 And they departed from Hasmona, and lay at Moseroth." 
Numbers33:31 And they departed from Moseroth, and pitched among the children of Jaken." 
Numbers33:32 And they removed from the children of Bane Jaken, and lay at Horgadgad." 
Numbers33:33 And they went from Horgadgad, and pitched in Jathbatha." 
Numbers33:34 And they removed from Jathbatha, and lay at Abrona." 
Numbers33:35 And they departed from Abrona, and lay at Ezeon gaber." 
Numbers33:36 And they removed from Ezeon gaber, and pitched in the wilderness of Sin, which is Cades." 
Numbers33:37 And they removed from Cades, and pitched in mount Hor, in the edge of the land of Moab.(Edom)" 
Numbers33:38 And Aaron the priest went up into mount Hor at the commandment of the LORD and died there, even in the fortieth year, after the children of Israel were come out of the land of Egypt, and in the first day of the fifth month." 
Numbers33:39 And Aaron was an hundred and thirty three year old when he died in mount Hor. 
Numbers33:40 And king Erad the Cananite which dwelt in the south of the land of Canaan, heard that the children of Israel were come." 
Numbers33:41 And they departed from mount Hor, and pitched in Zalmona." 
Numbers33:42 And they departed from Zalmona, and pitched in Phimon," 
Numbers33:43 and they departed from Phimon, and pitched in Oboth." 
Numbers33:44 And they departed from Oboth, and pitched in Igim Abarim(Jehabarim) in the borders of Moab." 
Numbers33:45 And they departed from Igim,(Jehabarim) and pitched in Dibon Gad." 
Numbers33:46 And they removed from Dibon Gad, and lay in Almon Diblathama." 
Numbers33:47 And they removed from Almon Diblathama, and pitched in the mountains of Abarim before Nibo." 
Numbers33:48 And they departed from the mountains of Abarim, and pitched in the fields of Moab fast by Jordan nigh to Jericho." 
Numbers33:49 And they pitched upon Jordan, from Beth Haiesmoth unto the plain of Sitim(Abelsatim) in the fields of Moab." 
Numbers33:50 And the LORD spake unto Moses in the fields of Moab by Jordan nigh unto Jericho, saying:" 
Numbers33:51 speak unto the children of Israel and say unto them: when ye are come over Jordan into the land of Canaan," 
Numbers33:52 see that ye drive out all the inhabiters of the land before you, and destroy their imaginations(chapels) and all their Images of Metal, and pluck down all their altars built on hills:" 
Numbers33:53 And possess the land and dwell therein, for I have given you the land to enjoy it." 
Numbers33:54 And ye shall divide the inheritance of the land by lot among your kindreds, and give to the more the more inheritance, and to the fewer the less inheritance. And your inheritance shall be in the tribes of your fathers, in the place where every man's lot falleth." 
Numbers33:55 But and if ye will not drive out the inhabiters of the land before you, then these(those) which ye let remain of them, shall be thorns in your eyes and darts in your sides, and shall vex you in the land wherein ye dwell." 
Numbers33:56 Moreover it will come to pass, that I shall do unto you as I thought to do unto them." 
Numbers34:1 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying: 
Numbers34:2 command the children of Israel and say unto them: when ye come into the land of Canaan, this is the land that shall fall unto your inheritance, the land of Canaan with all her coasts." 
Numbers34:3 And your south quarter shall be from the wilderness of Zin along by the coast of Edom, so that your south quarter shall be from the side of the salt sea eastward," 
Numbers34:4 and shall fetch a compass from the south up to Acrabim, and reach to Zinna. And it shall go out on the south side of Cades Bernea, and go out also at Hazar Adar, and go along to Azmon." 
Numbers34:5 And shall fetch a compass from Azmon unto the river of Egypt, and shall go out at the sea." 
Numbers34:6 And your west quarter shall be the great sea, which coast shall be your west coast." 
Numbers34:7 And this shall be your north quarter: ye shall compass from the great sea unto mount Hor. 
Numbers34:8 And from mount Hor, ye shall compass and go unto Hemath, and the end of the coast shall be at Zedada," 
Numbers34:9 and the coast shall reach out to Ziphron and go out at Hazor enan. And this shall be your north quarter. 
Numbers34:10 And ye shall compass your east quarter from Hazar Enan to Sepham. 
Numbers34:11 And the coast shall go down from Sepham to Ribla on the east side of Ain. And then descend and go out at the side of the sea of Cenereth eastward. 
Numbers34:12 And then go down along by Jordan, and leave at the salt sea. And this shall be your land with all the coasts thereof round about." 
Numbers34:13 And Moses commanded the children of Israel, saying: this is the land which ye shall inherit by lot, and which the LORD commanded to give unto nine tribes and an half:" 
Numbers34:14 for the tribe of the children of Ruben, have received, in the households of their fathers, and the tribe of the children of Gad in their fathers' households, and half the tribe of Manasse, have received their inheritance," 
Numbers34:15 that is to wete two tribes and a half have received their inheritance on the other side of Jordan by Jericho eastward, toward the son rising." 
Numbers34:16 And the LORD spake to Moses saying: 
Numbers34:17 These are the names of the men, which shall divide you the land to inherit. Eleazer the priest, and Josua the son of Nun." 
Numbers34:18 And ye shall take also a lord of every tribe to divide the land," 
Numbers34:19 whose names are these: in the tribe of Juda, Caleb the son of Jephune." 
Numbers34:20 And in the tribe of the children of Simeon, Semuel the son of Amiud," 
Numbers34:21 and in the tribe of BenJamin, Elidad the son of Cislon." 
Numbers34:22 And in the tribe of the children of Dan, the lord Bucki the son of Jagli." 
Numbers34:23 And among the children of Joseph: in the tribe of the children of Manasse, the lord Haniel the son of Ephod." 
Numbers34:24 And in the tribe of the children of Ephraim, the lord Cemuel the son of Siphtan." 
Numbers34:25 And in the tribe of the sons of Zabulon, the lord Elizaphan the son of Parnac." 
Numbers34:26 And in the tribe of the children of Isachar, the lord Palthiel the son of Asan." 
Numbers34:27 And in the tribe of the sons of Aser, the lord Ahihud the son of Selomi." 
Numbers34:28 And in the tribe of the children of Naphthali, the lord Pada El the son of Ammihud." 
Numbers34:29 These are they which the LORD commanded to divide the inheritance unto the children of Israel, in the land of Canaan." 
Numbers35:1 And the LORD spake unto Moses in the fields of Moab by Jordan Jericho saying: 
Numbers35:2 command the children of Israel, that they give unto the Levites of the inheritance of their possession: cities to dwell in. And ye shall give also unto the cities of the Levites, suburbs round about them." 
Numbers35:3 The cities shall be for them to dwell in, and the suburbs for their cattle, possession and all manner beasts of theirs." 
Numbers35:4 And the suburbs of the cities which ye shall give unto the Levites, shall reach from the wall of the city outward, a thousand cubits round about." 
Numbers35:5 And ye shall measure without the city, and make the utmost border of the east side: two thousand cubits. And the utmost border of the south side: two thousand cubits. And the utmost border of the west side: two thousand cubits. And the utmost border of the north side: two thousand cubits also: and the city shall be in the midst. And these shall be the suburbs of their cities." 
Numbers35:6 And among the cities which ye shall give unto the Levites, there shall be six cities of franchise which ye shall give to that intent that he which killeth, may fly thither. And to them ye shall add forty two cities more:(mo)" 
Numbers35:7 so that all the cities which ye shall give the Levites shall be forty eight with their suburbs. 
Numbers35:8 And of the cities which ye shall give out of the possessions of the children of Israel, ye shall give many out of their possessions that have much and few out of their possessions that have little: so that every tribe shall give of his cities unto the Levites, according to the inheritance which he inheriteth." 
Numbers35:9 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying: 
Numbers35:10 speak unto the children of Israel and say unto them: when ye be come over Jordan into the land of Canaan," 
Numbers35:11 ye shall build cities which shall be privileged towns for you: that he which slayeth a man unawares, may fly thither." 
Numbers35:12 And the cities shall be to flee from the executer of blood, that he which killed die not, until he stand before the congregation in judgement." 
Numbers35:13 And of these six free cities 
Numbers35:14 which ye shall give three ye shall give on this side Jordan and three in the land of Canaan. 
Numbers35:15 And these six free cities shall be for the children of Israel and for the stranger and for him that dwelleth among you, that all they which kill any person unawares, may flee thither." 
Numbers35:16 If any man smite another with a weapon of iron that he die, then he is a murderer, and shall die for it." 
Numbers35:17 If he smite him with a throwing stone that he die therewith, then he shall die: For he is a murderer and shall be slain therefore." 
Numbers35:18 If he smite him with a hand weapon of wood that he die therewith, then he shall die: for he is a murderer and shall be slain therefore." 
Numbers35:19 The judge(justice) of blood shall slay the murderer, as soon as he findeth him:" 
Numbers35:20 If he thrust him of hate, or hurl at him with laying of wait that he die" 
Numbers35:21 or smite him with his hand of envy that he die, he that smote him shall die, for he is a murderer. The justice of blood shall slay him as soon as he findeth him." 
Numbers35:22 But and if he pushed him by chance and not of hate or cast at him with any manner of thing and not of laying of wait: 
Numbers35:23 or cast any manner of stone at him that he die therewith, and saw him not: And he cast it upon him and he died, but was not his enemy, neither sought him any harm:" 
Numbers35:24 Then the congregation shall judge between the slayer and the executer of blood in such cases. 
Numbers35:25 And the congregation shall deliver the slayer out of the hand of the judge of blood, and shall restore him again unto the franchised city, whither he was fled. And he shall bide there unto the death of the high priest which was anointed with holy oil." 
Numbers35:26 But and if he came without the borders of his privileged city whither he was fled," 
Numbers35:27 if the blood avenger find him without the borders of his free town, he shall slay the murderer and be guiltless," 
Numbers35:28 because he should have bidden in his free town until the death of the high priest, and after the death of the high priest, he shall return again unto the land of his possession." 
Numbers35:29 And this shall be an ordinance and a law unto you, among your children after you in all your habitations." 
Numbers35:30 Whosoever slayeth, shall be slain at the mouth of witnesses. For one witness shall not answer against one person to put him to death." 
Numbers35:31 Moreover ye shall take none amends for the life of the murderer which is worthy to die: But he shall be put to death. 
Numbers35:32 Also ye shall take none atonement for him that is fled to a free city, that he should come again and dwell in the land before the death of the high priest." 
Numbers35:33 And see that ye pollute not the land which ye are in, for blood defileth the land. And the land can none otherwise be cleansed of the blood that is shed therein, but by the blood of it that shed it." 
Numbers35:34 Defile not therefore the land which ye inhabit, and in the midst of which I also dwell, for I am, the LORD which dwell among the children of Israel." 
Numbers36:1 And the ancient heads of the children of Gilead the son of Machir the son of Manasse of the kindred of the children of Joseph, came forth and spake before Moses and the princes which were ancient heads among the children of Israel" 
Numbers36:2 and said: The LORD commanded my lord to give the land to inherit by lot to the children of Israel. And then my lord commanded in the name of the LORD to give the inheritance of Zelaphead our brother unto his daughters. 
Numbers36:3 Now when any of the sons of the tribes of Israel take them to wives, then shall their inheritance be taken from the inheritance of our fathers, and shall be put unto the inheritance of the tribe in which they are and shall be taken from the lot of our inheritance." 
Numbers36:4 And when the free year(of jubilee) cometh unto the children of Israel, then shall their inheritance be put unto the inheritance of the tribe where they are in, and so shall their inheritance be taken away from the inheritance of the tribe of our fathers." 
Numbers36:5 And Moses commanded the children of Israel at the mouth of the LORD saying: the tribe of the children of Joseph have said well. 
Numbers36:6 This therefore doeth the LORD: command the daughters of Zelaphead saying: let them be wives to whom they themself think best, but in the kindred of the tribe of their fathers shall they marry," 
Numbers36:7 that the inheritance of the children of Israel roll not from tribe to tribe. But that the children of Israel may abide, every man in the inheritance of the tribe of his fathers." 
Numbers36:8 And every daughter that possesseth any inheritance among the tribes of the children of Israel, shall be wife unto one of the kindred of the tribe of her father, that the children of Israel may enjoy every man the inheritance of his father," 
Numbers36:9 and that the inheritance go not from one tribe to another: but that the tribes of the children of Israel, may abide every man in his own inheritance." 
Numbers36:10 And as the LORD commanded Moses even so did the daughters of Zelaphead: 
Numbers36:11 Mahela, Thirza, Hagla, Milcha and Noa, and were married unto their fathers' brothers' sons," 
Numbers36:12 of the kindred of the children of Manasse the son of Joseph: and so they had their inheritance in the tribe of the kindred of their father. 
Numbers36:13 These are the commandments and laws which the LORD commanded thorow Moses, unto the children of Israel in the fields of Moab upon Jordan nigh unto Jericho. [The end of the .iiij. book of Moses.]" 
Deuteronomy1:1 These be the words which Moses spake unto all Israel, on the other side Jordan in the wilderness and in the fields by the red sea, between Pharan and Tophel, Laban, Hazeroth and Disahab" 
Deuteronomy1:2 twelve days journey from Horeb unto Cades barne, by the way that leadeth unto mount Seir." 
Deuteronomy1:3 And it fortuned the first day of the eleventh month in the fortieth year, that Moses spake unto the children of Israel according unto all that the LORD had given him in commandment unto them," 
Deuteronomy1:4 after that he had smote Sehon the king of the Amorites which dwelt in Hesbon, and Og king of Basan which dwelt at Astaroth in Edrei." 
Deuteronomy1:5 On the other side Jordan in the land of Moab, Moses began to declare this law saying:" 
Deuteronomy1:6 the LORD our God spake unto us in Horeb saying: Ye have dwelt long enough in this mount: 
Deuteronomy1:7 depart therefore and take your journey and go unto the hills of the Amorites and unto all places nigh there unto: both fields, hills and dales: and unto the south and unto the sea's side in the land of Canaan, and unto Libanon: even unto the great river Euphrates." 
Deuteronomy1:8 Behold, I have set the land before you: go in therefore and possess the land which the LORD sware unto your fathers Abraham, Isaac and Jacob, to give unto them and their seed after them." 
Deuteronomy1:9 And I said unto you the same season: I am not able to bear you myself alone. 
Deuteronomy1:10 For the LORD your God hath multiplied you: so that ye are this day as the stars of heaven in number 
Deuteronomy1:11 (the LORD God of your fathers make you a thousand times so many more as ye are, and bless you as he hath promised you)" 
Deuteronomy1:12 how (said I) can I myself alone, bear the cumbrance, charge and strife that is among you:" 
Deuteronomy1:13 bring therefore men of wisdom and of understanding and expert, known among your tribes, that I may make them rulers over you." 
Deuteronomy1:14 And ye answered me and said: that which thou hast spoken is good to be done. 
Deuteronomy1:15 And then I took the heads of your tribes, men of wisdom and that were expert, and made them rulers over you: captains over thousands and over hundreds, over fifty and over ten, and officers among your tribes." 
Deuteronomy1:16 And I charged your judges the same time saying: hear your brethren and judge righteously between every man and his brother and the stranger that is with him. 
Deuteronomy1:17 See that ye know no man in judgement: but hear the small as well as the great and be afraid of no man, for the law is God's.(judgment is Gods) And the cause that is too hard for you, bring unto me and I will hear it." 
Deuteronomy1:18 And I commanded you the same season, all the things which ye should do." 
Deuteronomy1:19 And then we departed from Horeb and walked thorow all that great and terrible wilderness as ye have seen along by the way that leadeth unto the hills of the Amorites, as the LORD our God commanded us, and came to Cades barne." 
Deuteronomy1:20 And there I said unto you: Ye are come unto the hills of the Amorites, which the LORD our God doth give unto us." 
Deuteronomy1:21 Behold the LORD thy God hath set the land before thee, go up and conquer it, as the LORD God of thy fathers sayeth unto thee: fear not, neither be discouraged." 
Deuteronomy1:22 And then ye came unto me every one and said: Let us send men before us, to search us out the land and to bring us word again, both what way we shall go up by, and unto what cities we shall come." 
Deuteronomy1:23 And the saying pleased me well, and I took twelve men of you, of every tribe one." 
Deuteronomy1:24 And they departed and went up into the high country and came unto the river Escol, and searched it out," 
Deuteronomy1:25 and took of the fruit of the land in their hands and brought it down unto us and brought us word again and said: it is a good land which the LORD our God doeth give us. 
Deuteronomy1:26 Notwithstanding ye would not consent to go up, but were disobedient unto the mouth of the LORD your God," 
Deuteronomy1:27 and murmured in your tents and said: because the LORD hateth us, therefore he hath brought us out of the land of Egypt, to deliver us into the hands of the Amorites and to destroy us." 
Deuteronomy1:28 How shall we go up? Our brethren have discouraged our hearts saying: the people is greater and taller than we, and the cities are great and walled even up to heaven, and moreover we have seen the sons of the Enakims there." 
Deuteronomy1:29 And I said unto you: dread not nor be afraid of them: 
Deuteronomy1:30 The LORD your God which goeth before you, he shall fight for you, according to all that he did unto you in Egypt before your eyes" 
Deuteronomy1:31 and in the wilderness: as thou hast seen how that the LORD thy God bare thee as a man should bear his son, thorowout all the way which ye have gone, until ye came unto this place." 
Deuteronomy1:32 And yet for all this saying ye did not believe the LORD your God 
Deuteronomy1:33 which goeth the way before you, to search you out a place to pitch your tents in, in fire by night, that ye might see what way to go and in a cloud by day." 
Deuteronomy1:34 And the LORD heard the voice of your words and was wroth and swore saying," 
Deuteronomy1:35 there shall not one of these men of this froward generation see that good land which I sware to give unto your fathers," 
Deuteronomy1:36 save Caleb the son of Jephune, he shall see it, and to him I will give the land which he hath walked in and to his children, because he hath continually followed the LORD." 
Deuteronomy1:37 Likewise the LORD was angry with me for your sakes saying: thou also shalt not go in thither. 
Deuteronomy1:38 But Josua the son of Nun which standeth before thee, he shall go in thither. Bold him therefore for he shall divide it unto Israel." 
Deuteronomy1:39 Moreover your children which ye said should be a prey, and your sons which know neither good nor bad this day, they shall go in thither and unto them I will give it, and they shall enjoy it." 
Deuteronomy1:40 But as for you, turn back and take your journey into the wilderness: even the way to the red sea." 
Deuteronomy1:41 Then ye answered and said unto me: We have sinned against the LORD: we will go up and fight, according to all that the LORD our God commanded us. And when ye had girded on every man his weapons of war and were ready to go up into the hills," 
Deuteronomy1:42 the LORD said unto me: say unto them, see that ye go not up and that ye fight not, for I am not among you: lest ye be plagued before your enemies." 
Deuteronomy1:43 And when I told you ye would not hear: but disobeyed the mouth of the LORD, and went presumptuously up into the hills." 
Deuteronomy1:44 Then the Amorites which dwelt in those hills, came out against you and chased you as bees do, and hewed you in Seir, even unto Horma." 
Deuteronomy1:45 And ye came again and wept before the LORD: but the LORD would not hear your voice nor give you audience. 
Deuteronomy1:46 And so ye abode in Cades a long season, according unto the time that ye there dwelt." 
Deuteronomy2:1 Then we turned and took our journey into the wilderness, even the way to the red sea as the LORD commanded me. And we compassed the mountains of Seir a long time." 
Deuteronomy2:2 Then the LORD spake unto me saying: 
Deuteronomy2:3 Ye have compassed these mountains long enough, turn you northward." 
Deuteronomy2:4 And warn the people saying: Ye shall go thorow the coasts of your brethren the children of Esau which dwell in Seir, and they shall be afraid of you: But take good heed unto your selves" 
Deuteronomy2:5 that ye provoke them not, for I will not give you of their land, no not so much as a foot breadth: because I have given mount Seir unto Esau to possess." 
Deuteronomy2:6 Ye shall buy meat of them for money to eat, and ye shall buy water of them for money to drink." 
Deuteronomy2:7 For the LORD thy God hath blessed thee in all the works of thine hand, and knew thee as thou wentest thorow this great wilderness. Moreover the LORD thy God hath been with thee this forty years, so that thou hast lacked nothing." 
Deuteronomy2:8 And when we were departed from our brethren the children of Esau which dwelt in Seir by the field way from Elath and Ezion Gaber, we turned and went the way to the wilderness of Moab." 
Deuteronomy2:9 Then the LORD said unto me: see that thou vex not the Moabites, neither provoke them to battle for I will not give thee of their land to possess: because I have given Ar unto the children of loth to possess." 
Deuteronomy2:10 The Emims dwelt therein in times past, a people great, many and tall, as the Enakims:" 
Deuteronomy2:11 which also were taken for giants as the Enakims: And the Moabites called them Emims. 
Deuteronomy2:12 In like manner the Horims dwelt in Seir before time which the children of Esau cast out, and destroyed them before them and dwelt there in their stead: as Israel did in the land of his possession which the LORD gave them." 
Deuteronomy2:13 Now rise up (said I) and get you over the river Zared: and we went over the river Zared. 
Deuteronomy2:14 The space in which we came from Cades barne until we were come over the river Zared was thirty eight years: until all the generation of the men of war were wasted out of the host as the LORD sware unto them. 
Deuteronomy2:15 For indeed the hand of the LORD was against them, to destroy them out of the host, till they were consumed." 
Deuteronomy2:16 And as soon as all the men of war were consumed and dead from among the people," 
Deuteronomy2:17 then the LORD spake unto me saying: 
Deuteronomy2:18 Thou shalt go thorow Ar the coast of Moab this day," 
Deuteronomy2:19 and shalt come nigh unto the children of Ammon: see that thou vex them not, nor yet provoke them. For I will not give thee of the land of the children of Ammon to possess, because I have given it unto the children of Loth to possess." 
Deuteronomy2:20 That also was taken for a land of giants and giants dwelt therein in old time, and the Ammonites called them Zamzumims." 
Deuteronomy2:21 A people that was great, many and tall, as the Enakims. But the LORD destroyed them before the Ammonites, and they cast them out and they dwelt there in their stead:" 
Deuteronomy2:22 as he did for the children of Esau which dwell in Seir: even as he destroyed the Horims before them, and they cast them out and dwell in their stead unto this day." 
Deuteronomy2:23 And the Avims which dwelt in Hazarim even unto Aza, the Caphthorims which came out of Caphthor destroyed them and dwelt in their rooms." 
Deuteronomy2:24 Rise up, take your journey and go over the river Arnon. Behold, I have given into thy hand Sehon the Amorite king of Hesbon, and his land. Go to and conquer and provoke him to battle." 
Deuteronomy2:25 This day I will begin to send the fear and dread of thee upon all nations that are under all ports of heaven: so that when they hear speak of thee, they shall tremble and quake for fear of thee." 
Deuteronomy2:26 Then I sent messengers out of the wilderness of Kedemoth unto Sihon king of Hesbon, with words of peace saying:" 
Deuteronomy2:27 Let me go thorow thy land. I will go always along by the high way and will neither turn unto the right hand nor to the left. 
Deuteronomy2:28 Sell me meat for money for to eat, and give me drink for money for to drink: I will go thorow by foot only" 
Deuteronomy2:29 (as the children of Esau did unto me which dwell in Seir and the Moabites which dwell in Ar) until I be come over Jordan, into the land which the LORD our God giveth us." 
Deuteronomy2:30 But Sihon the king of Hesbon would not let us pass by him, for the LORD thy God had hardened his spirit and made his heart tough because he would deliver him into thy hands as it is come to pass this day." 
Deuteronomy2:31 And the LORD said unto me: behold, I have begun to set Sihon and his land before thee: go to and conquer, that thou mayst possess his land." 
Deuteronomy2:32 Then both Sihon and all his people came out against us unto battle at Jahab. 
Deuteronomy2:33 And the LORD set him before us, and we smote him and his sons and all his people." 
Deuteronomy2:34 And we took all his cities the same season, and destroyed all the cities with men, women, and children and let nothing remain," 
Deuteronomy2:35 save the cattle only we caught unto our selves and the spoil of the cities which we took," 
Deuteronomy2:36 from Aroer upon the brink of the river of Arnon, and the city in the river, unto Gilead: there was not one city too strong for us. The LORD our God delivered all unto us:" 
Deuteronomy2:37 only unto the land of the children of Ammon ye came not, nor(ner) unto all the coast of the river Jabock nor(ner) unto the cities in the mountains, nor unto whatsoever the LORD our God forbade us." 
Deuteronomy3:1 Then we turned and went up the way to Basan. And Og the king of Basan came out against us: both he and all his people to battle at Edrei. 
Deuteronomy3:2 And the LORD said unto me: fear him not, for I have delivered him and all his people and his land into thy hand and thou shalt deal with him as thou dealest with Sihon king of the Amorites which dwelt at Hesbon." 
Deuteronomy3:3 And so the LORD our God delivered into our hands, Og also the king of Basan and all his folk. And we smote him until nought was left him." 
Deuteronomy3:4 And we took all his cities the same season (for there was not a city which we took not from them) even three score cities, all the region of Argob, the kingdom of Og in Basan." 
Deuteronomy3:5 All these cities were made strong with high walls, gates and bars, beside unwalled towns a great many." 
Deuteronomy3:6 And we utterly destroyed them, as we played with Sihon king of Hesbon: bringing to nought all the cities with men, women and children." 
Deuteronomy3:7 But all the cattle and the spoil of the cities, we caught for ourselves." 
Deuteronomy3:8 And thus we took the same season, the land out of the hand of two kings of the Amorites on the other side Jordan, from the river of Arnon unto mount Hermon" 
Deuteronomy3:9 (which Hermon the Sidons call Sirion, but the Amorites call it Senir)"
