2Kings25:15 And the firepans, and basins that were either of gold or silver, them took the chief Marshal away" 
2Kings25:16 with the two pillars and the sea of brass and the bottoms which Salomon had made in the house of the LORD. The brass of all these vessels was without weight. 
2Kings25:17 The height of the one pillar was eighteen cubits, and the head thereon was brass and three cubits long with a wreath and pomegranates round about upon the head, all of brass. And of the same fashion was the second pillar with a wreath." 
2Kings25:18 And the chief Marshal took Saraiah the chief priest, and Zophoniah the highest priest save one, and three keepers of the door." 
2Kings25:19 And out of the city he took a certain chamberlain that had the oversight of the men of war, and five men that were ever in the king's presence, which were found in the city, and Sopher the captain of the Host that taught the people of the land to make war, and three score men of the people of the land that were found in the city also." 
2Kings25:20 And Nabusaradan the chief Marshal took them and brought them to the king of Babylon to Reblah. 
2Kings25:21 And the king of Babylon smote them and slew them at Reblah in the land of Hemath. And so Juda was carried away out of all their land. 
2Kings25:22 And over the people that remained in the land of Juda, which Nabuchodonozor king of Babylon left, he set Godoliah the son of Ahikam the son of Saphan." 
2Kings25:23 And when all the captains of the men of war and the men heard that the king of Babylon had made Godoliah governor: there came to Godoliah to Mazphah: Ismael the son of Nathaniah and Johanan the son of Kareah and Saraiah the son of Thanehumeth the Netophathite and Jezoniah the son of Maachati and their men. 
2Kings25:24 And Godoliah sware to them and to their men, and said to them: fear not ye the servants of the Caldeyes. But dwell in the land and serve the king of Babylon and ye shall be in good condition." 
2Kings25:25 But it chanced the seventh month after, that Ismael the son of Nathaniah the son of Elisama of the king's blood came, and ten men with him and smote Godoliah that he died: and so did he the Jews and the Caldeyes that were with him at Mazphah." 
2Kings25:26 Thereupon all the people both small and great and the captains of the men of war arose and went to Egypt: for they were afraid of the Caldeyes. 
2Kings25:27 Notwithstanding yet the thirty seventh year after Jehoacin king of Juda was carried away, the twenty seventh day of the twelfth month, did Evilmerodach king of Babylon lift up the head of Jehoacin king of Juda out of the prison house," 
2Kings25:28 and spake kindly to him and set his seat above the seat of all the kings that were with him at Babilon," 
2Kings25:29 and changed his prison garments. And he did ever eat bread before him all the days of his life: 
2Kings25:30 And a continual portion was assigned him of the king, day by day as long as he lived. [The end of the fourth book of the kings, which after the Hebrews is seconde.]" 
1Chronicles1:1 Adam, Seth, Enos:" 
1Chronicles1:2 Kainan, Mahalehel, Jared:" 
1Chronicles1:3 Hanoch, Mathusaleh, Lamech:" 
1Chronicles1:4 Noah, Sem, Ham and Japheth." 
1Chronicles1:5 The sons of Japheth: were Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Thubal, Mosoch and Thiras." 
1Chronicles1:6 The sons of Gomer were: Ascanez, Ipheth and Togormah." 
1Chronicles1:7 The sons of Javan: Elisah, Tharsisah, Cethim and Dodanim." 
1Chronicles1:8 The sons of Ham: Cus, Mizraim, Phut and Canaan." 
1Chronicles1:9 The sons of Cus: Saba, Neuilah, Sabatha, Remah and Sabathaca. The sons of Remah: Saba and Dadan." 
1Chronicles1:10 And Cus begat Nemrod: which Nemrod began to wax mighty upon the earth. 
1Chronicles1:11 And Mizraim begat Ludim, Anamin, Laabim, Nephthuim," 
1Chronicles1:12 Phetrusim and Chasluim: of which came the Philistines and the Chaphtherites. 
1Chronicles1:13 And Canaan, begat Zidon his eldest son, and Heth," 
1Chronicles1:14 Jebusi, Amori, Gergesi," 
1Chronicles1:15 Hevi, Araki, Sini," 
1Chronicles1:16 Aruadi, Zamari and Hemathi." 
1Chronicles1:17 The sons of Sem: Elam, Assur, Arphacsad, Lud, Aram, Uz, Hul, Gether and Mosoch." 
1Chronicles1:18 And Arphacsad begat Salah and Salah begat Eber. 
1Chronicles1:19 And unto Eber were born two sons: the name of the one was Phaleg, because that in his days the land was divided. And his brother's name was Jektan." 
1Chronicles1:20 Jektan begat Elimodah, Saleph, Hazermoth and Jarah:" 
1Chronicles1:21 Haduram, Usal, and Deklah:" 
1Chronicles1:22 Ebal Abimael, and Saba:" 
1Chronicles1:23 Ophir, Hevilah, and Jobab. All these were the sons of Jektan."

1Chronicles1:24 Sem, Arphacsad, Salah:" 
1Chronicles1:25 Eber, Phaleg, Rau:" 
1Chronicles1:26 Serug, Nahor, Thareh:" 
1Chronicles1:27 Abram otherwise called Abraham. 
1Chronicles1:28 The sons of Abraham, Isaac and Ismael." 
1Chronicles1:29 And these are their generations: the eldest son of Ismael was Nabaioth, then Kedar, Adbeel, and Mabsam:" 
1Chronicles1:30 Masma, Dumah, Massa, Nadar and Thema:" 
1Chronicles1:31 Jatur, Naphis and Kedmah. These are the sons of Ismael." 
1Chronicles1:32 The sons of Keturah, Abraham's concubine: she bare Zamram, Jeksan, Madan, Madian, Jesbok and Suah. The sons of Jeksan: Saba and Dadan." 
1Chronicles1:33 The sons of Madian: Ephah, Epher, Henoch, Abida and Eldaah. All these are the sons of Keturah." 
1Chronicles1:34 Abraham begat Isaac. The sons of Isaac, Esau and Israel." 
1Chronicles1:35 The sons of Esau: Eliphaz, Ravel, Jehus, Jaalom and Koreh." 
1Chronicles1:36 The sons of Eliphaz: Theman, Omer, Zephi, Gaatham, Kenes, Themna and Amalek." 
1Chronicles1:37 The sons of Ravel: Nahath, Zarah, Samah and Mesah." 
1Chronicles1:38 The sons of Seir: Lotan, Sobal, Zebeon, Anah, Dison, Ezer and Disan." 
1Chronicles1:39 The sons of Lotan: Hori and Homam, and Thamna was Lotan's sister." 
1Chronicles1:40 The sons of Sobal: Alian, Manahath, Ebal, Sephi, and Onam. The sons of Zebeon: Aiah and Anah." 
1Chronicles1:41 The sons of Anah: Dison. The sons of Dison: Hamaran, Esebon, Jethran and Charan." 
1Chronicles1:42 The sons of Ezer: Balaan, Saavan and Jakan. The sons of Disan: Uz, and Aram." 
1Chronicles1:43 These are the kings that reigned in the land of Edom before any king reigned among the children of Israel: Bale the son of Beor, and the name of his city was Denahabath." 
1Chronicles1:44 And when Bale was dead, Jobab the son of Zareh of Bozrah reigned in his stead." 
1Chronicles1:44 And when Bale was dead, Jobab the son of Zareh of Bozrah reigned in his stead." 
1Chronicles1:45 And after the death of Jobab, Husam of the land of Themani reigned in his room," 
1Chronicles1:45 And after the death of Jobab, Husam of the land of Themani reigned in his room," 
1Chronicles1:46 and after the death of Husam, Hadad, the son of Badad which beat the Madianites in the fields of Moab, reigned in his room, and the name of his city was Avith." 
1Chronicles1:47 And after the death of Hadad, Semlah of Marekah reigned in his stead." 
1Chronicles1:48 And after the death of Semlah Saul of Rohoboth upon Euphrates reigned in his place. 
1Chronicles1:49 And after the death of Saul, Baal Hanan the son of Achobor reigned in his room." 
1Chronicles1:50 And after the death of Baal hanan, Hadad reigned in his place, and the name of his city was Phau, and his wife's name was Mehetabeel the daughter of Matred the daughter of Mesahab." 
1Chronicles1:51 But after the death of Hadad, there were dukes in Edom." 
1Chronicles1:52 Duke Thamna, duke Aliah, duke Jetheth: duke Oholbama, duke Elah, duke Phinon," 
1Chronicles1:53 duke Kenaz, duke Theman, duke Mabzar:" 
1Chronicles1:54 duke Magdiel and duke Iram. These were the dukes of Edom. 
1Chronicles2:1 These be the sons of Israel: Ruben, Simon, Levi, Juda, Isacar and Zabulon:" 
1Chronicles2:2 Dan, Joseph, Benjamin, Nepthali, Gad and Asser." 
1Chronicles2:3 The sons of Juda: Er, Onan, and Selah. These three were born him of the daughter of Sua the Cananitess. But Er the eldest the son of Juda was evil in the sight of the LORD, and therefore he slew him." 
1Chronicles2:4 And Thamar his daughter-in-law bare him Pharez and Zarah: so that all the sons of Juda were five. 
1Chronicles2:5 The sons of Pharez: Hezron and Hamul. 
1Chronicles2:6 The sons of Zarah: Zamri, Ethan, Heman, Chalchal and Dara: five in all." 
1Chronicles2:7 The sons of Carmi: Achar that troubled Israel, which transgressed in the unlawful things." 
1Chronicles2:8 The sons of Ethan: Azaria. 
1Chronicles2:9 The sons of Hezron that were born him: Jerhameel, Ram and Calubai." 
1Chronicles2:10 And Ram begat Aminadab: and Aminadab begat Nahazon a lord among the children of Juda. 
1Chronicles2:11 And Nahazon begat Salma: and Salma begat Booz: 
1Chronicles2:12 and Booz begat Obed: Obed begat Isai. 
1Chronicles2:13 And Isai begat his eldest son Eliab, and Abinadab the second, and Samaa the third," 
1Chronicles2:14 Nathanael the fourth, Radai the fifth," 
1Chronicles2:15 Ozem the sixth, and David the seventh." 
1Chronicles2:16 Whose sisters were Zaruiah and Abigail. The sons of Zaruiah: were Abisai, Joab and Azahel, three." 
1Chronicles2:17 And Abigail bare Amaza the father of which Amaza was Jether an Ismaelite. 
1Chronicles2:18 And Caleb the son of Hezron begat Asubah a woman and Jeroth whose sons are these: Jaser, Sobab and Ardon." 
1Chronicles2:19 But Asubah died, and Caleb took Ephratha which bare him Hur." 
1Chronicles2:20 And Hur begat Uri, and Uri begat Bezeleel." 
1Chronicles2:21 And afterward Hezron went in to the daughter of Machir the father of Gilead, and was when he took her, three score year old. And she bare him Segub." 
1Chronicles2:22 And Segub begat Jair which had twenty three cities in the land of Gilead. 
1Chronicles2:23 And he overcame the Gesurites and the Aramites, and took the towns of Jair from them, and Kenath with the towns that longed to the same, even three score towns. All these were the sons of Machir the father of Gilead." 
1Chronicles2:24 And after the death of Hezron at Caleb in Ephrata, Abia, his wife bare him Ashur the father of Thekua." 
1Chronicles2:25 And the sons of Jerhameel the eldest son of Hezron were, Ram, the eldest and Buna, Oram, Ozem and Ahaiah." 
1Chronicles2:26 And Jerhameel had yet another wife named Atarah which was the mother of Onam. 
1Chronicles2:27 And the sons of Ram, the eldest son of Jerhameel were, Maaz, Zamin an Akar." 
1Chronicles2:28 The sons of Onam were, Samai and Jada. The sons of Samai: Nadab and Abisur." 
1Chronicles2:29 And the wife of Abisur was called Abihahil which bare him Ahaban and Molid. 
1Chronicles2:30 The sons of Nadab: Saled and Appaim. But Saled died without children. 
1Chronicles2:31 The son of Appaim was Jesei. The son of Jesei was Sesan. And the son of Sesan was Oholai. 
1Chronicles2:32 And the sons of Jada the brother of Samai were Jethur and Jonathan. But Jethur died without children. 
1Chronicles2:33 The sons of Jonathan were Paleth and Ziza. These were the sons of Jerhameel. 
1Chronicles2:34 Sesan had no sons but only daughters. 
1Chronicles2:35 And Sesan had a servant that was an Egyptian named Jeraha to whom he gave his daughter to wife, and she bare him Athai." 
1Chronicles2:36 And Athai begat Nathan; And Nathan begat Zabad. 
1Chronicles2:37 And Zabad begat Ophlal. Ophlal begat Obed. 
1Chronicles2:38 Obed begat Jehu. Jehu begat Azariah. 
1Chronicles2:39 Azariah begat Helez. Helez begat Eleasah," 
1Chronicles2:40 Eleasah begat Sisamai. Sisamai begat Selum. 
1Chronicles2:41 Selum begat Jecamiah. Jecamiah begat Elisama. 
1Chronicles2:42 The sons of Caleb the brother of Jerhameel were Mesa his eldest son which was the father of Ziph: and the sons of Maresa the father of Hebron. 
1Chronicles2:43 The sons of Hebron were Coreh, Taphuah Rekem and Sama," 
1Chronicles2:44 Sama begat Raham the father of Jerakaam. And Rekem begat Samai. 
1Chronicles2:45 The son of Samai was Maon. And Maon was the father of Bethzur. 
1Chronicles2:46 And Ephah a concubine of Caleb's bare Haran, Mesa and Gazez: and Haran begat Gazez." 
1Chronicles2:47 The sons of Jahadai were Regem, Jotham, Gesam, Phalet, Ephah and Saaph." 
1Chronicles2:48 And Maachah another concubine of Caleb's bare Sabor Thahanah. 
1Chronicles2:49 And she bare also Saaph the father of Madmanah, and Sue the father of Machbenah and the father of Gabaa. And Caleb had a daughter called Acsah." 
1Chronicles2:50 These were the sons of Caleb the son of Hur the eldest son of Ephrata: Sobal the father of Kariath Jarim: 
1Chronicles2:51 and Salma the father of Bethlehem: and Hareph the father of Beth Geder. 
1Chronicles2:52 And Sobal the father of Kariath Jarim had sons, even the half kindred of Menuah." 
1Chronicles2:53 The kindreds of Kariath Jarim were the Jethrites the Puthites the Semathites and the Maserites. And of them came the Zarathites and the Esthaolites. 
1Chronicles2:54 The sons of Salma, Bethlehem and Netophathi that were the glory of the house of Joab, and half the Manathites and of the Zaraites." 
1Chronicles2:55 And so were the kindred of the writers that dwelt at Jabes, the Tirathites, the Simeathites and Suchathites which are the Kenites, that came of Hemath the father of Beth Rechab."

1Chronicles3:1 These are the sons of David which were born him in Hebron: the eldest Amnon of Ahinoam the Jezraelitess. The second Daniel by Abigail the Carmelitess. 
1Chronicles3:2 The third Absalom the son of Maacah daughter of Tholmai king of Gesur. The fourth Adoniah the son of Hagith. 
1Chronicles3:3 The fifth Saphatiah by Abital. The sixth Jethraam by Eglah his wife. 
1Chronicles3:4 These six were born him in Hebron where he reigned seven years and six months. And in Jerusalem he reigned thirty three year. 
1Chronicles3:5 And these were born him in Jerusalem: Samua, Sobab, Nathan, and Salomon: these four of the daughter of Bath Sua and the daughter of Amiel." 
1Chronicles3:6 Then Jebahar Elisama, Elphalet,"

1Chronicles4:1 The sons of Juda: Pharez, Hezron, Carmi, Hur and Sobal." 
1Chronicles4:2 And Reaiah the son of Sobal begat Jahath. And Jahath begat Ahimai and Laad which are the kindreds of the Zareathites. 
1Chronicles4:3 And of these came the father of Etam, Jezrael, Jesema and Jedebos, with their sister called Zalelphuni:" 
1Chronicles4:4 And Phanuel father of Gedor: And Eser the father of Husah. These are the sons of Hur the eldest son of Ephrata father to Bethlehem. 
1Chronicles4:5 And Ashur the father of Thekua had two wives: Halaah and Naarah. 
1Chronicles4:6 And Naarah bare him Ahusam, Hepher, Themani and Hahastari. These were the sons of Naarah." 
1Chronicles4:7 And the sons of Halaah were Zareth, Izoar and Ethnan." 
1Chronicles4:8 And Coz begat Anub and Zobedah, and the kindreds of Aharhel the son of Harum." 
1Chronicles4:9 And Jeabes was nobler than his brethren. And his mother called his name Jeabes saying: because I bare him with sorrow. 
1Chronicles4:10 But Jeabes called on the God of Israel, saying: If thou shalt bless me, and enlarge my coasts, and shalt let thine hand be with me, and wilt keep me from evil that it vex me not. And God sent him his desire." 
1Chronicles4:11 Calub the brother of Suah begat Mahir, which was the father of Esthon." 
1Chronicles4:12 And Esthon begat Beth Rapha and Phaseh and Thehenah the father of the city of Nahas which are the men of Rechah. 
1Chronicles4:13 The sons of Kenas: Othniel and Saraiah. And the sons of Othniel were Hathath. 
1Chronicles4:14 And Maonothi begat Ophrah. And Saraiah begat Joab the father of them of the valley of craftsmen, so called because they were craftsmen." 
1Chronicles4:15 And the sons of Caleb the son of Jephoneh were Hiru, Ela and Naem. And the son of Ela was Kenas." 
1Chronicles4:16 And the sons of Jehaleleel were Ziph and Ziphah, and Thiriah and Asarael." 
1Chronicles4:17 And the sons of Ezra: were Jether, Mered, Epher, Jalon, Thahar, Mariam, and Samai, and Jesbah the father of Esthamoa." 
1Chronicles4:17 And the sons of Ezra: were Jether, Mered, Epher, Jalon, Thahar, Mariam, and Samai, and Jesbah the father of Esthamoa." 
1Chronicles4:18 And his wife Jehudia bare Jared the father of Gedor, and Heber the father of Socoh, and Icuthiel the father of Zonoah. And these were the sons of Bethiah the daughter of Pharao which Mered took." 
1Chronicles4:19 The sons of the wife of Hodia the sister of Nahan the father of Keilah were Hagarmi and Esthamoa the Maachathite. 
1Chronicles4:20 The sons of Simon were Amnon and Rinah, Benhanan and Thilon. And the sons of Jesi were Zoheth and Benzoheth." 
1Chronicles4:21 The sons of Selah the son of Juda were Er the father of Lecah, and Laadah the father of Maresah, and the kindreds of the households of them that wrought byss in the house of Asbea." 
1Chronicles4:22 And Jokim and the men of Cozebah, and Joas and Saraph, which were inhabited in Moab, but returned to Lehem and to Debarim Aikim." 
1Chronicles4:23 These were potters and dwelt among trees and hedges and were in the king's work and dwelt even there. 
1Chronicles4:24 The sons of Simeon: Nameul, Jamin, Jarib, Zorah and Saul," 
1Chronicles4:25 whose son was Selum, and the son of him was Mabsam, and his son was Masma." 
1Chronicles4:26 And the son of Masma was Hamuel, and his son was Zachur, and the son of him was Semei." 
1Chronicles4:27 Semei had sixteen sons and six daughters. But his brethren had not many children, neither were the kindreds of them like to the children of Juda in multitude." 
1Chronicles4:28 And they dwelt at Bersabe, Moladah and at Hazar Sual," 
1Chronicles4:29 at Balaah, Ezem, Tholad," 
1Chronicles4:30 Bathuel, Hormah and at Zikeleg:" 
1Chronicles4:31 at Bethmarcaboth, Hazar Sufim, Bethberei and Saarim. These were their cities unto the reign of David." 
1Chronicles4:32 And their villages were Etam, Ain, Remon, Tochen and Asan, five towns" 
1Chronicles4:33 and all their villages that were round about the said cities unto Baal. 
1Chronicles4:34 This is the habitation of them and their genealogy. And Mosobab, Jemlech, Josah the son of Amasiah:" 
1Chronicles4:35 and Joel and Jehu the son of Josabiah the son of Saraiah the son of Asiel: 
1Chronicles4:35 and Joel and Jehu the son of Josabiah the son of Saraiah the son of Asiel: 
1Chronicles4:36 and Elioenai, Jakobah, Isohaiah: Asaiah, Adiel, Isimiel and Banaiah:" 
1Chronicles4:37 Ziza the son of Sephei the son of Alon the son of Idaiah the son of Zemri the son of Samaiah. 
1Chronicles4:38 These are such as came by name, heads of these kindreds." 
1Chronicles4:39 And the ancient households of them spread in multitude. And they went as far as Gabor, even unto the east side of the valley, to seek pasture for their cattle." 
1Chronicles4:40 And they found fat pasture and good and a wide land both quiet and fruitful: for they of Ham dwelt there before. 
1Chronicles4:41 And these now afore written by name went in the days of Hezekiah king of Juda, and smote the tents of them and the habitations that were found there, and destroyed them utterly unto this day, and there dwelt in their rooms: because there was pasture there for their sheep." 
1Chronicles4:42 And thereto there went of the said children of Simeon five hundredth men: Phaalthiah, Naariah, Raphiah and Oziel the sons of Jesi being their heads:" 
1Chronicles4:43 and smote the rest of the Amalekites that were escaped and they dwelt there unto this day. 
1Chronicles5:1 The sons of Ruben the eldest son of Israel: for he was the eldest. But because he defied his father's bed, his birthright was given unto the sons of Joseph the son of Israel, and so he is not reckoned unto the birth right." 
1Chronicles5:2 For unto Juda which was mighty among his brethren, was given the principality before him. But the birth right was given Joseph." 
1Chronicles5:3 The sons then of Ruben the eldest son of Israel, were Henoch, Phalu, Hezron and Carmi." 
1Chronicles5:4 The sons of Joel: Samaiah, and his son Gog, and his son Semei," 
1Chronicles5:4 The sons of Joel: Samaiah, and his son Gog, and his son Semei," 
1Chronicles5:5 and his son Micah, and the son of him was Reaiah, and his son was Baal," 
1Chronicles5:5 and his son Micah, and the son of him was Reaiah, and his son was Baal," 
1Chronicles5:6 and Beerah was his son. Which Beerah Thiglath Phalneser, king of Assiria carried away: for he was a great lord among the Rubenites." 
1Chronicles5:7 But unto his brethren in their kindreds, when they are reckoned after their birth, were Jeiel and Zachariah the chief heads." 
1Chronicles5:8 And Bala the son of Azan the son of Sema the son of Joel, dwelt in Aroer and so forth unto Nebo and Baalmaon." 
1Chronicles5:8 And Bala the son of Azan the son of Sema the son of Joel, dwelt in Aroer and so forth unto Nebo and Baalmaon." 
1Chronicles5:9 And eastward he inhabited until the wilderness, even upon the river Euphrates, for their cattle was much in the land of Gilead." 
1Chronicles5:10 And in the days of Saul they warred with the Hagarites which were overthrown by their hands. And they dwelt in their tents thorowout all the east land of Gilead. 
1Chronicles5:11 And the Children of Gad dwelt over against them in the land of Basan, even unto Selcah." 
1Chronicles5:12 And in Basan Joel was the chiefest and Sapham the next, then Jaanai and Saphat." 
1Chronicles5:12 And in Basan Joel was the chiefest and Sapham the next, then Jaanai and Saphat." 
1Chronicles5:13 And their brethren in the ancient households of them, were Michael, Mosolam, Seba, Jorai, Joacan, Zia, Eber and Senen." 
1Chronicles5:14 These were the children of Abihail the son of Hurl the son of Jeroah Gilead the son of Michael the son of Jesisai the son of Jahado the son of Buz. 
1Chronicles5:15 Ahi was the son of Abdiel the son of Guni the head of an ancient household among them. 
1Chronicles5:16 And they dwelt in Gilead and in Basan and her towns, and in all the suburbs of Saron, even unto the end of their borders." 
1Chronicles5:17 And they were all reckoned by kindreds in the days of Jotham king of Juda, and in the days of Jeroboam king of Israel." 
1Chronicles5:18 The sons of Ruben of Gad and of half the tribe of Manasse, even of fighting men able to bear shield and sword, and shoot with bow, and taught to make war, were four and forty thousand seven hundredth and three score that went out to battle." 
1Chronicles5:19 And they fought with the Hagarities and with Jesur, Naphas and Nobab." 
1Chronicles5:20 And they were holp against them, and the Hagarites were delivered into their hands with all that were with the Hagarites. For they cried to God in their battle, and he heard them, because they trusted to him." 
1Chronicles5:21 And they took of their cattle fifty thousand camels, and two hundredth and fifty thousand sheep, and two thousand asses, and of the people an hundredth thousand:" 
1Chronicles5:22 for there fell many dead because the war was of God. And they dwelt there in their steads until the time that they were carried away. 
1Chronicles5:23 And the children of half the tribe of Manasse dwelt in the land, from Basan unto Baal Hermon and Samir and unto mount Hermon, and they were many."

1Chronicles7:3 The sons of Ozi: Izrahiah. The sons of Izrahiah: Michael, Obadiah Joel, Jesiah, five head men in all." 
1Chronicles7:3 The sons of Ozi: Izrahiah. The sons of Izrahiah: Michael, Obadiah Joel, Jesiah, five head men in all."

1Chronicles7:5 And of their brethren among all the kindreds of Isacar were reckoned of men of war four score and seven thousand in all. 
1Chronicles7:6 The sons of Benjamin: Bale, Bochor and Jadiael, three." 
1Chronicles7:7 The sons of Bale: Ezbon, Ozi, Oziel, Jerimoth and Uri, five heads of ancient houses and men of might and were in number twenty two thousand and thirty four."

1Chronicles7:9 All these are the children of Bochor, and were reckoned in their kindreds with the heads of the ancient households of them that were men of power, twenty thousand and two hundredth."

1Chronicles7:11 All these are the sons of Jediael and ancient heads and men of might seventeen thousand and two hundredth that went out to battle. 
1Chronicles7:12 And Suphim and Huphim were the children of Ir. And the Husites were the children of Aher.

1Chronicles7:17 These are the sons of Gilead the son of Machir the son of Manasseh.

1Chronicles7:21 and Sabad his son, and Suthelah his son, and Eser and Elead. And the men of Geth that were born in the land, slew them, because they were come down to take away their cattle."

1Chronicles9:18 For unto the time they had watched at the king's gate eastward in the tents of the children of Levi by companies. 
1Chronicles9:19 And Selum the son of Coreh the son of Abiasaph the son of Coreh with his brethren the Corehites in the house of their fathers had their business and office to keep the door of the tabernacle: for their fathers in the Host of the LORD kept the entering. 
1Chronicles9:20 And Phinehes the son of Eleazar was ruler over them before the LORD which was with him. 
1Chronicles9:21 And Zachariah the son of Moselamiah kept the door of the tabernacle of witness. 
1Chronicles9:22 All these were chosen fellows to keep the thresholds, two hundredth and twelve, and were numbered in their villages. And them did David and Samuel the sear institute in their fidelity." 
1Chronicles9:23 And their children had the oversight of the gates of the house of the LORD and of the house of the tabernacle, to keep them." 
1Chronicles9:24 In four quarters were the keepers: toward the east, west, north and south." 
1Chronicles9:25 And their brethren that were in the country came in their weeks as their course came about with them. 
1Chronicles9:26 For the Levites were committed to be principal porters of fidelity. And they had the oversight of the vestries and treasures of the house of God," 
1Chronicles9:27 and lay all night round about the house of God: because the keeping thereof pertained to them and to open it every morning. 
1Chronicles9:28 And certain of them had the rule of the ministering vessels and brought them in and out by tale. 
1Chronicles9:29 And of them were certain appointed to oversee the vessels and all holy ornaments, and of the flour, wine, oil, frankincense and sweet odours." 
1Chronicles9:30 But certain of the sons of the priests were set to make sweet savours. 
1Chronicles9:31 And Mathathiah one of the Levites, the eldest son of Selum: the Corathite, had the oversight of the things that were baken in the frying pan." 
1Chronicles9:32 And other of their brethren the sons of Cahath had the oversight of the shewbread, to prepare against all Sabbaths." 
1Chronicles9:33 These are the singers ancient heads among the Levites divided by chambers: for they had to do both day and night. 
1Chronicles9:34 These were the ancient among the Levites in their generations. And these dwelt at Jerusalem.

1Chronicles10:1 And the Philistines fought against Israel. And the men of Israel fled before the Philistines and were overthrown and slain in mount Gelboe. 
1Chronicles10:2 And the Philistines followed Saul and his sons and slew Jehonathan, Abinadab and Melchisua the sons of Saul." 
1Chronicles10:3 And the battle went sore against Saul, in so much that the shooters met him, and he was wounded of the shooters." 
1Chronicles10:4 Then said Saul to his weapon bearer draw thy sword and thrust me thorow therewith, that these uncircumcised come not and do me shame. And his weapon bearer would not, but feared exceedingly. Whereupon Saul caught a sword and fell upon it." 
1Chronicles10:5 And when his harness bearer saw that Saul was dead, he fell on a sword also and died." 
1Chronicles10:6 And thus Saul and his three sons and all his house died together. 
1Chronicles10:7 And when all the men that dwelt in the valleys, saw how they fled, and that Saul and his sons were dead, they forsook their cities and ran away, and the Philistines came and dwelt in them." 
1Chronicles10:8 And on the morrow when the Philistines came to strip the dead bodies, they found Saul and his sons overthrown in mount Gelboe." 
1Chronicles10:9 And when they had stripped him they took his head and his harness and sent them into the land of the Philistines round about to shew tidings unto their gods(Idols) and to the people. 
1Chronicles10:10 And they put his harness in the houses of their gods. But his skull they hanged in the house of Dagon. 
1Chronicles10:11 And when all they of Jabes in Gilead heard all that the Philistines had done to Saul," 
1Chronicles10:12 all the men of war arose and fetched away the body of Saul, and the bodies of his sons and brought them to Jabes and buried the bones of them under an oak in Jabes, and fasted seven days." 
1Chronicles10:13 And so Saul died for his trespass that he trespassed against the LORD, in that he kept not the word of the LORD, and in that he asked counsel of a woman that wrought with a spirit," 
1Chronicles10:14 and asked not of the LORD. And therefore the Lord(he) killed him and turned the kingdom unto David the son of Jesse.(Iesai) 
1Chronicles11:1 Then all Israel gathered themselves to David unto Hebron, and said: see we be thy bones and thy flesh." 
1Chronicles11:2 And moreover in time past, even when Saul was king, thou leddest Israel out and in. And the LORD thy God said unto thee: thou shalt feed my people Israel, and thou shalt be captain over my people Israel." 
1Chronicles11:3 And when all the elders of Israel were come to the king to Hebron, David made a covenant with them there before the LORD. And they anointed David king over Israel according to the word of the LORD by the hand of Samuel." 
1Chronicles11:4 And David and all Israel went to Jerusalem which is Jebus: for there dwelt the Jebusites the inhabiters of the land. 
1Chronicles11:5 And the enhabiters of Jebus said to David, thou comest not here. Neverthelater David won the castle of Sion, which is called the city of David." 
1Chronicles11:6 Then said David: whosoever smite the Jebusites first, shall be the principal captain and a lord. And Joab the son of Zaruiah went first up, and was therefore the chiefcaptain." 
1Chronicles11:7 And because David dwelt in the castle, therefore it was called the city of David." 
1Chronicles11:8 And he built the city on every side even from Mello round about. But Joab repaired the rest of the city. 
1Chronicles11:9 And David prospered and waxed great, and the LORD of Hosts was with him." 
1Chronicles11:10 These are the principal men of power that clave to David in his kingdom with all Israel, to make him king upon Israel according to the word of the LORD." 
1Chronicles11:11 And this is the number of the mighty men which David had: Isabaam the son of Hachamoni the chief of thirty, he lifted up his spear against three hundredth and slew them at one time." 
1Chronicles11:12 And after him Eleazar the son of Dodo an Ahothite which was one of the three mightiest. 
1Chronicles11:13 He was with David at Phasdamin where the Philistines were gathered to battle. And there was there a parcel of ground full of barley, and the people fled for fear of the Philistines." 
1Chronicles11:14 And they stepped forth into the midst of the furlong and saved it and slew the Philstines. And the LORD gave a great victory. 
1Chronicles11:15 And the three of the thirty chiefs went to a rock to David at the cave Odolam. And the Host of the Philistines had pitched in the valley of Raphaim. 
1Chronicles11:16 And David was in an hold. And there was a garrison of the Philistines at Bethlehem that same time. 
1Chronicles11:17 And David longed and said: Oh that one would give me of the water of the well of Bethlehem that is in the gate, for to drink." 
1Chronicles11:18 And the three brake thorow the Host of the Philistines and drew of the water of the well of Bethlehem that was in the gate, and took it and brought it to David. Nevertheless David would not drink of it, but offered it to the LORD," 
1Chronicles11:19 and said: God forbid it me, that I should do this thing, that I should drink the blood of these men that have put their lives in Jeopardy (for with the Jeopardy of their lives they brought it) and therefore he would not drink it. This did the three mightiest." 
1Chronicles11:20 And Abisai the brother of Joab was captain of the three, and he lifted up his spear against three hundredth and slew them, and had a name among the three." 
1Chronicles11:21 And he was much more nobler than any of the three, and was their captain. But was not like to any of the three in acts." 
1Chronicles11:22 Banaiah the son of Jehoiada the son of a strong man that had done great deeds and was of Cabzeel: he slew two strong lions of Moab, and went down and slew a Lion in a pit in time of snow." 
1Chronicles11:23 And he slew an Egyptian which was a man of size, even five cubits long, and in his hand had a spear like a weaver's beam. And the other went to him with a waster and plucked the spear out of the Egyptian's hand, and slew him with his spear." 
1Chronicles11:24 Such things did Banaiah the son of Jehoihada, and had a name with the three mightiest," 
1Chronicles11:25 was the gloriousest of thirty: but attained not unto the three. And David made him of his secret counsel. 
1Chronicles11:26 And these were the best men of war of the Host: Asahel the brother of Joab and Elhanan the son of Dodo out of Bethlehem. 
1Chronicles11:27 Samoth the Nazorite, Helez the Phalonite:" 
1Chronicles11:28 Ira the son of Akes the Thekuite, Abiezer the Anathothite:"

1Chronicles11:32 Haurai of the river of Gaas, and Abiel the Arbathite:" 
1Chronicles11:33 Azmoth the Baharumite, Elhaba the Saalbonite."

1Chronicles11:37 Hezro the Carmelite and Naari the son of Azbai:

1Chronicles11:38 Joel the brother of Nathan: Mibahar the son of Gari:

1Chronicles12:1 And these following came to David to Zikelag, while he yet kept himself close for fear of Saul the son of Cis: and they were of the men that succoured the battle," 
1Chronicles12:2 weaponed with bows, and could handle stones, arrows and bow as well with the left hand as with the right, and were of Saul's brethren, even of the tribe of Benjamin." 
1Chronicles12:3 The chiefest were Ahiezer and Joas the sons of Samaah a Gobaonite, and Jaziel a Phalite, the sons of Azmoth. Baracah and Jehu of Anathoth." 
1Chronicles12:4 And Isamaiah a Gabaonite as mighty as any of thirty and more mighty too. And Jeremiah, Jehaziel, Johanan and Josabad of Gedor." 
1Chronicles12:4 And Isamaiah a Gabaonite as mighty as any of thirty and more mighty too. And Jeremiah, Jehaziel, Johanan and Josabad of Gedor." 
1Chronicles12:5 Eluesai, Jerimoth, Baaliah, Samariah and Saphatiah: Haraphites," 
1Chronicles12:6 Elcanah, Jesia, Azrael, Joezer, Joseboam of Karehim." 
1Chronicles12:7 And Joelah and Zabadiah the sons of Jeroham of Gedor. 
1Chronicles12:7 And Joelah and Zabadiah the sons of Jeroham of Gedor. 
1Chronicles12:8 And of the Gadites there separated themselves unto David when he kept an hold in the wilderness, men of might and men apt for war and could handle shield and spear, whose faces were like the faces of Lions, and they were thereto as swift as the Roes of the mountains:" 
1Chronicles12:9 Ezer the first, Obdiah the second, Eliab the third," 
1Chronicles12:10 Masmannah the fourth, Jeremiah the fifth," 
1Chronicles12:10 Masmannah the fourth, Jeremiah the fifth," 
1Chronicles12:11 Ethi the sixth, Eliel the seventh," 
1Chronicles12:12 Johanan the eighth, Elsabad the ninth," 
1Chronicles12:13 Jeremiah the tenth, and Macahbanai the eleventh." 
1Chronicles12:13 Jeremiah the tenth, and Macahbanai the eleventh." 
1Chronicles12:14 These were of the sons of Gad, and were captains over the men of war, the small over an hundredth, and the great over a thousand." 
1Chronicles12:15 These are they that went over Jordan in the first month, when he had spilled over all his bank." 
1Chronicles12:16 And they put to flight them of the valley both of the east side and also of the west. And there came of the children of Benjamin and Juda to the hold of David. 
1Chronicles12:17 And David went out to them and answered and said to them: If ye be come peaceably unto me, to help me, mine heart shall be together with you. But and if you come to beguile me and to be mine adversary, seeing there is no wickedness in mine hands, the God of our fathers look thereon and rebuke it." 
1Chronicles12:18 And the spirit came upon Amasai a captain over thirty and he said, thine are we David, and on thy side thou son of Jesse:(Heb. Iesay) peace be with thee, and peace be with thine helpers, for thy God helpeth thee. Then David received them and made them heads of companies of the men of war." 
1Chronicles12:19 And there fell of Manasse to David when he went with the Philistines against Saul to battle, but holp them not. For the lords of the Philistines took counsel and sent him away saying: he will fall to his master Saul to the Jeopardy of our heads." 
1Chronicles12:20 And as he went to Zikelag there fell to him of Manasse: Ednah, Jozabad, Jediel, Michael, and Jozabad, Elihu and Zalthai, heads of the thousands of Manasse." 
1Chronicles12:21 And they holp David against those rovers. For they were all mighty men and Captains in war. 
1Chronicles12:22 And there came one or other to David day by day to help him: until it was a great Host, like the Host of God." 
1Chronicles12:23 And this is the number of the heads prepared to battle, that came to David to Hebron, to turn the kingdom of Saul to him, according to the mouth of the LORD." 
1Chronicles12:24 The children of Juda that bare shield and spear were six thousand and eight hundredth trimmed to war. 
1Chronicles12:25 Of the children of Simeon, men of might to war, seven thousand and one hundredth." 
1Chronicles12:26 And of the children of Levi were four thousand and six hundredth. 
1Chronicles12:27 And Jehoiada was the chief of them of Aaron and with him three thousand and seven hundredth. 
1Chronicles12:28 And Zadock a young man of great power had his father's household with Captains twenty two. 
1Chronicles12:29 And of the children of Benjamin the brethren of Saul, were three thousand. And a great part of them did yet follow the house of Saul." 
1Chronicles12:30 And of the children of Ephraim, were twenty thousand and eight hundredth, men of might and named men in the households of their fathers." 
1Chronicles12:31 And of the half tribe of Manasseh eighteen thousand which were appointed by name to go to make David king. 
1Chronicles12:32 And of the children of Isacar which were men that had understanding in the right time to know what Israel ought to do: The heads of them were two hundredth, and all their brethren at their will." 
1Chronicles12:33 And of Zabulon that went out with the Host and proceeded in the array to battle with all manner of weapons of war, fifty thousand that kept in order with one accord." 
1Chronicles12:34 And of Nephtali a thousand Captains, and with them with shield and spear. Thirty seven thousand." 
1Chronicles12:35 And of Dan prepared to battle, were twenty eight thousand and six hundredth." 
1Chronicles12:36 And of Asser that went out with the Host to make battle, forty thousand." 
1Chronicles12:37 And of the other side Jordan, of the Rubenites, Gadites and of the half tribe of Manasse with all manner of instruments of war an hundredth and twenty thousand." 
1Chronicles12:38 All these being men of war able to order the array, came to Hebron with pure heart, to make David king upon all Israel. And thereto all the rest of Israel was of one accord to make David king." 
1Chronicles12:39 And there they were with David three days eating and drinking: for their brethren prepared for them. 
1Chronicles12:40 Moreover they that were nigh to them, as Isacar, Zabulon and, Nephthali brought bread on asses, Camels, mules and oxen, and meat: flour, figs, raisins, wine, oil, oxen, and sheep abundantly. For there was mirth in Israel." 
1Chronicles13:1 And David counselled with the Captains of thousands and of hundreds, and with all the lords," 
1Chronicles13:2 and said unto all the congregation of Israel: If it seem you good and also to be of the LORD our God. Let us take and send unto the remnant of our brethren thorowout all the land of Israel, and to the priests and Levites with them, in their cities and suburbs, and gather them together to us." 
1Chronicles13:3 And let us bring again the Ark of the Lord(God) to us: for we regarded it not in the days of Saul. 
1Chronicles13:4 And all the congregation was content so to do, for the thing seemed good in the eyes of all the people." 
1Chronicles13:5 And David gathered all Israel together from Sihor in Egypt unto Hemath, to bring the Ark of the Lord(God) from Kariath Jarim." 
1Chronicles13:6 And David went up and all Israel to Baalah, and so to Kariath Jarim that is in Juda, to fetch thence the Ark of the God and LORD that sitteth on the Cherubs. Where his name is called on." 
1Chronicles13:7 And they carried the Ark of God in a new cart out of the house of Abinadab. And Oza and his brother drave the cart. 
1Chronicles13:8 And David and all Israel played before God with all their might, with singing, harps, psalteries, timbrels and trumpets." 
1Chronicles13:9 And when they came on the threshing floor of Chidon, Oza put forth his hand to hold the ark, for the oxen stumbled." 
1Chronicles13:10 And the LORD was wroth with Oza and smote him, because he put his hand to the Ark." 
1Chronicles13:11 And there he died before God. And David was displeased, because the LORD had rent a rent in Oza, and called the said place Perez Oza unto this day." 
1Chronicles13:12 And David was so afraid of God that day, that he said, how shall I bring the Ark of God home to me?" 
1Chronicles13:13 And so David brought not the Ark home to him in the city of David: But turned it in to the house of Obed Edom o Gethite 
1Chronicles13:14 and the Ark of God was with Obed Edom in his house, three months. And the LORD blessed the house of Obed Edom and all that he had." 
1Chronicles14:1 After that Hiram king of Tire sent messengers to David, and timber of Cedar trees with masons and Carpenters, to build him an house." 
1Chronicles14:2 Whereby David perceived that the LORD had prepared him to be king upon Israel, in that his kingdom was lifted up on high, because of his people Israel." 
1Chronicles14:3 And David took yet more wives at Jerusalem and begat more sons and daughters. 
1Chronicles14:4 And these are the names of his children which he had born him at Jerusalem: 
1Chronicles14:5 Samua, Sobab, Nathan, and Salomon: Jebhar, Elisua and Eliphelet:" 
1Chronicles14:6 Noga, Nepheg and Japhiah." 
1Chronicles14:7 Elisama, Baliada and Eliphelet." 
1Chronicles14:8 And when it was known among the Philistines that David was anointed king upon all Israel, all the Philistines went up to seek David. And David heard of it and went out against them." 
1Chronicles14:9 And the Philistines came and pranced in the valley of Raphaim. 
1Chronicles14:10 And David asked God saying: shall I go against the Philistines and wilt thou deliver them into mine hands? And the LORD said to him: go, for I will deliver them into thine hand." 
1Chronicles14:11 And when they were come as far as Baal Pharazim, David beat them there. And David said: God hath divided mine enemies with mine hand as a man would divide water. And therefore they called the name of the said place Baal Pharazim." 
1Chronicles14:12 And they left their gods there, which David bade to be burnt with fire." 
1Chronicles14:13 And the Philistines came again and ran abroad in the valley. 
1Chronicles14:14 Then David asked God: And God said to him: Go not after them, but turn away from them, and come upon them straight from the pear trees." 
1Chronicles14:15 And when thou hearest a sound go in the tops of the pear trees, then go out to battle: for God will go out before thee to smite the host of the Philistines." 
1Chronicles14:16 And David did as God commanded him. And they smote the host of the Philistines all the way from Gabaon to Gazer. 
1Chronicles14:17 And the fame of David went out into all lands, and the LORD made all nations fear him." 
1Chronicles15:1 And David made him houses in the city of David, and prepared a place for the Ark of God, and pitched for it a tent." 
1Chronicles15:2 And then David said, the Ark of God ought not to be carried but of the Levites. For them hath the LORD chosen to bear the Ark of the LORD and to minister to him for ever." 
1Chronicles15:3 And therefore David gathered all Israel to Jerusalem, to fetch the Ark of the LORD unto the place which he had ordained for it." 
1Chronicles15:4 And David brought together the children of Aaron and the Levites. 
1Chronicles15:5 Of the sons of Cahath: Uriel the chief, and of his brethren an hundredth and twenty." 
1Chronicles15:6 And of the children of Merari: Asaiah the chief, and of his brethren two hundredth and twenty." 
1Chronicles15:7 And of the sons of Gersom: Joel the chief, and of his brethren an hundredth and thirty." 
1Chronicles15:7 And of the sons of Gersom: Joel the chief, and of his brethren an hundredth and thirty." 
1Chronicles15:8 And of the children of Elizaphan: Semeiah the chief and of his brethren two hundredth. 
1Chronicles15:9 And of the sons of Hebron, Eliel the chief, and of his brethren eighty." 
1Chronicles15:10 And of the sons of Oziel, Aminadab the chief, and of his brethren an hundredth and twelve." 
1Chronicles15:11 And David called to Zadock and Abiathar the priests, and to the Levites Uriel, Asaiah, Joel, Semeiah, Eliel and Aminadab:" 
1Chronicles15:11 And David called to Zadock and Abiathar the priests, and to the Levites Uriel, Asaiah, Joel, Semeiah, Eliel and Aminadab:" 
1Chronicles15:12 and said unto them: ye are the principal heads of the Levites, sanctify therefore yourselves and your brethren and bring the Ark of the LORD God of Israel unto the place that I have prepared for it." 
1Chronicles15:13 For because ye were not there at the first time, the LORD our God made a rent among us, for that we sought him not as the fashion ought to be." 
1Chronicles15:14 And the priests and the Levites sanctified themselves to fetch the Ark of the LORD God of Israel. 
1Chronicles15:15 And the children of the Levites bare the Ark of God(Ark of God the LORDE) upon their shoulders with staves thereon as Moses commanded according to the word of the LORD. 
1Chronicles15:16 And David spake to the chief heads of the Levites, that they should appoint of their brethren to sing with instruments of music: psalteries, harps and cymbals that sounded, and to sing on high with Joyfulness." 
1Chronicles15:17 And the Levites appointed Heman the son of Joel: and of his brethren, Asaph the son of Barachiah. And of the sons of Merari their brethren: Ethan the son of Cusaiahu." 
1Chronicles15:17 And the Levites appointed Heman the son of Joel: and of his brethren, Asaph the son of Barachiah. And of the sons of Merari their brethren: Ethan the son of Cusaiahu." 
1Chronicles15:18 And with them their brethren of the second degree: Zachariah, Ben, Jaaziel, Semiramoth, Jahiel, Uri, Eliab, Banaiah, Maasaiah, Mathathiah, Eliphelehu, Makeniah, Obed Edom and Jeiel, porters." 
1Chronicles15:19 Heman, Asaph and Ethan sang with cymbals of brass to make a sound." 
1Chronicles15:20 And Zachariah, Oziel, Semiramoth, Jehiel, Ani, Eliab, Maasaiah and Banaiah sang with psalteries on an Almoth." 
1Chronicles15:21 And Mathathiah, Eliphelehu, Makeniah, Obed Edom, Jeiel, and Ozaziah sang with harps an eight above to courage with all." 
1Chronicles15:22 And Conaniah the chief of the Levites was master of the song and taught to sing, for he was a man of understanding." 
1Chronicles15:23 And Barachiah and Elcanah kept the door of the Ark. 
1Chronicles15:24 And Sacaniah, Jehosaphat, Nathaniel, Amasai, Zachariah, Banaiah and Eliezer priests were blowers with trumpets before the Ark of God. And Obed Edom and Jahiah were keepers of the door of the Ark." 
1Chronicles15:25 And David and the elders of Israel and the Captains over thousands went to fetch the Ark of the appointment of the LORD out of the house of Obed Edom with gladness. 
1Chronicles15:26 And when God had holp the Levites that bare the Ark of the appointment of the LORD, they offered seven oxen and seven rams." 
1Chronicles15:27 And David had on him an alb of byss, and so had the Levites that bare the Ark, and so had the singers, and Conaniah the ruler of the song and of the singers. And David had moreover upon him an Ephod of linen." 
1Chronicles15:28 And all Israel brought the Ark of the LORD's covenant with shouting and blowing of horns, and with trumpets and cymbals that sounded, and with psalteries and harps." 
1Chronicles15:29 And as the Ark of the appointment of the LORD came unto the city of David, Michol the daughter of Saul looked out at a window: and when she saw king David dancing and playing, she despised him in her heart." 
1Chronicles16:1 When they had brought in the Ark of God they set it in the tent that David had pitched for it. And they brought burnt sacrifice and peaceofferings before God. 
1Chronicles16:2 And when David had made an end of offering of burntofferings and of peaceofferings, he blessed the people with the name of the LORD." 
1Chronicles16:3 And he dealt all Israel both man and woman a cracknel of bread, a piece of flesh and a mess of sops." 
1Chronicles16:4 And he appointed certain of the Levites to minister before the LORD, and to repeat, and to thank and praise the LORD God of Israel:" 
1Chronicles16:5 even Asaph the chief, and next to him Zachariah then Jeiel, Semiramoth, Jehiel, Mathathaih, Eliab, Banaiah, Obed Edom, and Jeiel with Psalteries and harps. But Asaph with sounding cymbals" 
1Chronicles16:6 and Banaiah and Jahaziel priests with trumpets continually before the Ark of the covenant of God. 
1Chronicles16:7 And that same time David did appoint chiefly to thank the LORD by Asaph and his brethren. 
1Chronicles16:8 Thank the LORD, call on his name, make his Acts known among the people." 
1Chronicles16:9 Sing unto him and play unto him: and record all his wonderful deeds. 
1Chronicles16:10 Praise his holy name, and let the hearts of them that seek the LORD rejoice." 
1Chronicles16:11 Seek the LORD and his strength: Seek his presence always. 
1Chronicles16:12 Remember his marvels which he did, and his wonders, and the judgements of his mouth:" 
1Chronicles16:13 The seed of Israel are his servants: the children of Jacob are his chosen. 
1Chronicles16:14 He is the LORD our God: in all lands are his judgements. 
1Chronicles16:15 Think on his appointment ever, and on the word he commanded to a thousand generations." 
1Chronicles16:16 Of his covenant to Abraham, and of his oath to Isaac:" 
1Chronicles16:17 which he set before Jacob for a decree, and to Israel for an everlasting covenant, saying:" 
1Chronicles16:18 to thee I will give the land of Canaan, to be the portion of your inheritance." 
1Chronicles16:19 When you were a small company in number, even but a few, and thereto strangers therein." 
1Chronicles16:20 And they went from people to people, and from one kingdom to another nation." 
1Chronicles16:21 He suffered no man to do them wrong: But rebuked kings for their sakes. 
1Chronicles16:22 Touch not mine anointed, nor do my Prophets any harm." 
1Chronicles16:23 Sing unto the LORD all the earth: and shew from day to day his victories. 
1Chronicles16:24 Tell among the heathen of his glory: and among all nations of his wonderful deeds. 
1Chronicles16:25 For great is the LORD and to be praised exceedingly: and terrible is he above all Gods. 
1Chronicles16:26 For all the gods of the heathen are Idols: But the LORD made heaven.(the heavens) 
1Chronicles16:27 Praise and honour are in his presence: strength and gladness are in his place. 
1Chronicles16:28 Ascribe to the LORD ye kindreds of people; Ascribe to the LORD Glory and strength. 
1Chronicles16:29 Give honour unto the LORD's name: bring presents and come before him, and bow to the LORD in holy apparel." 
1Chronicles16:30 All the earth fear him, he stablished the earth that it can not move." 
1Chronicles16:31 The heaven rejoice and the earth be glad, and let men tell among the nations that the LORD is a king." 
1Chronicles16:32 Let the sea thunder and his fulness, and let the fields rejoice and all that is therein." 
1Chronicles16:33 Moreover let the trees of the wood ring at the presence of the LORD: for he cometh to judge the earth. 
1Chronicles16:34 Thank the LORD for he is good, and his mercy lasteth ever:" 
1Chronicles16:35 and say: save us o God our saviour and gather us together and deliver us from among the heathen, to thank thy holy name, and to praise thee with the praise that becometh thee." 
1Chronicles16:36 Blessed be the LORD God of Israel for ever and ever, and all nations say Amen, so praising the LORD." 
1Chronicles16:37 And he left there before the ark of the LORD's covenant, Asaph and his brethren, to minister before the Ark evermore day by day." 
1Chronicles16:38 And Obed Edom with his brethren three score and eight, and Obed Edom the son of Iduthun and Ozah to be porters." 
1Chronicles16:39 And Zadock the priest with his brethren the priests set he before the tabernacle of the LORD at the hillaltar of Gabaon," 
1Chronicles16:40 to offer burntofferings unto the LORD upon the burntoffering altar perpetually at morning and evening, according to all that is written in the law of the LORD which he commanded Israel." 
1Chronicles16:41 And with them Heman and Iduthun, and other that were chosen and named by name, to thank the LORD that his mercy lasteth ever." 
1Chronicles16:42 And with the said Heman and Iduthun were trumpets and sounding cymbals, and instruments of the melody of God. And the sons of Iduthun were porters." 
1Chronicles16:43 And then all the people departed, every man to his house: and David returned to salute his household." 
1Chronicles17:1 And as David dwelt in his house he said to Nathan the prophet: lo, I dwell in an house of Cedar tree: and the Ark of the LORD's covenant dwelleth under curtains." 
1Chronicles17:2 And Nathan said to David: do all that is in thine heart, for God is with thee." 
1Chronicles17:3 But the same night the word of God came to Nathan saying: 
1Chronicles17:4 go and tell David my servant, thus sayeth the LORD, thou shalt not build me an house to dwell in." 
1Chronicles17:5 For I have dwell in no house since I brought out the children of Israel unto this day: But have gone from tent to tent and from one tabernacle to another. 
1Chronicles17:6 Neither in any place wheresoever I have walked thorowout all Israel, spake I ever one word to any of the judges of Israel which I commanded to feed my people, saying: why build ye me not an house of Cedar tree?" 
1Chronicles17:7 Now therefore thus say unto my servant David: thus sayeth the LORD of Hosts: I took thee out of the pasture from after sheep, that thou shouldest be captain over my people Israel." 
1Chronicles17:8 And I have been with thee in all thou tookest in hand, and have weeded out all thine enemies out of thy sight, and have made thee a name like the name of the greatest men of the earth." 
1Chronicles17:9 And I will ordain a place for my people Israel, and will make it fast, and they shall dwell under it and shall move no more. Neither shall the children of wickedness vex them any more as at the beginning," 
1Chronicles17:10 and since the time I commanded judges to be over my people Israel, and will bring under all thine enemies (for I told thee that the LORD would build thee an house)." 
1Chronicles17:11 But when thy days be expired, that thou must go after thy fathers, I will set up thy seed after thee, which shall be of thy sons and will settle his kingdom." 
1Chronicles17:12 He shall build me an house, and I will stablish his seat for ever." 
1Chronicles17:13 I will be his father and he shall be my son, and I will not put my mercy away from him as I did from him that was before thee." 
1Chronicles17:14 But I will set him in mine house and in my kingdom for ever, and his seat shall be sure for ever." 
1Chronicles17:15 When Nathan had told David according to all these words, and to all this vision, king David went" 
1Chronicles17:16 and sat him before the LORD and said: what am I O LORD God and what is mine household, that thou hast promoted me thus far." 
1Chronicles17:17 And yet this seemed little in thine eyes O God. But that thou shouldest speak of thy servant's house for a great while to come: and hast looked upon me as upon a man of high degree O LORD God. 
1Chronicles17:18 What can David desire more of thee, wherein thou shouldest make thy servant more glorious? Thou knowest thy servant:" 
1Chronicles17:19 And LORD for thy servant's sake, even according to thine own heart thou hast done all this magnificence," 
1Chronicles17:20 to shew all bounteousness LORD, there is none like thee, nor any God save thou, according to all that we have heard with our ears." 
1Chronicles17:21 Moreover what nation on the earth is like thy people Israel, which God went and redeemed to be his own people, and to make thee a name of excellency and terribleness, with casting out nations from before thy people," 
1Chronicles17:22 for ever, and thou LORD art become their God." 
1Chronicles17:23 And now LORD the thing thou hast spoken concerning thy servant and his house be true for ever, and do as thou hast said." 
1Chronicles17:24 And thy name continue and wax great for ever, that it be said: the LORD of Hosts, the God of Israel is God in Israel, and the house of David thy servant be stable before thee." 
1Chronicles17:25 For thou Lord(God) hast told thy servant, that thou wilt build him an house. And therefore thy servant hath found in his heart to pray before thee." 
1Chronicles17:26 And now LORD thou art the very God, and hast promised this goodness unto thy servant." 
1Chronicles17:27 Now therefore go to and bless the house of thy servant, that it may continue before thee for ever. For what thou blessest, O LORD that is blessed for ever." 
1Chronicles18:1 And after that David beat the Philistines and subdued them, and took Gath and the towns that longed thereto, out of the hands of the Philistines." 
1Chronicles18:2 And he beat the Moabites, and the Moabites became David's servants and paid him tribute." 
1Chronicles18:3 And David beat Hadadezer king of Zobah unto Hemath, as he went to bring his dominion unto the river Euphrates." 
1Chronicles18:4 And David took from him a thousand chariots and seven thousand horsemen and twenty thousand footmen, and brake all the chariots, save that he reserved of them an hundredth." 
1Chronicles18:5 Then came the Sirians of Damasco to help Hadadezer king of Zobah. And David slew of the Sirians twenty two thousand," 
1Chronicles18:6 and put garrisons in Siria Damasco: And the Sirians became David's servants and brought him tribute. For the LORD holp David in all that he went to. 
1Chronicles18:7 And David took the shields of gold that were on the servants of Hadadezer and brought them to Jerusalem. 
1Chronicles18:8 And from Thebahath and Chun, cities of Hadadezer, brought David exceeding much brass. Wherewith Salomon made the brazen sea and the pillars and the vessels of brass." 
1Chronicles18:9 And when Thou king of Hemath, heard how David had beat all the host of Hadadezer king of Zobah," 
1Chronicles18:10 he sent Haduram his son to king David, to salute him and to bless him, because he had fought with Hadadezer and beaten him (for Thou had war with Hadadezer) and sent all manner of Jewels of gold, silver and brass with him." 
1Chronicles18:11 And king David dedicated them also unto the LORD with the silver and gold that he brought from all nations, from the Edomites, from the Moabites, from the children of Ammon, from the Philistines, from the Amalekites." 
1Chronicles18:12 And Abisai the son of Zaruiah slew of the Edomites in the salt valley eighteen thousand," 
1Chronicles18:13 and put Soldiers in Edom, and all Edom became David's servants: for the LORD kept David in all that he took in hand." 
1Chronicles18:14 And David reigned over all Israel and did right and equity unto all his people. 
1Chronicles18:15 And Joab the son of Zaruiah was over the host, and Jehosaphat the son of Ahilud recorder," 
1Chronicles18:16 and Zadock the son of Ahitob and Abimelech the son of Abiathar were the priests, and Susa was scribe," 
1Chronicles18:17 and Banaiah the son of Jehoiada was over the Cerethites and the Phelethites and the eldest sons of David waited at the king's hand. 
1Chronicles19:1 After this it chanced that Nahas king of the children of Ammon died, and his son made king in his stead." 
1Chronicles19:2 Then said David, I will shew kindness unto Hanon the son of Nahas, because his father dealt kindly with me: and thereupon David sent messengers to comfort him over the death of his father. And when the servants of David were come into the land of the children of Ammon to Hanon to comfort him," 
1Chronicles19:3 the lords of the children of Ammon said to Hanon: thinkest thou that David doth honour thy father in thy sight, that he hath sent comforters unto thee? Nay, but it is to search and overthrow and to spy out the land, that his servants be come unto thee." 
1Chronicles19:4 Whereupon Hanon took David's servants and shaved them, and cut off their coats hard by their buttocks and sent them away." 
1Chronicles19:5 And there went that told David how the men were served. And the king sent to meet them (for the men were exceedingly ashamed) and said: tarry at Jericho until your beards be grown, and then return." 
1Chronicles19:6 And when the children of Ammon saw that they stank in the nose of David, Hanon, and the children of Ammon sent a thousand talents of silver to hire the chariots and horsemen out of Mesopotamia and out of Siria Maacah and out of Zobah." 
1Chronicles19:7 And they hired thirty two thousand chariots, and the king of Maacah and his people, which came and pitched before Midba. And the children of Ammon gathered themselves together from their cities and went out to battle." 
1Chronicles19:8 And as soon as David heard of it, he sent Joab with all the host of strong men." 
1Chronicles19:9 And the children of Ammon came out and put them in array to battle before the gate of the city. And the kings that were come kept them by themselves in the field. 
1Chronicles19:10 When Joab saw that the front of the battle was before him and after, he chose out of all the youth of Israel and put them in array against the Sirians." 
1Chronicles19:11 And the rest of the people he delivered unto Abisai his brother, that they should put themselves in array against the children of Ammon," 
1Chronicles19:12 and said: If the Sirians be too strong for me, succour me, and if the children of Ammon be too good for thee, I will help thee." 
1Chronicles19:13 Pluck up thine heart and let us play the men for our people's sake, and for the cities of our God, and the LORD to do what seemeth him best." 
1Chronicles19:14 And Joab and the people that were with him drew nigh to the Sirians, to fight, and they fled before him." 
1Chronicles19:15 And when the children of Ammon saw that the Sirians were fled, they ran away likewise, from Abisai his brother and gat them into the city. And Joab came to Jerusalem." 
1Chronicles19:16 And when the Sirians saw that they were put to the worse of Israel, they sent messengers and fetched out the Sirians that are beyond the river with Sophach the Captain of the host of Hadadezer before them." 
1Chronicles19:17 Which when it was told David, he gathered all Israel, and went over Jordan and went and set upon them. And David put himself in array against the Sirians, and they fought with him." 
1Chronicles19:18 But the Sirians fled before Israel, and David destroyed of the Sirians seven thousand chariots, and slew forty thousand footmen and killed Sophach the Captain of the host." 
1Chronicles19:19 And when the servants of Hadadezer saw that they were put to the worse of Israel, they made peace with David and served him. Neither would the Sirians help the children of Ammon any more." 
1Chronicles20:1 And the year following about the time that kings go out a warfare, Joab carried out the army of the host and destroyed the country of the children of Ammon, and went and besieged Rabah and destroyed it: But David bode at Jerusalem while Joab smote Rabah and destroyed it:" 
1Chronicles20:2 And David took the crown of their king off his head, and found therein the weight of a talent of gold, and there were precious stones in it, and it was set on David's head. And he brought out also the spoil of the city which was exceeding much." 
1Chronicles20:3 And he brought out the people that were in it and tamed them with saws and harrows of iron and with brakes, and so dealt David with all the cities of the children of Ammon. And then David and all the people came again to Jerusalem." 
1Chronicles20:4 After that there arose war at Gazer with the Philistines. At which time Sobocai the Husathite slew Saphai that was of the children of Raphaim, and they were subdued." 
1Chronicles20:5 And there was battle again with the Philistines, and Elhanan the son of Jair slew Lahemi the brother of Goliath the Gethite, whose spear was like the beam of a weaver." 
1Chronicles20:6 And there chanced yet again war at Geth, where was a man of a size with twenty four fingers and toes, six on every hand and six on every foot, and was the son of Haraphah and defied Israel." 
1Chronicles20:7 And Jehonathan the son of Samaa David's brother slew him. 
1Chronicles20:8 These were born of Haraphah at Geth and were overthrown by the hand of David and of his servants. 
1Chronicles21:1 And Satan stood up against Israel, and persuaded David to number Israel." 
1Chronicles21:2 And David said to Joab and the rulers of the people, go ye and number Israel from Bersabe to Dan, and bring it to me that I may know the number of them." 
1Chronicles21:3 And Joab answered: the LORD make thy people an hundredth times so many more as they be. But my lord king, are they not all my lord's servants? Why then doth my lord require this thing? why should my lord be a cause of trespass to Israel?" 
1Chronicles21:4 Neverthelater the king's words prevailed against Joab. And Joab took his way and went thorowout all Israel, and came to Jerusalem" 
1Chronicles21:5 and gave the number of the count of the people unto David. And all Israel were in number a thousand, thousand and an hundredth thousand men that drew sword: and Juda was four hundredth and seventy thousand men that drew sword." 
1Chronicles21:6 But the Levites and Benjamin he counted not among them. For the king's deed seemed abominable to Joab. 
1Chronicles21:7 And the Lord(God) was displeased with this thing, and smote Israel." 
1Chronicles21:8 The said David to God: I have sinned exceedingly in doing this thing. Nevertheless yet do away the wickedness of thy servant, for I have done above measure foolishly." 
1Chronicles21:9 And the LORD spake unto Gad David's seer of visions saying: 
1Chronicles21:10 go and tell David saying: Thus sayeth the LORD: I give thee the choice of three things: choose thee one of them, that I may do unto thee." 
1Chronicles21:11 And Gad came to David and said to him: Thus sayeth the LORD. 
1Chronicles21:12 Choose thee either three years' famishment, or three months to be consumed of thine adversaries, the sword of thine enemies overtaking thee: or else the sword of the LORD three days and pestilence in the land, the Angel of the LORD destroying thorowout all the coasts of Israel. And now advise thyself what word I shall bring again unto him that sent me." 
1Chronicles21:13 And David said to Gad, I am in an exceeding strait. But let me fall in to the hands of the LORD, for passing great is his mercy, and let me not fall unto the hands of men." 
1Chronicles21:14 And the LORD sent pestilence upon all Israel, so that there were overthrown of Israel seventy thousand men." 
1Chronicles21:15 And God sent the Angel to Jerusalem to destroy it. And as he was about to destroy, the LORD beheld and had compassion on the wretchedness, and said to the Angel that destroyed, it is enough, now cease thine hand. And the Angel of the LORD stood by the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite." 
1Chronicles21:16 And David lifted up his eyes and saw the Angel of the LORD stand between the earth and heaven with a drawn sword in his hand stretched out toward Jerusalem. Then fell David and the elders of Israel clothed in sack upon their faces. 
1Chronicles21:17 And David said unto God: Is it not I that commanded to number the people? And I am he that have sinned and done evil in deed. But what have these sheep done? Let thine hand therefore O LORD God be on me and on my father's house, and not on thy people to destroy them." 
1Chronicles21:18 And the Angel commanded Gad to say to David, that David should go and rear up an altar unto the LORD, in the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite." 
1Chronicles21:19 And David went at the saying of Gad which spake in the name of the LORD. 
1Chronicles21:20 And Ornan turned about and saw the Angel, and his four sons with him, and hid themselves: for Oman was threshing wheat." 
1Chronicles21:21 And David came to Ornan. And when Ornan looked and saw David, he went out of the threshing floor and bowed himself to David with his face to the ground." 
1Chronicles21:22 And David said to Ornan: give me the place of the threshing floor, that I may build an altar therein unto the LORD. Let me have it for as much money as it is worth, that the plague may cease from the people." 
1Chronicles21:23 And Ornan said to David: take it to thee, and let my lord king do that seemeth good in his eyes. See, I give thee oxen for a burntsacrifice, and the threshing sleds for wood, and wheat for meatoffering: I give it all." 
1Chronicles21:24 But king David said to Ornan: not so, but I will buy it for as much money as it is worth. I will not take that which is thine, for the LORD, and offer burntoffering without cost." 
1Chronicles21:25 And so David gave to Ornan for the place sicles of gold six hundredth by weight. 
1Chronicles21:26 And David built there an altar unto the LORD and offered burntofferings and peaceofferings and called unto the LORD and he heard him from heaven in fire upon the altar of burntoffering. 
1Chronicles21:27 And the LORD commanded the Angel to put up his sword again to the sheath of it. 
1Chronicles21:28 At that time when David saw, that the LORD had heard him in the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite: he used to offer there." 
1Chronicles21:29 For the tabernacle of the LORD which Moses made in the wilderness, and the altar of burntoffering were that season in the hill at Gabaon." 
1Chronicles21:30 And David could not go before it to seek God, because he was afraid of the sword of the Angel of the LORD." 
1Chronicles22:1 And David said: this is the house of the LORD God, and this the burntoffering altar for Israel." 
1Chronicles22:2 And David commanded to gather the strangers that were in the land of Israel, and set hewers to hew stone, to build the house of God." 
1Chronicles22:3 And David prepared plenty of iron for nails to the doors of the gates and to join with all, and abundance of brass without weight," 
1Chronicles22:4 and of Cedar trees without number. For the Zidons and they of Tire brought much Cedar wood to David. 
1Chronicles22:5 For David thus thought, Salomon my son is young and tender, and the house that is to be built for the LORD, must exceed in greatness, that it may be spoken of and praised in all lands. I will therefore make ordinance for it. And so David prepared abundance before his death." 
1Chronicles22:6 And he called Salomon his son and charged him to build an house for the LORD God of Israel: 
1Chronicles22:7 And David said to Salomon: my son, I had in mine heart to build an house unto the name of the LORD my God." 
1Chronicles22:8 But the word of the LORD came to me saying: thou hast shed much blood, and hast made many battles. Thou shalt not build an house for my name, for thou hast shed much blood to the earth in my sight." 
1Chronicles22:9 Behold a son shall be born thee which shall be a man of rest, for I will give him rest from all his enemies round about. And his name shall be Salomon: for I will send rest and peace upon Israel in his days." 
1Chronicles22:10 He shall build an house for my name, and he shall be my son, and I will be his father, and will stablish the seat of his kingdom upon Israel for ever." 
1Chronicles22:11 Now my son, the LORD be with thee, that thou mayest prosper and build the house of the LORD thy God, as he hath said of thee." 
1Chronicles22:12 And the LORD give thee wisdom and understanding, and make thee governor of Israel, and to keep the law of the LORD thy God." 
1Chronicles22:13 For then thou shalt prosper: If thou shalt be diligent to do the ordinances and laws which the LORD charged Moses with, to deliver to Israel. Pluck up thine heart and be strong, dread not nor be discouraged." 
1Chronicles22:14 Behold in mine adversity I have prepared for the house of the LORD an hundredth thousand talents of gold and a thousand, thousand talents of silver, and as for brass and iron it can not be numbered, it is so much. And I have prepared timber and stone, and shalt provide more thereto." 
1Chronicles22:15 Moreover thou hast workmen enough and masons and carpenters to work in stone and timber, and all manner of wise men for whatsoever work it be." 
1Chronicles22:16 And of gold, silver, brass and iron there is no number. Up therefore and set upon it, and the LORD shall be with thee." 
1Chronicles22:17 And David commanded all the lords of Israel, to help Salomon his son saying:" 
1Chronicles22:18 Is not the LORD your God with you? and hath he not given you rest on every side? for he hath given the inhabiters of the land into mine hand and the land is subdued before the LORD and before his people. 
1Chronicles22:19 Now therefore set your hearts and your souls, to seek the LORD your God. And up and build ye the temple of the LORD God, to bring the Ark of the covenant of the LORD, and the holy vessels of God into the house so built for the name of the LORD." 
1Chronicles23:1 And when David was old and stricken in year, he made Salomon his son king over Israel." 
1Chronicles23:2 And then he gathered together all the lords of Israel with the Priests and the Levites. 
1Chronicles23:3 And the Levites were numbered from thirty year and above, and the tale of them in men poll by poll was thirty eight thousand." 
1Chronicles23:4 Of which twenty four thousand were set to further the work of the house of the LORD. And six thousand were officers and judges. 
1Chronicles23:5 Four thousand were porters and four thousand praised the LORD with such instruments as David had made to praise withal. 
1Chronicles23:6 And David put an order among the children of Levi: Gerson, Cahath and Merari:" 
1Chronicles23:7 of the Gersonites was Laadan and Semei. 
1Chronicles23:8 The sons of Laadan: the chief was Jehiel and then Zethan and Joel, three." 
1Chronicles23:8 The sons of Laadan: the chief was Jehiel and then Zethan and Joel, three." 
1Chronicles23:9 The sons of Semei, Salomith, Haziel, and Haran, three. These were the ancient heads of Laadan." 
1Chronicles23:10 And the sons of Semei were also Jahath, Zinah, Jaus and Bariah: these four were the sons of Semei." 
1Chronicles23:11 And Jahath was the chief, Zinah the second. But Jaus and Bariah had not many sons, and therefore they were reckoned for one ancient household." 
1Chronicles23:12 The sons of Cahath: Amram, Izahar, Hebron and Uziel, four." 
1Chronicles23:13 The sons of Amram: Aaron and Moses. And Aaron was appointed to consecrate in the place most holy, with his sons for ever: and to burn the offerings before the LORD, and to minister, and to bless in his name for ever." 
1Chronicles23:14 And as for Moses the man of God, his children were named with the tribe of Levi." 
1Chronicles23:15 The sons of Moses: Gersom and Eliezer. 
1Chronicles23:16 And of the sons of Gersom: Sabael was the chief. 
1Chronicles23:17 The sons of Eliezer: Rohabiah the chief. And Eliezer had no other sons: But the sons of Rohabiah were very many. 
1Chronicles23:18 And of the sons of Izabar: Salomi, the chief." 
1Chronicles23:19 The sons of Hebron: Jeriahu the first. Amariah the second, Jahaziel the third and Jecmaam the fourth." 
1Chronicles23:20 The sons of Uziel: Micah the first and Jesiah the second. 
1Chronicles23:20 The sons of Uziel: Micah the first and Jesiah the second. 
1Chronicles23:21 The sons of Merari: Mahali: and Musi, the sons of Mahali: Eleazar and Cis." 
1Chronicles23:22 And Eleazar died and had no sons, but daughters only, which their brethren the sons of Cis took." 
1Chronicles23:23 The sons of Musi: Mahali, Eder, and Jerimuth, three." 
1Chronicles23:24 These are the children of Levi in the households of their fathers the ancient heads, viewed and numbered by name and poll by poll from twenty year and above, to work in the service of the house of the LORD." 
1Chronicles23:25 For David said: the LORD God of Israel hath given rest unto his people, and they shall dwell in Jerusalem for ever." 
1Chronicles23:26 Moreover the Levites should now no more bear the tabernacle and all the vessels that pertain to the service thereof. 
1Chronicles23:27 But at the last commandment of David, the Levites were numbered from twenty year and above," 
1Chronicles23:28 to wait at the hands of the sons of Aaron in the service of the house of the LORD, and to have the oversight of the courts and of the store houses, and of the purifying of all holy things, and of the work of the things that served for the house of God," 
1Chronicles23:29 as the shewbread, the fine flour, the meatofferings, the flawnes of sweet bread, and of the things baken in the frying pan, or broiled on the gridiron, and on all manner measures and size" 
1Chronicles23:30 and to wait every morning to thank and praise the LORD, and likewise at evening." 
1Chronicles23:31 And to wait on every offering of burntsacrifices unto the LORD, the Sabbaths and first days of the months, and other feastful days by number and custom, was their office ever before the LORD." 
1Chronicles23:32 And that they should wait on the tabernacle of witness and on the holy place, and on the sons of Aaron their brethren, in the service of the house of the LORD." 
1Chronicles24:1 The division of the sons of Aaron: Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar." 
1Chronicles24:2 But Nadab and Abihu died before their father and had no children. And Eleazar and Ithamar were the Priests. 
1Chronicles24:3 And David ordered them on this manner: Zadock of the sons of Eleazar and Ahimelek of the sons of Ithamar to be in office by course. 
1Chronicles24:4 And there were more mighty heads found of the sons of Eleazar than of the sons of Ithamar. And they divided them into sixteen heads of ancient households of the sons of Eleazar, and eight of the sons of Ithamar." 
1Chronicles24:5 And they put them in order by lot, one with another that there should be rulers in the sanctuary and lords before God as well of the sons of Ithamar as of the sons of Eleazar." 
1Chronicles24:6 And Semeiah the son of Nathaneel the Scribe of the Levites wrote them before the king and the lords, and before Zadock the priest and Ahimelek the son of Abiathar, and before the ancient heads of the priests and of the Levites: one principal household for Eleazar and one for Ithamar." 
1Chronicles24:7 The first lot fell to Jehoiarib. The second to Jedaiah. 
1Chronicles24:8 The third to Harim. The fourth to Seorim. 
1Chronicles24:9 The fifth to Melciah. The sixth to Miamin. 
1Chronicles24:10 The seventh to Hakes. The eighth to Abiah.
