Job1:21 and said: Naked came I out of my mother's womb, and naked shall I turn thither again. The LORD gave, and the LORD hath taken away, (The Greek and Origen add herunto: As it hath pleased the LORD so is it done.) now blessed be the name of the LORD." 
Job1:22 In all these things did Job not offend, nor murmured foolishly against God." 
Job2:1 It happened also upon a time, that when the servants of God came and stood before the LORD, Sathan (also) came among them, and stood before him." 
Job2:2 And the LORD said unto Sathan: From whence comest thou? Sathan answered and said: I have gone about the land, and walked thorow it." 
Job2:3 Then said the LORD unto Sathan: Hast thou not considered my servant Job, how that he is an innocent and virtuous man such one as feareth God, and escheweth evil, and that there is none like him in the land? But thou movedst me against him, to punish him: yet is it in vain, for he continueth still in his godlyness." 
Job2:4 Sathan answered the LORD, and said: Skin for skin? yea a man will give all that ever he hath, for his life." 
Job2:5 But lay thine hand upon him, touch him once upon the bone and flesh, and (I hold) he shall curse thee to thy face." 
Job2:6 Then said the LORD unto Sathan: lo, there hast thou him in thy power, but spare his life." 
Job2:7 So went Sathan forth from the LORD, and smote Job with marvelous sore boils,(byles) from the sole of the foot unto his crown." 
Job2:8 So that he sat upon the ground in the ashes, and scraped off the filth(atter) of his sores with a potsherd." 
Job2:9 Then said his wife unto him: Dost thou (yet) continue in thy perfectness? curse God, and die." 
Job2:10 But Job said unto her: Thou speakest like a foolish woman. Seeing we have received prosperity at the hand of God, wherefore should we not be content with adversity also? In all these things, did not Job sin with his lips." 
Job2:11 Now when Job's friends heard of all the trouble, that happened to him, there came three of them, every one from his own place: namely, Eliphas the Thamanite, Baldad the Suhite, and Zophar the Naamathite. For they were agreed together to come, to shew their compassion on him, and to comfort him." 
Job2:12 So when they lift up their eyes afar off, they knew him not. Then they cried, and wept: then every one of them rent his clothes, and sprinkled dust upon their heads in the air." 
Job2:13 They sat them down by him also upon the ground seven days and seven nights. Neither was there any of them that spake one word unto him, for they saw that his pain was very great." 
Job3:1 After this opened Job his mouth, and cursed his day," 
Job3:2 and said: lost be the day, wherein I was born:" 
Job3:3 and the night, in the which it was said: there is a man child conceived." 
Job3:4 The same day be turned to darkness, and not regarded of God from above, neither be shined upon with light:" 
Job3:5 but be covered with darkness, and the shadow of death. Let the dim cloud fall upon it, and let it be lapped in with sorrow." 
Job3:6 Let the dark storm overcome that(the) night, let it not be reckoned among the days of the year, nor counted in the months." 
Job3:7 Despised be that night, and discommended: let them that curse the day," 
Job3:8 even those that be ready to raise up mourning give it also their curse.(geue it their curse also, euen those that be ready to rayse vp Leuiathan.)" 
Job3:9 Let the stars be dim thorow darkness of it. Let it look for light, but let it see none, neither the raising up of the fair morning:" 
Job3:10 because it shut not up the womb that bare me, nor hide these sorrows from mine eyes." 
Job3:11 Alas, why died I not in the birth? Why did not I perish, as soon as I came out of my mother's womb?" 
Job3:12 Why set they me upon their knees? Why gave they me suck with their breasts? 
Job3:13 Then should I now have layen still, I should have slept, and been at rest:" 
Job3:14 like as the kings and lords of the earth, which build themselves special places:" 
Job3:15 As the princes that have great substance of gold, and their houses full of silver." 
Job3:16 O that I utterly had no being, or were as a thing born out of time (that is put aside) ether as young children, which never saw the light." 
Job3:17 There must the wicked cease from their tyranny, there such as are overlaboured be at rest:" 
Job3:18 there are those let out free, which have been in prison, so that they hear no more the voice of the oppressor:" 
Job3:19 There are small and great: the bondman, and he that is free from his master." 
Job3:20 Wherefore is the light given to him that is in misery? and life unto them, that have heavy hearts?" 
Job3:21 Which long for death, if it come not:" 
Job3:22 and search for it more than for treasure which also would be exceedingly glad, and rejoice if they found their grave.(Which long for death, and it commeth not: for if they might find their grave, they would be marvelous glad, as those that dig up treasure)" 
Job3:23 That should be joy to the man whose way is hid, which God keepeth back from him." 
Job3:24 For my sighs come(This is the cause, that I sigh) before I eat, and my roarings fall out like flowing water.(a water floude)" 
Job3:25 For the thing that I feared, is come upon me: and the thing that I was afraid of, is happened unto me." 
Job3:26 Was I not happy? Had I not quietness? Was I not in rest? And now cometh such misery upon me. 
Job4:1 Then answered Eliphaz the Temanite and said unto him: 
Job4:2 If we begin to commune(common) with thee, peradventure thou wilt be discontent, but who can withhold himself from speaking?" 
Job4:3 Behold, thou hast been a teacher of many, and hast comforted the weary hands." 
Job4:4 Thy words have set up those that were fallen, thou hast refreshed the weak knees." 
Job4:5 But now that the plague is come upon thee, thou shrinkest away: now that it hath touched thy self, thou art faint hearted." 
Job4:6 Is not this thy fear,(Where is now thy fear of God) thy steadfastness, thy patience, and the perfectness of thy ways?(life)" 
Job4:7 Consider (I pray thee) who ever perished being an innocent? Or, when were the godly destroyed?" 
Job4:8 As I have seen them that plow vanity(wickedness) and sow malice(mischief) reap the same. 
Job4:9 With the blast of God did they perish, and with the breath of his anger consumed they away.(For when God bloweth upon them, they perish, and are destroyed thorow the blast of his wrath.)" 
Job4:10 The roaring of the lion, the voice(crying) of the lioness, and the teeth of the lions'(lyos) whelps are broken." 
Job4:11 The (greate) lion perisheth, for lack of(because he can get no) prey and the lion's whelps are scattered abroad." 
Job4:12 And unto me was the word hid, and mine ear hath received a little thereof.(There is spoken unto me a thing in councell, which hath given a terrible sound in mine eare)" 
Job4:13 In the fantasies and thoughts of the visions of the night, when sleep cometh on men:(with a vision in the night, when men are fallen a slepe.)" 
Job4:14 Fear came upon me and dread and made all my bones to shake. 
Job4:15 And when the wind passed by before my presence it made the hairs of my flesh stand(stode) up. 
Job4:16 He stood there and I knew not his face, an image there was before me and there was stillness,(Then stode there one before me, whose face I knewe not: an ymage there was, and the wether was still) so that I heard this voice." 
Job4:17 Shall man be more just than God? Or shall man be purer than his maker?(May a man be justified before God? May there any man be judged to be clean, by reason of his own works?)" 
Job4:18 Behold there is no trust to his servants, and in his angels hath he found frowardness.(Behold, he hath found unfaithfulness among his own servants, and proud disobedience among his angels.)" 
Job4:19 How much more in them(then shall they) that dwell in houses of clay, whose foundation is but earth: which shall be consumed by the moth?(be moth eaten)" 
Job4:20 They shall be smitten(destroyed) from morning unto the evening: yea they shall perish everlastingly, and no man think thereon.(or ever they be aware:)" 
Job4:21 Is not their dignity taken away with them, they shall die and not in wisdom.(and be taken away so clene, that none of them shall remain, but be dead, or ever they be aware of it.)" 
Job5:1 Name me one else, if thou can find any: yea(yee) look about thee, upon any of the holy men." 
Job5:2 As for the foolish man, displeasure killeth him and anger slayeth the ignorant." 
Job5:3 I have seen my self, when the foolish was deep rooted, that his beauty was suddenly(sodely) destroyed," 
Job5:4 that his children were without prosperity or health: that they were slain in the door, and no man to deliver them:" 
Job5:5 that his harvest was eaten up of the hungry: that the weaponed man had spoiled it, and that the thirsty had drunk up his riches." 
Job5:6 Is it not the earth that bringeth forth travail, neither cometh sorrow out of the ground:" 
Job5:7 but it is man, that is born unto misery, like as the bird for to flee." 
Job5:8 But now will I speak of(off) the Lord,(LORDE) and talk of God:" 
Job5:9 which doth things, that are unsearchable, and marvelous without number:" 
Job5:10 Which giveth rain upon the earth, and poureth water upon all things:" 
Job5:11 which setteth up them of low degree, and sendeth prosperity, to those that are in heaviness:" 
Job5:12 Which destroyeth the devices of the subtle, so that they are not able to perform the things that they take in hand:" 
Job5:13 which compass the wise in their own craftiness, and overthroweth the counsel of the wicked?" 
Job5:14 In so much that they run in to darkness by fair day, and grope about them at the noon day, like as in the night." 
Job5:15 And so he delivereth the poor from the sword, from their mouth, and from the hand of the cruel," 
Job5:16 that the poor may have hope, and that the mouth of the oppressor may be stopped." 
Job5:17 Behold, happy is the man, whom God punisheth: therefore, despise not thou the chastening of the Almighty." 
Job5:18 For though he make a wound, he giveth a medicine again: though he smite, his hand maketh whole again." 
Job5:19 He delivereth thee out of six troubles, so that in the seventh there can no harm touch thee." 
Job5:20 In the middest of hunger he saveth thee from death: and when it is war, from the power of the sword." 
Job5:21 He shall keep thee from the perilous tongue so that when trouble cometh, thou shalt not need to fear." 
Job5:22 In destruction and dearth thou shalt be merry, and shalt not be afraid for the beasts of the earth:" 
Job5:23 But the castles in the land shall be confederate with thee, and the beasts of the field, shall give thee peace." 
Job5:24 Yea thou shalt know,(see) that thy dwelling place shall be in rest: thou shalt behold thy substance, and be no more punished for sin." 
Job5:25 Thou shalt see also, that thy seed shall increase, and that thy posterity shall be as the grass upon the earth." 
Job5:26 Thou shalt come to thy grave in a fair age, like as the corn sheaves are brought in to the barn in due season." 
Job5:27 Lo, this is the matter, as we ourselves have proved by experience. Therefore now that thou hearest it, take better heed to thyself." 
Job6:1 Job answered, and said: Oh that my misery were weighed, and my punishment laid in the balances:" 
Job6:2 For then should it be heavier, than the sand of the sea." 
Job6:3 This is the cause, that my words are so sorrowful." 
Job6:4 For the arrows of the allmighty are in me, whose indignation hath drunk up my spirit, and the terrible fears of God fight against me." 
Job6:5 Doth the wild ass roar when he hath grass? Or crieth the ox, when he hath fodder enough?" 
Job6:6 That which is unsavory, shalt it be eaten without salt, or is there any taste in the white of an egg?(Maye a thynge be eaten unseasoned, or without salt? What taist hath ye whyte within the yoke an egg?)" 
Job6:7 The things that some time I might not away withal, are now my meat for very sorrow." 
Job6:8 O that I might have my desire: O that God would grant me the thing, that I long for:" 
Job6:9 That he would begin and smite me: that he would let his hand go, and hew me down." 
Job6:10 Then should I have some comfort: yea I would desire him in my pain, that he should not spare, for I will not be against the words of the holy one." 
Job6:11 What power have I to endure? Or, what is mine end, that my soul might be patient?" 
Job6:12 Is my strength the strength of stones? Or is my flesh made of brass? 
Job6:13 Is it not so that there is in me no help: and that my substance is taken from me.(Am I able to help my self? Is not my strength gone from me,)" 
Job6:14 He that is in tribulation ought to be comforted of his neighbour: but the fear of the LorD is clean away:(like as if one withdrew a good deed from his friend, and forsook the fear of the Almighty God?)" 
Job6:15 Mine own brethren pass over by me as the water brook, that hastily runneth thorow the valleys." 
Job6:16 But they that fear the hoarfrost, the snow shall fall upon them." 
Job6:17 When their time cometh, they shall be destroyed and perish: and when they be set on fire, they shall be removed out of their place," 
Job6:18 for the paths that they go in, are crooked: they haste after vain things, and shall perish." 
Job6:19 Consider the paths of Theman, and the ways of Saba, wherein they have put their trust." 
Job6:20 Confounded are they, that put any confidence in them: For when they came to obtain the things that they looked for, they were brought to confusion." 
Job6:21 Even so are ye also come unto me: but now that ye see my misery, ye are afraid." 
Job6:22 Did I desire you, to come hither? Or, to give me any of your substance?" 
Job6:23 To deliver me from the enemy's hand, or to save me from the power of the mighty?" 
Job6:24 Teach me and I will hold my tongue: and if I do err, shew me wherein." 
Job6:25 Wherefore blame ye then the words, that are well and truly spoken?" 
Job6:26 which of you can reprove them? Saving only that ye are subtle to check men's sayings, and can speak many words in the wind." 
Job6:27 Ye fall upon the fatherless, and go about to overthrow your own friend." 
Job6:28 Wherefore look not only upon me, but upon yourselves: whether I lie, or no." 
Job6:29 Turn into your own selves (I pray you) be indifferent judges, and consider mine unguiltiness:" 
Job6:30 whether there be any unrighteousness in my tongue, or vain words in my mouth." 
Job7:1 Is not the life of man upon earth a very battle? Are not his days like the days of an hired servant? 
Job7:2 For like as a bond servant desireth the shadow, and as an hireling would fain have an end of his work:" 
Job7:3 Even so have I laboured whole months long (but in vain) and many a careful night have I told. 
Job7:4 When I laid me down to sleep, I said: O when shall I rise? Again, I longed sore for the night. Thus am I full of sorrow, till it be dark." 
Job7:5 My flesh is clothed with worms, filthiness and dust: my skin is withered, and crumpled together:" 
Job7:6 my days pass over more speedily, than a weaver can weave out his web: and are gone, or I am aware.(awarre)" 
Job7:7 O remember, that my life is but a wind, and that mine eye shall no more see the pleasures thereof," 
Job7:8 yea and that none other man's eye shall see me anymore. 
Job7:9 For if thou fasten thine eyes upon me, I come to naught like as a cloud is consumed and vanisheth away, even so he that goeth down to hell, cometh no more up," 
Job7:10 nor turneth again into his house, neither shall his place know him any more." 
Job7:11 Therefore I will not spare my mouth, but will speak in the trouble of my spirit, in that bitterness of my mind will I talk." 
Job7:12 Am I a sea, or a whalefish, that thou keepest me so in prison?" 
Job7:13 When I think: my bed shall comfort me. I shall have some refreshing by talking by myself upon my couch: 
Job7:14 Then troublest thou me with dreams, and makest me so afraid thorow visions," 
Job7:15 that my soul wisheth rather to be strangled,(hanged) and my bones to be dead." 
Job7:16 I can see no remedy, I shall live no more: O spare me then, for my days are but vain." 
Job7:17 What is man, that thou hast him in such reputation, and setest so much by him?" 
Job7:18 Thou takest diligent care for him, and suddenly(sodely) doest thou try him." 
Job7:19 Why goest thou not from me, nor letest me alone, so long till I swallow down my spittle?" 
Job7:20 I have offended, what shall I do unto thee, O thou preserver of men? Why hast thou made me to stand in thy way, and am so heavy a burden unto myself?" 
Job7:21 Why doest thou not forgive my sin? Wherefore takest thou not away my wickedness. Behold, now must I sleep in the dust: And if thou seekest me tomorrow in the morning, I shall be gone." 
Job8:1 Then answered Baldad the Suhite, and said:" 
Job8:2 How long wilt thou talk of such things? how long shall thy mouth speak so proud words? 
Job8:3 Doth God pervert the thing that is lawful? Or, doth the Almighty destroy the thing that is right?" 
Job8:4 When thy sons sinned against him, did not he punish them for their wickedness?" 
Job8:5 If thou wouldest now resort unto God by times, and make thy humble prayer to the Almighty:" 
Job8:6 If thou wouldest live a pure and godly life: should he not wake up unto thee immediately, and give thee the beauty of righteousness again?" 
Job8:7 In so much, that wherein soever thou hadst little afore, thou shouldest now have great abundance." 
Job8:8 Enquire of them that have been before thee, search diligently among thy forefathers:" 
Job8:9 Namely, that we are but of yesterday, and consider not, that our days upon earth are but a very shadow." 
Job8:10 They shall shew thee, they shall tell thee, yea they will gladly confess the same." 
Job8:11 May a rush be green without moistness? may the grass grow without water? 
Job8:12 No: but (or ever it be shot forth, and or ever it be gathered) it withereth, before any other herb." 
Job8:13 Even so goeth it with all them, that forget God: and even thus also shall the hypocrite's hope come to naught." 
Job8:14 His confidence shall be destroyed, for he trusteth in a spider's web." 
Job8:15 He leaneth him upon his house, but he shall not stand: he holdeth him fast by it, yet shall he not endure." 
Job8:16 Often time a thing doth flourish, and men think that it may abide the Son shining:(Sonneshyne) it shooteth forth the branches in his garden," 
Job8:17 it taketh many roots, in so much that it is like a house of stones." 
Job8:18 But if it be taken out of his place, every man denyeth it, saying: I know thee not." 
Job8:19 Lo, thus is it with him, that rejoiceth in his own doings: and as for others, they grow out of the earth." 
Job8:20 Behold, God will not cast away a virtuous man, neither will he help the ungodly." 
Job8:21 Thy mouth shall be still with laughing, and thy lips with gladness." 
Job8:22 They that hate thee, shall be confounded, and the dwellings of the ungodly shall come to naught." 
Job9:1 Job answered, and said:" 
Job9:2 As for that, I know it is so of a truth, that a man compared unto God, can not be justified." 
Job9:3 If he will argue with him, he shall not be able to answer him unto one among a thousand." 
Job9:4 He is wise of heart, and mighty in strength. Who ever prospered that took part against him?" 
Job9:5 He translateth the mountains, or ever they be aware,(awarre) and overthroweth them in his wrath." 
Job9:6 He removeth the earth out of her place, that her pillars shake withal." 
Job9:7 He commandeth the Son, and it riseth not: he closeth up the stars, as it were under a signet." 
Job9:8 He himself alone spreadeth out the heavens, and goeth upon the waves of the sea." 
Job9:9 He maketh the vaines(wains) of heaven, the Orions, the seven stars and the secret places of the south." 
Job9:10 He doth great things, such as are unsearchable, yea and wonders without number." 
Job9:11 If he came by me, I might not look upon him: if he went his way, I should not perceive it." 
Job9:12 If he be hasty to take anything away, who will make him restore it again? Who will say unto him: what doest thou?" 
Job9:13 He is God, whose wrath no man may withstand:(with stode) but the proudest of all must stoop(stoup) under him." 
Job9:14 How should I then answer him? or what words should I find out against him? 
Job9:15 Yea though I be righteous, yet will I not give him one word again, but meekly submit myself to my judge." 
Job9:16 All be it that I call upon him, and he hear me, yet am I not sure, that he hath heard my voice:" 
Job9:17 he troubleth me so with the tempest, and woundeth me out of measure without a cause." 
Job9:18 He will not let my spirit be in rest, but filleth me with bitterness." 
Job9:19 If men will speak of strength, he is the strongest of all: if men will speak of righteousness, who dare be my record." 
Job9:20 If I will justify my self, mine own mouth shall condemn me: if I will put forth my self for a perfect man, he shall prove me a wicked doer:" 
Job9:21 For that I should be an innocent, my conscience knoweth it not, yea I my self am weary of my life." 
Job9:22 This one thing will I say: He destroyeth both the righteous and ungodly. 
Job9:23 And though he slay suddenly with the scourge, yet laugheth he at the punishment of the innocent." 
Job9:24 As for the world, he giveth it over in to the power of the wicked, such as the rivers be, whereof all lands are full. Is it not so? where is there any, but he is such one?" 
Job9:25 My days have been more swift than a runner: they are gone suddenly, and have seen no good thing." 
Job9:26 They are passed away, as the ships that be good under sail, and as the Aegle that hasteth to the prey." 
Job9:27 When I am purposed to forget my complainings, to change my countenance, and to comfort myself:" 
Job9:28 Then am I afraid of all my works, for I know, thou favourest not an evil doer." 
Job9:29 If I be then a wicked one, why have I laboured in vain?" 
Job9:30 Though I washed my self with snow water, and made mine hands never so clean," 
Job9:31 yet shouldest thou dip me in the mire, and mine own clothes should defile me." 
Job9:32 For he that I must give answer unto, and with whom I go to law, is not a man as I am." 
Job9:33 Neither is there any daysman to reprove both the parts, or to lay his hand betwixt us." 
Job9:34 Let him take his rod away from me, yea let him make me no more afraid of him," 
Job9:35 and then shall I answer him without any fear. For as long as I am in such fearfulness, I can make no answer: And why?" 
Job10:1 it grieveth my soul to live. Nevertheless, now will I put forth my words: I will speak out of the very heaviness of my soul," 
Job10:2 and will say unto God: O do not condemn me: but shew me the cause, wherefore thou judgest me on this manner." 
Job10:3 Thinkest thou it well done, to oppress me, to cast me off (being a work of thine hands) and to maintain the counsel of the ungodly?" 
Job10:4 Hast thou fleshly eyes then, or doest thou look as a man looketh?" 
Job10:5 Are thy days as the days of man, and thy years as man's years?" 
Job10:6 That thou makest such inquisition for my wickedness, and searchest out my sin?" 
Job10:7 Whereas (notwithstanding) thou knowest that I am no wicked person, and that there is no man able to deliver me out of thine hand." 
Job10:8 Thy hands have made me, and fashioned me altogether round about, wilt thou then destroy me suddenly?" 
Job10:9 O remember (I beseek thee) how that thou madest me of the mould of the earth, and shalt bring me to earth again." 
Job10:10 Hast thou not milked me, as it were milk: and turned me to curdles like cheese?" 
Job10:11 Thou hast covered me with skin and flesh, and joined me together with bones and sinews." 
Job10:12 Thou hast granted me life, and done me good: and the diligent heed that thou tookest upon me, hath preserved my spirit." 
Job10:13 Though thou hidest these things in thine heart, yet am I sure, that thou rememberest them all." 
Job10:14 Wherefore didst thou keep me, when I sinned, and hast not cleansed me from mine offense?" 
Job10:15 If I do wickedly, woe is me therefore: If I be righteous, yet dare I not lift up my head: so full am I of confusion, and see mine own misery." 
Job10:16 Thou huntest me out (being in heaviness) as it were a Lion, and troublest me out of measure." 
Job10:17 Thou bringest fresh witnesses against me, thy wrath increasest thou upon me, very many are the plagues that I am in." 
Job10:18 Wherefore hast thou brought me out of my mother's womb? O that I had perished, and that no eye had seen me." 
Job10:19 If they had carried me to my grave, as soon as I was born, then should I be now, as though I had never been." 
Job10:20 Shall not my short life come so soon to an end? O hold thee from me, let me alone, that I may ease myself a little" 
Job10:21 afore I go thither, from whence I shall not turn again. Namely, to that land of darkness and shadow of death:" 
Job10:22 yea into that dark cloudy land and deadly shadow, where as is no order, but terrible fear as in the darkness." 
Job11:1 Then answered Zophar the Naamathite, and said:" 
Job11:2 Should not he that maketh many words, be answered? Should he that bableth much, be commended therein?" 
Job11:3 Should men give ear unto thee only? Thou wilt laugh other men to scorn, and shall nobody mock thee again?" 
Job11:4 Wilt thou say unto God: The thing that I take in hand is perfect, and I am clean in thy sight?" 
Job11:5 O that God would speak, and open his lips against thee," 
Job11:6 that he might shew thee (out of his secret wisdom) how many fold his law is: then shouldest thou know, that God had forgotten thee, because of thy sins." 
Job11:7 Wilt thou find out God with thy seeking? Wilt thou attain to the perfectness of the Almighty? 
Job11:8 He is higher than heaven, what wilt thou do? Deeper than hell, how wilt thou then know him?" 
Job11:9 His length exceedeth the length of the earth, and his breadth the breadth of the sea." 
Job11:10 Though he turn all things upside down, close them in, or thrust them together, who dare check him therefore?" 
Job11:11 For it is he that knoweth the vanity of men: he seeth their wickedness also, should he not then consider it?" 
Job11:12 A vain body exalteth himself, and the son of man is like a wild ass's foal." 
Job11:13 If thou hadst now a right heart, and liftest up thine hands toward him:" 
Job11:14 if thou wouldest put away the wickedness which thou hast in hand, so that no ungodliness dwelt in thy house:" 
Job11:15 Then mightest thou lift up thy face without shame, then shouldest thou be sure, and have no need to fear." 
Job11:16 Then shouldest thou forget thy misery, and think no more upon it, than upon the waters that run by." 
Job11:17 Then should thy life be as clear as the noon day, and spring forth as the morning." 
Job11:18 Then mightest thou have comforth, in the hope that thou hast: and sleep quietly, when thou art buried." 
Job11:19 Then shouldest thou take thy rest, and no man to make thee afraid, yea many one should set much by thee." 
Job11:20 As for the eyes of the ungodly, they shall be consumed, and not escape: their hope shall be misery and sorrow of mind." 
Job12:1 So Job answered, and said:" 
Job12:2 Then (no doubt) ye are the men alone, and wisdom shall perish with you." 
Job12:3 But I have understanding as well as ye, and am no less than ye. Yea who knoweth not these things?" 
Job12:4 Thus he that calleth upon God, and whom God heareth, is mocked of his neighbour: the godly and innocent man is laughed to scorn." 
Job12:5 Godliness is a light despised in the hearts of the rich, and is set for them to stumble upon." 
Job12:6 The houses of robbers are in wealth and prosperity, and they that maliciously meddle against God, dwell without care: yea God giveth all things richly with his hand." 
Job12:7 Ask the cattle, and they shall inform thee: the fowls of the air, and they shall tell thee:" 
Job12:8 Speak to the earth, and it shall shew thee: Or to the fishes of the sea, and they shall certify thee." 
Job12:9 What is he, but he knoweth that the hand of the LORD made all these?" 
Job12:10 In whose hand is the soul of every living thing, and the breath of all men." 
Job12:11 Have not the ears pleasure in hearing, and the mouth in tasting the thing that it eateth?" 
Job12:12 Among old persons there is wisdom, and among the aged is understanding." 
Job12:13 Yea with God is wisdom and strength, it is he that hath counsel and foreknowledge." 
Job12:14 If he break down a thing, who can set it up again? If he shut a thing, who will open it?" 
Job12:15 Behold, if he withhold the waters, they dry up: If he let them go, they destroy the earth." 
Job12:16 With him is strength and wisdom: he knoweth both the deceiver and him that is deceived. 
Job12:17 He carrieth away the wise men, as it were a spoil, and bringeth the judges out of their wits." 
Job12:18 He lowseth the girdle of kings, and girdleth their loins with a bond." 
Job12:19 He leadeth away the Priests in to captivity, and turneth the mighty up side down." 
Job12:20 He taketh the verity from out of the mouth, and disappointeth the aged of their wisdom." 
Job12:21 He poureth out confusion upon princes, and comforteth them that have been oppressed." 
Job12:22 Look what lieth hid in darkness, he declareth it openly: and the very shadow of death bringeth he to light." 
Job12:23 He both increaseth the people and destroyeth them: He maketh them to multiply, and driveth them away." 
Job12:24 He changeth the heart of the princes and kings of the earth, and disapointeth them: so that they go wandering out of the way," 
Job12:25 and grope in the dark without light, staggering to and fro like drunken men." 
Job13:1 Lo, all this have I seen with mine eye, heard with mine ear, and understand it." 
Job13:2 Look what ye know, that same do I know also, neither am I inferior unto you." 
Job13:3 Nevertheless I am purposed to talk with the almighty, and my desire is to commune(common) with God." 
Job13:4 As for you, ye are workmasters of lies, and unprofitable Physicians altogether." 
Job13:5 Would God ye kept your tongue, that ye might be taken for wise men." 
Job13:6 Therefore hear my words, and ponder the sentence of my lips." 
Job13:7 Will ye make an answer for God with lies, and maintain him with deceit?" 
Job13:8 Will ye accept the person of God, and entreat for him?" 
Job13:9 Shall that help you, when he calleth you to reckoning. Think ye to beguile him, as a man is beguiled?" 
Job13:10 He shall punish you, and reprove you,(Punysh you shall he and reprove you) if ye do secretly accept any person." 
Job13:11 Shall he not make you afraid, when he sheweth himself? Shall not his terrible face fall upon you?" 
Job13:12 your remembrance shall be like unto the dust, and your pride shall be turned to clay." 
Job13:13 Hold your tongues now, and let me speak, for there is something come into my mind." 
Job13:14 Wherefore do I bear my flesh in my teeth, and my soul in my hands?" 
Job13:15 Lo, there is neither comfort nor hope for me, if he will slay me. But if I shew and reprove mine own ways in his sight," 
Job13:16 he is even the same that maketh me whole: And why? There may no hypocrite come before him. 
Job13:17 Hear my words, and ponder my sayings with your ears." 
Job13:18 Behold, now have I prepared my judgement, and know that I shall be found righteous.(Beholde, though sentence were geuen vpon me, I am sure to be known for unguilty.)" 
Job13:19 What is he that will go to law with me? For if I hold my tongue, I shall die." 
Job13:20 Nevertheless grant me two things, and then will I not hide my self from thee." 
Job13:21 Withdraw thine hand from me, and let not the fearful dread of thee make me afraid." 
Job13:22 And then send for me to the law, that, I may answer for myself: or else, let me speak, and give thou the answer." 
Job13:23 How great are my misdeeds and sins? Let me know my transgressions and offenses. 
Job13:24 Wherefore hidest thou thy face, and holdest me for thine enemy?" 
Job13:25 Wilt thou be so cruel and extreme unto a flying leaf, and follow upon dry stubble?" 
Job13:26 That thou layest so sharply to my charge, and will utterly undo me, for the sins of my youth?" 
Job13:27 Thou hast put my foot in the stocks: thou lookest narrowly unto all my paths, and markest the steps of my feet:" 
Job13:28 where as I (notwithstanding) must consume like as a foul carrion, and as a cloth that is moth eaten." 
Job14:1 Man that is born of a woman, hath but a short time to live, and is full of divers miseries." 
Job14:2 He cometh up and falleth away like a flower. He flyeth as it were a shadow, and never continueth in one state." 
Job14:3 Thinkest thou it now well done, to open thine eyes upon such one, and to bring me before thee in judgement?" 
Job14:4 Who can make it clean, that cometh from an unclean thing? Nobody." 
Job14:5 The days of man are short, the number of his months are known only to thee. Thou hast appointed him his bounds, he can not go beyond them." 
Job14:6 Go from him, that he may rest a little: until his day come, which he looketh for, like as an hireling doth." 
Job14:7 If a tree be cut down, there is some hope yet, that it will sprout and shoot forth the branches again;" 
Job14:8 For though a root be waxen old and dead in the ground," 
Job14:9 yet when the stock geteth the scent of water, it will bud, and bring forth bows, like as when it was first planted." 
Job14:10 But as for man, when he is dead, perished and consumed away, what becometh of him?" 
Job14:11 The floods when they be dried up, and the rivers when they be empty, are filled again thorow the flowing waters of the sea:" 
Job14:12 but when man sleepeth, he riseth not again, until the heaven perish: he shall not wake up nor rise out of his sleep." 
Job14:13 O that thou wouldest keep me, and hide me in the hell, until thy wrath were stilled: and to appoint me a time, wherein thou mightest remember me." 
Job14:14 May a dead man live again? All the days of this my pilgrimage am I looking, when my changing shall come." 
Job14:15 If thou wouldest but call me, I should obey thee: only despise not the work of thine own hands." 
Job14:16 For thou hast numbered all my goings, yet be not thou too extreme upon my sins." 
Job14:17 Thou hast sealed up mine offenses, as it were in a bag: but be merciful unto my wickedness." 
Job14:18 The mountains fall away at the last, the rocks are removed out of their place," 
Job14:19 the waters pierce thorow the very stones by little and little, the floods wash away the gravel and the earth: Even so destroyest thou the hope of man in like manner." 
Job14:20 Thou prevailest against him, so that he passeth away: thou changes his estate, and puttest him from thee." 
Job14:21 Whether his children come to worship or no, he can not tell: And if they be men of low degree, he knoweth not." 
Job14:22 While he liveth, his flesh must have travail: And while the soul is in him, he must be in sorrow." 
Job15:1 Then answered Eliphaz the Themanite, and said:" 
Job15:2 Should a wise man answer as the science of the wind, and fill his belly with the wind of the east?(Should a wise man give such an answer (as it were one that spake in the wind) and fill his stomach with anger?)" 
Job15:3 Thou reprovest with words, that are nothing worth:(wroth) and speakest the things, which can do no good." 
Job15:4 As for shame, thou hast set it aside, else wouldest thou not make so many words before God:" 
Job15:5 but thy wickedness teacheth thy mouth, and so thou hast chosen thee a crafty tongue." 
Job15:6 Thine own mouth condemneth thee, and not I: yea thine own lips shape the answer." 
Job15:7 Art thou the first man, that ever was born? Or, wast thou made before the hills?" 
Job15:8 hast thou heard the secret counsel(councell) of God, that all wisdom is too little for thee?" 
Job15:9 What knowest thou, that we know not? What understandest thou, but we can the same?" 
Job15:10 With us are old and aged men, yea such as have lived longer than thy forefathers." 
Job15:11 Thinkest thou it a small thing of the consolations of God? and are they with the living word.(Dost thou no more regard the comfort of God? but thy wicked words wil not suffer thee.) 
Job15:12 Why doth thine heart make thee so proud? Why standest thou so greatly in thine own conceit? Where unto look thine eyes," 
Job15:13 that thy mind is so puffed up against God and lettest such words go out of thy mouth? 
Job15:14 What is man, that he should be clean? what hath he (which is born of a woman) whereby he might be known to be righteous?" 
Job15:15 Behold there is no trust to his saints:(he hath founde unfaithfulnesse amoge his owne sanctes) yea the very heavens are not clean in his sight. 
Job15:16 How much more then an abominable and vile man, which drinketh wickedness like water?" 
Job15:17 I will tell thee, hear me: I will shew thee a thing, that I know:" 
Job15:18 which wise men have told, and hath not been hid from their fathers:" 
Job15:19 unto whom only the land was given, that no stranger should come among them." 
Job15:20 The ungodly despaireth all the days of his life, and the number of a tyrant's years is unknown." 
Job15:21 A fearful sound is ever in his ears, and when it is peace yet feareth he destruction:" 
Job15:22 He believeth never to be delivered out of darkness, the sword is alway before his eyes." 
Job15:23 When he goeth forth to get his living, he thinketh plainly, that the day of darkness is at hand." 
Job15:24 Sorrow and carefulness make him afraid, and compass him round about, like as it where a king with his host ready to the battle." 
Job15:25 For he hath stretched out his hand against God, and armed himself against the Almighty." 
Job15:26 He runneth proudly upon him, and with a stiffneck fighteth he against him:" 
Job15:27 where as he covereth his face with fatness, and maketh his body well liking." 
Job15:28 Therefore shall his dwelling be in desolate cities, and in houses which no man inhabits, but are become heaps of stones." 
Job15:29 He shall not be rich, neither shall his substance continue, nor increase upon earth." 
Job15:30 He shall never come out of darkness, the flame shall dry up his branches, with the blast of the mouth of God shall he be taken away." 
Job15:31 He will never apply himself to faithfulness and truth, so sore is he deceived with vanity." 
Job15:32 He shall perish, afore his time be worn out, and his hand shall not be green." 
Job15:33 He shall be plucked off as an untimely grape from the vine, and shall let his flower fall, as the olive doth." 
Job15:34 For the congregation of hypocrites is unfruitful, and the fire shall consume the houses of such, as are greedy to receive gifts." 
Job15:35 He conceiveth travail, he beareth mischief, and his body bringeth forth deceit." 
Job16:1 Job answered, and said:" 
Job16:2 I have oft times heard such things. Miserable givers of comfort are ye, all the sort of you." 
Job16:3 Shall not thy vain words come yet to an end? Or, hast thou yet any more to say?" 
Job16:4 I could speak as ye do also. But would God, that your soul were in my soul's stead: then should I heap up words against you, and shake my head at you." 
Job16:5 I should comfort you with my mouth, and release your pain with the talking of my lips." 
Job16:6 But what shall I do? For all my words, my sorrow will not cease: and though I hold my tongue, yet will it not depart from me." 
Job16:7 And now that I am full of pain, and all that I have destroyed" 
Job16:8 (whereof my wrinkles bear witness) there standeth up a dissembler to make me answer with lies to my face. 
Job16:9 He is angry at me, he hateth me, and gnasheth upon me with his teeth. Mine enemy scowleth(skouleth) upon me with his eyes." 
Job16:10 They have opened their mouths wide upon me, and smitten me upon the cheek despitefully, they have eased themselves thorow mine adversity." 
Job16:11 God hath given me over to the ungodly, and delivered me in to the hands of the wicked." 
Job16:12 I was some time in wealth, but suddenly hath he brought me to naught. He hath taken me by the neck, he hath rent me, and set me, as it were a mark for him to shoot at." 
Job16:13 He hath compassed me round about with his darts, he hath wounded my loins, and not spared. My bowels hath he poured upon the ground." 
Job16:14 He hath given me one wound upon another, and is fallen upon me like a giant." 
Job16:15 I have sowed a sack cloth upon my skin, and lie with my strength in the dust." 
Job16:16 My face is swollen with weeping, and mine eyes are waxen dim." 
Job16:17 Howbeit there is no wickedness in my hands, and my prayer is clean." 
Job16:18 O earth, cover not my blood, and let my crying find no room." 
Job16:19 For lo, my witness is in heaven, and he that knoweth me, is above in the height.(heyth)" 
Job16:20 My friends laugh me to scorn, but mine eye poureth out tears unto God." 
Job16:21 Though a body might plead with God, as one man doth with another," 
Job16:22 yet the number of my years are come, and I must go the way, from whence I shall not turn again." 
Job17:1 My breath faileth, my days are shortened. I am hard at death's door." 
Job17:2 I have deceived no man, yet must mine eye continue in heaviness." 
Job17:3 O deliver me and set me by thee: who shall then be able to thrust my hands together? 
Job17:4 Thou hast withholden their hearts from understanding, therefore shall they not be set up on high.(an hye)" 
Job17:5 He promiseth his friends part of his good, but his own children spend it." 
Job17:6 He hath made me as it were a byword of the common people. I am his jesting stock among them. 
Job17:7 My countenance is heavy for very anger, and the members of my body are become like a shadow." 
Job17:8 Virtuous men therefore shall well consider this, and the innocent shall take part against the hypocrite." 
Job17:9 The righteous will keep his way, and he that hath clean hands, will ever be stronger and stronger." 
Job17:10 As for you, turn you, and get you hence, for I can not see one wise man among you." 
Job17:11 My days are past, my thoughts are vanished away, which have vexed my heart," 
Job17:12 changing the night in to day, and the light into darkness." 
Job17:13 Though I tarry never so much, yet the grave is my house, and I must make my bed in the dark." 
Job17:14 I call corruption my father, and the worms call I my mother and my sister." 
Job17:15 What helpeth then my long tarrying? Or, who will fulfill the thing, that I look for?" 
Job17:16 All that I have, shall go down into the pit, and lie with me in the dust." 
Job18:1 Then answered Baldad the Suhite, and said:" 
Job18:2 when will ye make an end of your words? Mark well, and consider, we will speak also." 
Job18:3 Wherefore are we counted as beasts, and reputed so vile in your sight?" 
Job18:4 Why destroyest thou thy self with anger? Shall the earth be forsaken, or the stones removed out of their place because of thee?" 
Job18:5 Shall not the light of the ungodly be put out? Yea the flame of his fire shall not burn. 
Job18:6 The light shall be dark in his dwelling, and his candle shall be put out with him." 
Job18:7 His presumptious goings shall be kept in, and his own counsel shall cast him down." 
Job18:8 For his feet shall be taken in the net, and he shall walk in the snare." 
Job18:9 His foot shall be holden in the gilder, and the thirsty shall catch him." 
Job18:10 The snare is laid for him in the ground, and a pitfall in the way." 
Job18:11 Fearfulness shall make him afraid on every side, that he shall not know, where to get out." 
Job18:12 Hunger shall be his substance, and misfortune shall hang upon him." 
Job18:13 He shall eat the strength of his skin, the firstborn of death shall eat his members.(He shall eate his owne skynne, yee his owne armes shall he devoure, beynge a firstborne of death)" 
Job18:14 All his comfort and hope shall be rooted out of his dwelling, very fearfulness shall bring him unto the king." 
Job18:15 Other men shall dwell in his house (which now is none of his) and brimstone shall be scattered upon his habitation. 
Job18:16 His roots shall be dried up beneath, and above shall his harvest be cut down." 
Job18:17 His rememberance shall perish from the earth, and his name shall not be praised in the streets:" 
Job18:18 he shall be driven from the light into darkness, and cast clean out of the world." 
Job18:19 He shall neither have children nor kinfolks among his people, no nor any posterity in his country:" 
Job18:20 young and old shall be astonied at his death. 
Job18:21 Such are now the dwellings of the wicked, and this is the place of him that knoweth not God." 
Job19:1 Job answered, and said:" 
Job19:2 How long will ye vex my mind, and trouble me with words?" 
Job19:3 Lo, ten times have ye reproved me: are ye not ashamed, for to laugh me to scorn?" 
Job19:4 If I go wrong, I go wrong to myself." 
Job19:5 But if ye will enhance yourselves against me, and accuse me to be a wicked person because of the shame that is come upon me:" 
Job19:6 know this then, that it is God, which hath handled me so violently, and hath compassed me about with his scourges." 
Job19:7 Behold, though I cry, yet violence is done unto me, I can not be heard: Though I complain, there is none to give sentence with me." 
Job19:8 He hath hedged up my path, I can not get away, he hath set darkness in my gate." 
Job19:9 He hath spoiled me of mine honour, and taken the crown away from my head." 
Job19:10 He hath destroyed me on every side, and I am undone: My hope hath he taken away from me, as it were a tree plucked up by the root." 
Job19:11 His wrath is kindled against me, he taketh me, as though I were his enemy." 
Job19:12 His men of war came together, which made their way over me, and besieged my dwelling round about." 
Job19:13 He hath put my brethren far away from me, and such as were of mine acquaintance, are become strangers unto me." 
Job19:14 Mine own kinfolk have forsaken me, and my friends have put me out of remembrance." 
Job19:15 The servants and maids of mine own house take me for a stranger, and I am become as an alien(aleaunt) in their sight." 
Job19:16 When I call upon my servant, he giveth me no answer: no though I pray him with my mouth." 
Job19:17 Mine own wife may not abide my breath, I am faint(fayne) to speak fair unto the children of mine own body." 
Job19:18 Yea the very desert fools despise me, and when I am gone from them, they speak evil upon me." 
Job19:19 All such as were my most familiars, abhor me: And they whom I loved best, are turned against me." 
Job19:20 My bone hangeth to my skin, and my(the) flesh is away, only there is left me the skin about my teeth." 
Job19:21 Have pity upon me, have pity upon me (O ye my friends) for the hand of the Lord(God) hath touched me." 
Job19:22 Why do ye persecute me as God, and are not satisfied of my flesh?(Seynge God persecuteth me, wil ye vexe me also? Have ye not yet ynough of the trouble of my flesh?)" 
Job19:23 O that my words were written, O that they were put in a book:" 
Job19:24 would God they were graven with an iron pen in lead or in stone. 
Job19:25 For I am sure, that my redeemer liveth, and that I shall rise out of the earth in the latter day:" 
Job19:26 that I shall be clothed again with this skin, and see God in my flesh." 
Job19:27 Yea I my self shall behold him, not with other but with these same eyes. My reins are consumed within me," 
Job19:28 when ye say: Why do not we persecute him? We have found an occasion against him. 
Job19:29 But beware of the sword, for the sword will be avenged of wickedness, and be sure, that there is a judgement." 
Job20:1 Then answered Sophar the Naamathite, and said:" 
Job20:2 For the same cause do my thoughts compel me to answer. And why? My mind is tossed here and there. 
Job20:3 I have sufficiently heard thy checking and reproof, therefore am I purposed to make answer after mine understanding." 
Job20:4 Knowest thou not this, namely: that from the beginning (ever since the creation of man upon earth)" 
Job20:5 the praise of the ungodly hath been short, and that the joy of the Hypocrites continued but the twinkling of an eye?" 
Job20:6 Though he be magnified up to the heaven, so that his head reacheth into the clouds:" 
Job20:7 yet he perish at the last like dung: In so much that they which have seen him, say: Where is he?" 
Job20:8 He vanisheth as a dream, so that he can no more be found, and passeth away as a vision in the night." 
Job20:9 So that the eye which saw him before, getteth now no sight of him, and his place knoweth him no more." 
Job20:10 His children go a begging, their hands bring them to sorrow and heaviness." 
Job20:11 From his youth his bones are full of vice, which shall lie down with him in the earth." 
Job20:12 When wickedness is sweet in his mouth, he hideth it under his tongue." 
Job20:13 That he favoureth, that will he not forsake, but keepeth it close in his throat." 
Job20:14 The meat that he eateth, shall be turned to the poison of serpents, within his body." 
Job20:15 The riches that he devoureth, shall he perbreak again, for God shall draw them out of his belly" 
Job20:16 he shall suck the serpent's head,(The serpentes heade shall sucke him,) and the adder's tongue shall slay him:" 
Job20:17 so that he shall no more see the rivers, and brooks of honey and butter:" 
Job20:18 But labour shall he, and yet have nothing to eat. Great travail shall he make for riches, but he shall not enjoy them." 
Job20:19 And why? he hath oppressed the poor, and not helped them: houses hath he spoiled, and not builded them." 
Job20:20 His belly could never be filled, therefore shall he perish in his covetousness." 
Job20:21 He devoured so greedily, that he left nothing behind, therefore his goods shall not prosper." 
Job20:22 Though he had plenteousness of everything, yet was he poor, and therefore he is but a wretch on every side." 
Job20:23 For though the wicked have never so much to fill his belly, yet God shall send his wrath upon him, and cause his battle to rain over him:" 
Job20:24 so that if he flee the iron weapons, he shall be shot with the steel bow." 
Job20:25 The arrow shall be taken forth, and go out at his back, and a glistering sword thorow the gall of him, fear shall come upon him." 
Job20:26 There shall no darkness be able to hide him. And unkindled fire shall consume him, and look what remaineth in his house, it shall be destroyed." 
Job20:27 The heaven shall declare his wickedness, and the earth shall take part against him." 
Job20:28 The substance that he hath in his house, shall be taken away and perish, in the day of the Lord's(LORDES) wrath." 
Job20:29 This is the portion that the wicked shall have of God, and the heritage that he may look for of the Lord.(LORDE)" 
Job21:1 Job answered, and said:" 
Job21:2 O hear my words, and amend yourselves." 
Job21:3 Suffer me a little, that I may speak also, and then laugh my words to scorn, if ye will." 
Job21:4 Is it with a man, that I make this disputation? Which if it were so, should not my spirit be then in sore trouble?" 
Job21:5 Mark me well, be abashed, and lay your hand upon your mouth." 
Job21:6 For when I ponder and consider this, I am afraid, and my flesh is smitten with fear." 
Job21:7 Wherefore do wicked men live in health and prosperity, come to their old age, and increase in riches?" 
Job21:8 Their children's children live in their sight, and their generation before their eyes." 
Job21:9 Their houses are safe from all fear, for the rod of God doth not smite them." 
Job21:10 Their bullock gendereth, and that not out of time: their cow calveth, and is not unfruitful." 
Job21:11 They send forth their children by flocks, and their sons lead the dance." 
Job21:12 They bear with them taberetts and harps, and have instruments of music at their pleasure." 
Job21:13 They spend their days in wealthiness: but suddenly they go down to hell. 
Job21:14 They say unto God: go from us, we desire not the knowledge of thy ways." 
Job21:15 What manner of fellow is the Almighty, that we should serve him? What profit should we have, to submit our selves unto him?" 
Job21:16 Lo, there is utterly no goodness in them, therefore will not I have to do with the counsel of the ungodly." 
Job21:17 How oft shall the candle of the wicked be put out? How oft cometh their destruction upon them? Oh what sorrow shall God give them for their part in his wrath. 
Job21:18 Yea they shall be even as chaff before the wind, and as dust that the storm carrieth away." 
Job21:19 And though God save their children from such sorrow, yet will he so reward themselves, that they shall know it." 
Job21:20 Their own destruction and misery shall they see with their eyes, and drink of the fearful wrath of the Almighty." 
Job21:21 For what careth he, what be come of his household after his death? Whose months pass away swifter than an arrow." 
Job21:22 In as much then as God hath the highest(hyest) power of all, who can teach him any knowledge?" 
Job21:23 One dieth now when he is mighty and at his best, rich, and in prosperity:" 
Job21:24 even when his bowels are at the fattest, and his bones full of merry." 
Job21:25 An other dieth in sorrow and heaviness, and never had good days." 
Job21:26 Now sleep they both alike in the earth, and the worms cover them." 
Job21:27 But I know what ye think, yea and what ye imagine against me unrighteously." 
Job21:28 For ye say: Where is the prince's palace? where is the dwelling of the ungodly: 
Job21:29 Ask any man that goeth by the way, and (if ye will not regard their tokens and deeds) he shall tell you," 
Job21:30 that the wicked is kept unto the day of destruction, and that the ungodly shall be brought forth in the day of wrath." 
Job21:31 Who dare reprove him for his ways to his face? Who rewardeth him for the ungraciousness that he doth? 
Job21:32 Yet shall he be brought to his grave, and watch among the heap of the dead." 
Job21:33 Then shall he be fain to be buried among the stones by the brook side. All men must follow him, and there are innumerable gone before him." 
Job21:34 O how vain is the comfort that ye give me? Are not your answers clean contrary to right and truth? 
Job22:1 So Eliphas the Themanite gave answer, and said:" 
Job22:2 May a man be compared unto God in wisdom, though he seem to himself, for to be like him?" 
Job22:3 What pleasure hath God in that thou art righteous? Or what doth it profit him, that thy ways are perfect?" 
Job22:4 Is he afraid to reprove thee, and to step forth with thee in to judgement?" 
Job22:5 Cometh not this for that(the) great wickedness, and for thine ungracious deeds which are innumerable?" 
Job22:6 Thou hast taken the pledge from thy brethren for naught, and robbed the naked of their clothing:" 
Job22:7 To such as were weary, hast thou given no water to drink, thou hast withdrawn bread from the hungry:" 
Job22:8 Should such one then as useth violence, wrong and oppression (doing all things of partiality, and having respect of persons) dwell in the land?" 
Job22:9 Thou hast sent widows away empty and oppressed the poor fatherless. 
Job22:10 Therefore art thou compassed about with snares on every side, and suddenly vexed with fear." 
Job22:11 Shouldest thou then see no darkness? Should not the water flood run over thee? 
Job22:12 Now because that God is higher than the heavens, and because thou seest the stars are so high," 
Job22:13 wilt thou therefore say: Tush, how should God know? Doth his dominion reach beyond the clouds?" 
Job22:14 Tush, the clouds cover him that he may not see, for he dwelleth in heaven." 
Job22:15 Well, thou wilt keep the old way, that all wicked men have gone:" 
Job22:16 both old and young, whose foundation is a running water," 
Job22:17 which sayeth unto God: go from us, and after this manner: Tush, what will the Almighty do unto us?" 
Job22:18 where as he (notwithstanding) filleth their houses with all good. Which meaning of the ungodly be far from me. 
Job22:19 For with joy shall the godly, and with gladness shall the innocent see," 
Job22:20 that their increase shall be hewn down, and their posterity consumed with (the) fire." 
Job22:21 Therefore reconcile thee unto God, and be content, so shall all things prosper with thee rightwell." 
Job22:22 Receive the law at his mouth, and lay up his words in thine heart." 
Job22:23 For if thou wilt turn to the Almighty, thou shalt stand fast, and all unrighteousness shall be far from thy dwelling:" 
Job22:24 He shall give thee an harvest, which in plenty and abundance shall exceed the dust of the earth, and the gold of Ophir like river stones." 
Job22:25 Yea the Almighty his own self shall be thine harvest, and the heap of thy money." 
Job22:26 Then shalt thou have thy delight(delyte) in the Almighty, and lift up thy face unto God." 
Job22:27 Then shalt thou make thy prayer unto him, and he shall hear thee, and thou shalt keep thy promises." 
Job22:28 Then, look what thou takest in hand, he shall make it to prosper with thee, and the light shall shine in thy ways." 
Job22:29 For who so humbleth himself, him shall he set up: and who so looketh meekly, shall be healed." 
Job22:30 If thou be innocent, he shall save thee: and thorow the unguiltyness of thine hands shalt thou be delivered." 
Job23:1 Job answered, and said:" 
Job23:2 My saying is yet this day in bitterness, and my hand heavy among my groanings." 
Job23:3 O that I might see him and find him: O that I might come before his seat," 
Job23:4 to plead my cause before him, and to fill my mouth with arguments:" 
Job23:5 That I might know, what answer he would give me: and that I might understand, what he would say unto me." 
Job23:6 Will he plead against me with his great power and strength, or will he lean himself utterly upon me?" 
Job23:7 Oh no, let him not do so with me. But let him give me like power to go to law, then am I sure to win my matter." 
Job23:8 For though I go before, I find him not: If I come behind, I can get no knowledge of him:" 
Job23:9 If I go on the left side to ponder his works, I can not attain unto them. Again, if I go on the right side, he hideth himself, that I can not see him." 
Job23:10 But as for my way, he knoweth it: and tryeth me as the gold in the fire." 
Job23:11 Nevertheless my feet keep his path, his high street have I holden, and not gone out of it." 
Job23:12 I have not forsaken the commandment of his lips, but look what he charged me with his mouth, that have I shut up in my heart." 
Job23:13 It is he him self alone, who will turn him back? He doth as him listeth, and bringeth to pass what he will." 
Job23:14 He rewardeth me in to my bosom, and many other things more doth he, as he may by his power." 
Job23:15 This is the cause, that I shrink at his presence, so that when I consider him, I am afraid of him." 
Job23:16 For in so much as he is God, he maketh my heart soft: and seeing that he is Almighty, he putteth me in fear." 
Job23:17 Thus can not I get out of darkness, the cloud hath so covered my face." 
Job24:1 Considering then that there is no time hid from the Almighty, how happeneth it, that they which know him, will not regard his days?" 
Job24:2 For some men there be, that remove other men's landmarks: that rob them of their cattle, and keep the same for their own:" 
Job24:3 that drive away the ass of the fatherless: that take the widow's ox for a pledge: 
Job24:4 that thrust the poor out of the way, and oppress the simple of the world together." 
Job24:5 Behold, the wild asses in the desert go by times, as their manner is, to spoil: Yea the very wilderness ministereth food for their children." 
Job24:6 They reap the corn field that is not their own: and gather the grapes out of his vineyard, whom they have oppressed by violence." 
Job24:7 They are the cause that so many men are naked and bare, having no clothes to cover them and keep them from cold:" 
Job24:8 So that when the showers in the mountains have rained upon them, and they be all wet, they have none other succour, but to keep them among the rocks." 
Job24:9 They spoil the sucking fatherless children, and put the poor in prison:" 
Job24:10 In so much that they let them go naked without clothing, and yet the hungry bear the sheaves." 
Job24:11 The poor are fain to labour in their oil mills, yea and to tread in their wine presses, and yet to suffer thirst." 
Job24:12 The whole city crieth unto the Lord(LORDE) with sighing, the souls of the slain make their complaint: But God destroyeth them not for all this," 
Job24:13 where as they (notwithstanding) are rebellious and disobedient enemies: which seek not his light and way, nor turn again in to his path." 
Job24:14 Timely in the morning do they arise, to murder the simple and poor, and in the night they go a stealing." 
Job24:15 The eye of the ungodly is like the advouterer, that waiteth for the darkness, and sayeth thus in him self: Tush, there shall no man see me, and so he disguiseth his face." 
Job24:16 In the night season they search the houses, and hide them selves in the day time, but will not know the light." 
Job24:17 For as soon as the day breaketh, the shadow of death cometh upon them, and they go in horrible darkness." 
Job24:18 The ungodly is very swift: O that his portion also upon earth were swifter than the running water, which suffereth not the shipman to behold the fair and pleasant vineyards." 
Job24:19 O that they (for the wickedness which they have done) were drawn to the hell, sooner than snow melteth at the heat." 
Job24:20 O that all compassion upon them were forgotten: that their dainties were worms, that they were clean put out of remembrance, and utterly hewn down like an unfruitful tree." 
Job24:21 For they maintain the barren, and make them that they can not bear, and unto widows they do no good." 
Job24:22 They pluck down the mighty with their power, and when they themselves are gotten up, they are never without fear, as long as they live." 
Job24:23 And though they might be safe, yet they will not receive it, for their eyes look upon their own ways." 
Job24:24 They are exalted for a little, but shortly are they gone, brought to extreme poverty, and taken out of the way: yea and utterly plucked off, as the ears of corn." 
Job24:25 Is it not so? Who will then reprove me as a liar, and say that my words are nothing worth." 
Job25:1 Then answered Baldad the Suhite, and said:" 
Job25:2 Power and fear is with him above, that maketh peace, sitting in his highness," 
Job25:3 whose men of war(warre) are innumerable, and whose light ariseth over all." 
Job25:4 But how may a man compared unto God be justified? Or, how can he be clean, that is born of a woman?" 
Job25:5 Behold, the Moon shineth nothing in comparison to him, and the stars are unclean in his sight." 
Job25:6 How much more than man, that is but corruption: and the son of man, which is but a worm?" 
Job26:1 Job answered, and said:" 
Job26:2 O how helpest thou the weak? what comfort givest thou unto him that hath no strength? 
Job26:3 Where is the counsel that thou shouldest give him, which hath no wisdom? Wilt thou so shew thine excellent righteousness?" 
Job26:4 Before whom hast thou spoken those words? Who made the breath to come out of the mouth: 
Job26:5 The giants and worthies that are slain, and lie under the world with their companions:" 
Job26:6 yea and all they which dwell beneath in the hell are not hid from him, and the very destruction it self can not be kept out of his sight." 
Job26:7 He stretcheth out the north over the empty, and hangeth the earth upon nothing." 
Job26:8 He bindeth the water in his clouds, that they fall not down together." 
Job26:9 He holdeth back his stole, that it can not be seen, and spreadeth his clouds before it." 
Job26:10 He hath compassed the waters with certain bounds, until the day and night come to an end." 
Job26:11 The very pillars of heaven tremble and quake at his reproof. 
Job26:12 He filleth the sea with his power, and thorow his wisdom hath he set forth the world." 
Job26:13 With his spirit hath he garnished the heavens, and with his hand hath he wounded the rebellious serpent." 
Job26:14 This is now a short sum of his doings. But who is able sufficiently to rehearse his works? Who can perceive and understand the thunder of his power. 
Job27:1 And Job(Iob also) proceeded and went(wete) forth in his communication, saying:" 
Job27:2 As truly as God liveth (which hath taken away my power from me) and the Almighty, that hath vexed my mind:" 
Job27:3 My lips shall talk of no vanity, and my tongue shall speak no deceit," 
Job27:4 while my breath is in me, and as long as the wind (that God hath given me) is in my nostrils." 
Job27:5 God forbid, that I should grant your cause to be right. As for me, until mine end come will I never go from my innocency." 
Job27:6 My righteous dealing keep I fast, which I will not forsake: my heart shall not reprove me of my days.(My rightuous dealynge wil I kepe fast, and not forsake it: For my conscience reproueth me not in all my conuersacion.)" 
Job27:7 Therefore mine enemy shall be found as the ungodly, and he that taketh part against me, as the unrighteous." 
Job27:8 What hope hath the hypocrite, though he have great good, and though God give him riches after his heart's desire?" 
Job27:9 Doth God hear him the sooner, when he crieth unto him in his necessity?" 
Job27:10 Hath he such pleasure and delight in the Almighty, that he dare alway call upon God?" 
Job27:11 I will teach you in the name of God? and the thing that I have of the Almighty, will I not keep from you." 
Job27:12 Behold, ye stand in your own conceit, as though ye knew all things. Wherefore then do ye go about with such vain words," 
Job27:13 saying: This is that portion that the wicked shall have of God, and the heritage that Tyrants shall receive of the Almighty." 
Job27:14 If he get many children, they shall perish with the sword, and his posterity shall have scarceness of bread." 
Job27:15 Look whom he leaveth behind him, they shall die and be buried, and no man shall have pity of his widows." 
Job27:16 Though he have as much money as the dust of the earth, and raiment as ready as the clay," 
Job27:17 he may well prepare it: but the godly shall put it upon him, and the innocent shall deal out the money." 
Job27:18 His house shall endure as the moth, and as a booth that the watchman maketh." 
Job27:19 When the rich man dieth, he carrieth nothing with him: he is gone in the twinkling of an eye." 
Job27:20 Destruction taketh hold upon him as a water flood, and the tempest stealeth him away in the night season." 
Job27:21 A vehement wind carrieth him hence, and departeth: a storm plucketh him out of his place." 
Job27:22 It rusheth in upon him, and spareth him not, he may not escape from the power thereof." 
Job27:23 Then clap men their hands at him, yea and jest of him, when they look upon his place." 
Job28:1 There are places were silver is molten, and where gold is tried:" 
Job28:2 where iron is digged out of the ground, and stones resolved to metal." 
Job28:3 The darkness shall once come to an end, he can seek out the ground of all things: the stones, the dark, and the horrible shadow." 
Job28:4 With the river of water parteth he a sunder the strange people, that knoweth no good neighbourhead: such as are rude, unmannerly and boisterous:" 
Job28:5 He bringeth food out of the earth, and that which is under, consumeth he with fire." 
Job28:6 There is found a place, whose stones are clean Sapphires, and where the clots of the earth are gold." 
Job28:7 There is a way also that the birds know not, that no vulture's eye hath seen:" 
Job28:8 wherein the proud and high minded walk not, and where no lion cometh." 
Job28:9 There putteth he his hand upon the stony rocks, and overthroweth the mountains." 
Job28:10 Rivers flow out of the rocks, and look what is pleasant, his eye seeth it." 
Job28:11 Out of drops bringeth he great floods together, and the thing that is hid bringeth he to light." 
Job28:12 How cometh a man then by wisdom? Where is the place that men find understanding? 
Job28:13 Verily no man can tell how worthy a thing she is, neither is she found in the land of the living." 
Job28:14 The deep sayeth: she is not in me. The sea sayeth: she is not with me. 
Job28:15 She can not be gotten for the most fine gold, neither may the price of her be bought with any money." 
Job28:16 No wedges of gold of Ophir, no precious Onyx stones, no Sapphire may be compared unto her." 
Job28:17 No, neither gold nor Crystal, neither sweet odours nor golden plate." 
Job28:18 There is nothing so worthy, or so excellent, as once to be named unto her: for perfect wisdom goeth far beyond them all." 
Job28:19 The Topaz that cometh out of Inde, may in no wise be likened unto her: yea no manner of apparel how pleasant and fair soever it be." 
Job28:20 From where then cometh wisdom? and where is the place of understanding? 
Job28:21 She is hid from the eyes of all men, yea and from the fouls of the air." 
Job28:22 Destruction and death say: We have heard tell of her with our ears. 
Job28:23 But God seeth her way, and knoweth her place." 
Job28:24 For he holdeth the ends of the world and looketh upon all that is under the heaven. 
Job28:25 When he weighed the winds, and measured the waters:" 
Job28:26 When he set the rain in order, and gave the mighty floods a law." 
Job28:27 Then did he see her, then declared he her, prepared her and knew her." 
Job28:28 And unto man he said: Behold, to fear the Lord,(LORDE) is wisdom: and to forsake evil, is understanding." 
Job29:1 So Job proceeded and went forth in his communication, saying:" 
Job29:2 Oh that I were as I was in the months by past, and in the days when God preserved me:" 
Job29:3 When his light shined upon my head: when I went after the same light and shining even thorow the darkness. 
Job29:4 As it stood with me, when I was wealthy and had ynough: when God prospered my house:" 
Job29:5 When the Almighty was with me: when my household folks stood about me: 
Job29:6 When my ways ran over with butter, and when the stony rocks gave me rivers of oil:" 
Job29:7 When I went thorow the city unto the gate, and when they set me a chair in the street:" 
Job29:8 When the young men (as soon as they saw me) hid themselves, and when the aged arose, and stood up unto me:" 
Job29:9 when the princes left of their talking, and laid their hand to their mouth:" 
Job29:10 when the mighty kept still their voice, and when their tongues cleaved to the roof of their mouths." 
Job29:11 When all they that heard me, called me happy: and when all they that saw me, wished me good." 
Job29:12 For I delivered the poor when he cried, and the fatherless that wanted help." 
Job29:13 He that should have been lost, gave me a good word, and the widow's heart praised me." 
Job29:14 And why? I put upon me righteousness, which covered me as a garment, and equity was my crown." 
Job29:15 I was an eye unto the blind, and a foot to the lame," 
Job29:16 I was a father unto the poor, and when I knew not their cause, I sought it out diligently." 
Job29:17 I brake the chafes of the unrighteous, and plucked the spoil out of their teeth." 
Job29:18 Therefore, I thought verily, that I should have died in my nest: and that my days should have been as many as the sands of the sea." 
Job29:19 For my root was spread out by the water's side, and the dew lay upon my corn." 
Job29:20 My honor increased(encreased) more and more, and my bow was ever the stronger in my hand." 
Job29:21 Unto me men gave ear, me they regarded, and with silence they tarried for my counsel." 
Job29:22 If I had spoken, they would have it none other ways, my words were so well taken among them." 
Job29:23 They waited for me, as the earth doth for the rain: and gaped upon me, as the ground doth to receive the latter shower." 
Job29:24 When I laughed, they knew well it was not earnest: and this testimony of my countenance pleased them nothing at all." 
Job29:25 When I agreed unto their way, I was the chief, and sat as a king among his servants: Or as one that comforteth such as be in heaviness." 
Job30:1 But now they that are mine inferiors and younger than I, have me in derision: yea even they, whose fathers I would have thought scorn to have set with the dogs of my cattle." 
Job30:2 The power and strength of their hands might do me no good, and as for their age, it is spent and past away without any profit." 
Job30:3 For very misery and hunger, they went about in the wilderness like wretches and beggars," 
Job30:4 plucking up herbs from among the bushes, and the Junipers root was their meat." 
Job30:5 And when they were driven forth, men cried after them, as it had been after a thief." 
Job30:6 Their dwelling was beside foul brooks, yea in the caves and dens of the earth." 
Job30:7 Upon the dry heath went they about crying, and in the broom hills they gathered them together." 
Job30:8 They were children of fools and villains, which are dead away from the world." 
Job30:9 Now am I their song, and am become their jesting stock:" 
Job30:10 they abhor me, they flee far from me, and stain my face with spittle," 
Job30:11 for the Lord(LORDE) hath opened his quiver, he hath hit me and put a bridle in my mouth." 
Job30:12 Upon my right hand they rose together against me, they have hurt my feet, made a way to destroy me," 
Job30:13 and my path have they clean marred. It was so easy for them to do me harm, that they needed no man to help them." 
Job30:14 They fell upon me, as it had been the breaking in of waters, and came in by heaps to destroy me." 
Job30:15 Fearfulness is turned against me. Mine honour vanisheth away more swiftly than wind, and my prosperity departeth hence like as it were a cloud." 
Job30:16 Therefore is my mind poured full of heaviness, and the days of trouble have taken hold upon me." 
Job30:17 My bones are pierced thorow in the night season, and my sinews take no rest." 
Job30:18 With all their power have they changed my garment, and girded me therewith, as it were with a coat." 
Job30:19 I am even as it were clay, and am become like ashes and dust." 
Job30:20 When I cry unto thee, thou doest not hear me: and though I stand before thee, yet thou regardest me not." 
Job30:21 Thou art become mine enemy, and with thy violent hand thou takest part against me." 
Job30:22 In times past thou didst set me up on high,(an hye) as it were above the wind, but now hast thou given me a very sore fall." 
Job30:23 Sure I am, that thou wilt deliver me unto death: whereas a lodging is prepared for all men living." 
Job30:24 Now use not me to do violence unto them, that are destroyed already: but where hurt is done, there use they to help." 
Job30:25 Did not I weep in the time of trouble? Had not my soul compassion upon the poor? 
Job30:26 Yet nevertheless where as I looked for good, evil happened unto me: and where as I waited for light, there came darkness." 
Job30:27 My bowels seeth within me, and take no rest, for the days of my trouble are come upon me." 
Job30:28 Meekly and lowly came I in, yea and without any displeasure: I stood up in the congregation, and communed with them." 
Job30:29 But now I am a companion of dragons, and a fellow of Ostriches." 
Job30:30 My skin upon me is turned to black, and my bones are brent with heat:" 
Job30:31 my harp is turned to sorrow, and my pipe to weeping." 
Job31:1 I made a covenant with mine eyes, that I would not look upon a damsel." 
Job31:2 For how great a portion shall I have of God from above? and what inheritance from the almighty on high?(Almightie on hie) 
Job31:3 As for the ungodly and he that joineth himself to the company of wicked doers shall not destruction and misery come upon him? 
Job31:4 Doth not he see my ways, and tell all my goings." 
Job31:5 If I have cleaved unto vanity, or if my feet have run to deceive:" 
Job31:6 Let me be weighed in an even balance, that God may see my innocency." 
Job31:7 If so be that I have withdrawn my foot out of the right way, if my heart hath followed mine eyesight, if I have stained or defiled my hands:" 
Job31:8 O then is it reason that I sow, and another eat, yea that my generation and posterity be clean rooted out." 
Job31:9 If my heart hath lusted after my neighbour's wife, or if I have laid wait at his door." 
Job31:10 O then let my wife be another man's harlot and let other lie with her. 
Job31:11 For this is a wickedness and sin, that is worthy to be punished," 
Job31:12 yea a fire that utterly should consume, and root out all my substance." 
Job31:13 Did I ever think scorn to do right unto my servants and maidens, when they had any matter against me." 
Job31:14 But seeing that God will sit in judgement what shall I do? And for so much as he will needs visit me, what answer shall I give him?" 
Job31:15 He that fashioned me in my mother's womb, made he not him also? were we not both shapen alike in our mother's bodies?" 
Job31:16 When the poor desireth anything at me, have I denied it them? Have I caused the widow stand waiting for me in vain?" 
Job31:17 Have I eaten my portion alone, that the fatherless hath had no part with me?" 
Job31:18 For mercy grew up with me from my youth, and compassion from my mother's womb." 
Job31:19 Have I seen any man perish thorow nakedness and want of clothing? Or any poor man for lack of raiment," 
Job31:20 whose sides thanked me not, because he was warmed with the wool of my sheep?" 
Job31:21 Did I ever lift up my hand to hurt the fatherless? Yea in the gates where I saw myself to be in authority: 
Job31:22 then let mine arm fall from my shoulder, and mine arm holes be broken from the joints." 
Job31:23 For I have ever feared the vengeance and punishment of God, and knew very well, that I was not able to bear his burthen." 
Job31:24 Have I put my trust in gold? Or, have I said to the finest gold of all: thou art my confidence?" 
Job31:25 Have I rejoiced because my substance was great, and because my hand gat so much?" 
Job31:26 Did I ever greatly regard the rising of the son? Or, had I the going down of the moon in great reputation?" 
Job31:27 Hath my heart meddled privily with any deceit? Or, did I ever kiss mine own hand?" 
Job31:28 That were a wickedness worth to be punished, for then should I have denied the God that is above." 
Job31:29 Have I ever rejoiced at the hurt of mine enemy? Or was I ever glad, that any harm happened to him? Oh no." 
Job31:30 I never suffered my mouth to do such a sin, as to wish him evil." 
Job31:31 Yet they of mine own household say: who shall let us to have our belly full of his flesh? 
Job31:32 I have not suffered a stranger to lie without, but opened my doors unto him." 
Job31:33 Have I ever done any wicked deed where thorow I shamed myself before men: or any abomination, that I was fain to hide it?" 
Job31:34 For if I had feared any great multitude of people: or if I had been despised of the simple, Oh then should I have been afraid. Thus have I quietly spent my life, and not gone out at the door." 
Job31:35 O that I had one which would hear me. Lo, this is my cause. Let the almighty give me answer: and let him that is my contrary party, sue me with libel." 
Job31:36 Then shall I take it upon my shoulder, and as a garland about my head." 
Job31:37 I have told the number of my goings, and delivered them unto him as to a prince." 
Job31:38 But if cause be that my land cry against me, or that the furrows thereof make any complaint:" 
Job31:39 if I have eaten the fruits thereof unpaid for, yea if I have grieved any of the plowmen:" 
Job31:40 then let thistles grow in stead of my wheat, and thorns for my barley. (Here end the words of Iob.)"
