Job32:1 So these three men would strive no more with Job, because he held himself a righteous man." 
Job32:2 But Eliu the son of Barachell the Bussite of the kindred of Ram, was very sore displeased at Job, that he called him self just before God." 
Job32:3 And with Job's three friends he was angry also, because they had found no reasonable answer to overcome him." 
Job32:4 Now tarried Eliu till they had ended their communication with Job, for why? they were elder than he." 
Job32:5 So when Eliu the son of Barachell the Bussite saw, that these three men were not able to make Job answer, he was miscontent:" 
Job32:6 so that he gave answer himself, and said: Considering, that I am young, and ye be men of age, I was afraid, and durst not shew forth my mind," 
Job32:7 for I thought thus within myself: It becometh old men to speak, and the aged to teach wisdom." 
Job32:8 Every man (no doubt) hath a mind, but it is the inspiration of the Almighty that giveth understanding." 
Job32:9 All men are not wise, neither doth every aged man understand the thing that is lawful." 
Job32:10 Therefore will I speak also (in so far as I may be heard) and will shew you mine opinion. 
Job32:11 For when I had waited till ye made an end of your talking, and heard your wisdom, what arguments you made in your communication:" 
Job32:12 Yea when I had diligently pondered what ye said, I found not one of you that made any good argument against Job, or that directly could make answer unto his words:" 
Job32:13 Lest ye should praise yourselves, to have found out wisdom: because it is God that hath cast him out, and no man." 
Job32:14 Nevertheless, seeing he hath not spoken unto me, therefore will not I answer him as ye have done" 
Job32:15 (for they where so abashed that they could not make answer, nor speak one word)" 
Job32:16 but in so much as ye will not speak, standing still like dumb(domme) men, and making no answer:" 
Job32:17 I have a good hope for my part to shape him an answer, and to shew him my meaning." 
Job32:18 For I am full of words, and the spirit that is within me, compelleth me." 
Job32:19 Behold I am as the new wine, which hath no vent, and bursteth the new vessels in sunder." 
Job32:20 Therefore will I speak, that I may have vent: I will open my lips, and make answer." 
Job32:21 I will regard no manner of person, no man will I spare." 
Job32:22 For if I would go about to please men, I know not how soon my maker would take me away." 
Job33:1 Wherefore hear my words (O Job) and hearken to me all,(unto all) that I will say:" 
Job33:2 Behold, I will open my mouth, and my tongue shall speak out of my chaws." 
Job33:3 My heart shall order my words aright, and my lips shall talk of pure wisdom." 
Job33:4 The spirit of God hath made me, and the breath of the almighty hath given me my life." 
Job33:5 If thou canst, then give me answer: prepare thyself to stand(stode) before me face to face." 
Job33:6 Behold, before God am I, even as thou, for I am fashioned and made even of the same mould." 
Job33:7 Therefore, thou needest not be afraid of me, neither needest thou to fear, that my authority, shall be too heavy for thee." 
Job33:8 Now hast thou spoken in mine ears, and I have heard the voice of thy words:" 
Job33:9 I am clean without any fault, I am innocent, and there is no wickedness in me." 
Job33:10 But lo, he hath picked a quarrel against me, and taketh me for his enemy:" 
Job33:11 He hath put my foot in the stocks, and looketh narrowly unto all my paths." 
Job33:12 Behold, unto these unreasonable words of thine will I make answer. Should God be reproved of man?" 
Job33:13 Why dost thou then strive against him? because he giveth thee no accomptes of all his doings? 
Job33:14 For when God doth once command a thing, there should no man be curious, to search whether it be right." 
Job33:15 In dreams and visions of the night season (When slumbering cometh upon men, that they fall a sleep in their beds)" 
Job33:16 he roundeth(rowneth) them in the ears, he informeth them, and sheweth them plainly," 
Job33:17 that it is he, which withdraweth man from evil, delivereth him from pride," 
Job33:18 keepeth his soul from destruction, and his life from the sword." 
Job33:19 He chasteneth him with sickness, and bringeth him to his bed: he layeth sore punishment upon his bones," 
Job33:20 so that his life may away with no bread, and his soul abhorreth to eat any dainty meat." 
Job33:21 In so much that his body is clean consumed away, and his bones appear no more." 
Job33:22 His soul draweth on to destruction, and his life to death." 
Job33:23 Now if there be a messenger(angel) (one among a thousand) sent for to speak unto man, and to shew him the right way:" 
Job33:24 then the Lord(LORDE) is merciful unto him, and sayeth: He shall be delivered, that he fall not down to destruction, for I am sufficiently reconciled." 
Job33:25 Then his flesh (which hath been in misery and trouble) shall be as it was in his youth. 
Job33:26 For if he submit himself unto God, he is gracious, and sheweth him his countenance joyfully, and rewardeth man for his righteousness." 
Job33:27 Such a respect hath he unto men. Therefore let a man confess (and say) I offended, but he hath chastened and reformed me: I did unrighteously nevertheless he hath not recompensed me thereafter." 
Job33:28 Yea he hath delivered my soul from destruction, and my life, that it seeth the light." 
Job33:29 Lo, thus worketh God alway with man," 
Job33:30 that he keepeth his soul from perishing, and letteth him enjoy the light of the living." 
Job33:31 Mark well (O Job) and hear me: hold thee still until I have spoken. 
Job33:32 But if thou hast any thing to say, then answer me, and speak, for thy answer pleaseth me." 
Job33:33 If thou hast nothing, then hear me, and hold thy tongue, so shall I teach thee wisdom." 
Job34:1 Eliu proceeding forth in his communication, said:" 
Job34:2 Hear my words, (O ye wise men) hearken unto me, ye that have understanding." 
Job34:3 For like as the mouth tasteth the meats, so the ear proveth and discerneth the words." 
Job34:4 As for the judgment, let us seek it out among ourselves, that we may know what is right." 
Job34:5 And why? Job hath said: I am righteous, but God doth me wrong." 
Job34:6 I must needs be a liar, though my cause be right, and violently am I plagued where as I made no fault:" 
Job34:7 where is there such one as Job, that drinketh up scornfulness like water?" 
Job34:8 Which goeth in company of wicked doers, and walketh with ungodly men:" 
Job34:9 For he sayeth: Though a man be good, yet is he naught before God." 
Job34:10 Therefore hearken unto me, ye that have understanding. Far be it from God, that he should meddle with wickedness: and far be it from the almighty, that he should meddle with unrighteous dealing:" 
Job34:11 but he rewardeth the works of man, and causeth every man to find according to his ways." 
Job34:12 For sure it is, that God condemneth no man wrongously, and the judgement of the Almighty is not unrighteous." 
Job34:13 Who ruleth the earth in his stead? Or whom hath he set to govern the whole world? 
Job34:14 To whom hath he given his heart, for to draw his spirit and breath unto him?" 
Job34:15 All flesh shall come together unto naught, and all men shall turn again unto earth." 
Job34:16 If thou now have understanding, hear what I say, and hearken to the voice of my words." 
Job34:17 May he be made whole, that loveth no right? If thou were a very innocent man, shouldest thou then be punished?" 
Job34:18 For he is even the same, that knoweth the rebellious kings, and sayeth to princes:" 
Job34:19 Ungodly men are ye. He hath none respect unto the persons of the lordly, and regardeth not the rich more than the poor. For they be all the work of his hands." 
Job34:20 In the twinkling of an eye shall they be slain: and at midnight, when the people and the tyrants rage, then shall they perish, and be taken away without hands." 
Job34:21 And why? his eyes look upon the ways of man, and he seeth all his goings." 
Job34:22 There is no darkness nor thick shadow, that can hide the wicked doers from him." 
Job34:23 For no man shall be suffered to go into judgement with God. 
Job34:24 Many one, yea innumerable doth he punish, and setteth other in their stead." 
Job34:25 For he knoweth their evil and dark works, therefore shall they be destroyed." 
Job34:26 They that were in the stead of Sears, dealt like ungodly men." 
Job34:27 Therefore turned they back traitorously and unfaithfully from him, and would not receive his ways." 
Job34:28 In so much that they have caused the voice of the poor to come unto him, and now he heareth the complaint of such as are in necessity." 
Job34:29 If he deliver and grant pardon, who will judge or condemn? But if he hide away his countenance, who will turn it about again, whether it be to the people or to any man?" 
Job34:30 From the wickedness and sin of the people, he maketh an hypocrite to reign over them." 
Job34:31 For so much as I have begun to talk of God, I will not hinder thee;" 
Job34:32 If I have gone amiss, enform me: if I have done wrong, I will leave off." 
Job34:33 Wilt thou not give a reasonable answer? Art thou afraid of anything, seeing thou beganest first to speak, and not I?" 
Job34:34 For else the men of understanding and wisdom, that have heard me, might say: What canst thou speak?" 
Job34:35 As for Job he hath neither spoken to the purpose nor wisely. 
Job34:36 O father let Job be well tried, because he hath turned himself to the wicked:" 
Job34:37 yea above his sins he hath blasphemed, which offense he hath done even before us, in that he striveth against God with his words." 
Job35:1 Eliu spake moreover, and said:" 
Job35:2 Thinkest thou it right, that thou saidst: I am righteous before God?" 
Job35:3 Seeing thou sayest so, how doest thou know it? What thing hast thou more excellent than I, that I am a sinner?(than I that am a sinner?)" 
Job35:4 Therefore will I give answer unto thee and thy friends: 
Job35:5 Look unto the heaven, and behold it: consider the clouds, how they are higher than thou." 
Job35:6 If thou sinnest, what doest thou unto him? If thine offenses be many, how gettest thou his favour?" 
Job35:7 If thou be righteous, what givest thou him? Or what receiveth he of thy hands?" 
Job35:8 Of such an ungodly person as thou, and of the son of man that is righteous as thou pretendest to be:" 
Job35:9 there is a great cry and complaint made by them that are oppressed with violence, yea every man complaineth upon the cruel arm of tyrants." 
Job35:10 For such one never sayeth: Where is God that made me? and that shineth upon us, that we might praise him in the night?" 
Job35:11 Which giveth us more understanding than he doth the beasts of the earth, and teacheth us more than the fouls of heaven." 
Job35:12 If any such complain, no man giveth answer, and that because of the wickedness of proud tyrants." 
Job35:13 But if a man call upon God, doth not he hear him? Doth not the almighty accept his cry?" 
Job35:14 When thou speakest then, should not he pardon thee, if thou open thyself before him, and put thy trust in him?" 
Job35:15 Then useth he no violence in his wrath, neither hath he pleasure in curious and deep inquisitions." 
Job35:16 Therefore hath Job opened his mouth but in vain, and foolishly hath he made so many words." 
Job36:1 Eliu proceeded forth in his talking and said: 
Job36:2 Hold thee still a little, and I shall shew thee, what I have yet to speak on God's behalf." 
Job36:3 I will open unto thee yet more of mine understanding, and prove my maker righteousness." 
Job36:4 True are my words, and no lie: and the knowledge wherewithall I argue against thee, is perfect." 
Job36:5 Behold, God casteth not away the mighty, for he himself is mighty in power and wisdom." 
Job36:6 As for the ungodly, he preserveth them not, but helpeth the poor to their right." 
Job36:7 He turneth not his eyes away from the righteous, he setteth up kings in their throne, and confirmeth them, so that they alway sit therein." 
Job36:8 But if they be laid in prison and chains, or bound with the bonds of poverty:" 
Job36:9 then sheweth he them their works and deeds and the sins where with they have used cruel violence. 
Job36:10 He with punishing and nurturing of them, roundeth(rowneth) them in the ears, warneth them to leave off from their wickedness, and to amend." 
Job36:11 If they now will take heed and be obedient, they shall wear out their days in prosperity, and their years in pleasure and joy." 
Job36:12 But if they will not obey, they shall go thorow the sword, and perish or ever they be aware.(awarre)" 
Job36:13 As for such as be fained, dissemblers and hypocrites, they heap up wrath for themselves: for they call not upon him, though they be his prisoners." 
Job36:14 Thus their soul perisheth in foolishness, and their life with the condemned." 
Job36:15 The poor delivereth he out of his straitness, and comforteth such as be in necessity and trouble." 
Job36:16 Even so shall he keep thee (if thou wilt be content) from the bottomless pit that is beneath: and if thou wilt hold the quiet, he shall fill thy table with plenteousness." 
Job36:17 Nevertheless thou hast condemned the judgement of the ungodly, yea even such a judgment and sentence shalt thou suffer." 
Job36:18 For then shall not thy cause be stilled with cruelty, nor pacified with many gifts." 
Job36:19 Hath God ordained(ordened) then, that the glorious life of thee, and all such mighty men should not be put down?" 
Job36:20 Prolong not thou the time, till there come a night for thee, to set other people in thy stead." 
Job36:21 But beware that thou turn not aside to wickedness and sin, which hither to thou hast chosen more than meekness." 
Job36:22 Behold, God is of a mighty high power: Where is there such a guide and law giver as he?" 
Job36:23 Who will reprove him of his way? Who will say unto him: thou hast done wrong? 
Job36:24 O consider how great and excellent his works be, whom all men love and praise:" 
Job36:25 yea(yee) wonder at him, and yet they see him but afar off." 
Job36:26 Behold, so great is God, that he passeth our knowledge, neither are we able to come to the experience of his years." 
Job36:27 He turneth the water to small drops, he driveth his clouds" 
Job36:28 together for to rain, so that they pour down and drop upon men." 
Job36:29 He can spread out the clouds (a covering of his tabernacle) 
Job36:30 and cause his light to shine upon them, and to cover the bottom of the sea." 
Job36:31 By these things governeth he his people, and giveth them abundance of meat." 
Job36:32 In the turning of a hand he hideth the light, and at his commandment it cometh again." 
Job36:33 The rising up thereof sheweth he to his friends and to the cattle. 
Job37:1 At this, my heart is astonied, and moved out of his place." 
Job37:2 Hear then the sound of his voice, and the noise that goeth out of his mouth." 
Job37:3 He governeth everything under the heaven, and his light reacheth unto the end of the world." 
Job37:4 A roaring voice followeth him: for his glorious majesty giveth such a thunder clap, that (though a man hear it) yet may he not perceive it afterward. It giveth an horrible sound," 
Job37:5 when God sendeth out his voice: great things doth he, which we can not comprehend." 
Job37:6 When he commandeth the snow, it falleth upon the earth: As soon as he giveth the rain a charge, immediately the showers have their strength, and fall down." 
Job37:7 He sendeth fear upon every man, that they might know their own works." 
Job37:8 The beasts creep into their dens, and take their rest." 
Job37:9 Out of the south cometh the tempest, and cold out of the north." 
Job37:10 At the breath of God, the frost cometh, and the waters are shed abroad." 
Job37:11 The clouds do their labour in getting moistness, the clouds pour down the rain." 
Job37:12 He distributeth also on every side, according as it pleaseth him to deal out his works, that they may do, whatsoever he commandeth them thorow the whole world:" 
Job37:13 whether it be to punish any land, or to do good unto them, that seek him." 
Job37:14 Hearken unto this, (O Job) stand still, and consider the wonderous works of God." 
Job37:15 Art thou of counsel with God, when he doth these things? When he causeth the light to come forth of his clouds?" 
Job37:16 Art thou of his counsel, when he spreadeth out the clouds? Hast thou the perfect knowledge of his wonders?" 
Job37:17 and how thy clothes are warm, when the land is still thorow the South wind?" 
Job37:18 Hast thou helped him to spread out the heaven, which is to look upon, as it were cast of clear metal?" 
Job37:19 Teach us what we shall say unto him, for we are unmete because of darkness." 
Job37:20 Shall it be told him what I say? Should a man speak, or should he keep it back?" 
Job37:21 For every man seeth not the light, that he keepeth clear in the clouds, which he cleanseth when he maketh the wind to blow." 
Job37:22 Gold is brought out of the North, but the praise and honour of God's fear cometh from God himself." 
Job37:23 It is not we that can find out the almighty: for in power, equity, and righteousness, he is higher than can be expressed." 
Job37:24 Seeing then that every body feareth him, why should not all wise men also stand(stode) in fear of him." 
Job38:1 Then spake the LORD unto Job out of the storm, and said:" 
Job38:2 What is he that hideth his mind with foolish words? 
Job38:3 Gird up thy loins like a man, for I will question thee, see thou give me a direct answer." 
Job38:4 Where wast thou, when I laid the foundations of the earth? Tell plainly if thou hast understanding." 
Job38:5 Who hath measured it, knowest thou? Or, who hath spread the line upon it?" 
Job38:6 Where upon stand(stode) the pillars of it? Or, who laid the corner stone?" 
Job38:7 Where wast thou, when the morning stars praised me together, and all the children(angels) of God rejoiced triumphantly?" 
Job38:8 Who shut the sea with doors, when it brake forth as a child out of his mother's womb?" 
Job38:9 When I made the clouds to be a covering for it, and swaddled it with the dark?" 
Job38:10 When I gave it my commandment, making doors and bars for it," 
Job38:11 saying: Hither to shalt thou come, but no further, and here shalt thou lay down thy proud and high waves." 
Job38:12 Hast thou given the morning his charge (as soon as thou wast born) and shewed the day spring his place," 
Job38:13 that it might take hold of the corners of the earth, and that the ungodly might be shaken out?" 
Job38:14 Their tokens and weapons hast thou turned like clay, and set them up again as the changing of a garment." 
Job38:15 Yea thou hast spoiled the ungodly of their light, and broken the arm of the proud." 
Job38:16 Camest thou ever into the ground of the sea: or hast thou walked in the low corners of the deep? 
Job38:17 Have the gates of death been open unto thee, or hast thou seen the door of everlasting treasure?" 
Job38:18 Hast thou also perceived how broad the earth is? Now if thou hast knowledge of all," 
Job38:19 then shew me where light dwelleth, and where darkness is:" 
Job38:20 that thou mayest bring us unto their quarters, if thou canst tell the way to their houses." 
Job38:21 Knowest thou (when thou wast born) how old thou shouldest be? 
Job38:22 Wentest thou ever into the treasures of the snow? or hast thou seen the secret places of the hail," 
Job38:23 which I have prepared against the time of trouble, against the time of battle and war?(warre)" 
Job38:24 By what way is the light parted, and the heat dealt out upon the earth?" 
Job38:25 Who divideth the abundance of waters into rivers, or who maketh a way for the stormy weather," 
Job38:26 that it watereth and moistureth the dry and barren ground: 
Job38:27 to make the grass grow in places where no body dwelleth, and in the wilderness where no man remaineth?" 
Job38:28 Who is the father of rain? Or who hath begotten the drops of dew? 
Job38:29 Out of whose womb came the ice? Who hath gendered the coldness of the air? 
Job38:30 That the waters are as hard as stones, and lie congealed above the deep." 
Job38:31 Hast thou brought the seven stars together? Or art thou able to break the Circle of heaven? 
Job38:32 Canst thou bring forth the morning star or the evening star at convenient time, and convey them home again?" 
Job38:33 Knewest thou the course of heaven, that thou mayest set up the ordinance thereof upon the earth?" 
Job38:34 Moreover, canst thou lift up thy voice to the clouds, that they may pour down a great rain upon thee?" 
Job38:35 Canst thou thunder also that they may go their way, and be obedient unto thee, saying: Lo here are we." 
Job38:36 Who giveth sure wisdom or steadfast understanding? 
Job38:37 Who numbereth the clouds in wisdom? Who stilleth the vehement waters of heaven? 
Job38:38 Who turneth the clots to dust, and then to be clots again?" 
Job38:39 Huntest thou the prey from the lion," 
Job38:40 or feedest thou his whelps living in their dens, and lurking in their couches?" 
Job38:41 Who provideth meat for the raven, when his young ones cry unto God, and fly about for want of meat?" 
Job39:1 Knowest thou the time when the wild goats bring forth their young among the stony rocks? Or layest thou wait when the hinds use to fawn? 
Job39:2 Reckonest thou the months after they engender, that thou knowest the time of their bearing?" 
Job39:3 Or when they lie down when they cast their young ones, and when they are delivered of their travail and pain?" 
Job39:4 How their young ones grow up and wax great thorow good feeding? 
Job39:5 who letteth the wild ass go free, or who lowseth the bonds of the mule?" 
Job39:6 Unto whom I have given the wilderness to be their house, and the untilled land to be their dwelling place." 
Job39:7 That they may give no force for the multitude of people in the cities, neither (to) regard the crying of the driver:" 
Job39:8 but (to) seek their pasture about the mountains and (to) follow (upon) the green grass. 
Job39:9 Will the unicorn be so tame as to do the service, or to abide still by thy crib?" 
Job39:10 Canst thou bind the yoke about him in thy furrows, to make him plow after thee in the valleys?" 
Job39:11 Mayest thou trust him (because he is strong) or commit thy labour unto him? 
Job39:12 Mayest thou believe him, that he will bring home the(thy) corn, or to carry anything unto the(thy) barn?" 
Job39:13 The Ostrich (whose feathers are fairer than the wings of the sparrow hawk) 
Job39:14 when he hath laid his eggs upon the ground, he bredeth them in the dust," 
Job39:15 and forgetteth them: so that they might be trodden upon with feet, or broken with some wild beast." 
Job39:16 So hard is he to his young ones, as though they were not his, and laboureth in vain without any fear." 
Job39:17 And that because God hath taken wisdom from him, and hath not given him understanding." 
Job39:18 When his time is, he flyeth up on high,(an hye) and careth neither for horse nor man." 
Job39:19 Hast thou given the horse his strength? or learned him to bow down his neck with fear: 
Job39:20 that he letteth himself be driven forth like a grasshopper, where as the stout neighing that he maketh, is fearful?" 
Job39:21 he breaketh the ground with the hoofs of his feet cheerfully in his strength, and runneth to meet the harness(haruest) men." 
Job39:22 He layeth aside all fear, his stomach is not abated, neither starteth he a back for any sword." 
Job39:23 Though the quivers rattle upon him, though the spear and shield glister:" 
Job39:24 yet rusheth he in fearlessly, and beateth upon the ground. He feareth not the noise of the trumpets," 
Job39:25 but as soon as he heareth the shawmes blow, tush (sayeth he) for he smelleth the battle afar off, the noise, the Captains and the shouting." 
Job39:26 Cometh it thorow thy wisdom, that the Goshawk flyeth toward the South?" 
Job39:27 Doth the Aegle mount up, and make his nest on high,(hye) at thy commandment?" 
Job39:28 He abideth in the stony rocks, and upon the high tops of hard mountains, where no man can come." 
Job39:29 From thence may he behold his pray, and look far about with his eyes." 
Job39:30 His young ones are fed with blood, and where any dead body lieth, there is he immediately." 
Job40:1 Moreover, GOD spake unto Job and said:" 
Job40:2 Can he that striveth with the almighty, be at rest? Should not he which disputeth with God, give him an answer?" 
Job40:3 Job answered the LORD, saying:" 
Job40:4 Behold, I am too vile a person, to answer thee, therefore will I lay my hand upon my mouth." 
Job40:5 Once or twice have I spoken, but I will say no more." 
Job40:6 Then spake the LORD unto Job out of the storm, and said:" 
Job40:7 Gird up thy loins like a man, and tell me the things that I will ask thee." 
Job40:8 Wilt thou disannul my judgment? Or wilt thou condemn me, that thou thyself mayest be made righteous?" 
Job40:9 Is thine arm then like the arm of God? Maketh thy voice such a sound as his doth? 
Job40:10 Then arm thyself with thine own power, up, deck thee in thy jolly array," 
Job40:11 pour out thy indignation of thy wrath: see that thou cast down all the proud," 
Job40:12 look well, that thou makest all such as be stubburn, to obey: tread all the ungodly under thy feet," 
Job40:13 cast them down into the mire, and cover their faces with darkness:" 
Job40:14 Then will I confess also, that thy own right hand hath saved thee." 
Job40:15 Behold Behemoth(the cruell beaste) (whom I made with thee) which eateth hay as an ox: 
Job40:16 lo, how strong he is in his loins, and what power he hath in the navel of his body." 
Job40:17 He spreadeth out his tail like a Cedar tree, all his veins are stiff." 
Job40:18 His shins are like pipes of brass, his rig bones are like staves of iron." 
Job40:19 First when God made him, he ordained(ordened) the wilderness for him," 
Job40:20 that the mountains should give him grass, where all the beasts of the field take their pastime." 
Job40:21 He lieth among the reeds in the Mosses," 
Job40:22 the fens hide him with their shadow, and the willows of the brook cover him round about." 
Job40:23 Lo, without any labour might he drink out of the whole flood, and sup of Jordan without any trail.(travail)" 
Job40:24 Who dare lay a hand on him openly, and undertake to catch him? Or, who dare put a hook thorow his nose, and lay a snare for him?" 
Job41:1 Darest thou draw out Leviathan with an angle? or bind his tongue with a snare? 
Job41:2 Canst thou put a ring in the nose of him? or bore his chaftes thorow with a nawl?(naule) 
Job41:3 Will he make many fair words with thee (thinkest thou) or flatter thee? 
Job41:4 Will he make a covenant with thee? Or art thou able for to compel him to do thee continual service? 
Job41:5 Wilt thou take thy pastime with him as with a bird? Or give him unto thy maidens," 
Job41:6 that thy companions may hew him in pieces, to be parted among the merchant men?" 
Job41:7 Canst thou fill the net with his skin, or the fish panner with his head?" 
Job41:8 Darest thou lay hand upon him? It is better for thee to consider what harm might happen thee there thorow, and not to touch him." 
Job41:9 For when thou thinkest to have hold upon him, he shall beguile thee: Every man also that seeth him, shall go back." 
Job41:10 And why? there dare none be so bold as to raise him up. Who is able to stand before me? 
Job41:11 Or, who hath given me anything afore hand, that I am bound to reward him again? All things under heaven are mine." 
Job41:12 I fear him not, whether he threaten or speak fair." 
Job41:13 Who lifteth him up, and stripeth him out of his clothes, or who taketh him by the bit of his bridle?" 
Job41:14 Who openeth the doors of his face? for he hath horrible teeth round about," 
Job41:15 His body is covered with scales as it were with shields, locked(lockt) in, kept, and well compact together." 
Job41:16 One is so joined to another, that no air can come in:" 
Job41:17 Yea one hangeth so upon another, and sticketh so together, that they can not be sundered." 
Job41:18 His nesing is like a glistering fire, and his eyes like the morning shine." 
Job41:19 Out of his mouth go torches and fire brands," 
Job41:20 out of his nostrils there goeth smoke, like as out of an hot seething pot." 
Job41:21 His breath maketh the coals burn, the flame goeth out of his mouth." 
Job41:22 In his neck remaineth strength, and before his face sorrow is turned to gladness." 
Job41:23 The members of his body are joined so strait one to another, and cleave so fast together, that he can not be moved." 
Job41:24 His heart is as hard as stone, and as fast as the stithy that the hammer man smiteth upon." 
Job41:25 When he goeth: the mightiest of all are afraid, and the waves heave." 
Job41:26 If he draw out the sword, there may neither spear, nor breast plate, abide him." 
Job41:27 He sitteth as much by a straw as by iron, and as much by a rotten stock as by metal." 
Job41:28 He starteth not away for him that bendeth the bow: and as for sling stones, he careth as much for stubble as for them." 
Job41:29 He counteth the hammer no better than a straw, he laugheth him to scorn that shaketh the spear." 
Job41:30 He treadeth the gold in the mire like the sharp potsherds. 
Job41:31 He maketh the deep to seethe and boil like a pot, and stirreth the sea together like an ointment." 
Job41:32 The way is light after him, the deep is his walking place." 
Job41:33 Upon earth is there no power like unto his, for he is so made that he feareth not." 
Job41:34 If a man will consider all high things, this same is a king over all the children of pride." 
Job42:1 Then Job answered the LORD, and said:" 
Job42:2 I know that thou hast power over(of) all things, and that there is no thought hid unto thee." 
Job42:3 For who can keep his own counsel(councell) so secret, but it shall be known? Therefore have I spoken unwisely, seeing these things are so high, and pass mine understanding." 
Job42:4 O hearken thou unto me also, and let me speak: answer me unto the thing that I will ask thee." 
Job42:5 I have given diligent ear unto thee, and now I see thee with mine eyes." 
Job42:6 Wherefore I give mine own self the blame, and take repentance in the dust and ashes." 
Job42:7 Now when the LORD had spoken these words unto Job, he said unto Eliphas the Themanite: I am displeased with thee and thy two friends, for ye have not spoken the thing that is right before me, like as my servant Job hath done." 
Job42:8 Therefore take seven oxen and seven rams, and go to my servant Job, offer up also for yourselves a brentoffering: and let my servant Job pray for you. Him will I accept, and not deal with you after your foolishness: in that ye have not spoken the thing which is right, like as my servant Job hath done." 
Job42:9 So Eliphas the Themanite, Baldad the Suhite and Sophar the Naamathite went their way, and did according as the LORD commanded them. The LORD also accepted the person of Job," 
Job42:10 and the LORD turned him unto Job, when he prayed for his friends: Yea the LORD gave Job twice as much as he had afore." 
Job42:11 And then came there unto him all his brethren, all his sisters, with all them that had been of his acquaintance afore, and ate bread with him in his house, wondering at him, and comforting him over all the trouble, that the LORD had brought upon him. Every man gave him a sheep and a Jewel of gold." 
Job42:12 And the LORD made Job richer than he was before: for he had fourteen thousand sheep, and six thousand camels, and a thousand yoke of oxen, and a thousand asses." 
Job42:13 He had children also, seven sons and three daughters." 
Job42:14 The first (he) called Day: the second Poverty: the third All plenteousness. 
Job42:15 In all the land were none found so fair, as the daughters of Job, and their father gave them inheritance among their brethren." 
Job42:16 After this lived Job forty years, so that he saw his children, his childrens' children to the fourth generation;" 
Job42:17 And so he died, being old and of a perfect age." 
Psalms1:1 O Blessed is the man that goeth not in the counsel of the ungodly: that abideth not in the way of sinners, and sitteth not in the seat of the scornful." 
Psalms1:2 But delighteth in the law of the LORD, and exerciseth himself in his law, both day and night." 
Psalms1:3 Such a man is like a tree planted by the water side, that bringeth forth his fruit in due season. His leaves shall not fall off, and look whatsoever he doth, it shall prosper." 
Psalms1:4 As for the ungodly, it is not so with them: but they are like the dust, which the wind scattereth away from the ground." 
Psalms1:5 Therefore the ungodly shall not be able to stand in the judgment, neither the sinners in the congregation of the righteous." 
Psalms1:6 For the LORD alloweth the way of the righteous, but the way of the ungodly shall perish." 
Psalms2:1 Why do the heathen grudge? Why do the people imagine vain things? 
Psalms2:2 The kings of the earth stand(stode) up, and the rulers are come together, against the LORD, and against his anointed." 
Psalms2:3 Let us break their bonds asunder, and cast away their yoke from us." 
Psalms2:4 Nevertheless he that dwelleth in heaven, shall laugh them to scorn: yea even the Lord(LORDE) himself shall have them in derision." 
Psalms2:5 Then shall he speak unto them in his wrath, and vex them in his sore displeasure." 
Psalms2:6 Yet have I set my king upon my holy hill of Sion. 
Psalms2:7 As for me, I will preach the law, whereof the LORD hath said unto me: Thou art my son, this day have I begotten thee." 
Psalms2:8 Desire of me, and I shall give thee the Heathen for thine inheritance; Yea the uttermost(utmost) parts of the world for thy possession." 
Psalms2:9 Thou shalt rule them with a rod of iron, and break them in pieces like an earthen vessel." 
Psalms2:10 Be wise now therefore, O ye kings, be warned, ye that are judges of the earth." 
Psalms2:11 Serve the LORD with fear, and rejoice before him with reverence." 
Psalms2:12 Kiss the Son, lest the Lord(LORDE) be angry, and so ye perish from the right way. For his wrath shall be kindled shortly: blessed are all they that put their trust in him." 
Psalms3:1 A Psalm of David when he fled from the face of Absalom. Why are they so many, O LORD, that trouble me? a great multitude are they, that rise against me." 
Psalms3:2 Yea many one there be that say of my soul: There is no help for him in God. Selah. 
Psalms3:3 But thou, O LORD, art my defender, my worship, and the lifter up of my head." 
Psalms3:4 I call upon the LORD with my voice, and he heareth me out of his holy hill. Selah." 
Psalms3:5 I laid me down and slept, but I rose up again, for the LORD sustained me." 
Psalms3:6 I am not afraid for thousands of the people, that compass me round about." 
Psalms3:7 Up LORD, and help me, O my God: for thou smitest all mine enemies upon the cheek bones, and breakest the teeth of the ungodly." 
Psalms3:8 Help belongeth unto the LORD, therefore let thy blessing be upon thy people." 
Psalms4:1 To the Chanter in Neginoth, a Psalm of David Hear me when I call, O God of my righteousness: thou that comfortest me in my trouble: have mercy upon me, and harken unto my prayer." 
Psalms4:2 O ye sons of men, how long will ye blaspheme mine honour? why have ye such pleasure in vanity, and seek after lies? Selah." 
Psalms4:3 Know this, that the LORD dealeth marvelously with his saint: and when I call upon the LORD, he heareth me." 
Psalms4:4 Be angry, but sin not: come with your own hearts upon your beds, and remember your selves. Selah." 
Psalms4:5 Offer the sacrifice of righteousness, and put your trust in the LORD." 
Psalms4:6 There be many that say: Who will do us any good? LORD lift up upon(where as thou (o LORD) hast shewed) us the light of thy countenance. 
Psalms4:7 Thou rejoicest mine heart, though their increase be great both in corn and wine." 
Psalms4:8 Therefore will I lay me down in peace, and take my rest: for thou, LORD only settest me in a sure dwelling." 
Psalms5:1 To the Chanter in Nehiloth, a Psalm of David. Hear my words (O LORD) consider my calling." 
Psalms5:2 O mark the voice of my petition, my King and my God, for unto thee will I make my prayer." 
Psalms5:3 Hear my voice by times (O LORD) for early in the morning will I get me unto thee, yea and that with diligence." 
Psalms5:4 For thou art not the God that hath pleasure in wickedness, there may no ungodly person dwell with thee." 
Psalms5:5 Such as be cruel may not stand in thy sight, thou art an enemy unto all wicked doers." 
Psalms5:6 Thou destroyest the liars: the LORD abhoreth the bloodthirsty and deceitful. 
Psalms5:7 But as for me, I will come into thy house, even upon the multitude of thy mercy: and in thy fear will I worship toward thy holy temple." 
Psalms5:8 Lead me (O LORD) in thy righteousness because of mine enemies, and make thy way plain before me." 
Psalms5:9 For there is no faithfulness in their mouths: they dissemble in their hearts: their throat is an open sepulchre: with their tongues they deceive. 
Psalms5:10 Punish them (O God) that they may perish in their own imaginations: cast them out because of the multitude of their ungodliness, for they rebel against thee." 
Psalms5:11 Again, let all them that put their trust in thee, rejoice: yea let them ever be giving of thanks, because thou defendest them: that they which love thy name, may be joyful in thee." 
Psalms5:12 For thou LORD givest thy blessing unto the righteous: and with thy favourable kindness thou defendest him, as with a shield." 
Psalms6:1 To the Chanter in Neginoth upon Sheminith, a Psalm of David. Oh LORD, rebuke me not in thine anger: Oh chasten me not in thy heavy displeasure." 
Psalms6:2 Have mercy upon me (O LORD) for I am weak: O LORD heal me, for all my bones are vexed." 
Psalms6:3 My soul also is in great trouble, but LORD, how long?" 
Psalms6:4 Turn thee (O LORD) and deliver my soul: Oh save me for thy mercies' sake. 
Psalms6:5 For in death no man remembereth thee: Oh who will give thee thanks in the hell? 
Psalms6:6 I am weary of groaning: every night wash I my bed, and water my couch with my tears." 
Psalms6:7 My countenance is changed for very inward grief, I consume away, I have so many enemies." 
Psalms6:8 Away from me all ye wicked doers, for the LORD hath heard the voice of my weeping." 
Psalms6:9 The LORD hath heard my humble petition, the LORD hath received my prayer." 
Psalms6:10 All mine enemies shall be confounded and sore vexed: yea they shall be turned back and put to shame, and that right soon." 
Psalms7:1 Sigaion of David, which he sang for the words of Cus the son of Jemini. O LORD(Lorde) my God, in thee do I trust: save me from all them that persecute me, and deliver me." 
Psalms7:2 Lest he hantche up my soul like a lion, and tear it in pieces, while there is none to help." 
Psalms7:3 O LORD my God, if I have done any such thing, if there be any unrighteousness in my hands:" 
Psalms7:4 If I have rewarded evil unto them that dealt friendly with me, or hurt them that without any cause are mine enemies:" 
Psalms7:5 Then let mine enemy persecute my soul, and take me: yea let him tread my life down in the earth, and lay mine honor in the dust. Selah." 
Psalms7:6 Stand(Stode) up (O LORD) in thy wrath, lift up thyself over the furious indignation of mine enemies: arise up (for me) in the vengeance that thou hast promised." 
Psalms7:7 That the congregation of the people may come about thee, for their sakes therefore lift up thyself again." 
Psalms7:8 The LORD is judge over the people: avenge me then (O LORD) according to my righteousness and innocence. 
Psalms7:9 Oh let the wickedness of the ungodly come to an end: but maintain the just, thou righteous God, that triest the very hearts and reins." 
Psalms7:10 My help cometh of God, which preserveth them that are true of heart." 
Psalms7:11 God is a righteous judge, and God is ever threatening." 
Psalms7:12 If men will not turn, he hath whet his sword: he hath bent his bow and made it ready." 
Psalms7:13 He hath prepared(prepayred) him the weapons of death, and ordained his arrows to destroy." 
Psalms7:14 Behold, he travaileth with mischief, he hath conceived unhappiness, and brought forth a lie." 
Psalms7:15 He hath graven and digged up a pit, but he shall fall himself into the pit that he hath made." 
Psalms7:16 For his unhappiness shall come upon his own head, and his wickedness shall fall upon his own pate." 
Psalms7:17 As for me, I will give thanks unto the LORD for his righteousness' sake, and will praise the name of the LORD the most highest.(hyest)" 
Psalms8:1 To the Chanter upon Githith, a Psalm of David. O LORD(Lorde) our governour: how wonderfull is thy name in all the world? how excellent is thy glory above the heavens." 
Psalms8:2 Out of the mouth of the very babes and sucklings, thou hast ordained(ordened) praise, because of thine enemies, that thou mightest destroy the enemy and the avenger." 
Psalms8:3 For I consider thy heavens, even the work of thy fingers: the moon and the stars which thou hast made." 
Psalms8:4 Oh what is man, that thou art so mindful of him? either the son of man that thou visitest him?" 
Psalms8:5 After thou hadst for a season made him lower than the angels, thou crownest him with honor and glory." 
Psalms8:6 Thou hast set him above the works of thy hands: and thou hast put all things in subjection under his feet. 
Psalms8:7 All sheep and oxen, yea and the beasts of the field." 
Psalms8:8 The fowls of the air, and the fish of the sea, and whatsoever walketh thorow the ways of the sea." 
Psalms8:9 O LORD our governour, how wonderful is thy name in all the world?" 
Psalms9:1 To the Chanter upon Almuth Laben, a Psalm of David. I will give thanks unto thee (O LORD) with my whole heart, I will speak of all thy marvelous works." 
Psalms9:2 I will be glad, and rejoice in thee, yea my songs will I make of thy name, O thou most highest.(hyest)" 
Psalms9:3 Because thou hast driven mine enemies aback, they were discomfited, and perished at thy presence." 
Psalms9:4 For thou hast maintained my right and my cause: thou sittest in the throne that art the true judge. 
Psalms9:5 Thou rebukest the heathen, and destroyest the ungodly, thou puttest out their name for ever and ever." 
Psalms9:6 The enemie's swords are come to an end, thou hast overthrown their cities, their memorial is perished with them." 
Psalms9:7 But the LORD endureth for ever, he hath prepared his seat unto judgment." 
Psalms9:8 He governeth the world with righteousness, and ministereth true judgment unto the people." 
Psalms9:9 The LORD is a defense for the poor, a defense in the time of trouble." 
Psalms9:10 Therefore they that know thy name, put their trust in thee: for thou (LORD) never failest them that seek thee." 
Psalms9:11 O praise the LORD, which dwelleth in Sion, shew the people of his doings." 
Psalms9:12 And why? he maketh inquisition for their blood, and remembereth them: he forgetteth not the complaint of the poor." 
Psalms9:13 Have mercy upon me,(O LORD) consider the trouble that I am in among mine enemies, thou that liftest me up from the gates of death." 
Psalms9:14 That I may shew all thy praises within the ports of the daughter of Sion, and rejoice in thy saving health." 
Psalms9:15 As for the Heathen, they are sunk down in the pit that they made: in the same net which they spread out privily, is their own foot taken." 
Psalms9:16 Thus the LORD is known to execute true judgment, when the ungodly is trapped in the works of his own hands. Selah." 
Psalms9:17 The wicked must be turned unto hell, and all the Heathen that forget God." 
Psalms9:18 But the poor shall not all way be out of remembrance, the patient abiding of such as be in trouble shall not perish for ever." 
Psalms9:19 Up LORD, let no man have the upper hand, let the Heathen be condemned before thee." 
Psalms9:20 O LORD set a school master over them, that the Heathen may know themselves to be but men. Selah." 
Psalms10:1 Why art thou gone so far off, O LORD? whilt thou hide thyself in time of trouble?" 
Psalms10:2 While the ungodly hath the over hand, the poor must suffer persecution: O that they were taken in the imagination which they go about." 
Psalms10:3 For the ungodly maketh boast(boost) of his own heart's desire, the covetous blesseth himself, and blasphemeth the LORD." 
Psalms10:4 The ungodly is so proud and full of indignation, that he careth not: neither is God before his eyes." 
Psalms10:5 His ways are alway filthy, thy judgments are far out of his sight, he defyeth all his enemies." 
Psalms10:6 For he sayeth in his heart: Tush, I shall never be cast down, there shall no harm happen to me." 
Psalms10:7 His mouth is full of cursing, fraud and deceit: under his tongue is travail and sorrow." 
Psalms10:8 He sitteth lurking in the gardens, that he may privily murder the innocent, his eyes are set upon the poor." 
Psalms10:9 He lieth waiting secretly, as it were a lion in his den. He lurketh that he may ravish the poor, yea to ravish the poor when he hath gotten him into his net." 
Psalms10:10 Then smiteth he, then oppresseth he and casteth down the poor with his authority." 
Psalms10:11 For he sayeth in his heart: Tush, God hath forgotten, he hath turned away his face, so that he will never see it." 
Psalms10:12 Arise O LORD God, lift up thine hand and forget not the poor." 
Psalms10:13 Wherefore should the wicked blaspheme God and say in his heart: tush, he careth not for it:" 
Psalms10:14 This thou seest, for thou considerest the misery and sorrow. The poor giveth himself over in to thy hand, and committeth him unto thee, for thou art the helper of the friendless." 
Psalms10:15 Break thou the arm off the ungodly and malicious, search out the wickedness which he hath done, that he may perish." 
Psalms10:16 The LORD is King for ever, the Heathen shall perish out of his land." 
Psalms10:17 LORD, thou hearest the desirous longing of the poor: their heart is sure, that thine ear hearkeneth thereto." 
Psalms10:18 Help the fatherless and the poor unto their right, that the ungodly be no more exalted upon earth." 
Psalms11:1 To the Chanter, of David. In the LORD put I my trust: how will ye then say to my soul: that she should flee as a bird upon your hill?" 
Psalms11:2 For lo, the ungodly have bend their bow, and made ready their arrows in the quiver, that they may privily shoot at them, which are true of heart." 
Psalms11:3 The very foundations have they cast down, what can the righteous then do with all?" 
Psalms11:4 But the LORD is in his holy temple, the LORD's seat is in heaven: he considereth with his eyes, his eyelids behold the children of men." 
Psalms11:5 The LORD seeth both the righteous and ungodly, but who so delighteth in wickedness, him his soul abhoreth." 
Psalms11:6 Upon the ungodly he shall rain snares, fire, brimstone, storm and tempest: this reward shall they have to drink." 
Psalms11:7 For the LORD is righteous, and he loveth righteousness: his countenance beholdeth the thing that is just." 
Psalms12:1 To the Chanter upon Sheminith, a Psalm of David. Help LORD, for there is not one saint more: very few faithful are there among the children of men." 
Psalms12:2 Every man telleth lies to his neighbour, they do but flatter with their lips and dissemble in their heart." 
Psalms12:3 O that the LORD would root out all deceitful lips, and the tongue that speaketh proud things." 
Psalms12:4 Which said: our tongue should prevail: we are they that ought to speak, who is lord over us?" 
Psalms12:5 Now for the trouble's sake of the oppressed, and because of the complaint of the poor, I will up (sayeth the LORD) I will help them, and set them at rest." 
Psalms12:6 The words of the LORD are pure words: even as the silver, which from the earth is tried and purified seven times in the fire." 
Psalms12:7 Keep them therefore (O LORD) and preserve us from this generation for ever. 
Psalms12:8 And why? when vanity and idleness getteth the overhand among the children of men, all are full of the ungodly." 
Psalms13:1 To the chanter, a Psalm of David How long wilt thou forget me, O LORD? for ever? how long wilt thou hide thy face from me?" 
Psalms13:2 Oh how long shall I seek counsel in my soul? how long shall I be so vexed in my heart? how long shall mine enemy triumph over me? 
Psalms13:3 Consider, and hear me, O LORD my God: lighten mine eyes, that I sleep not in death." 
Psalms13:4 Lest mine enemy say: I have prevailed against him: for if I be cast down, they that trouble me will rejoice at it." 
Psalms13:5 But my trust is in thy mercy, and my heart is joyful in thy saving health." 
Psalms13:6 I will sing of the LORD, that dealeth so lovingly with me. (Yea I will praise the name of the LORD the most highest.)(hyest)" 
Psalms14:1 To the chanter, a Psalm of David The foolish bodies say in their hearts: Tush, there is no God. They are corrupt, and become abominable in their doings, there is not one that doth good." 
Psalms14:2 The LORD looked down from heaven upon the children of men, to see if there were any that would understand and seek after God." 
Psalms14:3 But they are all gone out of the way, they are all together become unprofitable: there is none that doth good, no not one." 
Psalms14:4 How can they have understanding, that work mischief, eating up my people, as it were bread, and call not upon the LORD?" 
Psalms14:5 Therefore shall they be brought in great fear, for God standeth by the generation of the righteous." 
Psalms14:6 As for you, ye have made a mock at the counsel of the poor, because he putteth his trust in the LORD." 
Psalms14:7 Oh that the saving health were given unto Israel out of Sion. Oh that the LORD would deliver his people out of captivity. Then should Jacob rejoice, and Israel should be right glad." 
Psalms15:1 A Psalm of David. LORD,(Lorde) who shall dwell in thy tabernacle? who shall rest upon thy holy hill?" 
Psalms15:2 Even he that leadeth an uncorrupt life: that doth the thing which is right, and that speaketh the truth from his heart." 
Psalms15:3 He that useth no deceit in his tongue: he that doth no evil to his neighbour, and slandereth(sclaundereth) not his neighbours." 
Psalms15:4 He that sitteth not by the ungodly, but maketh much of them that fear the LORD: he that sweareth unto his neighbour, and disappointeth(dispointeth) him not." 
Psalms15:5 He that giveth not his money upon usury, and taketh no reward against the innocent. Who so doth these things, shall never be removed." 
Psalms16:1 Michtam of David Preserve me, (O God) for in thee do I trust." 
Psalms16:2 I have said unto the LORD: thou art my God, my goods are nothing unto thee." 
Psalms16:3 All my delight is upon the saints that are in the earth, and upon such like." 
Psalms16:4 But they that run after another, shall have great trouble. Their drink offerings of blood will not I offer, neither make mention of their name in my mouth." 
Psalms16:5 The LORD him self is my good and my portion, thou maintainest mine inheritance." 
Psalms16:6 The lot is fallen unto me in fair ground, yea I have a goodly heritage." 
Psalms16:7 I will thank the LORD for giving me warning: my reins also have chastened me in the night season. 
Psalms16:8 Afore hand saw I GOD alwayes before me, for he is on my right hand, that I should not be moved." 
Psalms16:9 Therefore did my heart rejoice, and my tongue was glad, my flesh also shall rest in hope." 
Psalms16:10 For why? thou shalt not leave my soul in hell, neither shalt thou suffer thy saint to see corruption." 
Psalms16:11 Thou hast shewed me the ways of life: thou shalt make me full of joy with thy countenance. At thy right hand there is pleasure and joy for evermore. 
Psalms17:1 A prayer of David. Hear the right (O LORD) consider my complaint: harken to my prayer, that goeth not out of a feigned mouth." 
Psalms17:2 Let my sentence come forth from thy presence: and look upon the thing that is equal. 
Psalms17:3 Thou hast proved and visited mine heart in the night season: thou hast tried me in the fire: and hast found no wickedness in me: for I utterly purposed that my mouth should not offend. 
Psalms17:4 Because the words of thy lips, I have kept me from the works of men: in the way of the murderer." 
Psalms17:5 Oh order thou my goings in thy paths: that my footsteps slip not. 
Psalms17:6 For unto thee I cry: hear me O God: incline thine ears unto me: and harken unto my words. 
Psalms17:7 Shew thy marvelous loving-kindness, thou that savest them which put their trust in thee, from such as resist thy right hand." 
Psalms17:8 Keep me as the apple of an eye, defend me under the shadow of thy wings." 
Psalms17:9 From the ungodly that trouble me, from my enemies which compass my soul round about." 
Psalms17:10 Which maintain their own wealthiness with oppression, and their mouth speaketh proud things." 
Psalms17:11 They lie waiting in our way on every side, turning their eyes down to the ground." 
Psalms17:12 Like as a lion that is greedy of his prey, and as it were a lion's whelp lurking in his den." 
Psalms17:13 Up LORD, disappoint(dispoynte) him and cast him down: deliver my soul with thy sword from the ungodly." 
Psalms17:14 From the men of thy hand (O LORD) from the men of the world, which have their portion in this life: whose bellies thou fillest with thy treasure." 
Psalms17:15 They have children at their desire, and leave the rest of their substance for their babes. But as for me, I will behold thy presence in righteousness: and when thy glory appeareth, I shall be satisfied." 
Psalms18:1 To the chanter of David, servaunt of the LORD, which saide unto the LORD the words of this song, on the day in which the LORD delivered him from the hand of all his enemies, and from the hand of Saul, and said: I will love thee, (O LORD) my strength." 
Psalms18:2 The LORD is my succor, my refuge, my Savior: my God, my helper, in whom I trust: my buckler, the horn of my health, and my protection." 
Psalms18:3 I will praise the LORD, and call upon him, so shall I be save from mine enemies." 
Psalms18:4 The sorrows of death compassed me, and the brooks of ungodliness made me afraid." 
Psalms18:5 The pains of hell came about me, the snares of death took hold upon me." 
Psalms18:6 Yet in my trouble I called upon the LORD, and complained unto my God. So he heard my voice out of his holy temple, and my complaint came before him, yea even into his ears." 
Psalms18:7 Then the earth trembled and quaked, the very foundations of the hills shook and were removed, because he was wroth." 
Psalms18:8 There went a smoke out of his nostrils, and a consuming fire out of his mouth, so that coals were kindled at it." 
Psalms18:9 He bowed the heavens, and came down, and it was dark under his feet." 
Psalms18:10 He rode upon the Cherub, and did fly: he came flying with the wings of the wind." 
Psalms18:11 He made darkness his pavilion round about him, with dark waters and thick clouds to cover him." 
Psalms18:12 At the brightness of his presence the clouds removed, with hail stones and coals of fire." 
Psalms18:13 The LORD also thundered out of the heaven, and the height gave his thunder with hail stones and coals of fire." 
Psalms18:14 He sent out his arrows and scattered them, he cast sore lightnings, and destroyed them." 
Psalms18:15 The springs of waters were seen, and the foundations of the round world were discovered at thy chiding (O LORD) at the blasting and breath of thy displeasures." 
Psalms18:16 He sent down from the height(heyth) to fetch me, and took me out of great waters." 
Psalms18:17 He delivered me from my strong enemies, and from my foes which were to mighty for me." 
Psalms18:18 They prevented me in the time of my trouble, but the LORD was my defense." 
Psalms18:19 He brought me forth also into liberty: and delivered me, because he had a favor unto me." 
Psalms18:20 The LORD shall reward me after my righteous dealing, and according to the cleanness of my hands shall he recompense me." 
Psalms18:21 For I have kept the ways of the LORD: and have not behaved myself wickedly against my God. 
Psalms18:22 I have an eye unto all his laws, and cast not out his commandments from me." 
Psalms18:23 Uncorrupt will I be before him, and will eschew my own wickedness." 
Psalms18:24 Therefore shall the LORD reward me after my righteous dealing, and according to the cleanness of my hands in his eyesight." 
Psalms18:25 With the holy thou shalt be holy, and with the innocent thou shalt be innocent." 
Psalms18:26 With the clean thou shalt be clean, and with the froward thou shalt be froward." 
Psalms18:27 For thou shalt save the poor oppressed, and bring down the high looks of the proud." 
Psalms18:28 Thou lightest my candle, O LORD my God: thou makest my darkness to be light." 
Psalms18:29 For in thee I can discomfort an host of men: yea in my God I can leap over the wall. 
Psalms18:30 The way of God is a perfect way: the words of the LORD are tried in the fire: he is a shield of defense, for all them that trust in him." 
Psalms18:31 For who is God, but the LORD? Or, who hath any strength, but our God?" 
Psalms18:32 It is God that hath girded me with strength, and made my way uncorrupt." 
Psalms18:33 He hath made my feet like hart's feet, and set me up on high.(an hye)" 
Psalms18:34 He teacheth my hands to fight, and maketh mine arms to break even a bow of steel." 
Psalms18:35 Thou hast given me the defense of thy health, thy right hand upholdeth me, and thy loving correction maketh me great." 
Psalms18:36 Thou hast made room enough under me for to go, that my foot steps should not slide." 
Psalms18:37 I will follow upon mine enemies, and take them: I will not turn till they be discomfited." 
Psalms18:38 I will smite them, they shall not be able to stand, but fall under my feet." 
Psalms18:39 Thou hast girded me with strength unto the battle, thou hast thrown them all down under me, that rose up against me." 
Psalms18:40 Thou hast made mine enemies to turn their backs upon me, thou hast destroyed them that hated me." 
Psalms18:41 They cried, but there was none to help them: yea even unto the LORD, but he heard them not." 
Psalms18:42 I will beat them as small as the dust clay in(before) the wind, I will cast them out as the clay in the streets." 
Psalms18:43 Thou shalt deliver me from the strivings of the people, thou shalt make me the head of the Heathen. A people whom I have not known shall serve me." 
Psalms18:44 As soon as they hear of me, they shall obey me, but the strange children dissemble with me." 
Psalms18:45 The strange children are waxen old, and go halting out of their paths." 
Psalms18:46 The LORD liveth: and blessed be my helper, praised be the God of my health." 
Psalms18:47 Even the God which seeth that I be avenged, and subdueth the people unto me." 
Psalms18:48 It is he that delivereth me from my cruel enemies: thou shalt lift me up from them that rise against me, thou shalt rid me from the wicked man." 
Psalms18:49 For this cause I will give thanks unto thee (O LORD) among the gentiles, and sing praises unto thy name." 
Psalms18:50 Great prosperity giveth he unto his king, and sheweth loving-kindness unto David his anointed, yea and to his seed for evermore." 
Psalms19:1 To the Chanter, a Psalm of David. The very heavens declare the glory of God, and the very firmament sheweth his handiwork." 
Psalms19:2 One day telleth another, and one night certifieth another." 
Psalms19:3 There is neither speech nor language, but their voices are heard among them." 
Psalms19:4 Their sound is gone out into all lands, and their words in to the ends of the world. In them hath he set a tabernacle for the Sun,(Sone)" 
Psalms19:5 which cometh forth as a bridegroom out of his chamber, and rejoiceth as a giant to run his course." 
Psalms19:6 It goeth forth from the one end of the heaven, and runneth about unto the same end again, and there may no man hide himself from the heat thereof." 
Psalms19:7 The law of the LORD is a perfect law, it quickeneth the soul. The testimony of the LORD is true, and giveth wisdom even unto babes." 
Psalms19:8 The statutes of the LORD are right, and rejoice the heart: the commandment of the LORD is pure, and giveth light unto the eyes." 
Psalms19:9 The fear of the LORD is clean, and endureth for ever: the judgments of the LORD are true and righteous altogether." 
Psalms19:10 More pleasant are they than gold, yea than much fine gold: sweeter than honey and the honeycomb." 
Psalms19:11 These thy servant keepeth, and for keeping of them there is great reward." 
Psalms19:12 Who can tell, how oft he offendeth? Oh cleanse thou me from my secret faults." 
Psalms19:13 Keep thy servant also from presumptuous sins, lest they get dominion over me: so shall I be undefiled and innocent from the great offense." 
Psalms19:14 Yea the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart shall be acceptable unto thee, O LORD, my helper and my redeemer." 
Psalms20:1 To the Chanter, a Psalm of David. The LORD hear thee in the time of trouble, the name of the God of Jacob defend thee." 
Psalms20:2 Send thee help from the Sanctuary, and strengthen thee out of Sion." 
Psalms20:3 Remember all thy offerings, and accept thy brent sacrifice. Selah." 
Psalms20:4 Grant thee thy heart's desire, and fulfil all thy mind." 
Psalms20:5 We will rejoice in thy health, and triumph in the name of the LORD our God: the LORD performe all thy petitions." 
Psalms20:6 Now know I, that the LORD helpeth his anointed, and will hear him from his holy heaven: mighty is the help of his right hand." 
Psalms20:7 Some put their trust in chariots, and some in horses: but we will remember the name of the LORD our God." 
Psalms20:8 They are brought down and fallen, but we are risen and stand(stonde) up right." 
Psalms20:9 Save, LORD, and help us, O king, when we call upon thee." 
Psalms21:1 To the Chanter, a Psalm of David LORD,(Lorde) how joyful is the king in thy strength? O how exceedingly glad is he of thy saving health?" 
Psalms21:2 Thou hast given him his heart's desire, and hast not put him from the request of his lips. Selah." 
Psalms21:3 For thou hast prevented him with liberal blessings, and set a crown of gold on his head." 
Psalms21:4 He asked life of thee, and thou gavest him a long life, even for ever and ever." 
Psalms21:5 His honour is great in thy saving health, glory and great worship shalt thou lay upon him." 
Psalms21:6 For thou shalt give him everlasting felicity, and make him glad with the joy of thy countenance." 
Psalms21:7 And why? because the king putteth his trust in the LORD, and in the mercy of the most highest(hiest) he shall not miscarry." 
Psalms21:8 Let all thine enemies feel thy hand: let thy right hand find out all them that hate thee. 
Psalms21:9 Thou shalt make them like a fire oven in the time of thy wrath: the LORD shall destroy them in his displeasure, and the fire shall consume them." 
Psalms21:10 Their fruit shalt thou root out of the earth, and their seed from among the children of men." 
Psalms21:11 For they intended mischief against thee, and imagined such devices, as they were not able to perform." 
Psalms21:12 Therefore shalt thou put them to flight, and with thy strings thou shalt make ready thine arrows against the faces of them." 
Psalms21:13 Be thou exalted, LORD, in thine own strength, so will we sing and praise thy power." 
Psalms22:1 To the Chanter upon Aieleth of the dawning, a Psalm of David. My God, my God: why hast thou forsaken me? the words of my complaint are far from my health." 
Psalms22:2 O my God, I cry in the daytime, but thou hearest not: and in the night season also I take no rest." 
Psalms22:3 Yet dwellest thou in the sanctuary, O thou worship of Israel." 
Psalms22:4 Our fathers hoped in thee, they trusted in thee, and thou didst(diddest) deliver them." 
Psalms22:5 They called upon thee, and were helped: they put their trust in thee, and were not confounded." 
Psalms22:6 But as for me, I am a worm and no man: a very scorn of men and the outcast of the people." 
Psalms22:7 All they that see me, laugh me to scorn: they shoot out their lips, and shake their heads." 
Psalms22:8 He trusted in God, let him deliver him: let him help him, if he will have him." 
Psalms22:9 But thou art he that took me out of my mother's womb: thou wast my hope, when I hanged yet upon my mother's breasts." 
Psalms22:10 I have been left unto thee ever since I was born, thou art my God, even from my mother's womb." 
Psalms22:11 O go not far from me then, for trouble is hard at hand, and here is none to help me." 
Psalms22:12 Great bulls are come about me, fat oxen close me in on every side." 
Psalms22:13 They gape upon me with their mouths, as it were a rampaging and roaring lion." 
Psalms22:14 I am poured out like water, all my bones are out of joint: my heart in the middest of my body is even like melting wax." 
Psalms22:15 My strength is dried up like a potsherd, and my tongue cleaveth to my gums, and thou hast brought me into the dust of death." 
Psalms22:16 For dogs are come about me, the counsel of the wicked hath laid siege against me." 
Psalms22:17 They pierced my hands and my feet, I might have told all my bones: as for them, they stood staring and looking upon me." 
Psalms22:18 They have parted my garments among them, and cast lots upon my vesture." 
Psalms22:19 But be not thou far from me, O LORD: thou art my succour, haste thee to help me." 
Psalms22:20 Deliver my soul from the sword, my dearling from the power of the dog." 
Psalms22:21 Save me from the lion's mouth, and hear me from among the horns of the unicorns." 
Psalms22:22 So will I declare thy name unto my brethren, in the middest of the congregation will I praise thee." 
Psalms22:23 O praise the LORD ye that fear him: Magnify him all ye seed of Jacob, and let all the seed of Israel fear him." 
Psalms22:24 For he hath not despised nor abhorred the miserable estate of the poor: he hath not hid his face from me, but when I called unto him, he heard me." 
Psalms22:25 I will praise thee in the great congregation, and perform my vows in the sight of all them that fear thee." 
Psalms22:26 The poor shall eat and be satisfied: they that seek after the LORD shall praise him: our heart shall live for ever. 
Psalms22:27 All the ends of the world shall remember themselves, and be turned unto the LORD: and all the generations of the Heathen shall worship before him." 
Psalms22:28 For the kingdom is the LORD's, and he shall be the governour of the Heathen." 
Psalms22:29 All such as be fat upon earth, shall eat also and worship. All they that lie in the dust, and live so hardly, shall fall down before him." 
Psalms22:30 The seed shall serve him, and preach of the Lord(LORDE) forever." 
Psalms22:31 They shall come, and declare his righteousness: unto a people that shall be born, whom the Lord(LORDE) hath made." 
Psalms23:1 A Psalm of David. The LORD is my shepherd, I can want nothing." 
Psalms23:2 He feedeth me in a green pasture, and leadeth me to a fresh water." 
Psalms23:3 He quickeneth my soul, and bringeth me forth in the way of righteousness for his name's sake." 
Psalms23:4 Though I should walk now in the valley of the shadow of death, yet I fear no evil, for thou art with me: thy staff and thy sheephook comfort me." 
Psalms23:5 Thou preparest a table before me against mine enemies: thou anointest my head with oil, and fillest my cup full." 
Psalms23:6 Oh let thy loving-kindness and mercy follow me all the days of my life, that I may dwell in the house of the LORD for ever." 
Psalms24:1 A Psalm of David. The earth is the LORD's, and all that therein is: the compass of the world, and all that dwell therein." 
Psalms24:2 For he hath founded it upon the seas, and builded it upon the floods." 
Psalms24:3 Who shall go up in to the hill of the LORD? Or, who shall remain in his holy place?" 
Psalms24:4 Even he that hath innocent hands, and a clean heart: which lifteth not up his mind unto vanity, and swear not to deceive." 
Psalms24:5 He shall receive the blessing from the LORD, and mercy from God his saviour." 
Psalms24:6 This is the generation of them that seek him, of them that seek thy face, O Jacob. Selah;" 
Psalms24:7 Open your gates (O ye princes) let the everlasting doors be open, that the King of glory may come in." 
Psalms24:8 Who is this King of glory? It is the LORD strong and mighty, even the LORD mighty in battle." 
Psalms24:9 Open your gates (O ye princes) let the everlasting doors be open, that the King of glory may come in." 
Psalms24:10 Who is this King of glory? It is the LORD of Hosts, he is the King of glory. Selah." 
Psalms25:1 Of David Unto thee, (O LORD) I lift up my soul." 
Psalms25:2 My God, I trust in thee: Oh let me not be confounded, lest mine enemies triumph over me." 
Psalms25:3 For all they that hope in thee shall not be ashamed: but such as be scornful dispisers without a cause, they shall be put to confusion." 
Psalms25:4 Shew me thy ways, O LORD, and teach me thy paths." 
Psalms25:5 Lead me in thy truth, and learn me, for thou art the God of my health, and in thee is my hope all the day long." 
Psalms25:6 Call to remembrance, O LORD, thy tender mercies and thy lovingkindnesses, which have been ever of old." 
Psalms25:7 Oh remember not the sins and offenses of my youth, but according unto thy mercy think upon me, O LORD, for thy goodness." 
Psalms25:8 O how friendly and righteous is the LORD, therefore will he teach sinners in the way." 
Psalms25:9 He leadeth the simple a right, and such as be meek them learneth he his ways." 
Psalms25:10 All the ways of the LORD are mercy and faithfulness, unto such as keep his testament and covenant." 
Psalms25:11 For thy name's sake, O LORD, be merciful unto my sin, for it is great." 
Psalms25:12 Whatsoever he be that feareth the LORD, he shall shew him the way that he hath chosen." 
Psalms25:13 His soul shall dwell at ease, and his seed shall possess the land." 
Psalms25:14 The secret of the LORD is among them that fear him, and he sheweth them his covenant." 
Psalms25:15 Mine eyes are ever looking unto the LORD, for he shall pluck my feet out of the net." 
Psalms25:16 Turn thee unto me and have mercy upon me, for I am desolate and in misery." 
Psalms25:17 The sorrows of my heart, are great, O bring me out of my troubles." 
Psalms25:18 Look upon mine adversity and misery, and forgive me all my sins." 
Psalms25:19 Consider how mine enemies are many, and bear a malicious hate against me." 
Psalms25:20 O keep my soul, and deliver me: let me not be confounded, for I have put my trust in thee." 
Psalms25:21 Let innocency and righteous dealing wait upon me, for my hope is in thee." 
Psalms25:22 Deliver Israel, O God, out of all his trouble." 
Psalms26:1 Of David Be thou my Judge, O LORD, for I walk innocently: my trust is in the LORD, therefore shall I not fall." 
Psalms26:2 Examine me, O LORD, and prove me: try out my reins and my heart." 
Psalms26:3 For thy loving-kindness is before mine eyes, and I walk in thy truth." 
Psalms26:4 I sit not among vain persons, and have no fellowship with the deceitful." 
Psalms26:5 I hate the congregation of the wicked, and will not sit among the ungodly." 
Psalms26:6 I wash my hands with innocency, O LORD, and so go I to thine altar." 
Psalms26:7 That I may shew the voice of thy praise and tell of all thy wonderous works. 
Psalms26:8 LORD, I love the habitation of thy house, and the place where thy honour dwelleth." 
Psalms26:9 O destroy not my soul with the sinners, nor my life with the bloodthirsty." 
Psalms26:10 In whose hands is wickedness, and their right hand is full of gifts." 
Psalms26:11 But as for me, I will walk innocently: O deliver me, and be merciful unto me." 
Psalms26:12 My foot standeth right: I will praise thee (O LORD) in the congregations. 
Psalms27:1 Of David The LORD is my light and my health: whom then should I fear? the LORD is the strength of my life, for whom then should I be afraid?" 
Psalms27:2 Therefore when the wicked, even mine enemies and my foes, came upon me, to eat up my flesh, they stumbled and fell." 
Psalms27:3 Though an host of men were laid against me, yet shall not my heart be afraid: and though there rose up war against me, yet will I put my trust in him." 
Psalms27:4 One thing have I desired of the LORD, which I will require: namely, that I may dwell in the house of the LORD all the days of my life, to behold the fair beauty of the LORD, and to visit his temple." 
Psalms27:5 For in the time of trouble he hath hid me in his tabernacle, yea in the secret place of his dwelling hath he kept and set me up upon a rock of stone." 
Psalms27:6 And now hath he lift up my head above mine enemies, that compassed me round about. Therefore will I offer in his dwelling, the oblation of thanksgiving: I will (both) sing and speak praises unto the LORD." 
Psalms27:7 Harken unto my voice, O LORD, when I cry unto thee: have mercy upon me, and hear me." 
Psalms27:8 My heart speaketh unto thee, my face seeketh thee, yea LORD, thy face will I seek." 
Psalms27:9 O hide not thou thy face from me, cast not thy servant off in displeasure. Thou art my succour, leave me not, neither forsake me, O God my Saviour." 
Psalms27:10 For my father and my mother have forsaken me, but the LORD hath taken me up." 
Psalms27:11 Shew me thy way, O LORD, and lead me in the right path, because of mine enemies." 
Psalms27:12 Deliver me not into the wiles of mine adversaries, for there are false witnesses risen up against me, and they imagine mischief." 
Psalms27:13 Nevertheless I believe verily to see the goodness of the LORD in the land of the living. 
Psalms27:14 O tarry thou the LORD's leisure, be strong, let thine heart be of good comfort, and wait thou still for the LORD." 
Psalms28:1 Of David Unto thee will I cry, O my strong defense: think no scorn of me, lest, if thou make thee as though thou heardest not, I be come like them, that go down into the pit." 
Psalms28:2 Hear the voice of my humble petition, when I cry unto thee, and hold up my hands toward thy holy temple." 
Psalms28:3 O pluck me not away among the ungodly and wicked doers, which speak friendly to their neighbours, but imagine mischief in their hearts." 
Psalms28:4 Reward them according to their deeds and wickedness of their own inventions. Recompense them after the works of their hands, pay them that they have deserved." 
Psalms28:5 For they regard not the works of the LORD, nor the operation of his hands: therefore shall he break them down, and not build them up." 
Psalms28:6 Praised be the LORD, for he hath heard the voice of my humble petition." 
Psalms28:7 The LORD is my strength and my shield: my heart hoped in him, and I am helped: therefore my heart danceth for joy, and I will sing praises unto him." 
Psalms28:8 The LORD is the strength of his people, he is the defender and Saviour of his anointed." 
Psalms28:9 O help thy people, give thy blessing unto thine inheritance: feed them, and set them up for ever." 
Psalms29:1 A Psalm of David. Ascribe unto the LORD, O ye mighty, ascribe unto the LORD worship and strength." 
Psalms29:2 Give the LORD the honour of his name, bow yourselves to the holy majesty of the LORD." 
Psalms29:3 It is the LORD that commandeth the waters: It is the glorious God that maketh the thunder: it is the LORD that ruleth the sea. 
Psalms29:4 The voice of the LORD is mighty in operation, the voice of the LORD is a glorious voice." 
Psalms29:5 The voice of the LORD breaketh the cedar trees: yea the LORD breaketh the Cedars of Lebanon. 
Psalms29:6 He maketh them to skip like a calf: Lebanon and Sirion like a young unicorn. 
Psalms29:7 The voice of the LORD divideth the flames of fire: 
Psalms29:8 the voice of the LORD shaketh the wilderness, yea the LORD shaketh the wilderness of Cades." 
Psalms29:9 The voice of the LORD moveth the hinds and discovereth the thick bushes: in his temple shall every man speak of his honour. 
Psalms29:10 The LORD stilleth the water flood, and the LORD remaineth a King for ever." 
Psalms29:11 The LORD shall give power unto his people, the LORD shall give his people the blessing of peace." 
Psalms30:1 A Psalm and song of the dedication of the house, of David. I will magnify thee, O LORD, for thou hast set me up, and not suffered my foes to triumph over me." 
Psalms30:2 O LORD my God, I cried unto thee, and thou hast healed me." 
Psalms30:3 Thou LORD hast brought my soul out of hell: thou hast kept my life, where as they go down to the pit." 
Psalms30:4 Sing praises unto the LORD, (O ye saints of his) give thanks unto him for a remembrance of his holiness." 
Psalms30:5 For his wrath endureth but the twinkling of an eye, and his pleasure is in life: heaviness may well endure for a night, but joy cometh in the morning." 
Psalms30:6 As for me, when I was in prosperity I said: Tush, I shall never fall more. And why? thou LORD, of thy goodness hadst made my hill so strong." 
Psalms30:7 But as soon as thou turnedest thy face from me, I was brought in fear." 
Psalms30:8 Then cried I unto thee, O LORD, yea unto the LORD made I my prayer." 
Psalms30:9 What profit is there in my blood, if I go down to corruption? May the dust give thanks unto thee? Or shall it declare thy faithfulness?" 
Psalms30:10 Hear, O LORD, and have mercy upon me: LORD, be thou my helper." 
Psalms30:11 And so thou hast turned my heaviness into joy: thou hast put off my sackcloth, and girded me with gladness;" 
Psalms30:12 That mine honor might sing praises unto thee without ceasing: O LORD my God, I will give thanks unto thee for ever." 
Psalms31:1 To the chanter a Psalm of David. In thee, O LORD, is my trust: let me never be put to confusion, but deliver me in thy righteousness." 
Psalms31:2 Bow down thine ear to me, make haste to deliver me: be thou my strong rock, and a house of defense, that thou mayest save me." 
Psalms31:3 For thou art my stronghold and my castle: O be thou my guide, and lead me for thy name's sake." 
Psalms31:4 Draw me out of the net that they have laid privily for me, for thou art my strength." 
Psalms31:5 In to thy hands I comend my spirit: thou hast delivered me O LORD thou God of truth. 
Psalms31:6 I hate them that hold of vanities, and my trust is in the LORD." 
Psalms31:7 I will be glad and rejoice in thy mercy: for thou hast considered my trouble, thou hast known my soul in adversity;" 
Psalms31:8 Thou hast not delivered me over in to the hands of the enemy, but hast set my feet in a large room." 
Psalms31:9 Have mercy upon me, O LORD, for I am in trouble mine eye is consumed for very heaviness, yea my soul and my body." 
Psalms31:10 My life is waxen old with heaviness, and my years with mourning. My strength faileth me because of mine adversity, and my bones are corrupt." 
Psalms31:11 I am become a very reproof among all mine enemies, my neighbours and they of mine own acquaintance are afraid of me: they that see me in the street, convey themselves from me." 
Psalms31:12 I am clean forgotten and out of mind, as a dead man: I am become like a broken vessel." 
Psalms31:13 For I have heard the blasphemy of the multitude: every man abhoreth me: they have gathered a counsel together against me: and are purposed to take away my life. 
Psalms31:14 But my hope is in the(thee O) LORD, and I say: thou art my God." 
Psalms31:15 My time is in thy hand: deliver me from the hand of mine enemies, and from them that persecute me." 
Psalms31:16 Shew thy servant the light of thy countenance, help me for thy mercies' sake." 
Psalms31:17 Let me not be confounded, O LORD, for I call upon thee: let the ungodly rather be put to confusion, and brought unto the hell."
