Psalms31:18 Let the lying lips be put to silence, which cruelly, disdainfully(disdainedly) and despitefully speak against the righteous." 
Psalms31:19 O how great and manifold is thy good, which thou haste hid for them that fear thee? O what things bringest thou to pass for them, that put their trust in thee, even before the sons of men?" 
Psalms31:20 Thou hidest them privily by thine own presence from the proud men, thou keepest them secretly in thy tabernacle, from the strife of tongues." 
Psalms31:21 Thanks be to the LORD, for he hath shewed me marvelous great kindness in a strong city." 
Psalms31:22 For when the sudden fear came upon me, I said: I am cast out of thy sight. Nevertheless, thou heardest mine humble prayer, when I cried unto thee." 
Psalms31:23 O love the LORD, (all ye his saints) for the LORD preserveth the faithful, and plenteously rewardeth he the proud doer." 
Psalms31:24 Be strong therefore and take a good heart unto you, all ye that put your trust in the LORD." 
Psalms32:1 An Instruction of David. Blessed are they, whose unrighteousness is forgiven, and whose sins are covered." 
Psalms32:2 Blessed is the man, unto whom the LORD imputeth no sin, in whose spirit there is no guile." 
Psalms32:3 For while I held my tongue, my bones consumed away thorow my daily complainings." 
Psalms32:4 And because thy hand was so heavy on me both day and night, my moisture was like the drought in Summer. Selah." 
Psalms32:5 Therefore I confessed my sin unto thee, and hid not mine unrighteousness. I said: I will knowledge my offense, and accuse my self unto the LORD, and so thou forgavest me the wickedness of my sin. Selah." 
Psalms32:6 For this shall every saint make his prayer unto thee in due season, therefore shall not the great water floods come nigh him." 
Psalms32:7 Thou art my defense in the trouble that is come about me, O compass thou me about also with the joy of deliverance. Selah." 
Psalms32:8 I will enform thee, and shew thee the way wherein thou shalt go: I will fasten mine eyes upon thee." 
Psalms32:9 Be not ye now like horses and mules, which have no understanding. Whose mouths thou must hold with bit and bridle, if they will not obey thee." 
Psalms32:10 Great plagues shall the ungodly have, but who so putteth his trust in the LORD, mercy shall compass him on every side." 
Psalms32:11 Be glad, O ye righteous, and rejoice in the LORD, be joyful all ye that are true of heart." 
Psalms33:1 Rejoice in the LORD, O ye righteous, for it becometh well the just to be thankful." 
Psalms33:2 Praise the LORD with harp: sing psalms unto him with the lute and instrument of ten strings. 
Psalms33:3 Sing him a new song, yea sing lustily unto him, and with a good courage." 
Psalms33:4 For the word of the LORD is true, and all his works are faithful." 
Psalms33:5 He loveth mercy and judgment, the earth is full of the goodness of the LORD." 
Psalms33:6 By the word of the LORD were the heavens made, and all the Hosts of them by the breath of his mouth." 
Psalms33:7 He gathereth the waters together as it were in a bottle, and layeth up the deep in secret." 
Psalms33:8 Let all the earth fear the LORD, and let all them that dwell in the world, stand(stode) in awe of him." 
Psalms33:9 For look what he sayeth, it is done: and look what he commandeth, it standeth fast." 
Psalms33:10 The LORD bringeth the counsel of the Heathen to naught, and turneth the devices of the people." 
Psalms33:11 But the counsel of the LORD endureth, and the thoughts of his heart from generation to generation." 
Psalms33:12 Blessed are the people that hold the LORD for their God, and blessed are the folk whom he hath chosen to be his heritage." 
Psalms33:13 The LORD looketh down from heaven, and beholdeth all the children of men:" 
Psalms33:14 from his strong seat he considereth all them that dwell in the world. 
Psalms33:15 He only hath fashioned all the hearts of them, and knoweth all their works." 
Psalms33:16 A king is not helped by his own great host, neither is a giant saved thorow the might of his own strength." 
Psalms33:17 A horse is but a vain thing to save a man, it is not the power of his strength that can deliver him." 
Psalms33:18 Behold, the eye of the LORD looketh unto them that fear him, and put their trust in his mercy." 
Psalms33:19 That he may deliver their souls from death, and to feed them in the deare time." 
Psalms33:20 Let our soul patiently abide the LORD, for he is our help and shield." 
Psalms33:21 So shall our heart rejoice in him, because we have hoped in his holy name." 
Psalms33:22 Let thy merciful kindness, O LORD, be upon us, like as we put our trust in thee." 
Psalms34:1 Of David, when he fained him self to be made before Abimelech: which drove him away, and he departed. I will alway give thanks unto the LORD, his praise shall ever be in my mouth." 
Psalms34:2 My soul shall make her boast in the LORD: the humble(poor oppressed) shall hear thereof, and be glad." 
Psalms34:3 O praise the LORD with me, and let us magnify his name together." 
Psalms34:4 I sought the LORD, and he heard me, yea he delivered me out of all my fear." 
Psalms34:5 They that have a eye unto him, shall be lightened, and their faces shall not be ashamed." 
Psalms34:6 This poor man cried unto the LORD, and he heard him, yea and delivered him out of all his troubles." 
Psalms34:7 The Angel of the LORD pitcheth his tent round about him(them) that fear him, and delivereth them." 
Psalms34:8 O taste and see how friendly the LORD is, blessed is the man that trusteth in him." 
Psalms34:9 O fear the LORD, ye that be his saints: for they that fear him, lack nothing." 
Psalms34:10 The rich shall want and suffer hunger, but they which seek the LORD, shall want no manner of thing, that is good." 
Psalms34:11 Come hither, O ye children, hearken unto me, I will teach you the fear of the LORD." 
Psalms34:12 Who so listeth to live, and would fain see good days." 
Psalms34:13 Let him refrain his tongue from evil, and his lips that they speak no guile." 
Psalms34:14 Let him eschew evil, and do good: Let him seek peace and ensue it." 
Psalms34:15 For the eyes of the LORD are over the righteous, and his ears are open unto their prayers." 
Psalms34:16 But the face of the LORD beholdeth them that do evil, to destroy the remembrance of them out of the earth." 
Psalms34:17 When the righteous cry, the LORD heareth them, and delivereth them out of all their troubles." 
Psalms34:18 The LORD is nigh unto them that are contrite in heart, and will help such as be of an humble spirit." 
Psalms34:19 Great are the troubles of the righteous, but the LORD delivereth them out of all." 
Psalms34:20 He keepeth all their bones, so that not one of them is broken." 
Psalms34:21 But misfortune shall slay the ungodly, and they that hate the righteous shall be guilty." 
Psalms34:22 The LORD delivereth the souls of his servants, and all they that put their trust in him, shall not offend." 
Psalms35:1 Of David. Strive thou with them, O LORD, that strive with me, fight thou against them that fight against me." 
Psalms35:2 Lay hand upon the shield and spear, and stand up to help me." 
Psalms35:3 Draw out thy sword, and stop(stoppe) the way against them that persecute me, say unto my soul: I am thy help." 
Psalms35:4 Let them be confounded and put to shame, that seek after my soul: let them be turned back and brought to confusion, that imagine mischief for me." 
Psalms35:5 Let them be as the dust before the wind, and the angel of the LORD scattering them." 
Psalms35:6 Let their way be dark and slippery, and the angel of the LORD to persecute them." 
Psalms35:7 For they have privily laid their net to destroy me without cause, yea, and made a pit for my soul, which I never deserved." 
Psalms35:8 Let a sudden destruction come upon him unawares, and the net that he hath laid privily, catch himself, that he may fall into his own mischief." 
Psalms35:9 But let my soul be joyful in the LORD, and rejoice in his help." 
Psalms35:10 All my bones shall say: LORD, who is like unto thee? which deliverest the poor from those that are too strong for him, yea, the poor and the needy from his robbers." 
Psalms35:11 False witnesses are risen up, and lay to my charge things that I know not." 
Psalms35:12 They reward me evil for good, to the great discomforth of my soul." 
Psalms35:13 Nevertheless, when they were sick, I put on a sackcloth: I humbled my soul with fasting, and my prayer returned into mine own bosom." 
Psalms35:14 I behaved myself as though it had been my friend or my brother, I went heavily, as one that mourneth for his mother." 
Psalms35:15 But in mine adversity they rejoice, and gather them together: Yea, the very lame come together against me unawares, making mowes at me, and cease not." 
Psalms35:16 With the greedy and scornful hypocrites, they gnashed upon me with their teeth." 
Psalms35:17 Lord,(LORDE) when wilt thou look upon this? O restore my soul from the wicked rumour of them, my darling from the lions." 
Psalms35:18 So will I give thee thanks in the great congregation, and praise thee among much people." 
Psalms35:19 O let them not triumph over me, that are mine enemies for naught: O let them not wink with their eyes, that hate me without a cause." 
Psalms35:20 And why? their communing(comoning) is not for peace, but they imagine false words against the outcasts of the land." 
Psalms35:21 They gape upon me with their mouths, saying: there there: we see it with our eyes." 
Psalms35:22 This thou seest, O LORD: hold not thy tongue then, go not far from me, O Lord.(LORDE)" 
Psalms35:23 Awake Lord,(LORDE) and stand up: avenge thou my cause, my God, and my Lord.(LORDE)" 
Psalms35:24 Judge me, O LORD my God, according to thy righteousness, that they triumph not over me." 
Psalms35:25 O let them not say in their hearts: there there, so would we have it: O let them not say: we have overcome him." 
Psalms35:26 Let them be put to confusion and shame, that rejoice at my trouble: let them be clothed with rebuke and dishonour, that boast themselves against me." 
Psalms35:27 Let them also be glad and rejoice, that favour my righteous dealing: yea let them say alway: blessed be the LORD, which hath pleasure in the prosperity of his servant." 
Psalms35:28 And as for my tongue, it shall be talking of thy righteousness and of thy praise, all the day long." 
Psalms36:1 To the Chanter, of David the servant of the LORD My heart sheweth me the wickedness of the ungodly, that there is no fear of God before his eyes." 
Psalms36:2 For he dissembleth before his face, so long till his abominable sin be found out." 
Psalms36:3 The words of his mouth are unrighteousness and deceit, he will not be learned to do good." 
Psalms36:4 He imagineth mischief upon his bed, he will come in no good way, nor refuse the thing that is evil." 
Psalms36:5 Thy mercy, O LORD, reacheth unto the heaven, and thy faithfulness unto the clouds." 
Psalms36:6 Thy righteousness standeth like the strong mountains, and thy judgment like the great deep. Thou LORD preservest both men and beasts." 
Psalms36:7 How precious is thy mercy, O God, that the children of men may put their trust under the shadow of thy wings." 
Psalms36:8 They shall be satisfied with the plenteousness of thy house, and thou shalt give them drink of the river of thy pleasures." 
Psalms36:9 For by thee is the well of life, and in thy light, shall we see light." 
Psalms36:10 O spread forth thy loving-kindness unto them that know thee, and thy righteousness unto them that are true of heart." 
Psalms36:11 O let not the foot of pride overtake me, O let not the hand of the ungodly cast me down." 
Psalms36:12 As for wicked doers, they fall, they are cast down, and are not able to stand.(stode)" 
Psalms37:1 A Psalm of David. Fret not thyself at the ungodly, be not thou envious against the evil doers." 
Psalms37:2 For they shall soon be cut down like the grass, and be withered even as the green herb." 
Psalms37:3 Put thou thy trust in the LORD, and be doing good: so shalt thou dwell in the land, and verily it shall feed thee." 
Psalms37:4 Delight thou in the LORD, and he shall give thee thy heart's desire." 
Psalms37:5 Commit thy way unto the LORD, set thy hope in him, and he shall bring it to pass." 
Psalms37:6 Yea He shall make thy righteousness as clear as the light, and thy just dealing as the noonday." 
Psalms37:7 Hold thee still in the LORD, and abide patiently upon him: but grieve not thyself at one that hath prosperity, and liveth in abomination." 
Psalms37:8 Leave off from wrath, let go displeasure, let not thy jealousy move thee also to do evil." 
Psalms37:9 For wicked doers shall be rooted out, but they that patiently abide the LORD, shall inherit(enherit) the land." 
Psalms37:10 Suffer yet a little while, and the ungodly shall be clean gone: thou shalt look after his place, and he shall be away." 
Psalms37:11 But the meek spirited shall possess the earth, and have pleasure in much rest." 
Psalms37:12 The ungodly layeth wait for the just, and gnasheth upon him with his teeth." 
Psalms37:13 But the Lord(LORDE) laugheth him to scorn, for he seeth that his day is coming." 
Psalms37:14 The ungodly draw out the sword and bend their bow, to cast down the simple and poor, and to slay such as go the right way." 
Psalms37:15 Nevertheless, their sword shall go thorow their own heart, and their bow shall be broken." 
Psalms37:16 A small thing that the righteous hath, is better than the great riches of the ungodly." 
Psalms37:17 For the arms of the ungodly shall be broken, but the LORD upholdeth the righteous." 
Psalms37:18 The LORD knoweth the days of the godly, and their inheritance endureth for ever." 
Psalms37:19 They shall not be confounded in the perilous time, and in the days of dearth they shall have enough." 
Psalms37:20 As for the ungodly, they shall perish: and when the enemies of the LORD are in their flourish,(flouris, flurries) they shall consume, yea even as the smoke shall they consume away." 
Psalms37:21 The ungodly borroweth, and payeth not again, but the righteous is merciful and liberal." 
Psalms37:22 Such as be blessed of him, shall possess the land: and they whom he curseth, shall be rooted out." 
Psalms37:23 The LORD ordereth a good man's going, and hath pleasure in his way." 
Psalms37:24 Though he fall, he shall not be hurt, for the LORD upholdeth him with his hand." 
Psalms37:25 I have been young, and now am old: yet saw I never the righteous forsaken, nor his seed to seek their bread." 
Psalms37:26 The righteous is ever merciful, and lendeth gently, therefore shall his seed be blessed." 
Psalms37:27 Flee from evil, and do the thing that is good, so shalt thou dwell forever." 
Psalms37:28 For the LORD loveth the thing that is right, he forsaketh not his saints, but they shall be preserved for evermore: as for the seed of the ungodly, it shall be rooted out." 
Psalms37:29 Yea, the righteous shall possess the land, and dwell therein for ever." 
Psalms37:30 The mouth of the righteous is exercised in wisdom, and his tongue talketh of judgment." 
Psalms37:31 The law of his God is in his heart, therefore shall not his footsteps slide." 
Psalms37:32 The ungodly seeth the righteous, and goeth about to slay him." 
Psalms37:33 But the LORD will not leave him in his hands, nor condemn him when he is judged." 
Psalms37:34 Hope thou in the LORD, and keep his way. And he shall so promote thee, that thou shalt have the land by inheritance, and see, when the ungodly shall perish." 
Psalms37:35 I myself have seen the ungodly in great power, and flourishing like a green bay tree:" 
Psalms37:36 but when I went by, lo, he was gone: I sought him, but he could no where be found." 
Psalms37:37 Keep innocency, and take heed unto the thing that is right, for that shall bring a man peace at the last." 
Psalms37:38 As for the transgressors, they shall perish together, and the ungodly shall be rooted out at the last." 
Psalms37:39 The help of the righteous cometh of the LORD, he is their strength in the time of trouble." 
Psalms37:40 The LORD shall stand(stode) by them, and save them: he shall deliver them from the ungodly, and help them, because they put their trust in him." 
Psalms38:1 To the Chanter, to bring to remembrance Put me not to rebuke (Oh LORD) in thy anger: Oh chasten me not in thy heavy displeasure." 
Psalms38:2 For thine arrows stick fast in me, and thy hand presseth me sore." 
Psalms38:3 There is no whole part in my body, because of thy displeasure: there is no rest in my bones, by reason of my sins." 
Psalms38:4 For my wickednesses are gone over my head, and are like a sore burthen, too heavy for me to bear." 
Psalms38:5 My wounds stink and are corrupt, thorow my foolishness." 
Psalms38:6 I am brought into so great trouble and misery, that I go mourning all the day long." 
Psalms38:7 For my loins are clean dried up, and there is no whole part in my body." 
Psalms38:8 I am feeble and sore smitten, I roar for the very disquietness of my heart." 
Psalms38:9 Lord,(LORDE) thou knowest all my desire, and my groaning is not hid from thee." 
Psalms38:10 My heart panteth, my strength hath failed me, and the light of mine eyes is gone from me." 
Psalms38:11 My lovers and friends stand looking upon my trouble, and my kinsmen are gone afar off." 
Psalms38:12 They that sought after my life, and to do me evil, spake of lies and imagined deceit(disceate) all the day long." 
Psalms38:13 As for me, I was like a deaf man and heard not: as one that were dumb, not opening his mouth." 
Psalms38:14 I am become as a man that heareth not: and that can make no resistance with his mouth. 
Psalms38:15 For in thee, O LORD, is my trust, thou shalt hear me, O Lord(LORDE) my God." 
Psalms38:16 My desire is, that mine enemies triumph not over me: for if my foot slip, they rejoice greatly against me." 
Psalms38:17 I am ready to suffer trouble, and my heaviness is ever in my sight." 
Psalms38:18 For I confess my wickedness, and my sin grieveth me." 
Psalms38:19 But mine enemies live, and are mighty: and they that hate me without a cause, are many in number." 
Psalms38:20 They that reward me evil for good, speak evil of me, because I follow the thing that good is." 
Psalms38:21 Forsake me not (O LORD my God) O go not far from me. 
Psalms38:22 Haste thee to help me, O Lord(LORDE) my succour." 
Psalms39:1 To the Chanter for Iduthun,(praising) a Psalm of David. I said: I will keep my ways that I offended not in my tongue. And so I shut my mouth, while the ungodly laid wait for me." 
Psalms39:2 I held my tongue, I was dumb, I kept silence, yea even from good words, but it was pain and grief to me." 
Psalms39:3 My heart was hot within me, and while I was thus musing, the fire kindled: so that I spake with my tongue:" 
Psalms39:4 LORD, let me know mine end, and the number of my days: that I may be certified what I want." 
Psalms39:5 Behold, thou hast made my days a span long, and my life as it were nothing before thee. O how vain are all men living? Selah" 
Psalms39:6 Yea every man walketh as it were a shadow, and disquieteth himself in vain: he heapeth up riches, and can not tell to whom he gathereth them." 
Psalms39:7 And now Lord(LORDE) wherein shall I comfort me? my hope is in thee. 
Psalms39:8 Deliver me from all mine offenses, and make me not a scorn unto the foolish." 
Psalms39:9 I keep silence, and open not my mouth, for thou hast done it." 
Psalms39:10 Turn thy plagues away from me, for I am consumed thorow the fear of thy hand." 
Psalms39:11 When thou punishest man for sin, thou chastenest him: so that his beauty consumeth away, like as it were a moth. O how vain are all men. Selah." 
Psalms39:12 Hear my prayer O LORD, and consider my calling: shew not thyself as though thou sawest not my tears. For I am a stranger and pilgrim with thee, as all my forefathers were." 
Psalms39:13 Oh spare me a little that I may refresh myself, before I go hence, and be no more seen." 
Psalms40:1 To the chanter, a Psalm of David. I waited patiently for the LORD, which inclined(enclined) himself unto me: and heard my calling." 
Psalms40:2 He brought me out of the horrible pit, out of the mire and clay: he set my feet upon the rock, and ordered my goings." 
Psalms40:3 He hath put a new song in my mouth, even a thanksgiving unto our God. Many men seeing this, shall fear the LORD, and put their trust in him." 
Psalms40:4 Blessed is the man that setteth his hope in the LORD, and turneth not unto the proud, and to such as go about with lies." 
Psalms40:5 O LORD my God, great are thy wonderous works which thou hast done: and in thy thoughts toward us there may none be likened unto thee. I would declare them, and speak of them: but they are so many, that they can not be told." 
Psalms40:6 Sacrifice and offering thou wouldest not have, but a body hast thou ordained me: burntoffering and sacrifice for sin thou hast not allowed." 
Psalms40:7 Then said I: Lo, I come. In the beginning of the book it is written of me," 
Psalms40:8 that I should fulfill thy will O my God, and that am I content to do: yea thy law is within my heart." 
Psalms40:9 I will preach of thy righteousness in the great congregation: Lo, I will not refrained my lips, O LORD, and that thou knowest." 
Psalms40:10 I do not hide thy righteousness in my heart, my talking is of thy truth and saving health: I keep not thy loving mercy and faithfulness back from the great congregation." 
Psalms40:11 Turn not thou thy mercy from me, O LORD, but let thy loving-kindness and truth alway preserve me." 
Psalms40:12 For innumerable troubles are come about me: my sins have taken such hold upon me, that I am not able to look up: yea they are more in number than the hairs of my head, and my heart hath failed me." 
Psalms40:13 O LORD, let it be thy pleasure to deliver me, make haste (O LORD) to help me." 
Psalms40:14 Let them be ashamed and confounded, that seek after my soul, to destroy it: let them fall backward and be put to confusion, that wish me evil." 
Psalms40:15 Let them soon be brought to shame, that cry over me: there there." 
Psalms40:16 But let all those that seek thee, be joyful and be glad in thee: and let all such as delight in thy saving health, say alway: The LORD be praised." 
Psalms40:17 As for me, I am poor and in misery, but the Lord(LORDE) careth for me. Thou art my helper and redeemer, make no long tarrying, O my God." 
Psalms41:1 To the chanter, a Psalm of David. Blessed is he, that considereth the poor: the LORD shall deliver him in the time of trouble." 
Psalms41:2 The LORD shall preserve him, and keep him alive: he shall make him to prosper upon earth, and shall not deliver him into the will of his enemies." 
Psalms41:3 The LORD shall refresh him, when he lieth sick upon his bed, yea thou makest his bed in all his sickness." 
Psalms41:4 I said: LORD be merciful unto me, heal my soul, for I have sinned against thee." 
Psalms41:5 Mine enemies speak evil upon me: when shall he die, and his name perish?" 
Psalms41:6 Though he came in to see, yet meaned he falsity(falsede) in his heart, heaping mischief upon himself." 
Psalms41:7 All they that hate me, run together against me, and imagine evil against me." 
Psalms41:8 They have given a wicked sentence upon me: when he lieth, he shall rise up no more." 
Psalms41:9 Yea even mine own familiar friend, whom I trusted, which did eat my bread, hath lift up his heel against me." 
Psalms41:10 But be thou merciful unto me (O LORD) raise thou me up, and I shall reward them." 
Psalms41:11 By this I know thou favourest me, that my enemy shall not triumph over me." 
Psalms41:12 Thou hast upholden me because of my innocency, and set me before thy face for ever." 
Psalms41:13 O blessed be the LORD God of Israel, from hence forth and for evermore. Amen. Amen." 
Psalms42:1 To the Chanter, a monition of the sons of Korah Like as the hart desireth the water brooks, so longeth my soul after thee, O God." 
Psalms42:2 My soul is a thirst for God, yea even for the living God: when shall I come, and behold the face of God?" 
Psalms42:3 My tears are my meat day and night, while it is daily said unto me: Where is now thy God?" 
Psalms42:4 Now when I think there upon, I pour out my heart by myself: for I would fain go hence with the multitude, and pass over with them unto the house of God, in the voice of praise and thanksgiving, among such as keep holy day." 
Psalms42:5 Why art thou so full of heaviness (O my soul) and why art thou so unquiet within me? O put thy trust in God, for I will yet give him thanks, for the help of his countenance." 
Psalms42:6 My God, my soul is vexed within me: therefore I remember the land of Jordan, and the little hill of Hermonim." 
Psalms42:7 One deep calleth another with the voice of thy whistles, all thy waves(wawes) and water floods are gone over me." 
Psalms42:8 The LORD hath promised his loving-kindness daily, therefore will I praise him in the night season, and make my prayer unto the God of my life." 
Psalms42:9 I will say unto God my stony rock; Why hast thou forgotten me? why go I thus heavily, while the enemy oppresseth me?" 
Psalms42:10 While my bones are broken, and while mine enemies cast me in the teeth, daily saying unto me: Where is now thy God?" 
Psalms42:11 Why art thou so heavy (O my soul) and why art thou so disquieted within me? O put thy trust in God, for I will yet thank him for the help of his countenance, and because he is my God." 
Psalms43:1 Give sentence upon me (O God) and defend my cause against the unholy people: Oh deliver me from the deceitful, and wicked man." 
Psalms43:2 For thou (O God) art my strength: why hast thou shot me from thee? Why go I then so heavily, while the enemy oppresseth me?" 
Psalms43:3 Oh send out thy light and thy truth, that they may lead me and bring me unto thy holy hill, and to thy dwelling." 
Psalms43:4 That I may go in to the altar of God, even unto the God which is my joy and pleasure, and upon the harp to give thanks unto thee, O God, my God." 
Psalms43:5 Why art thou so heavy (O my soul) and why art thou so disquieted within me? O put thy trust in God, for I will yet give him thanks for the help of his countenance, and because he is my God." 
Psalms44:1 To the Chanter, an instruction of the sons of Korah We have heard with our ears, (O God) our fathers have told us, what thou hast done in their time of old." 
Psalms44:2 How thou hast driven out the Heathen with thy hand, and planted them in: how thou hast destroyed the nations and cast them out." 
Psalms44:3 For they gat not the land in possession thorow their own sword, neither was it their own arm that helped them. But thy right hand, thine arm and the light of thy countenance, because thou hadst a favour unto them." 
Psalms44:4 Thou art the King and my God, thou sendest help unto Jacob." 
Psalms44:5 Thorow thee, will we overthrow our enemies: and in thy name will we tread them under, that rise up against us." 
Psalms44:6 For I will not trust in my bow, it is not my sword that shall help me." 
Psalms44:7 But it is thou that savest us from our enemies, and puttest them to confusion that hate us." 
Psalms44:8 We will alway make our boast of God, and praise thy name for ever. Selah." 
Psalms44:9 But now thou forsakest us, and puttest us to confusion, and goest not forth with our Hosts." 
Psalms44:10 Thou makest us to turn our backs upon our enemies, so that they which hate us, spoil our goods." 
Psalms44:11 Thou lettest us be eaten up like sheep, and scatterest us among the Heathen." 
Psalms44:12 Thou sellest thy people for naught, and takest no money for them." 
Psalms44:13 Thou makest us to be rebuked of our neighbours, to be laughed to scorn and had in derision, of them that are round about us." 
Psalms44:14 Thou hast made(makest) us, a very byword among the Heathen, and that the people shake their heads at us." 
Psalms44:15 My confusion is daily before me, and the shame of my face covereth me." 
Psalms44:16 For the voice of the slanderer(sclaunderer) and blasphemer, for the enemy and avenger." 
Psalms44:17 All this is come upon us, and yet have we not forgotten thee, nor behaved our selves unfaithfully in thy covenant." 
Psalms44:18 Our heart is not turned back, neither our steps gone out of thy way." 
Psalms44:19 For thou hast smitten us in the place of dragons, and covered us with the shadow of death.(That thou smytest us so in the place of the serpet, and coverest us with ye shadowe of death.)" 
Psalms44:20 If we had forgotten the name of our God, and hold up our hands to any strange God." 
Psalms44:21 Should not God find it out? for he knoweth the very secrets of the heart. 
Psalms44:22 But for thy sake we are killed all the day long, and are counted as sheep appointed to be slain." 
Psalms44:23 Up Lord,(LORDE) why sleepest thou; Awake, and cast us not off for ever." 
Psalms44:24 Wherefore hidest thou thy face? wilt thou clean forget our misery and oppression? 
Psalms44:25 For our soul is brought low even unto the dust, and our belly cleaveth unto the ground." 
Psalms44:26 Arise O Lord,(LORDE) help us, and deliver us for thy mercy sake." 
Psalms45:1 To the Chanter, upon Sosanim, an instruction of the children of Korah, a song of love. My heart is inditing of a good matter, I speak of that, which I have made of the king: My tongue is the pen of a ready writer." 
Psalms45:2 Thou art the fairest among the children of men, full of grace are thy lips, therefore God blesseth thee for ever." 
Psalms45:3 Gird thee with thy sword upon thy thigh, (O thou mighty) with worship and renown." 
Psalms45:4 Good luck have thou with thine honour, ride on with the truth, meekness and righteousness: and thy right hand shall teach thee wonderful things." 
Psalms45:5 Thy arrows are sharp, the people shall be subdued unto thee, even in the middest among the king's enemies." 
Psalms45:6 Thy seat (O God) endureth for ever: the scepter of thy kingdom is a right scepter. 
Psalms45:7 Thou hast loved righteousness, and hated iniquity: wherefore God (which is thy God) hath anointed thee with the oil of gladness above thy fellows." 
Psalms45:8 All thy garments are like myrrh, Aloes and Cassia, when thou comest out of thine ivory palaces in thy beautiful glory." 
Psalms45:9 Kings' daughters go in thy goodly array, and upon thy right hand standeth the queen in a vesture of the most fine gold." 
Psalms45:10 Hearken (O daughter) consider, and incline thine ear: forget thine own people, and thy father's house." 
Psalms45:11 So shall the king have pleasure in thy beauty, for he is thy Lord,(LORDE) and thou shalt worship him." 
Psalms45:12 The daughters of Tyre shall be there with gifts, the rich among the people shall make their supplication before thee." 
Psalms45:13 The king's daughter is all glorious within, her clothing is of wrought gold." 
Psalms45:14 She shall be brought unto the king in raiment of needlework, and maidens after her: such as be next her shall be brought unto thee." 
Psalms45:15 With joy and gladness shall they be brought, and go into the king's palace." 
Psalms45:16 In stead of thy fathers thou hast gotten children, whom thou shalt make princes in all lands." 
Psalms45:17 I will remember thy name from one generation to another: therefore shall the people give thanks unto thee, world without end." 
Psalms46:1 To the Chanter, a song of the children of Korah upon almuth. In our troubles and adversity, we have found, that God is our refuge, our strength and help." 
Psalms46:2 Therefore will we not fear, though the earth fell, and though the hills were carried into the middest of the sea." 
Psalms46:3 Though the waters of the sea raged and were never so troublous, and though the mountains shook at the tempest of the same. Selah." 
Psalms46:4 For there is a flood, which with his rivers rejoiceth the city of God, the holy dwelling of the most highest." 
Psalms46:5 God is in the middest of her, therefore shall she not be removed: for God helpeth her, and that right early." 
Psalms46:6 The Heathen are mad, the kingdoms make much ado: but when he sheweth his voice, the earth melteth away." 
Psalms46:7 The LORD of Hosts is with us, the God of Jacob is our defense. Selah." 
Psalms46:8 O come hither, and behold the works of the LORD, what destructions he hath brought upon the earth." 
Psalms46:9 He hath made wars to cease in all the world: he hath broken the bow, he hath knapped the spear in sunder, and brent the chariots in the fire." 
Psalms46:10 Be still then and confess that I am God: I will be exalted among the Heathen, I will be exalted upon earth." 
Psalms46:11 The LORD of Hosts is with us, the God of Jacob is our defense. Selah." 
Psalms47:1 To the Chanter, a Psalm of the children of Korah O clap your hands together (all ye people) O sing unto God with the voice of thanksgiving." 
Psalms47:2 For the LORD the most highest(hyest) is to be feared, and he is the great King upon all the earth." 
Psalms47:3 He shall subdue the people under us, and the Heathen under our feet." 
Psalms47:4 He chooseth us for an heritage, the beauty of Jacob whom he loved. Selah." 
Psalms47:5 God is gone up with a merry noise, and the LORD with the sound of the trumpet." 
Psalms47:6 O Sing praises, sing praises unto God: O sing praises, sing praises unto our King." 
Psalms47:7 For God is King of all the earth, O sing praises unto him with understanding." 
Psalms47:8 God is king over the Heathen, God sitteth in his holy seat." 
Psalms47:9 The princes of the people are gathered together unto the God of Abraham: for God is far higher(farre farre hyer) exalted, than the mighty lords of the earth." 
Psalms48:1 A song of a Psalm of the children of Korah Great is the LORD, and highly to be praised, in the city of our God, even upon his holy hill." 
Psalms48:2 The hill of Sion is like a fair plant, whereof all the land rejoiceth: upon the north side lieth the city of the great King." 
Psalms48:3 God is well known in her palaces, that he is the defense of the same." 
Psalms48:4 For lo, kings are gathered, and gone by together." 
Psalms48:5 They marveled, to see such things: they were astonied, and suddenly cast down." 
Psalms48:6 Fear came there upon them, and sorrow as upon a woman in her travail." 
Psalms48:7 Thou shalt break the ships of the sea, thorow the east wind." 
Psalms48:8 Like as we have heard, so see we in the city of the LORD of Hosts, in the city of our God. God upholdeth the same forever. Selah." 
Psalms48:9 We wait for thy loving-kindness (O God) in the middest of thy temple. 
Psalms48:10 O God, according to thy name, so is thy praise unto the world's end: thy right hand is full of righteousness." 
Psalms48:11 Oh let the mount Sion rejoice, and the daughters of Judah be glad, because of thy judgments." 
Psalms48:12 Walk about Sion, go round about her, and tell her towers." 
Psalms48:13 Mark well her walls, set up her houses, that it may be told them that come after." 
Psalms48:14 For this God is our God for ever and ever, and he shall alway be our guide." 
Psalms49:1 To the Chanter, a Psalm of the children of Korah O Hear this, all ye people: ponder it well, all ye that dwell upon the earth." 
Psalms49:2 High and low, rich and poor, one with another." 
Psalms49:3 My mouth shall speak of wisdom, and my heart shall muse of understanding." 
Psalms49:4 I will incline mine ear to the parable, and shew my dark speech upon the harp." 
Psalms49:5 Wherefore should I fear the evil days, when the wickedness of my heels compasseth me round about?" 
Psalms49:6 They that put their trust in their good, and boast(boost) themselves in the multitude of their riches." 
Psalms49:7 No man may deliver his brother, nor make agreement unto him for God.(for him unto God)" 
Psalms49:8 For it costeth more to redeem their souls, so that he must let that alone for ever." 
Psalms49:9 Yea, though he live long, and see not the grave." 
Psalms49:10 For it shall be seen, that such wise men shall die and perish together, as well as the ignorant and foolish, and leave their goods for other." 
Psalms49:11 Look what is in their houses, it continueth still: their dwelling places endure from one generation to another, and are called after their own names upon the earth." 
Psalms49:12 Nevertheless man abideth not in such honor, but is compared unto the brute beasts, and becometh like unto them." 
Psalms49:13 This way of theirs is very foolishness, and yet their posterity praise it with their mouth. Selah." 
Psalms49:14 They lie in the hell like sheep, death shall gnaw upon them, and the righteous shall have domination of them in the morning by times, their strength shall consume, and hell shall be their dwelling." 
Psalms49:15 But God shall deliver my soul from the power of hell, when he receiveth me. Selah." 
Psalms49:16 O be not thou afraid, when one is made rich, and the glory of his house increased." 
Psalms49:17 For he shall carry nothing away with him when he dieth, neither shall his pomp follow him." 
Psalms49:18 While he liveth, he is counted an happy man: and so long as he is in prosperity, men speak good of him." 
Psalms49:19 But when he followeth his fathers' generation, he shall never see light any more." 
Psalms49:20 When a man is in honour and hath no understanding, he is compared unto the brute beasts, and becometh like unto them." 
Psalms50:1 The Psalm of Asaph The LORD even the mighty God hath spoken, and called the world from the rising up of the son unto the going down of the same." 
Psalms50:2 Out of Sion appeareth the glorious beauty of God. 
Psalms50:3 Our God shall come, and not keep silence: there goeth before him a consuming fire, and a mighty tempest round about him." 
Psalms50:4 He shall call the heavens from above, and the earth, that he may judge his people." 
Psalms50:5 Gather my saints together unto me, those that set more by the covenant than by any offering." 
Psalms50:6 And the heavens shall declare his righteousness, for God is judge himself. Selah." 
Psalms50:7 Hear, O my people: let me speak, let me testify among you, O Israel: I am God, even thy God." 
Psalms50:8 I reprove thee not because of thy sacrifice, thy burntofferings are alway before me." 
Psalms50:9 I will take no bullocks out of thy house, nor goats out of thy folds." 
Psalms50:10 For all the beasts of the field are mine, and thousands of cattle upon the hills." 
Psalms50:11 I know all the fowls upon the mountains, and the wild beasts of the field are in my sight." 
Psalms50:12 If I be hungry, I will not tell thee: for the whole world is mine, and all that therein is." 
Psalms50:13 Thinkest thou, that I will eat the flesh of oxen, or drink the blood of goats?" 
Psalms50:14 Offer unto God praise and thanksgiving, and pay thy vows unto the most highest." 
Psalms50:15 And call upon me in the time of trouble, so will I hear thee, that thou shalt thank me." 
Psalms50:16 But unto the ungodly sayeth God: Why doest thou preach my laws, and takest my covenant in thy mouth?" 
Psalms50:17 Where as thou hatest to be reformed, and castest my words behind thee?" 
Psalms50:18 If thou seest a thief, thou runnest with him, and art partaker with the advouterers." 
Psalms50:19 Thou lettest thy mouth speak wickedness, and thy tongue painteth deceit." 
Psalms50:20 Thou sittest and speakest against thy brother, yea and slanderest(sclaundrest) thine own mother's son." 
Psalms50:21 This thou doest, while I hold my tongue: and thinkest me to be even such one as thyself: but I will reprove thee, and set myself against thee." 
Psalms50:22 O consider this, ye that forget God: lest I pluck you away, and there be none to deliver you." 
Psalms50:23 Whoso offereth me thanks and praise, he honoureth me: and this is the way, whereby I will shew him the saving health of God." 
Psalms51:1 To the chanter, a Psalm of David, when the Prophet Nathan came unto him, after he was gone in to Bethsabe. Have mercy upon me (O God) after thy goodness, and according unto thy great mercies, do away mine offenses." 
Psalms51:2 Wash me well from my wickedness, and cleanse me from my sin." 
Psalms51:3 For I knowledge my faults, and my sin is ever before me." 
Psalms51:4 Against thee only, against thee have I sinned, and done evil in thy sight: that thou mightest be justified in thy sayings, and shouldest overcome when thou art judged." 
Psalms51:5 Behold, I was born in wickedness, and in sin hath my mother conceived me." 
Psalms51:6 But lo, thou hast a pleasure in the truth, and hast shewed me secret wisdom." 
Psalms51:7 O purge(reconcile) me with Isope, and I shall be clean: wash thou me, and I shall be whiter than snow." 
Psalms51:8 Oh let me hear of joy and gladness, that the bones which thou hast broken, may rejoice." 
Psalms51:9 Turn thy face from my sins, and put out all my misdeeds." 
Psalms51:10 Make me a clean heart, O God, and renew a right spirit within me." 
Psalms51:11 Cast me not away from thy presence, and take not thy holy spirit from me." 
Psalms51:12 O give me the comfort of thy help again, and establish me with thy free spirit." 
Psalms51:13 Then shall I teach thy ways unto the wicked, that sinners may be converted unto thee." 
Psalms51:14 Deliver me from bloodguiltiness O God, thou that art the God of my health, that my tongue may praise thy righteousness." 
Psalms51:15 Open my lips, O Lord,(LORDE) that my mouth may shew thy praise." 
Psalms51:16 For if thou hadst pleasure in sacrifice, I would give it thee: but thou delightest not in burntofferings." 
Psalms51:17 The sacrifice of God is a troubled spirit, a broken and a contrite heart, O God, shalt thou not despise." 
Psalms51:18 O be favourable and gracious unto Sion, that the walls of Jerusalem may be builded." 
Psalms51:19 For then shalt thou be pleased with the sacrifice of righteousness, with the burntofferings and oblations: then shall they lay bullocks upon thine altar." 
Psalms52:1 To the chanter, an exhortation of David when Doeg the Edomite came to Saul, and shewed him, saying: David is come to the house of Abimelech. Why boastest thou thyself, thou Tyrant, that thou canst do mischief? Where as the goodness of God endureth yet daily." 
Psalms52:2 Thy tongue imagineth wickedness, and with lies it cutteth like a sharp razor.(rasoure)" 
Psalms52:3 Thou lovest ungraciousness more than good, to talk of lies more than righteousness. Selah." 
Psalms52:4 Thou lovest to speak all words that may do hurt, O thou false tongue." 
Psalms52:5 Therefore shall God clean destroy thee, smite thee in pieces, pluck thee out of thy dwelling, and root thee out of the land of the living. Selah." 
Psalms52:6 The righteous shall see this, and fear, and laugh him to scorn." 
Psalms52:7 Lo, this is the man, that took not God for his strength, but trusted unto the multitude of his riches, and was mighty in his wickedness." 
Psalms52:8 As for me, I am like a green olive tree in the house of God, my trust is in the tender mercy of God for ever and ever." 
Psalms52:9 I will alway give thanks unto thee, for that thou hast done: and will hope in thy name, for thy saints like it well." 
Psalms53:1 To the chanter upon Mahalath, an instruction of David. The foolish bodies say in their hearts: Tush, there is no God. Corrupt are they, and become abominable in their wickednesses, there is not one that doth good." 
Psalms53:2 God looked down from heaven upon the children of men, to see if there were any that would understand, or seek after God." 
Psalms53:3 But they are all gone out of the way, they are all become unprofitable: there is none that doth good, no not one." 
Psalms53:4 How can they have understanding, that are the workers of wickedness, eating up my people as it were bread, and call not upon God." 
Psalms53:5 They are afraid, where no fear is: for God breaketh the bones of them that besiege thee: thou puttest them to confusion, for God despiseth them." 
Psalms53:6 Oh that the saving health were given unto Israel out of Sion: Oh that the Lord(God) would deliver his people out of captivity. Then should Jacob rejoice, and Israel should be right glad." 
Psalms54:1 To the chanter in Neginoth, an instruction of David when the Zephites came and said unto Saul: David is hid among us. Help me (O God) for thy name's sake, and deliver me in thy strength." 
Psalms54:2 Hear my prayer, (O God) consider the words of my mouth." 
Psalms54:3 For strangers are risen up against me, and the mighty (which have not God before their eyes) seek after my soul. Selah." 
Psalms54:4 But lo, God is my helper: it is he that upholdeth my soul." 
Psalms54:5 He shall reward evil unto mine enemies: and in thy truth shalt thou destroy them. 
Psalms54:6 A free will offering will I give thee, and praise thy name, O LORD, because it is so comfortable." 
Psalms54:7 For thou hast delivered me out of all my trouble, so that mine eye seeth his desire upon mine enemies." 
Psalms55:1 To the chanter in Neginoth, an instruction of David. Hear my prayer, O God, and hide not thyself from my petition." 
Psalms55:2 Take heed unto me, and hear me, how piteously I mourn and complain." 
Psalms55:3 The enemy crieth so, and the ungodly cometh on so fast: for they are minded to do me some mischief, so maliciously are they set against me." 
Psalms55:4 My heart is heavy within me, and the fear of death is fallen upon me." 
Psalms55:5 Fearfulness and trembling are come upon me, and an horrible dread hath overwhelmed me." 
Psalms55:6 And I said: O that I had wings like a dove, that I might flee somewhere, and be at rest." 
Psalms55:7 Lo, then would I get me away far off, and remain in the wilderness. Selah." 
Psalms55:8 I would make haste to escape, from the stormy wind and tempest." 
Psalms55:9 Destroy their tongues (O Lord)(o LORDE) and divide them, for I see unrighteousness and strife in the city." 
Psalms55:10 This goeth day and night about the walls, mischief and vice are in the middest of it." 
Psalms55:11 Wickedness is therein, deceit and guile go not out of her streets." 
Psalms55:12 If it were mine enemy that reviled me, I could bear it: or if one that ought me evil will did threaten me, I would hide myself from him." 
Psalms55:13 But it is thou my companion, my guide and mine own familiar friend." 
Psalms55:14 We had sweet and secret communication together (and lovingly walked we together) in the house of God. 
Psalms55:15 Let death come hastily upon them, and let them go down quick into hell, for wickedness is among them in their dwellings." 
Psalms55:16 As for me, I will call unto God, and the LORD shall help me." 
Psalms55:17 In the evening, morning and at noon day will I mourn and complain: and he shall hear my voice." 
Psalms55:18 It is he that delivereth my soul in peace, from them that lay wait for me: for they are many against me." 
Psalms55:19 Yea even God that endureth forever, shall hear me, and bring them down, Selah. For they will not turn: and why? they fear not God." 
Psalms55:20 Yea they lay hands upon such as be at peace with him, and so they break his covenant." 
Psalms55:21 Their mouths are softer than butter, and yet have they battle in their mind: their words are smoother than oil, and yet be they very swords." 
Psalms55:22 O cast thy burthen, or care, upon the LORD, he shall nourish thee, and not leave the righteous in unquietness." 
Psalms55:23 But as for them, thou (O God) shalt cast (them) down into the pit of destruction. The bloodthirsty and deceitful shall not live out half their days. Nevertheless my trust is in thee." 
Psalms56:1 To the chanter, upon the domme stock downe: which flyeth far off, Michtam of David, when the Philistines took him in Geth. Be merciful unto me (O God) for men will tread me down: they are daily fighting and troubling me." 
Psalms56:2 Mine enemies tread me daily under their feet, for they be many, that proudly fight against me." 
Psalms56:3 Nevertheless, when I am afraid, I put my trust in thee." 
Psalms56:4 I will comfort myself in God's word, yea I will hope in God, and not fear: What can flesh then do unto me." 
Psalms56:5 They vex me daily in my words: all that they imagine, is to do me evil." 
Psalms56:6 They hold all together, and keep themselves close: they mark my steps, how they may catch my soul." 
Psalms56:7 But in vain, for it shall escape them: and why? thou (O God) in thy displeasure shall cast down such people." 
Psalms56:8 Thou tellest my flittings, thou puttest my tears in thy bottle, and numberst them." 
Psalms56:9 Whensoever I call upon thee, mine enemies are put to flight: whereby I know that thou art my God." 
Psalms56:10 In God's word will I rejoice, in the LORD's word will I comfort me." 
Psalms56:11 Yea in God do I trust, and am not afraid: what can man then do unto me?" 
Psalms56:12 Unto thee (O God) will I pay my vows, unto thee will I give thanks and praise." 
Psalms56:13 For thou hast delivered my soul from death, and my feet from falling, that I may walk before God in the light of the living." 
Psalms57:1 To the Chanter, destroy not Michtam of David, when he fled from Saul into the cave. Be merciful unto me, O God, be merciful unto me, for my soul trusteth in thee: and under the shadow of thy wings shall be my refuge, until wickedness be overpast." 
Psalms57:2 I call unto God the most highest, even to the God that shall help me up again." 
Psalms57:3 He shall send from heaven, and save me from the reproof of him that would swallow me up. Selah. This shall God send, for his mercy and faithfulness' sake." 
Psalms57:4 I lie with my soul among the cruel lions: even among the children of men, whose teeth are spears and arrows, and their tongue a sharp sword." 
Psalms57:5 Set up thyself, O God, above the heavens, and thy glory above all the earth." 
Psalms57:6 They have laid a net for my feet, and pressed down my soul: they have digged a pit before me, and are fallen into it themselves. Selah." 
Psalms57:7 My heart is ready, O God, my heart is ready to sing and give praise." 
Psalms57:8 Awake, O my glory, awake lute and harp, I myself will awake right early." 
Psalms57:9 I will give thanks unto thee, O Lord,(o LORDE) among the people, I will sing praises unto thee among the Heathen." 
Psalms57:10 For the greatness of thy mercy reacheth unto the heavens, and thy faithfulness unto the clouds." 
Psalms57:11 Set up thyself, O God, above the heavens, and thy glory above all the earth." 
Psalms58:1 To the Chanter Destroy not Michtam of David If your minds be upon righteousness in deed, then judge the thing that is right, O ye sons of men." 
Psalms58:2 But ye imagine mischief in your hearts, and your hands deal with wickedness." 
Psalms58:3 The ungodly are froward, even from their mother's womb: as soon as they be born, they go astray and speak lies." 
Psalms58:4 They are as furious as the serpent, even like the deaf Adder that stoppeth her ears." 
Psalms58:5 That she should not hear the voice of the charmer, charm he never so wisely." 
Psalms58:6 Break their teeth (O God) in their mouths, smite the chaft bones of the lions' whelps in sonder, O LORD." 
Psalms58:7 That they may fall away, like water that runneth a pace: and that when they shoot their arrows, they may be broken." 
Psalms58:8 Let them consume away like a snail, and like the untimely fruit of a woman, and let them not see the sunne.(Sonne)" 
Psalms58:9 Or ever your thorns be sharp, that wrath shall take them away quick, like a stormy wind." 
Psalms58:10 The righteous shall rejoice when he seeth the vengeance, and shall wash his feet in the blood of the ungodly." 
Psalms58:11 So that men shall say, verily there is a reward for the righteous: doubtless, there is a God that judgeth the earth." 
Psalms59:1 To the Chanter, destroy not Michtam of David, when Saul sent to watch the house, to the intent to kill him. Deliver me from mine enemies (O my God) and defend me from them that rise up against me." 
Psalms59:2 O deliver me from the wicked doers, and save me from the bloodthirsty men." 
Psalms59:3 For lo, they lie in waiting for my soul: the mighty men are gathered together against me, without any offense or fault of me, O LORD." 
Psalms59:4 They run, and prepare themselves, without my fault: Arise, come thou help me, and behold." 
Psalms59:5 Stand(Stode) up O LORD God of Hosts, thou God of Israel to visit all Heathen: be not merciful unto them that offend of malicious wickedness. Selah." 
Psalms59:6 Let them go to and fro, and run about the city, howling(yowling) like dogs." 
Psalms59:7 Behold, they speak (against me) with their mouth, swords are under their lips, for who reproveth them?" 
Psalms59:8 But thou (O LORD) shalt have them in derision, thou shalt laugh all Heathen to scorn." 
Psalms59:9 My strength do I ascribe unto thee, for thou (O God) art my defender." 
Psalms59:10 God sheweth me his goodness plentiously, God letteth me see my desire upon mine enemies." 
Psalms59:11 Slay them not, lest my people forget it: but scatter them abroad with thy power, and put them down, O Lord my defense.(o LORDE our defence)" 
Psalms59:12 For the sin of their mouth, for the words of their lips, and because of their pride, let them be taken: and why? their preaching is of cursing and lies." 
Psalms59:13 Consume them in thy wrath, consume them that they may perish, and know that it is God, which ruleth in Jacob and in all the world. Selah." 
Psalms59:14 Let them go to and fro, and run about the city, howling(yowling) like dogs." 
Psalms59:15 Let them run here and there for meat, and grudge when they have not enough." 
Psalms59:16 As for me, I will sing of thy power, and praise thy mercy by times(betimes) in the morning: for thou art my defense and refuge in the time of my trouble." 
Psalms59:17 Unto thee (O my strength) will I sing, for thou (O God) art my defense, and my merciful God." 
Psalms60:1 To the chanter, upon the rose of witness, Michtam of David, for to teach: when he fought against Syria of Mesopotamia, and Syria of Zoba: and when Joab turned back, and slew twelve thousand Edomites, in the valley of Salt. O God, thou hast cast us out and scattered us abroad, thou that hast been so sore displeased at us, comfort us again." 
Psalms60:2 Thou that hast removed the land and divided it, heal the sores thereof, for it shaketh." 
Psalms60:3 Thou hast shewed thy people heavy things, thou hast given us a drink of wine that we slombre with all." 
Psalms60:4 Yet hast thou given a token, for such as fear thee, that they may be cast up in the truth. Selah." 
Psalms60:5 That thy beloved might be delivered, help them with thy right hand, and hear me." 
Psalms60:6 God hath spoken in his Sanctuary (which thing rejoiceth me) I will divide Sichem, and mete out the valley of Suchoth." 
Psalms60:7 Gilead is mine, and Manasses is mine, Ephraim is the strength of mine head, Judah is my Captain." 
Psalms60:8 Moab is my washpot, over Edom will I stretch out my shoe, Philistea, shall be glad of me." 
Psalms60:9 Who will lead me into the strong city? Who will bring me into Edom? 
Psalms60:10 Shall not thou do it, O God, thou that hast cast us out: thou God, that wentest not out with our hosts?" 
Psalms60:11 O be thou our help in trouble, for vain is the help of man." 
Psalms60:12 Thorow God we shall do great acts, for it is he that shall tread down our enemies." 
Psalms61:1 To the chanter upon Neginoth, of David. Hear my crying (O God) give heed unto my prayer." 
Psalms61:2 From the ends of the earth will I call unto thee, when my heart is in trouble:" 
Psalms61:3 Oh set me up upon an high rock. For thou art my hope, a strong tower for me against the enemy." 
Psalms61:4 I will dwell in thy tabernacle for ever, that I may be safe under the covering of thy wings. Selah." 
Psalms61:5 For thou, O Lord,(God) hast heard my desires, thou hast given an heritage unto those that fear thy name." 
Psalms61:6 Thou shalt grant the king a long life, that his years may endure thorowout all generations." 
Psalms61:7 That he may dwell before God for ever: Oh let thy loving mercy and faithfulness preserve him. 
Psalms61:8 So will I allway sing praises unto thy name, that I may daily perform my vows." 
Psalms62:1 To the chanter, for by Iduthun a Psalm of David. My soul waiteth only upon God, for of him cometh my help." 
Psalms62:2 He only is my strength, my salvation, my defense, so that I shall not greatly fall." 
Psalms62:3 How long will ye imagine mischief against every man? ye shall be slain all of the sort of you: yea as a tottering wall shall ye be, and like a broken hedge." 
Psalms62:4 Their device is only how to put him out, their delight is in lies: they give good words with their mouth, but curse with their heart. Selah." 
Psalms62:5 Nevertheless, my soul abideth only upon God, for he is my God." 
Psalms62:6 He only is my strength, my salvation, my defense: so that I shall not fall." 
Psalms62:7 In God is my health, my glory, my might and in God is my trust." 
Psalms62:8 O put your trust in him alway, (ye people) pour out your hearts before him, for God is our hope. Selah." 
Psalms62:9 As for men they are but vain, men are deceitful: upon the weights they are altogether lighter than vanity it self." 
Psalms62:10 O trust not in wrong or robbery, give not yourselves unto vanity: if riches increase, set not your heart upon them." 
Psalms62:11 God spake once a word, twice have I heard the same, that power belongeth unto God." 
Psalms62:12 That thou Lord(LORDE) art merciful, and that thou rewardest every man according to his works." 
Psalms63:1 A Psalm of David, when he was in the wilderness of Juda. O God, thou art my God, early will I seek thee, my soul thursteth for thee, my flesh longeth after thee in a barren and dry land, where no water is." 
Psalms63:2 Thus do I look for thee in thy Sanctuary, that I might behold thy power and glory." 
Psalms63:3 For thy loving-kindness is better than life, my lips shall praise thee." 
Psalms63:4 As long as I live will I magnify thee and lift up my hands in thy name. 
Psalms63:5 My soul is satisfied even as it were with marry and fatness, when my mouth praiseth thee with joyful lips." 
Psalms63:6 In my bed will I remember thee, and when I wake my talking shall be of thee." 
Psalms63:7 For thou hast been my helper, and under the shadow of thy wings will I rejoice." 
Psalms63:8 My soul hangeth upon thee, thy right hand upholdeth me." 
Psalms63:9 They seek after my soul, but in vain, for they shall go under the earth." 
Psalms63:10 They shall fall into the sword, and be a portion for foxes." 
Psalms63:11 But the king shall rejoice in God: all they that swear by him, shall be commended, for the mouth of liars shall be stopped." 
Psalms64:1 To the chanter a Psalm of David. Hear my voice, O God, in my complaint, preserve my life from fear of the enemy." 
Psalms64:2 Hide me from the gathering together of the froward, from the heap of wicked doers." 
Psalms64:3 Which whet their tongues like a sword, and shoot with venomous words like as with arrows." 
Psalms64:4 That they may privy hurt the innocent, and suddenly hit him without any fear." 
Psalms64:5 They have devised mischief, and communed among themselves, how they may lay snares: tush (say they) Who shall see them?" 
Psalms64:6 They imagine wickedness, and keep it secret among themselves, every man in the deep of his heart." 
Psalms64:7 But God shall suddenly shoot with an arrow, that they shall be wounded." 
Psalms64:8 Yea their own tongues shall make them fall, in so much that who so seeth them, shall laugh them to scorn." 
Psalms64:9 And all men that see it, shall say: this hath God done, for they shall perceive that it is his work." 
Psalms64:10 The righteous shall rejoice in the LORD, and put his trust in him: and all they that are true of heart, shall be glad thereof." 
Psalms65:1 To the chanter, a Psalm and song of David. Thou, O God, art praised in Sion, and unto thee is the vow performed." 
Psalms65:2 Thou hearest the prayer, therefore cometh all flesh unto thee." 
Psalms65:3 Our misdeeds prevail against us, oh be thou merciful unto our sins." 
Psalms65:4 Blessed is the man whom thou choosest and receivest unto thee, that he may dwell in thy court: he shall be satisfied with the pleasures of thy house, even of thy holy temple." 
Psalms65:5 Hear us, according unto thy wonderful righteousness, O God our salvation: thou that art the hope of all the ends of the earth, and of the broad sea." 
Psalms65:6 Which in his strength setteth fast the mountains, and is girded about with power:" 
Psalms65:7 Which stilleth the raging of the sea, the roaring of his waves, and the woodness of the people." 
Psalms65:8 They that dwell in the uttermost(utmost) parts are afraid at thy tokens, thou makest both the morning and evening stars to praise thee." 
Psalms65:9 Thou visitest the earth, thou waterest it, and makest it very plenteous." 
Psalms65:10 The river of God is full of waters, thou preparest man his corn, and thus thou providest for the earth." 
Psalms65:11 Thou waterest her furrows, thou breakest the hard clots thereof, thou makest it soft with the drops of rain, and blessest the increase of it." 
Psalms65:12 Thou crownest the year with thy good, and thy footsteps drop fatness." 
Psalms65:13 The dwellings of the wilderness are fat also, that they drop down withal: and the little hills are pleasant on every side. The folds are full of sheep, the valleys stand so thick with corn that they laugh and sing." 
Psalms66:1 To the chanter, a Psalm of David. O be joyful in God, all ye lands," 
Psalms66:2 sing praises unto the honor of his name, make his praise to be glorious." 
Psalms66:3 Say unto God: O how wonderful are thy works? thorow the greatness of thy power shall thine enemies be confounded. 
Psalms66:4 O that all the world would worship thee, sing of thee, and praise thy name. Selah." 
Psalms66:5 O come hither and behold the works of God, which is so wonderful in his doings among the children of men." 
Psalms66:6 He turned the sea into dry land, so that they went thorow the water on foot: therefore will we rejoice in him." 
Psalms66:7 He ruleth with his power for ever, his eyes behold the people: the runagates shall not be able to exalt themselves. Selah." 
Psalms66:8 O magnify our God, ye people, make the voice of his praise to be heard." 
Psalms66:9 Which holdeth our soul in life, and suffereth not our feet to slip." 
Psalms66:10 For thou, O God, hast proved us, thou hast tried us like as silver is tried." 
Psalms66:11 Thou hast brought us into captivity, and laid trouble upon our loins." 
Psalms66:12 Thou hast suffered men to ride over our heads, we went thorow fire and water, but thou hast brought us out, and refreshed us." 
Psalms66:13 Therefore will I go into thy house with brent offerings, to pay thee my vows," 
Psalms66:14 which I promised with my lips, and spake with my mouth, when I was in trouble." 
Psalms66:15 I will offer unto thee fat brentsacrifices with the smoke of rams, I will offer bullocks with goats. Selah." 
Psalms66:16 O come hither and harken, all ye that fear God, I will tell you, what he hath done for my soul." 
Psalms66:17 I called unto him with my mouth, and gave him praises with my tongue." 
Psalms66:18 If I incline unto wickedness with my heart, the Lord(LORDE) will not hear me." 
Psalms66:19 Therefore God hath heard me, and considered the voice of my prayer." 
Psalms66:20 Praised be God, which hath not cast out my prayer, nor turned his mercy from me." 
Psalms67:1 To the chanter, in Neginoth, a Psalm and song. God be merciful unto us, bless us, and shew the light of his countenance upon us. Selah." 
Psalms67:2 That we may know thy way upon earth, thy saving health among all Heathen." 
Psalms67:3 Let the people praise thee, O God, yea let all people praise thee." 
Psalms67:4 O let the people rejoice and be glad, that thou judgest the folk righteously, and governest the nations upon earth." 
Psalms67:5 Let the people praise thee, O God, let all people praise thee." 
Psalms67:6 God, even our own God, give us his blessings, that the earth may bring forth her increase." 
Psalms67:7 God bless us, and let all the ends of the world fear him." 
Psalms68:1 To the chanter, a Psalm and song of David. Let God arise, so shall his enemies be scattered, and they that hate him, shall flee before him." 
Psalms68:2 Like as the smoke vanisheth, so shalt thou drive them away: and like as wax melteth at the fire, so shall the ungodly perish at the presence of God." 
Psalms68:3 But the righteous shall be glad and rejoice before God, they shall be merry and joyful." 
Psalms68:4 Oh sing unto God, sing praises to his name: magnify him that rideth above the heavens (whose name is the LORD) and rejoice before him." 
Psalms68:5 He is a father of the fatherless, he is a defender of widows: even God in his holy habitation." 
Psalms68:6 He is the God that maketh men to be of one mind in a house, and bringeth the prisoners out of captivity in due season, but letteth the runagates continue in scarceness." 
Psalms68:7 O God, when thou wentest forth before the(thy) people, when thou wentest thorow the wilderness. Selah:" 
Psalms68:8 The earth shook, and the heavens dropped at the presence of God in Sinai, at the presence of God, which is the God of Israel." 
Psalms68:9 Thou, O God sendest a gracious rain, upon thine inheritance, and refreshest it, when it is dry." 
Psalms68:10 That thy beasts may dwell therein, which thou of thy goodness hast prepared for the poor." 
Psalms68:11 The Lord(LORDE) shall give the word, with great hosts of Evangelists." 
Psalms68:12 Kings with their armies shall flee, and they of the household shall divide the spoil." 
Psalms68:13 If so be that ye lie among the pales, the dove's feathers shall be covered with silver, and her wings the color of gold." 
Psalms68:14 When the Almighty setteth kings upon the earth, it shall be clear even in the darkness." 
Psalms68:15 The hill of Basan is God's hill, the hill of Basan is a plenteous hill." 
Psalms68:16 Why hop ye so, ye great hills? It pleaseth God to dwell upon this hill, yea the LORD will abide in it for ever." 
Psalms68:17 The chariots of God are many thousand times a thousand, the Lord(LORDE) is among them in the holy Sinai." 
Psalms68:18 Thou art gone up on high,(an hye) thou hast led captivity captive, and received gifts for men: yea even for thine enemies, that they might dwell with the LORD God." 
Psalms68:19 Praised be the Lord(LORDE) daily, even the God which helpeth us, and poureth his benefits upon us. Selah." 
Psalms68:20 The God that is our Saviour, even GOD the Lord by whom we escape death." 
Psalms68:21 The God that smiteth the(his) enemies upon the heads and upon the hairy scalps: such as go on still in their wickedness. 
Psalms68:22 The Lord hath said: some will I bring away from Basan, some will I bring again from the deep of the sea." 
Psalms68:23 That thy foot may be dipped in the blood of thine enemies, and that thy dogs may lick it up." 
Psalms68:24 It is well seen, O God, how thou goest, how thou my God and King goest in the Sanctuary." 
Psalms68:25 The singers go before, and then the minstrels among the maidens with the tumbrels." 
Psalms68:26 O give thanks unto God the LORD(Lorde)(the LORDE) in the congregation, for the wells of Israel." 
Psalms68:27 There little Benjamin, the princes of Judah, the princes of Zabulon, and the princes of Nephtali bear rule among them." 
Psalms68:28 Thy God hath committed strength unto thee, stablish the thing, O God, that thou hast wrought in us." 
Psalms68:29 For thy temple's sake at Jerusalem shall kings bring presents unto thee. 
Psalms68:30 Reprove the beasts among the reeds, the heap of bulls with the calves: those that drive for money. Oh scatter the people that delight in battle." 
Psalms68:31 The princes shall come out of Egypt, the Morians land shall stretch out her hands unto God." 
Psalms68:32 Sing unto God, O ye kingdoms of the earth: O sing praises unto the Lord.(LORDE) Selah." 
Psalms68:33 Which setteth in the heavens over all from the beginning: Lo, he shall send out his voice, yea and that a mighty voice." 
Psalms68:34 Ascribe ye the power unto God, his glory is in Israel, and his might in the clouds." 
Psalms68:35 God is wonderful in his Sanctuary, he is the God of Israel, he will give strength and power unto his people. Blessed be God." 
Psalms69:1 To the chanter upon Sosanim of David. Help me, O God, for the waters are come in, even unto my soul." 
Psalms69:2 I stick fast in the deep mire, where no ground is, I am come into deep waters, and the floods will drown me." 
Psalms69:3 I am weary of crying, my throat is dry, my sight faileth me, for waiting so long upon my God." 
Psalms69:4 They that hate me without a cause, are more than the hairs of my head, they that are mine enemies and would destroy me guiltless are mighty: I am fain to pay the things that I never took." 
Psalms69:5 God thou knowest my simpleness, and my faults are not hid from thee." 
Psalms69:6 Let not them that trust in thee, O Lord GOD(LORDE God) of Hosts, be ashamed for my cause: let not those that seek thee, be confounded thorow me, O God of Israel." 
Psalms69:7 And why? for thy sake do I suffer reproof, shame covereth my face." 
Psalms69:8 I am become a stranger unto my brethren, and an alien unto my mother's children." 
Psalms69:9 For the zeal of thine house hath even eaten me, and the rebukes of them that rebuked thee, is fallen upon me." 
Psalms69:10 I wept and chastened myself with fasting, and that was turned to my reproof." 
Psalms69:11 I put on a sackcloth, and therefore they jested upon me." 
Psalms69:12 They that sat in the gate, spake against me, and the drunkards made songs upon me." 
Psalms69:13 But LORD I made my prayer unto thee in an acceptable time. Hear me, O God, with thy great mercy and sure help." 
Psalms69:14 Take me out of the mire, that I sink not: Oh let me be delivered from them that hate me, and out of the deep waters." 
Psalms69:15 Lest the water flood drown me, that the deep swallow me not up, and that the pit shut not her mouth upon me." 
Psalms69:16 Hear me, O LORD, for thy loving-kindness is comfortable: turn thee unto me according unto thy great mercy." 
Psalms69:17 Hide not thy face from thy servant, for I am in trouble: O haste thee to help me." 
Psalms69:18 Draw nigh unto my soul, and save it: Oh deliver me because of mine enemies." 
Psalms69:19 Thou knowest my reproof, and my shame and my dishonor: mine adversaries are all in thy sight." 
Psalms69:20 The rebuke breaketh my heart, and maketh me heavy: I look for some to have pity upon me, but there is no man: and for some to comfort me, but I find none." 
Psalms69:21 They gave me gall to eat, and when I was thirsty they gave me vinegar to drink." 
Psalms69:22 Let their table be made a snare to take themselves withal, an occasion to fall and a reward unto them." 
Psalms69:23 Let their eyes be blinded, that they see not: and ever bow down their backs." 
Psalms69:24 Pour out thine indignation upon them, and let thy wrathful displeasure take hold of them." 
Psalms69:25 Let their habitation be void, and no man to dwell in their tents." 
Psalms69:26 For they persecute him whom thou hast smitten, and beside thy wounds they have given him more." 
Psalms69:27 Let them fall from one wickedness to another, and not come into thy righteousness." 
Psalms69:28 Let them be wiped out of the book of the living, and not be written among the righteous." 
Psalms69:29 As for me, I am poor and in heaviness, let thy help defend me, O God." 
Psalms69:30 That I may praise the name of God with a song, and magnify it with thanksgiving." 
Psalms69:31 This shall please the LORD better than a bullock, that hath horns and hoofs." 
Psalms69:32 O consider this and be glad (ye that be in adversity) seek after God and your soul shall live. 
Psalms69:33 For the LORD heareth the poor, and despiseth not his prisoners." 
Psalms69:34 Let heaven and earth praise him, the sea, and all that moveth therein." 
Psalms69:35 For God will save Sion, and build the cities of Judah, that men may dwell there, and have them in possession." 
Psalms69:36 The seed of his servants shall inherit it, and they that love his name shall dwell therein." 
Psalms70:1 To the chanter, of David to bring to remembrance. Haste thee, O God, to deliver me, and to help me, O LORD." 
Psalms70:2 Let them be shamed and confounded that seek after my soul: let them be turned backward, and put to confusion, that wish me evil." 
Psalms70:3 Let them soon be brought to shame, that cry over me, there there." 
Psalms70:4 But let all those that seek thee, be joyful and glad in thee: and let all such as delight in thy saving health, say alway: the Lord(God) be praised." 
Psalms70:5 As for me, I am poor and in misery, haste thee God for to help me. Thou art my help, my redeemer, and my GOD: oh make no long tarrying." 
Psalms71:1 In thee, O LORD, is my trust, let me never be put to confusion," 
Psalms71:2 but rid me and deliver me thorow thy righteousness: incline thine ear unto me, and help me." 
Psalms71:3 Be thou my stronghold, (whereunto I may alway flee) thou that hast promised to help me: for thou art my house of defense and my castle." 
Psalms71:4 Deliver me, O my God, out of the hand of the ungodly, out of the hand of the unrighteous and cruel man." 
Psalms71:5 For thou, O Lord GOD,(LORDE GOD) art the thing that I long for, thou art my hope even from my youth." 
Psalms71:6 I have leaned upon thee ever since I was born, thou art he that took me out of my mother's womb, therefore is my praise alway of thee." 
Psalms71:7 I am become a wonder unto the multitude, but my sure trust is in thee." 
Psalms71:8 Oh let my mouth be filled with thy praise and honour all the day long. 
Psalms71:9 Cast me not away in mine old age, forsake me not when my strength faileth me." 
Psalms71:10 For mine enemies speak against me, and they that lay wait for my soul, take their counsel together," 
Psalms71:11 saying: God hath forsaken him, persecute him, take him, for there is none to help him." 
Psalms71:12 Go not far from me, O God: my God, haste thee to help me." 
Psalms71:13 Let them be confounded and perish, that are against my soul: let them be covered with shame and dishonour, that seek to do me evil." 
Psalms71:14 As for me, I will patiently abide alway, and will ever increase thy praise." 
Psalms71:15 My mouth shall speak of thy righteousness and saving health all the day long, for I know no end thereof." 
Psalms71:16 Let me go in (O Lord GOD)(LORDE GOD) and I will make mention of thy power and righteousness only. 
Psalms71:17 Thou, O God, hast learned me from my youth up until now, therefore will I tell of thy wonderous works." 
Psalms71:18 Forsake me not, O God, in mine old age, when I am grey headed: until I have shewed thy arm unto children's children, and thy power to all them that are yet for to come." 
Psalms71:19 Thy righteousness, O God, is very high, thou that doest great things: O God, who is like unto thee?" 
Psalms71:20 O what great troubles and adversities hast thou shewed me? and yet didst thou turn and refresh me: yea and broughtest me from the deep of the earth again. 
Psalms71:21 Thou hast brought me to great honour, and comforted me on every side." 
Psalms71:22 Therefore will I praise thee and thy faithfulness, O God, playing on the lute, unto thee will I sing upon the harp, O thou holy one of Israel." 
Psalms71:23 My lips would fain sing praises unto thee: and so would my soul whom thou hast delivered. 
Psalms71:24 My tongue talketh of thy righteousness all the day long, for they are confounded and brought unto shame, that sought to do me evil." 
Psalms72:1 Of Solomon Give the king thy judgments, O God, and thy righteousness unto the king's son." 
Psalms72:2 That he may govern thy people according to right, and defend thy poor." 
Psalms72:3 That the mountains may bring peace, and the little hills righteousness unto the people." 
Psalms72:4 He shall keep the simple folk by their right, defend the children of the poor, and punish the wrongous doer." 
Psalms72:5 Thou shalt be feared as long as the sun and moon endureth, from one generation to another." 
Psalms72:6 He shall come down like the rain in to a fleece of wool, and like the drops that water the earth." 
Psalms72:7 In his time shall righteousness flourish, yea and abundance of peace, so long as the moon endureth." 
Psalms72:8 His dominion shall be from the one sea to the other, and from the flood unto the worlds end." 
Psalms72:9 They that dwell in the wilderness shall kneel before him, and his enemies shall lick the dust." 
Psalms72:10 The kings of the sea and of the isles shall bring presents, the kings of Araby and Saba shall offer gifts." 
Psalms72:11 All kings shall worship him, all Heathen shall do him service." 
Psalms72:12 For he shall deliver the poor when he crieth, and the needy that hath no help." 
Psalms72:13 He shall be favorable to the simple and poor, he shall preserve the souls of such as be in adversity." 
Psalms72:14 He shall deliver their souls from extortion and wrong, and dear shall their blood be in his sight." 
Psalms72:15 He shall live, and unto him shall be given of the gold of Arabia: prayer shall be made ever unto him, and daily shall he be praised." 
Psalms72:16 There shall be an heap of corn in the earth high upon the hills, his fruit shall shake like Libanus, and shall be green in the city, like grass upon the earth." 
Psalms72:17 His name shall endure forever, his name shall remain under the sun among the posterities, which shall be blessed thorow him, and all the Heathen shall praise him." 
Psalms72:18 Blessed be the LORD God, even the God of Israel, which only doth wonderous things." 
Psalms72:19 And blessed be the name of his majesty forever, and all the lands be fulfilled with his glory. Amen, Amen." 
Psalms72:20 Here end the prayers of David the son of Jesse. 
Psalms73:1 A Psalm of Asaph. O How loving is God unto Israel, to such as are of a clean heart?" 
Psalms73:2 Nevertheless my feet were almost gone, my treadings had well nigh slipped." 
Psalms73:3 And why? I was grieved at the wicked, to see the ungodly in such prosperity." 
Psalms73:4 For they are in no peril of death, but stand fast like a palace." 
Psalms73:5 They come in no misfortune like other folk, neither are they plagued like other men." 
Psalms73:6 And this is the cause that they be so puffed(puft) up in pride, and overwhelmed with cruelty and unrighteousness." 
Psalms73:7 Their eyes swell for fatness, they do even what they list." 
Psalms73:8 Corrupt are they, and speak blasphemies maliciously, proud and presumptuous are their words." 
Psalms73:9 They stretch forth their mouth unto the heaven, and their tongue goeth thorow the world." 
Psalms73:10 Therefore fall the people unto them, and there out suck they no small advantage." 
Psalms73:11 Tush (say they) How should God perceive it? is there knowledge in the most highest? 
Psalms73:12 Lo, these are the ungodly, these prosper in the world, these have riches in possession." 
Psalms73:13 Should I then cleanse my heart in vain, (thought I) and wash my hands in innocency?" 
Psalms73:14 Wherefore should I be then punished daily, and be chastened every morning?" 
Psalms73:15 Yea I had almost also said even as they do: but lo, then should I have condemned the generation of thy children." 
Psalms73:16 Then thought I to understand this, but it was too hard for me." 
Psalms73:17 Until I went into the Sanctuary of God, and considered the end of these men." 
Psalms73:18 Namely how thou hast set them in a slippery place, that thou mayest cast them down headlong(headlynges) and destroy them." 
Psalms73:19 O how suddenly do they consume, perish and come to a fearful end." 
Psalms73:20 Yea even like as a dream when one awaketh, so makest thou their image to vanish out of the city." 
Psalms73:21 Thus my heart was grieved, and it went even thorow my reins."
