Psalms101:1 A Psalm of David My song shall be of mercy and judgment, yea unto thee (O LORD) will I sing." 
Psalms101:2 Oh let me have understanding in the ways of Godliness, until the time that thou come unto me: and so shall I walk in my house with an innocent heart." 
Psalms101:3 I will take no wicked thing in hand, I hate the sin of unfaithfulness, it shall not cleave unto me." 
Psalms101:4 A froward heart shall depart from me I will not know a wicked person. 
Psalms101:5 Who so privily slandereth(sclaundereth) his neighbour, him will I destroy: who so hath a proud look and an high(hye) stomach, I may not away with him." 
Psalms101:6 Mine eyes shall look for such as be faithful in the land, that they may dwell with me: and who so leadeth a godly life, shall be my servant." 
Psalms101:7 There shall no deceitful person dwell in my house, he that telleth lies shall not tarry in my sight." 
Psalms101:8 I shall soon destroy all the ungodly of the land, that all wicked doers may be rooted out of the city of the LORD." 
Psalms102:1 A prayer of the afflicted, when he was in woe, and poured out his complaint before the LORD. Hear my prayer, O LORD, and let my crying come unto thee." 
Psalms102:2 Hide not thy face from me in the time of my trouble incline thine ears unto me when I call, O hear me and that right soon." 
Psalms102:3 For my days are consumed away like smoke, and my bones are brent up as it were a fire brand." 
Psalms102:4 My heart is smitten down and withered like grass, so that I forget to eat my bread." 
Psalms102:5 For the voice of my groaning, my bone will scarce cleave to my flesh." 
Psalms102:6 I am become like a pelican in the wilderness, and like an owl in a broken wall." 
Psalms102:7 I wake, and am even as it were a sparrow sitting alone upon the house top." 
Psalms102:8 Mine enemies revile me all the day long, they laugh me to scorn, and are sworn together against me." 
Psalms102:9 I eat ashes with my bread, and mingle my drink with weeping;" 
Psalms102:10 And that because of thine indignation and wrath, for thou hast taken me up, and cast me away." 
Psalms102:11 My days are gone like a shadow, and I am withered like grass." 
Psalms102:12 But thou, O LORD, endurest for ever, and thy remembrance thorowout all generations." 
Psalms102:13 Arise therefore and have mercy upon Sion, for it is time to have mercy upon her, yea, the time is come." 
Psalms102:14 And why? thy servants have a love to her stones, and it pitieth them to see her in the dust." 
Psalms102:15 The Heathen shall fear thy name, O LORD, and all the kings of the earth thy majesty." 
Psalms102:16 For the LORD shall build up Sion, and shall appear in his glory." 
Psalms102:17 He turneth him to the prayer of the poor destitute, and despiseth not their desire." 
Psalms102:18 This shall be written for those that come after, that the people which shall be born, may praise the LORD." 
Psalms102:19 For he looketh down from his Sanctuary, out of the heaven doth the LORD behold the earth." 
Psalms102:20 That he may hear the mournings of such as be in captivity, and deliver the children of death." 
Psalms102:21 That they may preach the name of the LORD in Sion, and his worship at Jerusalem." 
Psalms102:22 When the people are gathered together, and the kingdoms also to serve the LORD." 
Psalms102:23 He hath brought down my strength in my journey, and shortened my days." 
Psalms102:24 Yet will I say: O my God, take me not away in the middest of my age: as for thy years, they endure thorowout all generations." 
Psalms102:25 Thou LORD in the beginning hast laid the foundation of the earth, and the heavens are the works of thy hands." 
Psalms102:26 They shall perish, but thou shalt endure: they all shall wax old as doth a garment, and as a vesture shalt thou change them, and they shall be changed." 
Psalms102:27 But thou art the same, and thy years shall not fail." 
Psalms102:28 The children of thy servants shall continue, and their seed shall prosper in thy sight." 
Psalms103:1 Of David. Praise the LORD, O my soul, and all that is within me praise his holy name." 
Psalms103:2 Praise the LORD, O my soul, and forget not all his benefits." 
Psalms103:3 Which forgiveth all thy sins, and healeth all thy infirmities." 
Psalms103:4 Which saveth thy life from destruction, and crowneth thee with mercy, and loving-kindness." 
Psalms103:5 Which satisfieth thy desire with good things, making thee young and lusty as an Aegle." 
Psalms103:6 The LORD executeth righteousness and judgment, for all them that suffer wrong." 
Psalms103:7 He shewed his ways unto Moses, and his works unto the children of Israel." 
Psalms103:8 The LORD is full of compassion and mercy, longsuffering, and of great goodness." 
Psalms103:9 He will not alway be chiding, neither will he keep his anger forever." 
Psalms103:10 He hath not dealt with us after our sins, nor rewarded us according to our wickedness." 
Psalms103:11 For look how high(hye) the heaven is in comparison of the earth, so great is his mercy also toward them that fear him." 
Psalms103:12 Look how wide the east is from the west, so far hath he set our sins from us." 
Psalms103:13 Yea like as a father pitieth his own children, even so is the LORD merciful unto them that fear him." 
Psalms103:14 For he knoweth whereof we be made, he remembereth that we are but dust." 
Psalms103:15 That a man in his time is but as grass, and flourisheth as a flower of the field." 
Psalms103:16 For as soon as the wind goeth over it, it is gone, and the place thereof knoweth it no more." 
Psalms103:17 But the merciful goodness of the LORD endureth for ever and ever, upon them that fear him, and his righteousness upon their children's children." 
Psalms103:18 Such as keep his covenant, and think upon his commandments to do them." 
Psalms103:19 The LORD hath prepared his seat in heaven, and his kingdom ruleth over all." 
Psalms103:20 O praise the LORD ye angels of his, ye that be mighty in strength, fulfilling his commandment that men may hear the voice of his words." 
Psalms103:21 O praise the LORD all ye his Hosts, ye servants of his, that do his pleasure." 
Psalms103:22 O speak good of the LORD all ye works of his, in every place of his dominion: praise thou the LORD, O my soul." 
Psalms104:1 Praise the LORD O my soul: O LORD my God, thou art become exceedingly glorious, thou art clothed with majesty and honour." 
Psalms104:2 Thou deckest thyself with light, as it were with a garment, thou spreadest out the heaven like a curtain." 
Psalms104:3 Thou boltest it above with waters, thou makest the clouds thy chariot, and goest upon the wings of the wind." 
Psalms104:4 Thou makest thine angels spirits, and thy ministers flames of fire." 
Psalms104:5 Thou hast laid the earth upon her foundation, that it never moveth at any time." 
Psalms104:6 Thou coveredst it with the deep like as with a garment, so that the waters stand above the hills." 
Psalms104:7 But at thy rebuke they flee, at the voice of thy thunder they are afraid." 
Psalms104:8 (Then are the hills seen aloft, and the valleys beneath in their place which thou hast appointed for them)" 
Psalms104:9 Thou hast set them their bounds, which they may not pass, that they turn not again to cover the earth." 
Psalms104:10 Thou causest the wells to spring up among the valleys, and the waters to run among the hills." 
Psalms104:11 That all the beasts of the field may have drink, and that the wild asses may quench their thirst." 
Psalms104:12 Above upon the hills have the fowls of the air their habitation, and sing among the branches." 
Psalms104:13 Thou waterest the hills from above, the earth is filled with the fruits of thy works." 
Psalms104:14 Thou bringest forth grass for the cattle, and green herbs for the service of men." 
Psalms104:15 Thou bringest food out of the earth: wine to make glad the heart of man, oil to make him a cheerful countenance and bread to strengthen man's heart." 
Psalms104:16 The trees of the LORD are full of sap, even the trees of Lebanus which he hath planted." 
Psalms104:17 There make the birds their nests, and the fir hills(trees) are a dwelling for the stork." 
Psalms104:18 The hills are a refuge for the wild goats, and so are the stony rocks for the conies." 
Psalms104:19 Thou hast appointed the Moon for certain seasons, the sun(Sonne) knoweth his going down." 
Psalms104:20 Thou makest darkness, that it may be night, wherein all the beasts of the forest do move." 
Psalms104:21 Yea the young lions which roar after their prey, and seek their meat at God." 
Psalms104:22 But when the sun(Sonne) ariseth, they get them away together, and lie them down in their dens." 
Psalms104:23 Then goeth forth man to his work, and to till his land until the evening." 
Psalms104:24 O LORD, how manifold are thy works, right wisely hast thou made them all: yea the earth is full of thy riches." 
Psalms104:25 So is this great and wide sea also, wherein are things creeping innumerable, both small and great beasts." 
Psalms104:26 There go the ships over, and there is that Leviathan, whom thou hast made, to take his pastime therein." 
Psalms104:27 They wait all upon thee, that thou mayest give them meat in due season." 
Psalms104:28 When thou givest it them, they gather it: when thou openest thine hand, they are filled with good." 
Psalms104:29 But when thou hidest thy face, they are sorrowful: if thou takest away their breath, they die, and are turned again to their dust." 
Psalms104:30 Again, when thou lettest thy breath go forth, they are made, and so thou renewest the face of the earth." 
Psalms104:31 The glorious majesty of the LORD endureth for ever, and the LORD rejoiceth in his works." 
Psalms104:32 The earth trembleth at the look of him, he doth but touch the hills and they smoke." 
Psalms104:33 I will sing unto the LORD as long as I live, I will praise my God while I have my being." 
Psalms104:34 O that my words might please him, for my joy is in the LORD." 
Psalms104:35 As for the sinners, they shall be consumed out of the earth, and the ungodly shall come to an end: but praise thou the LORD, O my soul. Praise the everlasting.(Halleluya)" 
Psalms105:1 O give thanks unto the LORD, and call upon his name: tell the people what things he hath done." 
Psalms105:2 O let your songs be of him: praise him, and let your talking be of all his wonderous works." 
Psalms105:3 Give his holy name a good report, let their hearts rejoice that seek the LORD." 
Psalms105:4 Seek the LORD, and his strength, seek his face evermore." 
Psalms105:5 Remember the marvelous works that he hath done, his wonders and the judgments of his mouth." 
Psalms105:6 O ye seed of Abraham his servant, ye children of Jacob his chosen." 
Psalms105:7 He is the LORD our God, whose punishments are thorowout all the world." 
Psalms105:8 He is alway mindful of his covenant, and promise that he made to a thousand generations." 
Psalms105:9 Yea the covenant that he made with Abraham, and the oath that he sware unto Isaac." 
Psalms105:10 And appointed the same unto Jacob for a law, and to Israel for an everlasting testament." 
Psalms105:11 Saying: Unto thee will I give the land of Canaan, the lot of your heritage." 
Psalms105:12 When there was yet but a few of them, and the(they) strangers therein." 
Psalms105:13 What time as they went from one nation to another, from one kingdom to another." 
Psalms105:14 He suffered no man to hurt them, but reproved even kings for their sakes." 
Psalms105:15 Touch not mine anointed, do my prophets no harm." 
Psalms105:16 Moreover he called for a darth upon the land, and destroyed all the provision of bread." 
Psalms105:17 But he had sent a man before them, even Joseph which was sold to be a bond servant." 
Psalms105:18 They hurt his feet in the stocks, the iron pierced his heart." 
Psalms105:19 Until the time that his word came, and until the word of the LORD had tried him." 
Psalms105:20 Then sent the king and caused him to be delivered, the prince of the people had let him go." 
Psalms105:21 He made him lord of his house, and ruler of all his substance." 
Psalms105:22 That he might enforme his princes after his will, and teach his Senators wisdom." 
Psalms105:23 Israel also came into Egypt, and Jacob was a stranger in the land of Ham." 
Psalms105:24 But he increased his people exceedingly, and made them stronger than their enemies." 
Psalms105:25 Whose heart turned, so that they hated his people, and dealt untruly with his servants." 
Psalms105:26 Then sent he Moses his servant, and Aaron whom he had chosen." 
Psalms105:27 These did his tokens among them, and wonders in the land of Ham." 
Psalms105:28 He sent darkness and it was dark, for they were not obedient unto his word." 
Psalms105:29 He turned their waters into blood, and slew their fish." 
Psalms105:30 Their land brought forth frogs, yea even in their king's chambers." 
Psalms105:31 He spake the word, and there came all manner of flies and lice in all their quarters." 
Psalms105:32 He gave them hail stones for rain, and flames of fire in their land." 
Psalms105:33 He smote their vineyards and fig trees, and destroyed the trees that were in their coasts." 
Psalms105:34 He spake the word, and there were(came) grasshoppers and caterpillars innumerable." 
Psalms105:35 These ate up all the grass in their land, and devoured the fruits of their ground." 
Psalms105:36 He smote all the firstborn in their land, even the chief of all their substance." 
Psalms105:37 He brought them forth with silver and gold, there was not one feeble person among their tribes." 
Psalms105:38 Egypt was glad of their departing: for they were afraid of them. 
Psalms105:39 He spread out a cloud to be a covering, and fire to give light in the night season." 
Psalms105:40 At their desire, there came quails, and he filled them with the bread of heaven." 
Psalms105:41 He opened the rock of stone, and the waters flowed out: so that rivers ran in the wilderness:" 
Psalms105:42 For why? He remembered his holy promise which he had made to Abraham his servant. 
Psalms105:43 Thus he brought forth his people with joy, and his chosen with gladness." 
Psalms105:44 And gave them the lands of the Heathen, where they took the labours of the people in possession." 
Psalms105:45 That they might keep his statutes, and observe his laws. Praise the everlasting. (Halleluya)" 
Psalms106:1 Praise the everlasting. (Halleluya) O give thanks unto the LORD, for he is gracious, and his mercy endureth for ever." 
Psalms106:2 Who can express the noble acts of the LORD, or shew forth all his praise?" 
Psalms106:3 Blessed are they that alway keep judgment, and do righteousness." 
Psalms106:4 Remember us, O LORD, according to the favour that thou bearest unto thy people: O visit us with thy saving health." 
Psalms106:5 That we might see the pleasure of thy chosen, that we might rejoice in the gladness of thy people, and give thanks with thine inheritance." 
Psalms106:6 We have sinned with our fathers, we have done amiss, we have dealt wickedly." 
Psalms106:7 Our fathers regarded not thy wonders in Egypt, they kept not thy great goodness in remembrance: but were disobedient at the sea,(see) even at the reed sea.(see)" 
Psalms106:8 Nevertheless, he helped them for his name's sake, that he might make his power to be known." 
Psalms106:9 He rebuked the reed sea,(see) and it was dried up: so he led them thorow the deep as in a wilderness." 
Psalms106:10 Thus he saved them from the hand of the hater, and delivered them from the hand of the enemy." 
Psalms106:11 As for those that troubled them, the waters overwhelmed them, there was not one of them left." 
Psalms106:12 Then believed they in his word, and sang praise unto him." 
Psalms106:13 But within a while they forgot his works, and would not abide his counsel." 
Psalms106:14 A lust came upon them in the wilderness, so that they tempted God in the desert." 
Psalms106:15 Yet he gave them their desire, and sent them enough at their wiles." 
Psalms106:16 They angered Moses in the tents, and Aaron the saint of the LORD." 
Psalms106:17 So the earth opened and swallowed up Dathan, and covered the congregation of Abiram." 
Psalms106:18 The fire was kindled in their company, the flame brent up the ungodly." 
Psalms106:19 They made a calf in Horeb, and worshipped the molten image." 
Psalms106:20 Thus they turned their(his) glory into the similitude of a calf, that eateth hay." 
Psalms106:21 They forgot God their Savior, which had done so great things in Egypt." 
Psalms106:22 wonderous works in the land of Ham, and fearful things in the reed sea." 
Psalms106:23 So he said he would have destroyed them, had not Moses his chosen stood before him in that gap: to turn away his wrathful indignation, lest he should destroy them." 
Psalms106:24 Yea they thought scorn of that pleasant land, and gave no credence unto his word." 
Psalms106:25 But murmured in their tents, and hearkened not unto the voice of the LORD." 
Psalms106:26 Then lift he up his hand against them, to overthrow them in the wilderness." 
Psalms106:27 To cast out their seed among the nations, and to scatter them in the lands." 
Psalms106:28 They joined themselves unto Baal Peor, and ate the offering of the dead." 
Psalms106:29 Thus they provoked him unto anger with their own inventions, and the plague was great among them." 
Psalms106:30 Then stood up Phinehas, and executed justice, and so the plague ceased." 
Psalms106:31 And that was counted unto him for righteousness, among all posterities for evermore." 
Psalms106:32 They angered him also at the waters of strife, so that Moses was punished for their sakes." 
Psalms106:33 Because they provoked his spirit, and he told them plainly with his lips." 
Psalms106:34 Neither destroyed they the Heathen, as the LORD commanded them." 
Psalms106:35 But were mingled among the Heathen, and learned their works." 
Psalms106:36 In so much that they worshipped their images, which turned to their own decay." 
Psalms106:37 Yea they offered their sons and their daughters unto devils. 
Psalms106:38 And shed the innocent blood of their sons and of their daughters, whom they offered unto the images of Canaan, so that the land was defiled with blood." 
Psalms106:39 Thus were they stained with their own works, and went awhoring with their own inventions." 
Psalms106:40 Therefore was the wrath of the LORD kindled against his people, insomuch that he abhorred his own inheritance." 
Psalms106:41 And gave them over into the hand of the Heathen, and they that hated them, were lords over them." 
Psalms106:42 Their enemies oppressed them, and had them in subjection." 
Psalms106:43 Many a time did he deliver them, but they provoked him with their own inventions, and were brought down for their wickedness." 
Psalms106:44 Nevertheless when he saw their adversity, he heard their complaint." 
Psalms106:45 He thought upon his covenant, and pitied them, according unto the multitude of his mercies." 
Psalms106:46 Yea he made all those that had led them away captive, to pity them." 
Psalms106:47 Deliver us, O LORD our God, and gather us from among the Heathen: that we may give thanks unto thy holy name, and make our boast of thy praise." 
Psalms106:48 Blessed be the LORD God of Israel from everlasting and world without end, and let all people say: Amen, Amen. Praise the everlasting.(Halleluya.)" 
Psalms107:1 O give thanks unto the LORD, for he is gracious and his mercy endureth for ever." 
Psalms107:2 Let them give thanks whom the LORD hath redeemed, and delivered from the hand of the enemy." 
Psalms107:3 And gathered them out of the lands, from the East, from the West, from the North and from the South." 
Psalms107:4 They went astray in the wilderness in an untrodden way, and found no city to dwell in." 
Psalms107:5 Hungry and thirsty, their soul fainted in them." 
Psalms107:6 So they cried unto the LORD in their trouble, and he delivered them from their distress." 
Psalms107:7 He led them forth by the right way, that they might go to the city where they dwelt." 
Psalms107:8 O that men would praise the goodness of the LORD, and the wonders that he doth for the children of men." 
Psalms107:9 For he satisfied the empty soul, and filled the hungry soul with good." 
Psalms107:10 Such as sat in darkness and in the shadow of death, being fast bound in misery and iron." 
Psalms107:11 Because they were not obedient to the commandments of God, but lightly regarded the counsel of the most highest." 
Psalms107:12 Their heart was vexed with labor, they fell down, and there was none to help them." 
Psalms107:13 So they cried unto the LORD in their trouble, and he delivered them out of their distress." 
Psalms107:14 He brought them out of darkness and out of the shadow of death, and brake their bonds in sunder." 
Psalms107:15 O that men would praise the goodness of the LORD, and the wonders that he doth for the children of men." 
Psalms107:16 For he hath broken the gates of brass, and smitten the bars of Iron in sunder." 
Psalms107:17 Foolish men were plagued for their offense, and because of their wickedness." 
Psalms107:18 Their soul abhorred all manner of meat, they were even hard at death's door." 
Psalms107:19 So they cried unto the LORD in their trouble, and he delivered them out of their distress." 
Psalms107:20 He sent his word and healed them, and saved them from destruction." 
Psalms107:21 O that men would praise the goodness of the LORD, and the wonders that he doth for the children of men." 
Psalms107:22 That they would offer unto him the sacrifice of thanksgiving, and tell out his works with gladness." 
Psalms107:23 They that go down to the sea in ships, and occupy their business in great waters." 
Psalms107:24 These men see the works of the LORD, and his wonders in the deep." 
Psalms107:25 For at his word, the stormy wind ariseth, and lifteth up the waves thereof." 
Psalms107:26 They are carried up to the heaven, and down again to the deep, their soul melteth away in the trouble." 
Psalms107:27 They reel to and fro, they stagger like a drunken man, and are at their wits' end." 
Psalms107:28 So they cry unto the LORD in their trouble, and he delivereth them out of their distress." 
Psalms107:29 He maketh the storm to cease, so that the waves are still." 
Psalms107:30 Then are they glad because they be at rest, and so he bringeth them unto the haven where they would be." 
Psalms107:31 O that men would praise the goodness of the LORD, and the wonders that he doth for the children of men." 
Psalms107:32 That they would exalt him in the congregation of the people, and love him in the seat of the elders." 
Psalms107:33 Which turneth floods into dry land, and dryeth up the water springs." 
Psalms107:34 A fruitful land maketh he barren, for the wickedness of them that dwell therein." 
Psalms107:35 Again, he maketh the wilderness a standing water, and water springs of a dry ground." 
Psalms107:36 There he setteth the hungry, that they may build them a city to dwell in." 
Psalms107:37 That they may sow their ground, plant vineyards, to yield the fruits of increase." 
Psalms107:38 He blesseth them, so that they multiply exceedingly, and suffereth not their cattle to decrease." 
Psalms107:39 When they are minished and brought low thorow oppression, thorow any plague or trouble." 
Psalms107:40 Though he suffer them to be evil intreated thorow tyrants, or let them wander out of the way in the wilderness." 
Psalms107:41 Yet helpeth he the poor out of misery, (at the last) and maketh him an household like a flock of sheep." 
Psalms107:42 The righteous will consider this and rejoice, the mouth of all wickedness shall be stopped." 
Psalms107:43 Who so is wise, and pondereth these things well, shall understand the loving-kindness of the LORD." 
Psalms108:1 A song of Psalm of David. O God, my heart is ready to sing, and to give praise." 
Psalms108:2 Awake, O my glory, awake lute and harp, I myself will awake right early." 
Psalms108:3 I will give thanks unto thee, O LORD, among the people, I will sing praises unto thee among the Heathen." 
Psalms108:4 For the greatness of thy mercy is higher than the heavens, and thy faithfulness reacheth unto the clouds." 
Psalms108:5 Set up thyself, O God, above the heavens, and thy glory above all the earth." 
Psalms108:6 That thy beloved may be delivered: help then with thy right hand, and hear me." 
Psalms108:7 God hath spoken in his Sanctuary, which thing rejoiceth me. I will divide Shechem, and mete out the valley of Succoth." 
Psalms108:8 Gilead is mine, Manasses is mine, Ephraim is the strength of mine head," 
Psalms108:9 Judah is my captain. Moab is my washpot; over Edom will I stretch out my shoe, Philistia shall be glad of me." 
Psalms108:10 Who will lead me into the strong city? Who will bring me into Edom? 
Psalms108:11 Shalt not thou do it (O God) which hast cast us out: thou God, that wentest not forth with our Hosts?" 
Psalms108:12 O be thou our help in trouble, for vain is the help of man." 
Psalms108:13 Thorow God we shall do great acts, for it is he that shall tread down our enemies." 
Psalms109:1 To the chanter, a Psalm of David. Hold not thy tongue, O God of my praise." 
Psalms109:2 For the mouth of the ungodly, yea and the mouth of the deceitful is opened upon me, and speak against me with false tongues." 
Psalms109:3 They compass me about with words of hatred, and fight against me without a cause." 
Psalms109:4 For the love that I had unto thee, they take now my contrary part, but I give myself unto prayer." 
Psalms109:5 Thus they reward me evil for good, and hatred for my good will." 
Psalms109:6 Set an ungodly man to be ruler over him, and let Satan stand at his right hand." 
Psalms109:7 When sentence is given upon him, let him be condemned, and let his prayer be turned into sin." 
Psalms109:8 Let his days be few, and his bishopric let another take." 
Psalms109:9 Let his children be fatherless, and his wife a widow." 
Psalms109:10 Let his children be vagabonds, and beg their bread: let them seek it, as they that be destroyed." 
Psalms109:11 Let the extortioner consume all that he hath, and let strangers spoil his labour." 
Psalms109:12 Let there be no man to pity, nor to have compassion on his fatherless children." 
Psalms109:13 Let his end be destruction, and in the next generation let his name be clean put out." 
Psalms109:14 Let the wickedness of his fathers be had in remembrance in the sight of LORD, and let not the sin of his mother be done away." 
Psalms109:15 Let them be alway before the LORD, but as for the memorial of them selves, let it perish from out of the earth." 
Psalms109:16 And that because his mind was not to do good, but persecuted the poor helpless, and him that was vexed at the heart, to slay him." 
Psalms109:17 His delight was in cursing, and therefore shall it happen unto him: he loved not blessing, and that shall be far from him." 
Psalms109:18 He clothed himself with cursing like as with a raiment: yea it went into his bowels like water, and like oil into his bones." 
Psalms109:19 Let it be unto him as the cloak that he hath on him, and as the girdle that he is girded withal." 
Psalms109:20 Let it thus happen from the LORD unto mine enemies, and to those that speak evil against my soul." 
Psalms109:21 But deal thou with me, O Lord GOD,(o LORDE God) according unto thy name, for sweet is thy mercy." 
Psalms109:22 O deliver me, for I am helpless and poor, and my heart is wounded within me." 
Psalms109:23 I go hence like the shadow that departeth, and am driven away as the grasshoppers." 
Psalms109:24 My knees are weak thorow fasting, my flesh is dried up for want of fatness." 
Psalms109:25 I am become a rebuke unto them, they look upon me and shake their heads." 
Psalms109:26 Help me, O LORD my God, oh save me for thy mercies' sake." 
Psalms109:27 That they may know, how that this is thy hand, and that thou hast done it." 
Psalms109:28 Though they curse, yet bless thou: and let them be confounded, that rise up against me, but let thy servant rejoice." 
Psalms109:29 Let mine adversaries be clothed with their own shame, as with a cloak." 
Psalms109:30 As for me, I will give thanks unto the LORD with my mouth, and praise him among the multitude." 
Psalms109:31 For he standeth at the right hand of the poor, to save him from such as condemn his soul." 
Psalms110:1 A Psalm of David. The LORD said unto my Lord:(LORDE) Sit thou on my right hand, until I make thine enemies thy footstool." 
Psalms110:2 The LORD shall send the rod of thy power out of Sion, be thou ruler even in the middest among thine enemies." 
Psalms110:3 In the day of thy power shall thy people offer the freewill offerings with an holy worship, the dew of thy birth is of the womb of the morning." 
Psalms110:4 The LORD sware, and will not repent: Thou art a priest forever after the order of Melchizedek." 
Psalms110:5 The Lord upon thy right hand, shall smite even kings in the day of his wrath." 
Psalms110:6 He shall be judge among the Heathen, he shall fill them with dead bodies, and smite in sunder the heads over divers countries." 
Psalms110:7 He shall drink of the brook in the way, therefore shall he lift up his head." 
Psalms111:1 Praise the everlasting. I will give thanks unto the LORD with my whole heart: secretly(in council) among the faithful, and in the congregation." 
Psalms111:2 The works of the LORD are great, sought out of all them that have pleasure therein." 
Psalms111:3 His work is worthy to be praised and had in honour, and his righteousness endureth for ever." 
Psalms111:4 The merciful and gracious LORD hath so done his marvelous works, that they ought to be had in remembrance." 
Psalms111:5 He giveth meat unto them, that fear him, he is ever mindful of his covenant." 
Psalms111:6 He sheweth his people the power of his works, that he may give them the heritage of the Heathen." 
Psalms111:7 The works of his hands are verity and judgment, all his commandments are true." 
Psalms111:8 They stand fast for ever and ever, and are done in truth and equity." 
Psalms111:9 He sent redemption unto his people, he hath commanded his covenant for ever, holy and reverent is his name." 
Psalms111:10 The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom; a good understanding have all they that do thereafter: the praise of it endureth for ever. 
Psalms112:1 Praise the everlasting. Blessed is the man that feareth the LORD, and hath great delight in his commandments." 
Psalms112:2 His seed shall be mighty upon earth, the generation of the faithful shall be blessed." 
Psalms112:3 Riches and plentiousness shall be in his house, and his righteousness endureth for ever." 
Psalms112:4 Unto the godly there ariseth up light in the darkness: he is merciful, loving and righteous." 
Psalms112:5 Well is he(him) that is merciful, and lendeth gladly, and pondereth his words with discretion." 
Psalms112:6 For he shall never be moved, the righteous shall be had in an everlasting remembrance." 
Psalms112:7 He will not be afraid for any evil tidings, his heart standeth fast, and believeth in the LORD." 
Psalms112:8 His heart is stablished, he will not shrink, until he sees his desire upon his enemies." 
Psalms112:9 He hath sparsed abroad, and given to the poor, his righteousness remaineth for ever, his horn shall be exalted with honor." 
Psalms112:10 The ungodly shall see it, and it shall grieve him: he shall gnash with his teeth, and consume away, and the desire of the ungodly shall perish." 
Psalms113:1 Praise the everlasting. Praise the LORD (O ye servants) O praise the name of the LORD.(ALLELUYA) 
Psalms113:2 Blessed be the name of the LORD from this time forth for evermore. 
Psalms113:3 The LORD's name is worthy to be praised, from the rising up of the sun(Sonne) unto the going down of the same." 
Psalms113:4 The LORD is high above all Heathen, and his glory above the heavens." 
Psalms113:5 Who is like unto the LORD our God, that hath his dwelling so high,(hye)" 
Psalms113:6 which humbleth himself, to behold that is in heaven and earth." 
Psalms113:7 Which taketh up the simple out of the dust, and lifteth the poor out of the mire." 
Psalms113:8 That he may set him among the princes, even among the princes of his people." 
Psalms113:9 Which maketh the barren woman to keep house, and to be a joyful mother of children. Praise the everlasting. (Halleluya.)"

Psalms115:1 Not unto us, O LORD, not unto us, but unto thy name give the praise, for thy loving mercy and faithfulness." 
Psalms115:2 Wherefore shall the Heathen say: Where is now their God? 
Psalms115:3 As for our God, he is in heaven, he doth whatsoever it pleaseth him." 
Psalms115:4 Their images are but silver and gold, even the work of men's hands." 
Psalms115:5 They have mouths, and speak not: eyes have they, but they see not." 
Psalms115:6 They have ears, and hear not: noses have they, but they smell not." 
Psalms115:7 They have hands and handle not, feet have they, but they can not go, neither can they speak thorow their throat." 
Psalms115:8 They that make them, are like unto them, and so are all such as put their trust in them." 
Psalms115:9 But let Israel trust in the LORD, for he is their succour and defense." 
Psalms115:10 Let the house of Aaron put their trust in the LORD, for he is their succour and defense." 
Psalms115:11 They that fear the LORD, let them put their trust in the LORD, for he is their succour and defense." 
Psalms115:12 The LORD is mindful of us, and blesseth us: he blesseth the house of Israel, he blesseth the house of Aaron." 
Psalms115:13 Yea he blesseth all them that fear the LORD, both small and great." 
Psalms115:14 The LORD increase you more and more: you and your children. 
Psalms115:15 For ye are the blessed of the LORD, which made heaven and earth." 
Psalms115:16 All the whole heavens are the LORD's, but the earth hath he given unto the children of men." 
Psalms115:17 The dead praise not thee (O LORD) neither all they that go down into silence. 
Psalms115:18 But we will praise thee LORD, from this time forth for evermore. Praise the everlasting.(Halleluya.)" 
Psalms116:1 I am well pleased, that the LORD hath heard the voice of my prayer." 
Psalms116:2 That he hath inclined(enclined) his ear unto me, therefore will I call upon him as long as I live."

Psalms116:4 I found trouble and heaviness. Then called I upon the name of the LORD: O LORD deliver my soul. 
Psalms116:5 Gracious is the LORD, and righteous, yea our God is merciful." 
Psalms116:6 The LORD preserveth the simple, I was brought down and he helped me." 
Psalms116:7 Turn again then unto thy rest, O my soul, for the LORD hath given thee thy desire." 
Psalms116:8 And why thou hast delivered my soul from death, mine eyes from tears, and my feet from falling." 
Psalms116:9 I will walk before the LORD, in the land of the living." 
Psalms116:10 I believed, and therefore have I spoken, but I was sore troubled." 
Psalms116:11 I said in my haste: All men are liars.

Psalms118:5 I called upon the LORD in trouble, and the LORD heard me at large." 
Psalms118:6 The LORD is my helper, I will not fear what man doeth unto me." 
Psalms118:7 The LORD is my helper, and I shall see my desire upon mine enemies." 
Psalms118:8 It is better to trust in the LORD, than to put any confidence in man." 
Psalms118:9 It is better to trust in the LORD, than to put any confidence in princes."

Psalms118:13 They thrust at me, that I might fall, but the LORD was my help." 
Psalms118:14 The LORD is my strength, and my song, and is become my salvation." 
Psalms118:15 The voice of joy and mirth is in the dwellings of the righteous, for the right hand of the LORD hath gotten the victory."

Psalms118:22 The same stone which the builders refused, is become the head stone in the corner."

Psalms118:24 This is the day which the LORD hath made, let us rejoice and be glad in it."

Psalms118:28 Thou art my God, and I will thank thee: thou art my God, and I will praise thee." 
Psalms118:29 O give thanks unto the LORD, for he is gracious, and his mercy endureth for ever." 
Psalms119:1 Aleph. Blessed are those that be undefiled in the way, which walk in the law of the LORD." 
Psalms119:2 Blessed are they that keep his testimonies, and that seek him with their whole heart." 
Psalms119:3 Which walk in his ways, and do no wickedness." 
Psalms119:4 Thou hast given straight charge to keep thy commandments. 
Psalms119:5 O that my ways were stablished to keep thy statutes. 
Psalms119:6 So should I not be confounded, while I have respect unto all thy commandments." 
Psalms119:7 I will thank thee with an unfained heart, because I am learned in the judgements of thy righteousness." 
Psalms119:8 I will keep thy statutes, O forsake me not utterly." 
Psalms119:9 Beth. Wherewithal shall a young man cleanse his way? Even by ruling himself after thy word. 
Psalms119:10 With my whole heart do I seek thee, O let me not go wrong out of thy commandments." 
Psalms119:11 Thy words have I hid within my heart, that I should not sin against thee." 
Psalms119:12 Praised be thou O LORD, O teach me thy statutes." 
Psalms119:13 With my lips will I be telling out all the judgments of thy mouth. 
Psalms119:14 I have as great delight in the way of thy testimonies, as in all manner of riches." 
Psalms119:15 I will exercise myself in thy commandments, and have respect unto thy footpaths." 
Psalms119:16 My delight shall be in thy statutes, I will not forget thy words." 
Psalms119:17 Gimel. O do well unto thy servant, that I may live, and keep thy words." 
Psalms119:18 Open thou mine eyes, and so shall I spy out wonderous things in thy law." 
Psalms119:19 I am a stranger upon earth, O hide not thy commandments from me." 
Psalms119:20 My soul breaketh out, for the very fervent desire that I have alway unto thy judgments." 
Psalms119:21 Thou rebukest the proud, cursed are they that depart from thy commandments." 
Psalms119:22 O turn from me shame and rebuke, for I keep thy testimonies." 
Psalms119:23 Princes also sit and speak against me, but thy servant is occupied in thy statutes." 
Psalms119:24 In thy testimonies is my delight, they are my counsellors." 
Psalms119:25 Daleth. My soul cleaveth unto the dust, O quicken thou me according to thy word." 
Psalms119:26 I knowledged my ways, and thou heardest me, O teach me then thy statutes." 
Psalms119:27 Make me to understand the way of thy commandments, and so shall I talk of thy wonderous works." 
Psalms119:28 My soul melteth away for very heaviness, O set me up according unto thy word." 
Psalms119:29 Take from me the way of lying, and grant me thy law." 
Psalms119:30 I have chosen the way of truth, thy judgments have I laid before me." 
Psalms119:31 I stick unto thy testimonies, O LORD, confound me not." 
Psalms119:32 I will run the way of thy commandments, when thou hast comforted my heart." 
Psalms119:33 He. Teach me O LORD the way of thy statutes, and I shall keep it unto the end." 
Psalms119:34 O give me understanding, and I shall keep thy law, yea I shall keep it with my whole heart." 
Psalms119:35 Lead me in the path of thy commandments, for that is my desire." 
Psalms119:36 Incline mine heart unto thy testimonies, and not to covetousness." 
Psalms119:37 O turn away mine eyes, lest they behold vanity, and quicken me in thy way." 
Psalms119:38 O stablish thy word in thy servant, that I may fear thee." 
Psalms119:39 Take away the rebuke that I am afraid of, for thy judgments are amiable." 
Psalms119:40 Behold, my delight is in thy commandments, O quicken me in thy righteousness." 
Psalms119:41 Vav. Let thy loving mercies come unto me, O LORD, and thy saving health according to thy word." 
Psalms119:42 That I may give answer unto my blasphemers, for my trust is in thy word." 
Psalms119:43 O take not the word of truth utterly out of my mouth, for my hope is in thy judgments." 
Psalms119:44 So shall I alway keep thy law, yea for ever and ever." 
Psalms119:45 And I will walk at liberty, for I seek thy commandments." 
Psalms119:46 I will speak of thy testimonies even before kings, and will not be ashamed." 
Psalms119:47 My delight shall be in thy commandments, which I love." 
Psalms119:48 My hands also will I lift up unto thy commandments which I love, and my talking shall be of thy statutes." 
Psalms119:49 Zain. O think upon thy servant as concerning thy word, wherein thou hast caused me to put my trust." 
Psalms119:50 For it is my comfort in my trouble, yea thy word quickeneth me." 
Psalms119:51 The proud have me greatly in derision, yet shrink not I from thy law." 
Psalms119:52 I remember thine everlasting judgments, O LORD, and am comforted." 
Psalms119:53 I am horribly afraid for the ungodly, that forsake thy law." 
Psalms119:54 Thy statutes are my songs in the house of my pilgrimage. 
Psalms119:55 I think upon thy name, O LORD, in the night season, and keep thy law." 
Psalms119:56 It is mine own, for I keep thy commandments." 
Psalms119:57 Heth. Thou art my portion, O LORD, I am purposed to keep thy law." 
Psalms119:58 I make mine humble petition in thy presence with my whole heart, O be merciful unto me according unto thy word." 
Psalms119:59 I call mine own ways to rememberance, and turn my feet into thy testimonies." 
Psalms119:60 I make haste, and prolong not the time, to keep thy commandments." 
Psalms119:61 The congregations of the ungodly have robbed me, but I forget not thy law." 
Psalms119:62 At midnight stand I up, to give thanks unto thee, for the judgements of thy righteous." 
Psalms119:63 I am a companion of all them that fear thee, and keep thy commandments." 
Psalms119:64 The earth, O LORD, is full of thy mercy. O teach me thy statutes."

Psalms119:66 O learn me thy kindness, nurture and knowledge, for I believe thy commandments." 
Psalms119:67 Before I was troubled, I went wrong, but now I keep thy word." 
Psalms119:68 Thou art good and friendly, O teach me thy statutes." 
Psalms119:69 The proud imagine lies upon me, but I keep thy commandments with my whole heart." 
Psalms119:70 Their heart is as fat as brawne, but my delight is in thy law." 
Psalms119:71 It is good for me that I have been in trouble, that I may learn thy statutes." 
Psalms119:72 The law of thy mouth is dearer unto me, than thousands of gold and silver."

Psalms120:3 What reward shall be given or done unto thee, thou false tongue?" 
Psalms120:4 Even mighty and sharp arrows, with hot burning coals." 
Psalms120:5 Who is me that my banishment endureth so long: I dwell in the tabernacles of the sorrowful. 
Psalms120:6 My soul hath long dwelt among them, that be enemies unto peace." 
Psalms120:7 I laboured for peace, but when I spake thereof, they made them to battle."

Psalms121:2 My help cometh even from the LORD which hath made heaven and earth. 
Psalms121:3 He will not suffer thy foot to be moved, and he that keepeth thee, sleepeth not." 
Psalms121:4 Behold, he that keepeth Israel, doth neither slumber nor sleep." 
Psalms121:5 The LORD himself is thy keeper, the LORD is thy defense upon thy right hand." 
Psalms121:6 So that the sun(Sonne) shall not burn thee by day, neither the moon by night." 
Psalms121:7 The LORD preserveth thee from all evil, yea it is the LORD that keepeth thy soul." 
Psalms121:8 The LORD preserveth thy going out and thy coming in, from this time forth for evermore."

Psalms126:1 The song of the stairs. When the LORD turneth again the captivity of Sion, then shall we be like unto them that dream." 
Psalms126:2 Then shall our mouth be filled with laughter, and our tongue with joy. Then shall it be said among the Heathen: The LORD hath done great things for them." 
Psalms126:3 Yea the LORD hath done great things for us already, whereof we rejoice." 
Psalms126:4 Turn our captivity, O LORD, as the rivers in the south." 
Psalms126:5 They that sow in tears, shall reap in joy." 
Psalms126:6 He that now goeth in his way weeping and beareth forth good seed, shall come again with joy, and bring his sheaves with him."

Psalms127:4 Like as the arrows in the hand of the giant, even so are the young children." 
Psalms127:5 Happy is the man, that hath his quiver full of them: they shall not be ashamed, when they speak with their enemies in the gate."

Psalms129:2 Yea many a time have they fought against me from my youth up, but they have not overcome me." 
Psalms129:3 The plowers plowed upon my back, and made long furrows." 
Psalms129:4 But the righteous LORD hath hewn the yoke of the ungodly in pieces.

Psalms129:6 Let them be even as the hay upon the house tops, which withereth afore it be plucked(pluckte) up."

Psalms129:8 So that they which go by, say not so much as: the LORD prosper you, we wish you good luck in the name of the LORD."

Psalms135:5 For I know that the LORD is great, and that our Lord(LORDE) is above all gods.(goddes)" 
Psalms135:6 Whatsoever the LORD pleaseth that doth he in heaven and in earth, in the sea, and in all deep places." 
Psalms135:7 He bringeth forth the clouds from the ends of the world, he turneth the lightnings unto rain, bringing the winds out of their treasuries." 
Psalms135:8 Which smote the firstborn of Egypt, both of man and of beast." 
Psalms135:9 He hath sent tokens and wonders into the middest of thee, O thou land of Egypt, upon Pharaoh and all his servants." 
Psalms135:10 Which smote divers nations, and slew mighty kings." 
Psalms135:11 Sehon king of the Amorites, Og the king of Bashan, and all the kingdoms of Canaan."

Psalms135:17 They have ears, and yet they hear not, neither is there any breath in their mouths." 
Psalms135:18 They that make them, are like unto them, and so are all they that put their trust in them."

Psalms136:1 O give thanks unto the LORD, for he is gracious, and his mercy endureth forever." 
Psalms136:2 O give thanks unto the God of all gods,(goddes) for his mercy endureth forever." 
Psalms136:3 O thank the Lord(LORDE) of all lords, for his mercy endureth forever." 
Psalms136:4 Which only doth great wonders, for his mercy endureth forever." 
Psalms136:5 Which by his wisdom made the heavens, for his mercy endureth forever." 
Psalms136:6 Which laid out the earth above the waters, for his mercy endureth forever." 
Psalms136:7 Which hath made great lights, for his mercy endureth for ever." 
Psalms136:8 The sun(Sonne) to rule the day, for his mercy endureth for ever." 
Psalms136:9 The moon and the stars to govern the night, for his mercy endureth forever." 
Psalms136:10 Which smote Egypt with their firstborn, for his mercy endureth forever." 
Psalms136:11 And brought out Israel from among them, for his mercy endureth forever." 
Psalms136:12 With a mighty hand and stretched out arm, for his mercy endureth forever." 
Psalms136:13 Which divided the reed sea into parts, for his mercy endureth forever." 
Psalms136:14 And made Israel to go thorow the middest of it, for his mercy endureth forever:" 
Psalms136:15 But as for Pharaoh and his host, he overthrew them in the reed sea, for his mercy endureth forever." 
Psalms136:16 Which led his people thorow the wilderness, for his mercy endureth forever." 
Psalms136:17 Which smote great kings, for his mercy endureth forever." 
Psalms136:18 Yea and slew mighty kings, for his mercy endureth forever." 
Psalms136:19 Sehon king of the Amorites, for his mercy endureth forever." 
Psalms136:20 And Og the king of Bashan, for his mercy endureth forever." 
Psalms136:21 And gave away their land for an heritage, for his mercy endureth forever." 
Psalms136:22 Even for an heritage unto Israel his servant, for his mercy endureth forever." 
Psalms136:23 Which remembereth us, when we are in trouble, for his mercy endureth forever." 
Psalms136:24 (And hath delivered us from our enemies: for his mercy endureth for ever.) 
Psalms136:25 Which giveth food unto all flesh, for his mercy endureth forever." 
Psalms136:26 O give thanks unto the God of heaven, for his mercy endureth forever." 
Psalms137:1 By the waters of Babylon we sat down and wept, when we remembered Sion." 
Psalms137:2 As for our harps, we hanged them up upon the trees, that are therein." 
Psalms137:3 Then they that led us away captive, required of us a song and melody in our heaviness: sing us one of the songs of Sion." 
Psalms137:4 How shall we sing the LORD's song in a strange land? 
Psalms137:5 If I forget thee, O Jerusalem, let my right hand be forgotten." 
Psalms137:6 If I do not remember thee, let my tongue cleave to the roof of my mouth: yea if I prefer not Jerusalem in my mirth." 
Psalms137:7 Remember the children of Edom, O LORD, in the day of Jerusalem, how they said: down with it, down with it: even to the ground." 
Psalms137:8 O daughter of Babylon, thou shalt come to misery thy self: yea happy shall he be, that rewardeth thee as thou hast served us." 
Psalms137:9 Blessed shall he be, that taketh thy children, and throweth them against the stones." 
Psalms138:1 Of David I will give thanks unto thee, O Lord,(LORDE) with my whole heart, even before the gods, will I sing praises unto thee." 
Psalms138:2 I will worship toward thy holy temple, and praise thy name, because of thy loving-kindness and truth, for thou hast magnified thy word, according unto thy great name." 
Psalms138:3 When I call upon thee, thou hearest me, and endewest my soul with much strength." 
Psalms138:4 All the kings of the earth shall praise thee, O LORD, when they hear the words of thy mouth." 
Psalms138:5 Yea, they shall sing in the ways of the LORD, that great is the glory of the LORD." 
Psalms138:6 For though the LORD be high, yet hath he respect unto the lowly, as for the proud, he beholdeth them(him) afar off." 
Psalms138:7 Though I walk in the middest of trouble, yet shalt thou refresh me: thou shalt stretch forth thine hand upon the furriousness of mine enemies, and thy right hand shall save me." 
Psalms138:8 The LORD shall make good for me, yea thy mercy, O LORD, endureth forever: despise not then the works of thine own hands." 
Psalms139:1 To the chanter, a Psalm of David. O LORD, thou searchest me out, and knowest me." 
Psalms139:2 Thou knowest my down sitting and mine uprising, thou understandest my thoughts afar off." 
Psalms139:3 Thou art about my path and about my bed, and spiest out all my ways." 
Psalms139:4 For lo, there is not a word in(i) my tongue, but thou, O LORD, knowest it altogether." 
Psalms139:5 Thou hast fashioned me behind and before, and laid thine hand upon me." 
Psalms139:6 Such knowledge is too wonderful and excellent for me, I can not attain unto it." 
Psalms139:7 Whither shall I go then from thy spirit? or, whither shall I go then(flee) from thy presence?" 
Psalms139:8 If I climb up into heaven, thou art there: if I go down to hell, thou art there also." 
Psalms139:9 If I take the wings of the morning, and remain in the uttermost(utmost) part of the sea:" 
Psalms139:10 Even there also shall thy hand lead me, and thy right hand shall hold me." 
Psalms139:11 If I say: peradventure the darkness shall cover me, then shall my night be turned to day." 
Psalms139:12 Yea the darkness is no darkness with thee, but the night is as clear as the day, the darkness and light are both alike." 
Psalms139:13 For my reins are thine, thou hast covered me in my mother's womb." 
Psalms139:14 I will give thanks unto thee, for I am wonderfully made: marvelous are thy works, and that my soul knoweth right well." 
Psalms139:15 My bones are not hid from thee, though I be made secretly, and fashioned beneath in the earth." 
Psalms139:16 Thine eyes see mine unperfectness, they stand all written in thy book: my days were fashioned, when as yet there was not one of them." 
Psalms139:17 How dear are thy counsels unto me, O God? O how great is the sum of them." 
Psalms139:18 If I tell them, they are more in number than the sand: when I wake up, I am present with thee." 
Psalms139:19 Wilt thou not slay the wicked (oh God) that the bloodthirsty might depart from me? 
Psalms139:20 For they speak unright of thee, thine enemies exalt themselves presumptuously." 
Psalms139:21 I hate them, O LORD, that hate thee: and I may not away with those that rise up against thee?" 
Psalms139:22 Yea I hate them right sore, therefore are they mine enemies." 
Psalms139:23 Try me, O God, and seek the ground of mine heart: prove me, and examine my thoughts." 
Psalms139:24 Look well if there be any way of wickedness in me, and lead me in the way everlasting." 
Psalms140:1 To the chanter, a Psalm of David. Deliver me, o LORD, from the evil men, oh preserve me from the wicked men." 
Psalms140:2 Which imagine mischief in their hearts, and stir up strife all the day long." 
Psalms140:3 They sharpen their tongues like a serpent: adders' poison is under their lips. Selah. 
Psalms140:4 Keep me, O LORD, from the hand of the ungodly: preserve me from the wicked men, which are purposed to overthrow my goings." 
Psalms140:5 The proud have laid a snare for me, and spread a net abroad with cords, yea and set traps in my way. Selah." 
Psalms140:6 But my saying is unto the LORD: Thou art my God, hear the voice of my prayer, O LORD." 
Psalms140:7 O Lord GOD,(LORDE God) thou strength of my health, thou hast covered my head in the day of battle.(battayle)" 
Psalms140:8 Let not the ungodly have his desire, O LORD, let him not have his purpose, lest they be too proud. Selah." 
Psalms140:9 Let the mischief of their own lips fall upon the head of them, that compass me about." 
Psalms140:10 Let hot burning coals fall upon them, let them be cast into the fire, and into the pit, that they never rise up again." 
Psalms140:11 A man full of words shall not prosper upon the earth: a malicious and wicked person shall be hunted away, and destroyed." 
Psalms140:12 Sure I am, that the LORD will avenge the poor, and maintain the cause of the helpless." 
Psalms140:13 The righteous also shall give thanks unto thy name, and the just shall continue in thy sight." 
Psalms141:1 A Psalm of David. LORD,(Lorde) I call upon thee: haste thee unto me, and consider my voice, when I cry unto thee." 
Psalms141:2 Let my prayer be set forth in thy sight as the incense, and let the lifting up of my hands be an evening sacrifice." 
Psalms141:3 Set a watch, O LORD, before my mouth, yea a watch at the door of my lips." 
Psalms141:4 O let not mine heart be inclined(enclined) to any evil thing, to be minded as the ungodly or wicked men, lest I eat of such things as please them." 
Psalms141:5 Let the righteous rather smite me friendly, and reprove me: so will I take it as though he had poured oil on my head: it shall not hurt my head, yea I will pray yet for their wickedness." 
Psalms141:6 Their judges stumble at the stone, yet hear they my words, that they be joyful." 
Psalms141:7 Our bones lie scattered before the pit, like as when one graveth and diggeth up the ground." 
Psalms141:8 But mine eyes look unto thee, O Lord GOD:(o LORDE God) in thee is my trust, oh cast not out my soul." 
Psalms141:9 Keep me from the snare which they have laid for me, and from the traps of the wicked doers." 
Psalms141:10 Let the ungodly fall into their own nets together, until I be gone by them." 
Psalms142:1 The Instruction of David, a prayer when he was in the cave. I cry unto the LORD with my voice, yea even unto the LORD do I make my supplication." 
Psalms142:2 I pour out my complaint before him, and shew him of my trouble." 
Psalms142:3 When my spirit is in heaviness, for thou knowest my path: in the way wherein I walk have they privily laid a snare for me." 
Psalms142:4 I look upon my right hand, and see, there is no man that will know me. I have no place to flee unto, no man careth for my soul." 
Psalms142:5 Therefore do I cry unto thee, O LORD, and say: Thou art my hope and my portion in the land of the living." 
Psalms142:6 Consider my complaint, for I am brought very low. Oh deliver me from my persecutors, for they are too strong for me." 
Psalms142:7 Bring my soul out of prison, that I may give thanks unto thy name: which thing if thou wilt grant me, then shall the righteous resort unto my company." 
Psalms143:1 A Psalm of David. Hear my prayer, O LORD, consider my desire: answer me for thy truth and righteousness' sake." 
Psalms143:2 And enter not into judgment with thy servant, for in thy sight shall no man living be justified." 
Psalms143:3 For the enemy persecuteth my soul, he smiteth my life down to the ground, he layeth me in the darkness, as the dead men of the world." 
Psalms143:4 Therefore is my spirit vexed within me, and my heart within me is desolate." 
Psalms143:5 Yet do I remember the times past, I muse upon all thy works; Yea I exercise myself in the works of thy hands." 
Psalms143:6 I stretch forth my hands unto thee, my soul cryeth unto thee out of the thirsty land. Selah." 
Psalms143:7 Hear me, O LORD, and that soon, for my spirit waxeth faint: hide not thy face from me, lest I be like unto them that go down into the grave." 
Psalms143:8 Oh let me hear thy loving-kindness by times in the morning, for in thee is my trust: shew thou me the way that I should walk in, for I lift up my soul unto thee." 
Psalms143:9 Deliver me, O LORD, from mine enemies, for I resort unto thee." 
Psalms143:10 Teach me to do the thing that pleaseth thee, for thou art my God: let thy loving spirit lead me forth unto the land of righteousness." 
Psalms143:11 Quicken me, O LORD, for thy name's sake, and for thy righteousness' sake bring my soul out of trouble." 
Psalms143:12 And of thy goodness scatter mine enemies abroad, and destroy all them that vex my soul, for I am thy servant." 
Psalms144:1 Of David. Blessed be the LORD my refuge, which teacheth my hands to war, and my fingers to fight." 
Psalms144:2 My hope and my castle, my defense and my deliverer, my shield in whom I trust, which governeth the people that is under me." 
Psalms144:3 LORD, what is man, that thou hast such respect unto him? Or the son of man, that thou so regardest him?" 
Psalms144:4 Man is like a thing of naught, his time passeth away like a shadow." 
Psalms144:5 Bow thy heavens, O LORD, and come down, touch the mountains, that they may smoke withal." 
Psalms144:6 Send forth the lightning, and scatter them, shoot out thine arrows, and consume them." 
Psalms144:7 Send down thine hand from above, deliver me, and take me out of the greatwaters, from the hand of strange children." 
Psalms144:8 Whose mouth talketh of vanity, and their right hand is a right hand of falsity.(falsede)" 
Psalms144:9 That I may sing a new song unto thee, O God, and sing praises unto thee upon a ten stringed lute." 
Psalms144:10 Thou that givest victory unto kings, and hast delivered David thy servant from the peril of the sword." 
Psalms144:11 Save me, and deliver me from the hand of strange children, whose mouth talketh of vanity, and their right hand is a right hand of falsity." 
Psalms144:12 That our sons may grow up as the young plants, and that our daughters may be as the polished corners of the temple." 
Psalms144:13 That our garners may be full and plenteous with all manner of store: that our sheep may bring forth thousands and hundredth thousands in our villages. 
Psalms144:14 That our oxen may be strong to labour, that there be no mischance, no decay, and no complaining in our streets." 
Psalms144:15 Happy are the people that be in such a case: yea blessed are the people, which have the LORD for their God."

Psalms145:2 Every day will I give thanks unto thee, and praise thy name forever and ever." 
Psalms145:3 Great is the LORD, and marvelous worthy to be praised, there is no end to his greatness." 
Psalms145:4 One generation shall praise thy works unto another, and declare thy power." 
Psalms145:5 As for me I will be talking of thy worship, thy glory, thy praise, and wonderous works." 
Psalms145:6 So that men shall speak of the might of thy marvelous acts, and tell of thy greatness." 
Psalms145:7 The memorial of thy abundant kindness shall be shewed, and men shall sing of thy righteousness." 
Psalms145:8 The LORD is gracious and merciful, long suffering and of great goodness." 
Psalms145:9 The LORD is loving unto every man, and his mercy is over all his works."

Psalms145:11 They shew the glory of thy kingdom, and talk of thy power." 
Psalms145:12 That thy power, thy glory and mightiness of thy kingdom might be known unto men." 
Psalms145:13 Thy kingdom is an everlasting kingdom, and thy dominion endureth thorowout all ages." 
Psalms145:14 The LORD upholdeth all such as should fall, and lifteth up all those that be down."

Psalms146:2 while I live will I praise the LORD: yea as long as I have any being, I will sing praises unto my God." 
Psalms146:3 O put not your trust in princes, nor in any child of man, for there is no help in them." 
Psalms146:4 For when the breath of man goeth forth, he shall turn again to his earth, and so all his thoughts perish." 
Psalms146:5 Blessed is he that hath the God of Jacob for his help, and whose hope is in the LORD his God."

Psalms146:7 Which helpeth them to right that suffer wrong, which feedeth the hungry. The LORD lowseth men out of prison," 
Psalms146:8 the LORD giveth sight to the blind. The LORD helpeth them up that are fallen, the LORD loveth the righteous."

Psalms147:1 Praise the everlasting. Praise ye the LORD, for it is a good thing to sing praises unto our God: yea a joyful and pleasant thing is it to be thankful." 
Psalms147:2 The LORD shall build up Jerusalem, and gather together the outcasts of Israel." 
Psalms147:3 He healeth the contrite in heart, and bindeth up their wounds." 
Psalms147:4 He telleth the number of the stars, he calleth them all by their names." 
Psalms147:5 Great is our Lord,(LORDE) and great is his power: yea, his wisdom is infinite." 
Psalms147:6 The LORD setteth up the meek, and bringeth the ungodly down to the ground." 
Psalms147:7 O sing unto the LORD with thanksgiving, sing praises upon the harp unto our God." 
Psalms147:8 Which covereth the heaven with clouds, prepareth rain for the earth, and maketh the grass to grow upon the mountains." 
Psalms147:9 Which giveth fodder unto the cattle, and feedeth the young ravens that call upon him." 
Psalms147:10 He hath no pleasure in the strength of an horse, neither delighteth he in any man's legs." 
Psalms147:11 But the LORD's delight is in them that fear him, and put their trust in his mercy." 
Psalms147:12 Praise the LORD, O Jerusalem, praise thy God O Sion." 
Psalms147:13 For he maketh fast the bars of thy gates, and blesseth the children within thee." 
Psalms147:14 He maketh peace in thy borders, and filleth thee with the flour of wheat." 
Psalms147:15 He sendeth forth his commandment upon earth, his word runneth swiftly." 
Psalms147:16 He giveth snow like wool, and scattereth the hoarfrost like ashes." 
Psalms147:17 He casteth forth his ice like morsels, who is able to abide his frost?" 
Psalms147:18 He sendeth out his word and melteth them, he bloweth with his wind, and the waters flow." 
Psalms147:19 He sheweth his word unto Jacob, his statutes and ordinances unto Israel." 
Psalms147:20 He hath not dealt so with all the Heathen, neither have they knowledge of his laws. Praise the everlasting. (Halleluya.)" 
Psalms148:1 Praise the everlasting. O PRAISE the LORD of heaven, praise him in the height." 
Psalms148:2 Praise him all ye angels of his, praise him all his host." 
Psalms148:3 Praise him Sun and Moon, praise him all ye stars and light." 
Psalms148:4 Praise him all ye heavens, and ye waters that be under the heavens." 
Psalms148:5 Let them praise the name of the LORD, for he commanded and they were made." 
Psalms148:6 He hath made them fast for ever and ever, he hath given them a law which shall not be broken." 
Psalms148:7 Praise the LORD upon earth, ye whalefishes, and all deeps." 
Psalms148:8 Fire and hail, snow and vapors, wind and storm, fulfilling his word." 
Psalms148:9 Mountains and all hills, fruitful trees, and all Cedars." 
Psalms148:10 Beasts and cattle, worms and feathered fowls." 
Psalms148:11 Kings of the earth and all people, princes and all judges of the world." 
Psalms148:12 Young men and maidens, old men and children:" 
Psalms148:13 let them praise the name of the LORD, for his name only is excellent, and his praise above heaven and earth." 
Psalms148:14 He exalteth the horn of his people, all his saints shall praise him, the children of Israel, even the people that serveth him. Praise the everlasting. (Halleluya.)" 
Psalms149:1 Praise the everlasting. (Halleluya.) O sing unto the LORD a new song, and let the congregation of saints praise him." 
Psalms149:2 Let Israel rejoice in him that made him, and let the children of Sion be joyful in their King." 
Psalms149:3 Let them praise his name in the dance, let them sing praises unto him with tabretts and harps." 
Psalms149:4 For the LORD hath pleasure in his people, and helpeth the meek hearted." 
Psalms149:5 Let the saints be joyful with glory, let them rejoice in their beds." 
Psalms149:6 Let the praises of God be in their mouth, and sharp swords in their hands." 
Psalms149:7 To be avenged of the Heathen, and to rebuke the people." 
Psalms149:8 To bind their kings in chains, and their nobles with links of iron." 
Psalms149:9 That they may be avenged of them, as it is written, such honour have all his saints. Praise the everlasting. (Halleluya.)" 
Psalms150:1 Praise ye the everlasting. (Halleluya.) O PRAISE the LORD in his Sanctuary, praise him in the firmament of his power." 
Psalms150:2 Praise him in his noble acts, praise him in his excellent greatness." 
Psalms150:3 Praise him in the sound of the trumpet, praise him upon the lute and harp." 
Psalms150:4 Praise him in the cymbals and dance, praise him upon the strings and pipe." 
Psalms150:5 Praise him upon the well tuned cymbals, praise him upon the loud cymbals." 
Psalms150:6 Let every thing that hath breath, praise the LORD. Praise the everlasting. (Halleluya.) [The end of the Psalter.]" 
Proverbs1:1 The(These are the) proverbs of Solomon the son of David king of Israel: 
Proverbs1:2 To learn wisdom, instruction,(nurture) understanding, prudence," 
Proverbs1:3 righteousness, judgment and equity." 
Proverbs1:4 That the very babes might have wit, and that young men might have knowledge and understanding." 
Proverbs1:5 By hearing, the wise man shall come by more wisdom:" 
Proverbs1:6 and by experience he shall be more apt to understand a parable, and the interpretation thereof, the words of the wise, and the dark speeches of the same." 
Proverbs1:7 The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom. But fools despise wisdom and instruction.(nurture) 
Proverbs1:8 My son, hear thy father's doctrine, and forsake not the law of thy mother:" 
Proverbs1:9 for that shall bring grace unto thy head, and shall be a chain about thy neck." 
Proverbs1:10 My son, consent not unto sinners," 
Proverbs1:11 if they entice thee, and say: Come with us, let us lay wait for blood, and lurk privily for the innocent without a cause:" 
Proverbs1:12 Let us swallow them up like the hell, let us devour them quick and whole, as those that go down into the pit." 
Proverbs1:13 So shall we find all manner of costly riches, and fill our houses with spoils." 
Proverbs1:14 Cast in thy lot among us, we shall have all one purse." 
Proverbs1:15 My son, walk not thou with them, refrain thy foot from their ways." 
Proverbs1:16 For their feet run to evil, and are hasty to shed blood." 
Proverbs1:17 But in vain is the net laid forth before the bird's eyes. 
Proverbs1:18 Yea they themselves lay wait one for another's blood, and one of them would slay another." 
Proverbs1:19 These are the ways of all such as be covetous, that one would ravish another's life." 
Proverbs1:20 Wisdom crieth without and putteth forth her(hir) voice in the streets. 
Proverbs1:21 She calleth before the congregation in the open gates, and sheweth her words throw the city, saying:" 
Proverbs1:22 O ye children, how long will ye love childishness? how long will the scorners delight in scorning, and the unwise be enemies unto knowledge?" 
Proverbs1:23 O turn you unto my correction: lo, I will express my mind unto you, and make you understand my words." 
Proverbs1:24 Seeing then that I have called, and ye refused it: I have stretched out my hand, and no man regarded it," 
Proverbs1:25 but all my counsels have ye despised and set my corrections to naught. 
Proverbs1:26 Therefore shall I also laugh in your destruction, and mock you, when the thing that ye fear cometh upon you:" 
Proverbs1:27 even when the thing that ye be afraid of, falleth in suddenly like a storm, and your misery like a tempest: yea when trouble and heaviness cometh upon you." 
Proverbs1:28 Then shall they call upon me, but I will not hear: they shall seek me early, but they shall not find me:" 
Proverbs1:29 And that because they hated knowledge, and received not the fear of the LORD," 
Proverbs1:30 but abhorred my counsel, and despised my correction." 
Proverbs1:31 Therefore shall they eat the fruits of their own way, and be filled with their own counsels:" 
Proverbs1:32 for the turning away of the unwise shall slay them, and the prosperity of fools shall be their own destruction." 
Proverbs1:33 But who so hearkeneth unto me shall dwell safely, and have enough without any fear of evil." 
Proverbs2:1 My son, if thou wilt receive my words, and keep my commandments by thee," 
Proverbs2:2 that thine ear may hearken unto wisdom, apply thine heart then to understanding." 
Proverbs2:3 For if thou criest after wisdom, and callest after knowledge:" 
Proverbs2:4 If thou seekest after her as after money, and diggest for her as for treasures:" 
Proverbs2:5 then shalt thou understand the fear of the LORD, and find the knowledge of God." 
Proverbs2:6 For it is the LORD that giveth wisdom, out of his mouth cometh knowledge and understanding." 
Proverbs2:7 He preserveth the welfare of the righteous, and defendeth them that walk innocently:" 
Proverbs2:8 He keepeth them in the right path, and preserveth the way of his saints." 
Proverbs2:9 Then shalt thou understand righteousness, judgment and equity, yea and every good path." 
Proverbs2:10 If wisdom enter into thine heart, and thy soul delight in knowledge:" 
Proverbs2:11 then shall counsel preserve thee, and understanding shall keep thee." 
Proverbs2:12 That thou mayest be delivered from the evil way, and from the man that speaketh froward things." 
Proverbs2:13 From such as leave the high street, and walk in the ways of darkness:" 
Proverbs2:14 Which rejoice in doing evil, and delight in wicked things:" 
Proverbs2:15 Whose ways are crooked, and their paths slanderous.(sclaunderous)" 
Proverbs2:16 That thou mayest be delivered also from the strange woman, and from her that is not thine own: which giveth sweet words," 
Proverbs2:17 forsaketh the husband of her youth, and forgeteth the covenant of her God." 
Proverbs2:18 For her house is inclined unto death, and her paths into hell." 
Proverbs2:19 All they that go in unto her, come not again, neither take they hold of the way of life." 
Proverbs2:20 That thou mayest walk in the good way, and keep the paths of the righteous." 
Proverbs2:21 For the just shall dwell in the land, and the innocent shall remain in it." 
Proverbs2:22 But the ungodly shall be rooted out of the land, and the wicked doers shall be taken out of it." 
Proverbs3:1 My son, forget not my law, but see that thine heart keep my commandments." 
Proverbs3:2 For they shall prolong the days and years of thy life, and bring thee peace." 
Proverbs3:3 Let mercy and faithfulness never go from thee: bind them about thy neck, and write them in the tables of thine heart." 
Proverbs3:4 So shalt thou find favor and good understanding in the sight of God and men. 
Proverbs3:5 Put thy trust in the LORD with all thine heart, and lean not unto thine own understanding." 
Proverbs3:6 In all thy ways have respect unto him, and he shall order thy goings." 
Proverbs3:7 Be not wise in thine own conceit, but fear the LORD, and depart from evil:" 
Proverbs3:8 so shall thy navel be whole, and thy bones strong." 
Proverbs3:9 Honour the LORD with thy substance, and with the firstlings of all thine increase:" 
Proverbs3:10 So shall thy barns be filled with plenteousness, and thy presses shall flow over with sweet wine." 
Proverbs3:11 My son, despise not the chastening of the LORD, neither faint when thou are rebuked of him." 
Proverbs3:12 For whom the LORD loveth, him he chasteneth: and yet delighteth in him even as a father in his own son." 
Proverbs3:13 Well is him that findeth wisdom, and obtaineth understanding," 
Proverbs3:14 for the getting of it is better than any merchandise of silver, and the profit of it is better than gold." 
Proverbs3:15 Wisdom is more worth than precious stones, and all the things that thou canst desire, are not to be compared unto her." 
Proverbs3:16 Upon her right hand is long life, and upon her left hand is riches and honour." 
Proverbs3:17 Her ways are pleasant ways, and all her paths are peaceable." 
Proverbs3:18 She is a tree of life to them that lay hold upon her, and blessed are they that keep her fast." 
Proverbs3:19 With wisdom hath the LORD laid the foundation of the earth, and thorow understanding hath he stablished the heavens." 
Proverbs3:20 Thorow his wisdom the depths break up, and the clouds drop down of the dew." 
Proverbs3:21 My son, let not these things depart from thine eyes, but keep my law and my counsel:" 
Proverbs3:22 So shall it be life unto thy soul, and grace unto thy mouth." 
Proverbs3:23 Then shalt thou walk safely in thy way, and thy foot shall not stumble." 
Proverbs3:24 If thou sleepest thou shalt not be afraid, but shall take thy rest and sleep sweetly." 
Proverbs3:25 Thou needest not to be afraid of any sudden fear, neither for the violent rushing of the ungodly, when it cometh." 
Proverbs3:26 For the LORD shall be beside thee, and keep thy foot that thou be not taken." 
Proverbs3:27 Refuse not to do good unto him that should have it, so long as thine hand is able to do it." 
Proverbs3:28 Say not unto thy neighbour: Go thy way and come again, tomorrow will I give thee:" 
Proverbs3:29 where as thou hast now to give him. Intend no hurt unto thy neighbour, seeing he hopeth to dwell in rest by thee." 
Proverbs3:30 Strive not lightly with any man, where as he hath done thee no harm." 
Proverbs3:31 Follow not a wicked man, and choose none of his ways:" 
Proverbs3:32 for the LORD abhoreth the froward, but his secret is among the righteous." 
Proverbs3:33 The curse of the LORD is in the house of the ungodly, but he blesseth the dwellings of the righteous." 
Proverbs3:34 As for the scornful, he shall laugh them to scorn, but he shall give grace unto the lowly." 
Proverbs3:35 The wise shall have honour in possession, but shame is the promotion that fools shall have." 
Proverbs4:1 Hear, O ye children, the fatherly exhortation, and take good heed, that ye may learn wisdom." 
Proverbs4:2 Yea I shall give you a good reward, if ye will not forsake my law." 
Proverbs4:3 For when I my self was my father's dear son, and tenderly beloved of my mother," 
Proverbs4:4 he taught me also, saying: Let thine heart receive my words, keep my commandments, and thou shalt live." 
Proverbs4:5 Get thee wisdom, get thee understanding, forget not the words of my mouth, and shrink not from them." 
Proverbs4:6 Forsake her not, and she shall preserve thee: love her, and she shall keep thee." 
Proverbs4:7 The chief point of wisdom is, that thou be willing to obtain wisdom, and before all thy goods to get the understanding." 
Proverbs4:8 Make much of her and she shall promote thee: Yea if thou embracest her, she shall bring thee unto honour." 
Proverbs4:9 She shall make thee a gracious head, and garnish thee with a(the) crown of glory." 
Proverbs4:10 Hear, my son, and receive my words, that the years of thy life may be many." 
Proverbs4:11 I will shew thee, the way of wisdom, and lead thee in the right paths." 
Proverbs4:12 So that if thou goest therein, there shall no straitness hinder thee: and when thou runnest, thou shalt not fall." 
Proverbs4:13 Take fast hold of doctrine, let her not go: keep her, for she is thy life." 
Proverbs4:14 Come not in the path of the ungodly, and walk not in the way of the wicked." 
Proverbs4:15 Eschew it, and go not therein: depart aside, and pass over by it." 
Proverbs4:16 For they can not sleep, except they have first done some mischief: neither take they any rest, except they have first done some harm." 
Proverbs4:17 For they eat the bread of wickedness, and drink the wine of robbery." 
Proverbs4:18 The path of the righteous shineth as the light, and is ever brighter and brighter unto the perfect day." 
Proverbs4:19 But the way of the ungodly is as the darkness, wherein men fall, or they be aware.(awarre)" 
Proverbs4:20 My son, mark my words, and incline thine ear unto my sayings." 
Proverbs4:21 Let them not depart from thine eyes, keep them even in the middest of thine heart." 
Proverbs4:22 For they are life unto all those that find them, and health unto all their bodies." 
Proverbs4:23 Keep thine heart with all diligence, for there upon hangeth life." 
Proverbs4:24 Put away from thee a froward mouth, and let the lips of slander(sclaunder) be far from thee." 
Proverbs4:25 Let thine eyes behold the thing that is right, and let thine eyelids look straight before thee." 
Proverbs4:26 Ponder the path of thy feet, so shall all thy ways be sure." 
Proverbs4:27 Turn not aside, neither to the right hand nor to the left, but withhold thy foot from evil." 
Proverbs5:1 My son, give heed unto my wisdom, and bow thine ear to my prudence:" 
Proverbs5:2 that thou mayest regard good counsel, and that thy lips may keep nurture." 
Proverbs5:3 For the lips of an harlot are a dropping honeycomb, and her throat is softer than oil." 
Proverbs5:4 But at the last she is as bitter as wormwood, and as sharp as a two edged sword." 
Proverbs5:5 Her feet go down unto death, and her steps pierce thorow unto hell." 
Proverbs5:6 She regardeth not the path of life, so unsteadfast are her ways, that thou canst not know them." 
Proverbs5:7 Hear me therefore, (O my son) and depart not from the words of my mouth." 
Proverbs5:8 Keep thy way far from her, and come not nigh the doors of her house." 
Proverbs5:9 That thou give not thine honour unto another, and thy years to the cruel." 
Proverbs5:10 That other men be not filled with thy goods, and that thy labours come not in a strange house." 
Proverbs5:11 Yea that thou mourn not at the last, (when thou hast spent thy body and goods)" 
Proverbs5:12 and then say: Alas, why hated I nurture? why did my heart despise correction?" 
Proverbs5:13 Wherefore was not I obedient unto the voice of my teachers, and hearken not unto them that informed me?" 
Proverbs5:14 I am come almost into all misfortune, in the middest of the multitude and congregation." 
Proverbs5:15 Drink of the water of thine own well, and of the rivers that run out of thine own springs." 
Proverbs5:16 Let thy wells flow out abroad, that there may be rivers of water in the streets:" 
Proverbs5:17 but let them be only thine own, and not strangers' with thee." 
Proverbs5:18 Let thy well be blessed, and be glad with the wife of thy youth." 
Proverbs5:19 Loving is the hind, and friendly is the Roo: let her breasts alway satisfy thee, and hold thee ever content with her love." 
Proverbs5:20 My son, why wilt thou have pleasure in an harlot, and embrace the bosom of another woman?" 
Proverbs5:21 For every man's ways are open in the sight of the LORD, and he pondereth all their goings." 
Proverbs5:22 The wickedness of the ungodly shall catch himself, and with the snares of his own sins shall he be trapped." 
Proverbs5:23 Because he would not be reformed, he shall die: and for his great foolishness he shall be destroyed." 
Proverbs6:1 My son, if thou be surety for thy neighbour, thou hast fastened thine hand with another man:"
